Q3 2025 Record Financial Results: Q3 2025 revenue $180.2B (+13% YoY, +12% ex-FX). Net income $18.4B (EPS $1.95, beating $1.57 estimate by 24%). AWS revenue $33.0B (+20.2% YoY, highest growth in 11 quarters). Advertising revenue $17.7B (+24% YoY). Operating income $17.4B (including $2.5B FTC settlement, $1.8B severance). Q4 2025 guidance: revenue $206-213B, operating income $21-26B.​

Balance Sheet & Investment: FY2025 capex guidance raised to $125B (from prior guidance) for AI infrastructure, with further increases expected in 2026. Project Rainier ($11B AI data center for Anthropic) announced. Free cash flow $47.7B TTM. Workforce optimization: 14,000 job reductions announced. Investment-grade balance sheet maintained despite aggressive AI investment.​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 5-6% within global technology/e-commerce 10-12% allocation. Premier investment-grade credit (AA/A1) with dominant market positions, exceptional cash generation, and AI infrastructure leadership. Monitor: (1) Q4 2025 guidance delivery; (2) AWS growth acceleration trajectory; (3) AI infrastructure ROI; (4) Retail margin improvement; (5) Advertising revenue momentum.

issuers.name: AMDOCS LTD
issuers.creditComment:
AMDOCS LTD
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Affirmed Jan 2025) – Telecom/BSS-OSS Software & Services; High Customer Stickiness; Conservative Balance Sheet
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Amdocs Ltd [finance:Amdocs Ltd.] is an investment-grade software/services provider focused on telecom operators’ business support systems (BSS), operational support systems (OSS), and digital customer experience platforms. S&P affirmed Amdocs at ‘BBB’ with a Stable outlook in January 2025. Amdocs reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $4.53 billion (year ended Sept. 30, 2025), reflecting steady demand from communications and media enterprises undergoing cloud and AI-driven modernization.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) mission-critical nature of telecom billing/CRM platforms (high switching costs, long implementations); (b) sticky customer base; (c) recurring/long-duration contracts; (d) conservative financial profile typical of services + software hybrids. Risks include concentrated exposure to global telecom capex cycles, contract renewal timing, and competitive pressure from hyperscalers and systems integrators as telcos modernize to cloud-native stacks.
​

Financial Profile: While detailed leverage metrics are not in the search snippets, S&P’s ‘BBB’ Stable indicates modest leverage, solid FCF, and strong liquidity. Amdocs’ scale and contract structure generally support predictable cash generation, which is the core IG support for the name.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within IT Services/Software IG 4-5% allocation. Stable IG (BBB) with telecom modernization exposure. Monitor: (1) large-contract renewals; (2) telco capex and transformation budgets; (3) margin discipline amid delivery-cost inflation; (4) AI/cloud product attach rates.

issuers.name: AMERICA MOVIL SAB DE CV
issuers.creditComment:
AMERICA MOVIL, S.A.B. DE C.V.
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (S&P 2025) | A3/Stable (Moody's 2025) | A-/Stable (Fitch 2025) | A+/Stable (JCR 2025) – Leading Latin American Telecommunications Operator; 408M Access Lines; 323M Mobile Subscribers; MXN 233B Q3 2025 Revenue (+6.2% Organic); 1.4x Net Debt/EBITDA; 5G Coverage in 120+ Mexican Cities

Credit Momentum: Stable – América Móvil 
 maintains strong investment-grade ratings (A-/A3) reflecting leading market positions across Latin America, diversified geographic and product portfolio, and strong financial performance with Q3 2025 mobile service revenue growth of 7.1%—the fastest pace in two years. Fitch revised outlook to Stable from Negative in June 2025, noting improving net debt/EBITDA (1.8x LTM through March 2025) and CFO-capex/debt metrics. The company added 3+ million postpaid subscribers in Q3 2025 while maintaining conservative financial policy within 1.2-1.5x net debt/EBITDA target:

Credit Ratings & Sovereign Linkage:
América Móvil maintains A-/Stable ratings from S&P and Fitch, A3/Stable from Moody's, and A+/Stable from Japan Credit Rating Agency (JCR). JCR rates América Móvil above Mexico's sovereign rating (FC: A-/Stable) due to overseas revenue base from expanded international operations, solid financial position, and strong funding capacity in international capital markets; Mexico country ceiling is A+. Fitch June 2025: Revised outlook to Stable from Negative reflecting improving credit metrics; LTM net debt/EBITDA 1.8x (March 2025) with CFO-capex/debt 6.3%; Both indicators expected to improve over rating horizon. JCR assessment: Ratings primarily supported by leading position in Latin American telecom markets, diversified business bases from worldwide strategy, and stable funding capacity in international capital markets.​

Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
América Móvil reported strong Q3 2025 results (October 2025): Revenue: MXN 233 billion (+4.2% in Mexican peso terms, +6.2% at constant exchange rates). Mobile service revenue: +7.1% at constant FX—fastest pace in two years—with prepaid +3.9% (fastest in two years) and postpaid +9.1% (similar to Q2's 10-quarter high). EBITDA: MXN 94 billion (+4.9% MXN terms, +6.8% constant FX); Stable EBITDA margin. Net income: MXN 22.7 billion (threefold increase YoY), supported by reduced financing costs. Subscriber growth: Added 3+ million postpaid clients (Brazil 1.5M, Colombia 251K, Peru 198K, Mexico 98K); Fixed broadband +536K accesses (Mexico 211K, Brazil 86K, Argentina 56K, Colombia 51K). 5G expansion: Network now covers 120+ cities in Mexico, strengthening market leadership.​

Market Position & Geographic Diversification:
América Móvil operates the largest wireless and fixed-line telecom network in Latin America: Total access lines: 408 million (September 2025), including 323 million mobile subscribers and 140 million postpaid clients. Mexico (41% EBITDA): Leading position with ~55% wireless market share and ~35% fixed-line market share. Brazil: Second-largest wireless provider and leading fixed-line/pay TV provider in Latin America's largest telecom market. Other markets: Strong positions in Colombia, Chile, Argentina, Peru, Ecuador, Central America providing geographic diversification. Postpaid base: +8.1% YoY growth—fastest growing access category—followed by fixed broadband +5.1% YoY. América Móvil's scale and regional dominance provide competitive moats and pricing power across diverse Latin American economies.​

Leverage & Capital Allocation:
América Móvil maintains conservative financial policy: Net debt: Increased to MXN 485.1 billion (YE 2024) from MXN 385.4 billion (YE 2023); Net debt/EBITDA 1.4x (within 1.2-1.5x target range). November 2025 debt issuance: Ps.10 billion reopening of two series (Ps.3.5B 10.125% 2029 notes + Ps.6.5B 10.300% 2034 notes) for general corporate purposes and eligible sustainable projects. Bond debt outstanding approximately $19.3 billion across diversified instruments. Shareholder distributions: Significant capital returns and debt reduction reflecting robust cash flow management. JCR expects América Móvil to maintain financial soundness and keep net debt/EBITDA within target 1.2-1.5x range. Strong international capital markets access provides funding flexibility across currencies and tenors.​

Overall Assessment: América Móvil, S.A.B. de C.V. is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 5-6% within telecom/EM corporates 8-10% Latin America allocation. América Móvil represents strong investment-grade credit (A-/A3) with leading Latin American telecom position, diversified geographic/product portfolio, and accelerating revenue growth. Strengths include: (a) ratings above Mexico sovereign (A+/A-) reflecting international diversification and funding capacity; (b) leading Latin American telecom position (408M access lines, 323M mobile subscribers); (c) Q3 2025 mobile service revenue +7.1%—fastest pace in two years; (d) +3M postpaid subscriber net adds in Q3 demonstrating competitive strength; (e) conservative leverage (1.4x net debt/EBITDA within 1.2-1.5x target); (f) diversified revenue base (Mexico 41% EBITDA with strong Brazil/Colombia/Chile presence); (g) 5G leadership in Mexico (120+ cities covered); (h) strong international capital markets access and sustainable finance framework; (i) net income threefold increase YoY from reduced financing costs. Risks include: (a) Mexico country ceiling (A+) capping potential ratings upside; (b) Latin American macroeconomic volatility affecting consumer spending and currency translation; (c) regulatory risks (preponderant agent designations, spectrum auctions, pricing regulations); (d) fixed-line service revenue deceleration (4.7% Q3 vs 7.9% Q2) from corporate networks volatility; (e) prepaid net disconnections (31K) in Brazil/Ecuador/Chile indicating competitive pressure; (f) currency exposure across multiple Latin American markets; (g) competition from regional operators and global tech platforms. RECOMMENDED: Overweight leading LatAm telecom with accelerating growth, conservative leverage, and sovereign-plus ratings.

Monitor: (1) Mobile service revenue growth (7%+ pace sustainability and postpaid momentum); (2) Net debt/EBITDA (1.2-1.5x target maintenance amid capex requirements); (3) 5G deployment (120+ city coverage expansion and competitive positioning); (4) Brazil market share (Second-place wireless position and fixed-line leadership); (5) Mexico regulatory environment (Preponderant agent designations and competition policy).

issuers.name: AMERICAN EXPRESS CO
issuers.creditComment:
AMERICAN EXPRESS CO
Credit Rating: A/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Mar 2025) | A-/Stable (S&P Affirmed Jun 2025) – Premium Closed-Loop Payments Network; Strong Brand; Resilient Funding & Liquidity
​

Credit Momentum: Stable (High-Grade IG) – American Express Company [finance:American Express Company] remains a high-grade financial issuer with stable outlooks. Fitch affirmed the ‘A’ Long-Term IDR with Stable outlook in March 2025. S&P affirmed the ‘A-’ foreign-currency long-term rating with stable outlook in June 2025. The rating profile reflects AmEx’s premium customer base, resilient fee income, and integrated “closed-loop” model combining card issuance, network, and merchant acquisition.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) premium spend-focused customer franchise (higher FICO, stronger spend durability); (b) diversified revenue (discount revenue + net interest income + annual fees); (c) strong underwriting and risk management; (d) diversified funding strategy and liquidity management (including securitization programs and committed facilities). Risks are primarily cyclical (consumer and SME spending, credit costs in downturns) and competitive (co-brand economics, interchange/regulatory themes), but AmEx’s brand and affluent customer mix mitigate tail risk relative to mass-market card lenders.
​

Financial Profile: The A/A- category reflects strong capitalization and liquidity for a non-bank financial institution (and bank subsidiaries), with rating agencies emphasizing performance across stress scenarios. A key watch item is credit normalization (delinquencies/charge-offs) versus revenue resilience from premium spend and fees.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within US Payments/Consumer Finance IG 6-7% allocation. High-grade IG with strong franchise value. Monitor: (1) credit loss trends; (2) SME/consumer spend; (3) funding mix and deposit trends at bank subsidiaries; (4) co-brand renewal economics.

issuers.name: AMERIPRISE FINANCIAL INC
issuers.creditComment:
AMERIPRISE FINANCIAL INC
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Dec 2025) | A-/Stable (S&P; supported by note ratings) | A3/Stable (Moody’s, per company credit ratings page) – Wealth & Asset Management + Insurance/Risk Products; Strong Capital; Fee Revenue Base
​

Credit Momentum: Stable (Upper IG) – Ameriprise Financial, Inc. [finance:Ameriprise Financial, Inc.] is a diversified financial services firm spanning advice/wealth management, asset management, and insurance/risk products. Fitch affirmed Ameriprise at ‘A-’ with a Stable outlook in December 2025. Ameriprise’s own credit ratings page shows S&P: A- (Stable), Moody’s: A3 (Stable), and Fitch: A- (Stable), indicating consistent upper-investment-grade positioning.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) strong franchise in advice/wealth management and asset management with recurring fee revenues; (b) solid capitalization and liquidity; (c) diversified earnings streams; (d) prudent risk management in insurance/risk products. Risks include market sensitivity (AUM-based fees fall in equity drawdowns), spread/credit risk in insurance portfolios, and regulatory/capital framework changes. The A-/A3 profile implies agencies view Ameriprise as well-positioned to absorb market volatility without threatening debtholder protection.
​

Financial Profile: Upper-IG ratings for wealth/asset managers typically hinge on (1) earnings diversity and stability, (2) conservative leverage, and (3) strong holding company liquidity. Fitch’s stable outlook into late 2025 indicates Ameriprise continues to operate within those guardrails.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Asset/Wealth Management IG 5-6% allocation. Strong upper-IG (A-/A3) with market sensitivity. Monitor: (1) equity market volatility impacts on AUM fees; (2) insurance spread environment and credit losses; (3) capital return policy vs. holding company liquidity; (4) regulatory capital changes.

issuers.name: AMGEN INC
issuers.creditComment:
AMGEN INC.
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Jun 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BBB/Positive (Fitch Mar 2025) – Global Biotech Leader; 2024 Product Sales +19% YoY; $10B+ Debt Reduction by 2025; MariTide Obesity Drug Pipeline; ~50% EBITDA Margin; $8.7B IRS Tax Dispute Pending

Credit Momentum: Stable/Positive – Amgen 
 maintains solid investment-grade ratings with S&P at BBB+/Stable (outlook revised to Stable from Negative in June 2025) and Fitch at BBB/Positive (March 2025), reflecting successful debt reduction, strong product portfolio growth, and promising pipeline including obesity drug MariTide. Fitch revised outlook to Positive indicating potential upgrade if leverage declines below 3.25x and CFO-capex/debt exceeds 15% over next 12-24 months. Moody's rates Amgen Baa1/Stable. Company on track to repay over $10B debt by end of 2025 following Horizon Therapeutics acquisition:​

Credit Ratings & Deleveraging Progress:
Amgen maintains BBB+/Stable from S&P (June 2025), Baa1/Stable from Moody's, BBB/F2/Positive from Fitch (March 2025). S&P June 2025 revision: Outlook revised to Stable from Negative reflecting steady deleveraging and improved EBITDA from portfolio growth. Fitch Positive outlook: Based on expectation Amgen will reduce debt, increase revenues from new products, lower EBITDA leverage below 3.25x and boost CFO-capex/debt above 15% over next 12-24 months. Deleveraging target: On track to repay over $10B debt by end of 2025, positioning to meet or exceed positive rating sensitivities despite suspended share repurchases.​

Strong Product Portfolio Growth:
Amgen delivered exceptional 2024 performance: Product sales: +19% YoY driven by diverse portfolio of first-in-class medicines and ten products achieving double-digit growth. Horizon therapies: Contributing to top-line growth alongside established products and biosimilars. Profitability: ~50% EBITDA margin surpassing Bayer 
, Viatris 
, AstraZeneca 
, Novartis 
, and Roche 
. Revenue guidance: 5.5% CAGR through FY2028; Adjusted EBITDA margins 47-50% over forecast period. Free cash flow: Increasing FCF driven by sales growth, supporting dividends (6.7% CAGR) and resumption of $1.0B annual buybacks in FY2026.​

Pipeline & Key Risks:
Amgen advancing promising R&D pipeline: MariTide obesity drug: Expected to generate substantial revenue by 2030 capitalizing on growing obesity market; Successful launch could influence rating upgrade. Recent approvals: IMDELLTRA™ and BLINCYTO®; Positive clinical data for TEZSPIRE® and UPLIZNA®. R&D productivity: Fitch rates bbb+ on Rating Navigator—lower than AstraZeneca (a+), Novartis (aa), Roche (aa), indicating comparatively less robust pipeline. Patent exposure: Prolia and XGEVA (~19% of sales) faced patent expiration February 2025; Biosimilar competition affecting several products representing less than 5% total revenues. IRS dispute: Contesting $8.7B additional taxes plus interest/penalties for 2010-2015 related to US-Puerto Rico profit allocation; Decision expected 2026. Tariff risk: Proposed 25% pharmaceutical tariff could significantly impact global supply chain costs and pricing strategy.​

Overall Assessment: Amgen Inc. is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within biotech 6-7% healthcare allocation. Amgen represents solid investment-grade credit (BBB+/Baa1) with Positive outlook from Fitch, strong portfolio growth, and deleveraging progress toward potential upgrade. Strengths include: (a) BBB+/Baa1/BBB ratings with Fitch Positive outlook; (b) 2024 product sales +19% YoY with ten products in double-digit growth; (c) ~50% EBITDA margin—superior profitability vs biotech peers; (d) $10B+ debt reduction by 2025 supporting deleveraging; (e) MariTide obesity drug pipeline with substantial revenue potential by 2030; (f) Horizon Therapeutics integration enhancing growth profile; (g) strong FCF supporting dividends and capital allocation; (h) recent FDA approvals (IMDELLTRA, BLINCYTO) demonstrating execution. Risks include: (a) $8.7B IRS tax dispute with 2026 decision pending; (b) Prolia/XGEVA biosimilar competition (19% sales exposure); (c) proposed pharmaceutical tariffs affecting supply chain costs; (d) R&D productivity rated lower than AstraZeneca/Novartis/Roche; (e) formulary management affecting drug access and pricing; (f) MariTide competitive dynamics in crowded obesity market; (g) share repurchase suspension affecting capital returns. RECOMMENDED: Overweight biotech leader with deleveraging momentum, superior profitability, and Positive outlook supporting potential upgrade within 12-24 months.

Monitor: (1) Leverage trajectory (Below 3.25x debt/EBITDA target); (2) MariTide launch (Obesity drug commercialization and revenue ramp); (3) IRS dispute (2026 decision and financial impact); (4) Biosimilar competition (Prolia/XGEVA volume and pricing trends); (5) Fitch upgrade (Positive outlook resolution).

issuers.name: AMPHENOL CORP
issuers.creditComment:
AMPHENOL CORP
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (S&P Upgraded Feb 2025) | A3/Stable (Moody's Affirmed May 2025) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch estimated; not explicitly found in 2025 updates) – Interconnect & Sensor Leader; AI/Datacom Growth; Strong FCF
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgraded by S&P) – Amphenol Corp. [finance:Amphenol Corp.] has achieved positive rating momentum, reflecting its successful scaling and diversification. S&P upgraded the issuer credit rating to 'A-' from 'BBB+' in February 2025, with a Stable outlook. Moody's affirmed the 'A3' rating (equivalent to A-) with a Stable outlook in May 2025. The upgrades reflect Amphenol's strong execution, tripling of revenues/FCF over a decade, and successful integration of acquisitions (including Carlisle Interconnect and CommScope assets) while maintaining leverage below 1.5x.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) market leadership in interconnects, antennas, and sensors with broad diversification; (b) strong exposure to high-growth IT datacom (AI/cloud) and defense markets; (c) consistent EBITDA margins in mid-20% area; (d) robust free cash flow generation; (e) disciplined acquisition strategy with rapid deleveraging track record. S&P expects leverage to decrease to ~1.0x by YE2025 from a peak of ~1.4x post-acquisitions. The company has scale comparable to peer TE Connectivity. Risks include cyclical end-market exposure and integration of large acquisitions.
​

Financial Profile: Amphenol maintains a pristine balance sheet. S&P expects leverage to moderate to ~1.0x by end of 2025. The company generated ~$4 billion adjusted EBITDA in 2024 on $15.2 billion revenue. Liquidity is strong with $3 billion undrawn revolver and $1.2 billion cash. The company successfully funded the $2.1 billion CommScope asset purchase largely via debt but retains significant capacity. FCF generation supports both capital allocation (M&A/returns) and deleveraging.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Electronics Components 5-6% allocation. Core IG credit (A-/A3) with AI/Datacom tailwinds. Monitor: (1) AI datacom demand sustainability; (2) Integration of Carlisle/CommScope assets; (3) M&A pipeline and leverage discipline; (4) EBITDA margin stability (mid-20s% target).

issuers.name: AMRIZE FINANCE US LLC
issuers.creditComment:
AMRIZE FINANCE US LLC (AMRIZE LTD.)
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Mar 2025) – North American Building Solutions Spin-Off from Holcim; $11.5-12B Annual Revenue; 26-28% EBITDA Margins; <2x Leverage Target; Cement Leadership; Infrastructure Investment Beneficiary

Credit Momentum: Stable/New Issue – Amrize Ltd. 
 received BBB+/Stable ratings from S&P (March 2025) upon spin-off from Holcim 
, reflecting large scale and leadership position within North American cement industry, conservative leverage profile (<2x adjusted net leverage), and strong infrastructure demand tailwinds. The spin-off creates independent publicly listed company with $11.5-12B revenue, 26-28% EBITDA margins, and strong credit cushion with FFO-to-debt comfortably above 45%:

Credit Ratings & Spin-Off Structure:
Amrize maintains BBB+/Stable from S&P and A-2 short-term rating (assigned March 2025). S&P rationale: BBB+ reflects large scale and leadership position within North American cement industry; Adjusted leverage at or under 2x; FFO-to-debt well above 45%. Capital structure: $2B unsecured revolving credit facility (undrawn at close), $2B commercial paper program, ~$5.5B unsecured debt. Subsidiary ratings: BBB+ ratings extended to operating subsidiaries including Holcim (US) Inc.. Parent separation: Holcim completed spin-off H1 2025, creating independent publicly listed Amrize Ltd..​

Financial Profile & Projections:
S&P expects strong financial metrics post-spin: Revenue: $12.0-12.5B annually (2025-2026). EBITDA: $3.2-3.35B with 26-28% adjusted margins. Free cash flow: ~$1.5-1.6B annually (operating cash flow minus capex). 2025 metrics: Adjusted net debt ~$4.5B; Leverage 1x-1.5x; FFO-to-debt ~55%—representing strong credit cushion. Target metrics: Adjusted leverage at or under 2x; FFO-to-debt comfortably above 45%; FOCF-to-debt over 25%. Financial discipline around future investments key to preserving credit quality per S&P.​

Market Position & Growth Drivers:
Amrize operates premier North American building materials franchise: Market leadership: Leading position within North American cement industry with scale advantages. Infrastructure tailwinds: IIJA infrastructure spending supporting aggregates and cement demand. End markets: Residential, non-residential, and infrastructure construction providing diversified demand. Geographic focus: Pure-play North American exposure providing favorable regulatory environment. Growth strategy: Capital allocation focused on organic and inorganic growth initiatives. Holcim heritage: Benefits from Holcim's operational excellence and sustainability leadership.​

Overall Assessment: Amrize Finance US LLC (Amrize Ltd.) is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within building materials 6-7% North American industrials allocation. Amrize represents solid investment-grade credit (BBB+/Stable) with cement industry leadership, conservative leverage, and infrastructure demand tailwinds. Strengths include: (a) BBB+/Stable rating reflecting strong standalone credit profile; (b) North American cement leadership with scale advantages; (c) conservative <2x leverage target with 1x-1.5x pro-forma 2025; (d) 26-28% EBITDA margins demonstrating strong profitability; (e) $1.5-1.6B annual FCF generation capacity; (f) IIJA infrastructure spending supporting demand; (g) diversified end-market exposure; (h) strong credit cushion (FFO-to-debt ~55%). Risks include: (a) construction cycle sensitivity; (b) residential slowdown offsetting infrastructure; (c) M&A execution risk from growth strategy; (d) potential leverage elevation from acquisitions; (e) input cost inflation (energy, materials); (f) competition in regional cement markets. RECOMMENDED: Overweight North American cement leader with conservative leverage, strong margins, and infrastructure tailwinds supporting credit stability.

Monitor: (1) Leverage maintenance (<2x target and 1x-1.5x pro-forma execution); (2) Infrastructure demand (IIJA project flow-through to cement volumes); (3) M&A activity (Acquisition strategy and leverage impacts); (4) Margin sustainability (26-28% EBITDA through construction cycle); (5) FCF generation ($1.5-1.6B annual target achievement).

issuers.name: ANALOG DEVICES INC
issuers.creditComment:
ANALOG DEVICES INC
Credit Rating: A3/Stable (Moody's) | A-/Stable (Fitch) | BBB+/Stable (S&P) – Global Semiconductor Leader; FY2025 Revenue $11.0B +17% YoY; Operating Margin 41.9%; EPS $7.79 +22% YoY; Record Free Cash Flow $4.3B (39% of Revenue); Net Leverage 0.9x​

Credit Momentum: Strong with Upside Potential – Analog Devices Inc. maintains solid investment-grade ratings with improving credit trajectory driven by record earnings, exceptional cash generation, and conservative leverage profile. AI/data center demand acceleration provides secular growth tailwinds.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Analog Devices maintains A3/Stable from Moody's and A-/Stable from Fitch. Ratings reflect: (a) leading analog semiconductor market positions; (b) high recurring revenue from automotive, industrial, communications customers; (c) exceptional profitability (69.3% gross margin, 41.9% operating margin); (d) conservative leverage (0.9x net debt/EBITDA); (e) strong free cash flow generation enabling shareholder returns and deleveraging.​

FY2025 Record Financial Results: FY2025 revenue $11.0B (+17% YoY), with Q4 revenue $3.08B (+26% YoY, +7% QoQ). Gross margin 69.3% (+140 bps YoY), operating margin 41.9% (+100 bps YoY). EPS $7.79 (+22% YoY). Operating cash flow $4.8B, free cash flow $4.3B (39% of revenue, up from 33% prior year). All end markets achieved double-digit growth: Industrial (+34% YoY), Communications (+37%), Automotive (+19%), Consumer (+7%).​

Balance Sheet & Liquidity: Cash and short-term investments $3.7B. Net leverage ratio 0.9x. Channel inventory approximately six weeks (lean). Returned $4.1B to shareholders (dividends + buybacks), including 8% dividend increase. Q1 FY2026 guidance: revenue ~$3.1B, operating margin 43.5%, EPS ~$2.29.​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within global semiconductor 8-10% allocation. High-quality investment-grade credit with record profitability, AI/automotive growth catalysts, and conservative leverage. Strengths: (a) 69% gross margin leadership; (b) diversified end-market exposure; (c) record FCF (39% of revenue); (d) <1x net leverage. Risks: (a) semiconductor cyclicality; (b) inventory normalization. Monitor: (1) Q1 FY2026 guidance delivery; (2) AI revenue acceleration; (3) Automotive content gains; (4) Channel inventory trends.

issuers.name: ANGLO AMERICAN CAPITAL
issuers.creditComment:
ANGLO AMERICAN CAPITAL
Credit Rating: BBB/Negative (S&P 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Anglo American PLC Financing Subsidiary; Diversified Miner; Portfolio Simplification; >$7B Asset Sale Proceeds

Credit Momentum: Mixed – Anglo American Capital, the financing subsidiary of Anglo American PLC 
, carries divergent outlooks from S&P (BBB/Negative) versus Moody's/Fitch (Baa1/Stable, BBB+/Stable). S&P removed Anglo from CreditWatch Negative (June 2025, affirming BBB) with Negative outlook reflecting execution risks on planned $7B+ asset sale proceeds. The company's portfolio simplification strategy (exiting coal, nickel, PGM assets) is designed to create a leaner, iron ore/copper-focused company with limited debt. S&P expects leverage to decline from current ~12B to $2-3B by end-2026, supporting recovery. However, near-term uncertainty around Peabody coal asset sale ($3.8B) and timing of proceeds creates Negative watch. Moody's and Fitch maintain Stable outlooks, more favorable to execution assumptions.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Anglo American Capital holds ratings of BBB (S&P, Negative), Baa1 (Moody's, Stable), and BBB+ (Fitch, Stable). The rating divergence reflects S&P's more conservative view on execution risk. Leverage profile: Current adjusted debt ~$12B; S&P forecasts leverage declining to 2.0x-3.0x adjusted leverage by end-2026 post-proceeds.

Strategic Portfolio Simplification:
Anglo American announced major portfolio transformation (May 2024): Coal assets sale to Peabody Energy (~$3.8B; Q3 2025 completion); Nickel business sale (~$500M); PGM assets divestiture. YTD asset sale proceeds received ~$1.0B; ultimate realization $7B+ expected. New company focus: Iron ore (Kumba, Minas Rio high-margin), copper (Los Bronces, Collahuasi).

Financial Profile & Commodity Exposure:
Anglo's simpler portfolio focus provides clearer margins and deleveraging path. Iron ore and copper provide strong FCF generation under normal commodity pricing. Estimated FCF contributions: Iron ore $550-600M, Copper $450-500M annually under medium-term prices.

Strategic Initiatives:
Strategic Priorities: (a) Asset Sale Execution – Completing coal/nickel/PGM dispositions; (b) Leverage Reduction – Target adjusted debt $2-3B by end-2026; (c) Capital Allocation – Balance sheet strengthening over shareholder returns during transition; (d) FCF Growth – New portfolio generates simpler, higher-margin earnings.

Competitive Position:
Market position: Global diversified miner; post-simplification, focused on iron ore and copper leadership with strong cost positions.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
Capital prioritized toward debt reduction. Dividends modest during portfolio transition; normalization expected post-2026 when leverage targets achieved.

Overall Assessment: Anglo American Capital is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – Mining Diversification with Execution Risk. A global miner undergoing transformational portfolio simplification. Strengths: (a) BBB/Baa1/BBB+ ratings with clear deleveraging path; (b) >$7B asset sale proceeds (2/3 committed/anticipated); (c) Iron ore/copper portfolio provides simpler, high-margin earnings; (d) Strong FCF generation potential. Risks: (a) S&P Negative outlook reflects execution risk on Peabody coal sale; (b) Asset sale timing uncertainty; (c) Commodity price cyclicality; (d) China demand sensitivity (iron ore).

Monitor: (1) Peabody coal asset sale completion (critical for S&P outlook); (2) Asset sale proceeds timing and realization; (3) Leverage trajectory toward $2-3B target; (4) Iron ore/copper pricing and volume trends; (5) S&P rating actions.

issuers.name: ANHEUSER-BUSCH INBEV FIN
issuers.creditComment:
ANHEUSER-BUSCH INBEV FINANCE INC. (AB INBEV)
Credit Rating: A-/Positive (S&P Mar 2025) | A3/Positive (Moody's May 2025) | A (low)/Stable (DBRS 2025) – World's Largest Brewer; 2024 EBITDA Margin 36%; Debt/EBITDA 2.9x (Below 3.0x First Time Since 2015); $7.6B DCF Deployment; $11B Annual FOCF; ~27% Global Beer Volume Share

Credit Momentum: Positive/Upgrade Path – Anheuser-Busch InBev 
 achieved Positive outlooks from both S&P (March 2025) and Moody's (May 2025), reflecting materially stronger-than-expected 2024 performance with adjusted debt/EBITDA falling to 2.9x—below 3.0x for first time since 2015 and the pre-SABMiller acquisition era. S&P revised outlook to Positive, affirming A-/A-2 ratings; Moody's revised to Positive, affirming A3/P-2. Rating agencies expect continued deleveraging toward 2.4-2.6x by 2026 on $11B annual FOCF generation, positioning AB InBev for potential upgrade within 12-24 months if financial policy remains supportive:​

Credit Ratings & Deleveraging Achievement:
AB InBev maintains A-/Positive from S&P (March 2025), A3/Positive from Moody's (May 2025), A (low)/Stable from DBRS (2025). S&P March 2025 revision: Outlook changed to Positive from Stable, affirming A-/A-2; Debt/EBITDA improved to 2.9x from 3.4x (2023) and 3.6x (2022)—now below 3.0x for first time since pre-SABMiller 2015. Moody's May 2025 affirmation: A3/Positive reflecting continued strong operating performance and consistent financial policy. Leverage target: Company stated optimal reported net debt leverage target of 2.0x, which would support higher rating. 2026 trajectory: S&P expects debt/EBITDA falling toward 2.6-2.7x (2025) and 2.4-2.5x (2026)—metrics supporting potential upgrade if achieved on sustained basis.​

2024 Exceptional Performance:
AB InBev materially outperformed base-case forecasts: EBITDA margin: 36% (vs 35% forecast) representing 200bp improvement from 2023, demonstrating operational efficiency and digital infrastructure benefits. Revenue growth: 0.7% reported (+2.7% organic ex-FX) reflecting 1.4% volume decline offset by 4.3% net revenue per hectoliter growth from pricing, premiumization, and product mix. Free cash flow: $7.6B discretionary cash flow ($4.4-4.7B forecast) largely deployed to debt reduction. FOCF generation: Approximately $11B annually expected, supporting continued deleveraging. Market leadership: ~27% global beer volume share and ~30% value share—approximately double second-placed Heineken 
.​

Strategic Positioning & Outlook:
AB InBev well positioned for continued performance: Beer sector resilience: Beer category outperforming spirits due to affordability in weak consumer environment; Resilient and large category underpins debt reduction. Portfolio strength: Category-leading portfolio of brands with clear global leadership; Low-to-no-alcohol beer and Beyond Beer products growing. 2025 guidance: ~1% revenue growth from continued FX headwinds; Return to ~1% positive volume growth from US recovery, stable Middle America and EMEA. Developed market risk: North America and Europe (~30% reported EBITDA) historically in volume decline, which could slow deleveraging if consumer environment deteriorates. Upgrade path: Rating upgrade possible within 12-24 months if solid operating performance continues and financial policy supports debt/EBITDA sustainably below 3.0x.​

Overall Assessment: Anheuser-Busch InBev Finance Inc. is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within beverages 6-7% consumer staples allocation. AB InBev represents strong investment-grade credit (A-/A3) with Positive outlook from both S&P and Moody's, exceptional deleveraging progress, and global beer leadership positioning for potential upgrade. Strengths include: (a) A-/A3 ratings with Positive outlook from S&P and Moody's; (b) debt/EBITDA 2.9x—below 3.0x for first time since pre-SABMiller 2015; (c) 36% EBITDA margin (+200bp YoY) demonstrating operational excellence; (d) $7.6B DCF generation deployed to debt reduction; (e) ~27% global beer volume and ~30% value share—double nearest competitor; (f) $11B annual FOCF supporting continued deleveraging; (g) expected leverage 2.4-2.5x by 2026; (h) beer category resilience vs spirits in weak consumer environment; (i) 2.0x optimal leverage target supporting higher rating. Risks include: (a) developed market beer volume decline (North America, Europe); (b) emerging market FX headwinds affecting reported revenue; (c) consumer environment deterioration slowing deleveraging; (d) US market challenges post-2023/2024 volume decline; (e) category-wide alcohol consumption headwinds (Gen Z, GLP-1 medications); (f) M&A risk if financial policy shifts toward acquisitions. RECOMMENDED: Overweight global beer leader with Positive outlook, deleveraging momentum, and upgrade trajectory within 12-24 months.

Monitor: (1) Leverage trajectory (2.9x → 2.4-2.5x by 2026); (2) Volume trends (US recovery and developed market stabilization); (3) EBITDA margin (36% sustainability and expansion); (4) Rating upgrade (S&P/Moody's Positive outlook resolution); (5) Financial policy (2.0x optimal leverage target and capital allocation).

issuers.name: ANTOFAGASTA PLC
issuers.creditComment:
ANTOFAGASTA PLC
Credit Rating: Baa1/Stable (Moody's) | BBB+/Stable (S&P) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch New Notes April 2025) – Chile's Largest Private Copper Producer; YTD Production 476,600t (+2.8% YoY); Q3 Net Cash Cost $1.35/lb (Record By-Product Credit); 2025 Capex $3.6B; Los Pelambres & Centinela Operations​

Credit Momentum: Strong with Operational Excellence – Antofagasta plc maintains solid investment-grade ratings reflecting low-cost Chilean copper operations, strong by-product contribution, and conservative financial policy under controlling Luksic family ownership.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Antofagasta maintains Baa1/Stable from Moody's and BBB+/Stable from S&P. Fitch rated proposed senior unsecured notes BBB+ (April 2025). Ratings reflect: (a) Chile's largest private copper producer; (b) Tier-1 asset base (Los Pelambres, Centinela); (c) low net cash costs; (d) strong by-product credits (gold, molybdenum); (e) conservative financial policy; (f) experienced Luksic family ownership.​

Q3 2025 & Production Profile: Q3 2025 copper production 161,800t (+1% QoQ), YTD 476,600t (+2.8% YoY). FY2025 guidance: lower end of 660-700kt range. Record by-product credit $1.35/lb driven by higher gold production and favorable pricing. Net cash cost guidance lowered to $1.20-1.30/lb (from $1.50-1.70/lb). Cash costs before by-products: $2.25-2.45/lb (unchanged). 2025 capex guidance lowered to $3.6B (from $3.9B) due to peso depreciation.​

FY2026 Outlook & Projects: FY2026 guidance: 650,000-700,000t copper with higher output from Los Pelambres. Centinela Second Concentrator project advancing. Strong margins maintained at Los Pelambres and Centinela. Conservative balance sheet with net debt/EBITDA well below 1.0x. Dividend policy tied to underlying earnings.​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within global copper mining 5-6% allocation. Solid investment-grade copper credit (Baa1/BBB+) with low-cost operations, record by-product credits, and conservative financial policy. Monitor: (1) FY2025 production at lower guidance (660kt); (2) Centinela project execution; (3) Copper price environment; (4) Chile political/regulatory stability; (5) By-product contribution sustainability.

issuers.name: AP MOLLER-MAERSK A/S
issuers.creditComment:
A.P. MOLLER - MAERSK A/S
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Affirmed June 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's) – Global Shipping & Logistics Leader; Strong Balance Sheet Despite Sector Cyclicality
​
Credit Momentum: Stable – A.P. Moller - Maersk maintains a solid investment-grade profile. S&P affirmed its 'BBB+' rating with a stable outlook in June 2025, reflecting the company's robust balance sheet and successful diversification into logistics. Moody's revised Maersk's outlook to Positive in some recent reports, citing strong performance, though the core rating remains at Baa2 (equivalent to BBB).
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are underpinned by: (a) position as the world's second-largest container shipping carrier; (b) conservative financial policy with a net cash position (or very low leverage); (c) growing non-ocean logistics business (~25-30% of revenue) which provides more stable earnings; (d) massive liquidity buffer.
​
Financial Profile: Despite the normalization of freight rates from pandemic highs, Maersk's credit metrics remain very strong for its rating category. The company continues to use its cash pile for share buybacks and bolt-on acquisitions in land-based logistics to reduce dependency on volatile ocean freight rates.
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Global Transport/Logistics 5-6% allocation. Defensive cyclical credit (BBB+/Baa2) with "fortress" balance sheet. Monitor: (1) Global trade volume growth; (2) Container freight rate stability; (3) Integration of logistics acquisitions; (4) Fleet decarbonization capex (green methanol vessels).

issuers.name: APA CORP
issuers.creditComment:
APA CORP (APACHE CORPORATION)
Credit Rating: BBB-/Stable (S&P Assigned 2024; Affirmed by Fitch as well) | Ba2/Stable (Moody's, estimated) – Oil & Gas E&P; Merger of Apache & APA (formerly Altus Midstream); Disciplined Capex
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – APA Corp. 
 was formed through the merger of Apache Corporation and APA (formerly Altus Midstream, Apache's midstream subsidiary) in 2024. S&P assigned the 'BBB-' issuer credit rating at the time of the merger combination, with a stable outlook. Fitch affirmed APA's ratings at 'BBB-' following the acquisition announcement. The ratings reflect the combined entity's asset base, cost structure, and financial discipline. The company benefits from Apache's operational expertise in the Permian, Gulf of Mexico, and other key basins.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are driven by: (a) diversified geographic and product portfolio (oil, gas, LNG); (b) low-cost operations in key basins; (c) operational integration synergies from the merger; (d) disciplined financial approach (low leverage target). The rating is constrained by commodity price exposure and capital intensity of E&P operations.
​

Financial Profile: APA maintains a solid balance sheet aligned with investment-grade credit metrics. The company prioritizes debt reduction and disciplined capex aligned with cash flow generation. Liquidity is adequate with cash and revolver availability. The merger is expected to generate cost synergies that support credit quality. Free cash flow is expected to support debt service and shareholder returns.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within US E&P 4-5% allocation. Core IG E&P credit (BBB-) with integrated midstream exposure. Monitor: (1) Oil and gas prices; (2) Merger synergy realization; (3) Permian and GoM production trends; (4) Debt reduction pace post-integration.

issuers.name: APA INFRASTRUCTURE LTD
issuers.creditComment:
APA INFRASTRUCTURE LTD
Credit Rating: Baa2/Stable (Moody's 2025) – Australian Pipeline Operator; Essential Infrastructure Asset; Limited Public Rating Information; Regulated Tariff Model

Credit Momentum: Stable – APA Infrastructure Limited (APA Group), Australia's largest owner of energy infrastructure (pipelines, gas distribution, electricity transmission), maintains investment-grade rating of Baa2/Stable from Moody's (affirmed September 2024). The company operates essential infrastructure assets serving Australia's energy market with regulated tariff-based revenue, providing stable cash flows. Limited public ratings disclosure from S&P/Fitch; APA is primarily rated by Moody's on local currency basis. The company's stability reflects its essential role in Australia's energy infrastructure and consistent dividend policy supported by regulated returns.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
APA Infrastructure holds Moody's Baa2/Stable rating (local currency basis). The company maintains investment-grade positioning reflecting regulated utility characteristics. Leverage profile: As a regulated pipeline operator with take-or-pay contracts, APA maintains moderate leverage with high FCF visibility through regulated returns.

Financial Profile:
APA Group operates as Australia's leading pipeline and energy infrastructure operator. Assets include natural gas pipelines, distribution networks, electricity transmission, and logistics infrastructure. The company generates stable cash flows through regulated tariff mechanisms with cost recovery structures.

Strategic Initiatives:
Strategic Priorities: (a) Infrastructure Modernization – Investing in grid reliability and security; (b) Energy Transition – Supporting renewable energy integration; (c) Dividend Growth – Maintaining investor returns from stable cash flows; (d) Asset Management – Optimizing portfolio of complementary infrastructure assets.

Competitive Position:
Market position: Australia's largest energy infrastructure operator; quasi-monopoly position in several corridors with regulatory protection.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
APA prioritizes shareholder distributions through dividends, supported by stable cash flows from regulated operations. Capital deployed toward infrastructure maintenance and strategic upgrades.

Overall Assessment: APA Infrastructure Ltd is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – Australian Infrastructure Dividend Play. An essential pipeline operator with stable regulated cash flows. Strengths: (a) Baa2/Stable rating (investment-grade); (b) Essential infrastructure role with regulatory protection; (c) Stable dividend supported by regulated returns. Risks: (a) Energy transition pressure (gas pipeline demand); (b) Australian macro sensitivity; (c) Regulatory tariff pressure; (d) Limited credit agency coverage (Moody's only).

Monitor: (1) Dividend sustainability and growth; (2) Regulatory tariff outcomes; (3) Energy transition impact on gas infrastructure; (4) Australian energy market dynamics.

issuers.name: APPLE INC
issuers.creditComment:
APPLE INC
Credit Rating: Aaa/Stable (Moody's) | AA+/Stable (S&P) – World's Most Valuable Company; Q4 FY2025 Revenue $102.5B Record; Services $28.8B All-Time High +15% YoY; EPS $1.85 +13% YoY; FY2025 Revenue $416B Record; $24B Returned to Shareholders Q4​

Credit Momentum: Unmatched Financial Strength – Apple Inc. maintains the highest credit ratings among technology companies reflecting exceptional profitability, fortress balance sheet, and unrivaled consumer ecosystem loyalty.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Apple maintains Aaa/Stable from Moody's and AA+/Stable from S&P. Ratings reflect: (a) dominant premium consumer electronics positions (iPhone, Mac, iPad, Wearables); (b) Services revenue exceeding $100B annually; (c) exceptional margins (75.3% Services gross margin, 36.2% Products); (d) fortress balance sheet ($34B net cash); (e) unrivaled customer loyalty and ecosystem stickiness; (f) consistent shareholder returns.​

Q4 FY2025 Record Results: Q4 FY2025 revenue $102.5B (record, +8% YoY). Diluted EPS $1.85 (+13% YoY adjusted). Services revenue $28.8B (all-time record, +15% YoY). iPhone revenue $49.0B (September quarter record). Mac revenue $8.7B, iPad $7.0B, Other Products $9.0B. FY2025 total revenue $416B (record, +6% YoY). Customer satisfaction 95%. Active device installed base at all-time high across all categories.​

Balance Sheet & Capital Returns: Net cash $34B ($99B debt offset by $133B cash/securities). Q4 shareholder returns $24B ($20B buybacks + $3.9B dividends). FY2026 Q1 guidance: revenue growth 10-12% (double-digit iPhone growth), gross margin 47-48%, operating expenses $18.1-18.5B. Significant AI investment continuing.​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 6-8% within global technology 15-18% allocation. Premier investment-grade credit (Aaa/AA+) with exceptional profitability, fortress balance sheet, and unrivaled ecosystem. Monitor: (1) FY2026 Q1 guidance delivery (10-12% growth); (2) Services growth sustainability; (3) China market recovery; (4) AI/Apple Intelligence adoption; (5) iPhone 17 cycle performance.

issuers.name: APPLOVIN CORP
issuers.creditComment:
APPLOVIN CORPORATION
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BBB/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Mobile Advertising Technology Leader; $1.41B Q3 2025 Revenue (+68% YoY); 82% Adjusted EBITDA Margin; $1.05B Q3 Free Cash Flow; AXON AI Recommendation Engine; E-Commerce Expansion; S&P 500 Inclusion

Credit Momentum: Positive/Upgraded – AppLovin 
 achieved significant credit improvement with Fitch upgrading Long-Term IDR to BBB (from BBB-) in November 2025, and S&P upgrading to BBB (from BBB-) in July 2025, reflecting leading position in mobile gaming, increased scale with over $11 billion in gross spend across platforms, and stronger-than-expected operating performance. Q3 2025 delivered 68% revenue growth, 82% EBITDA margin, and $1.05 billion free cash flow, with guidance for continued margin expansion. The company maintains conservative financial policy with EBITDA leverage below 2x:

Credit Ratings & Recent Upgrades:
AppLovin maintains BBB/Stable ratings from S&P, Fitch, and Moody's following two-notch upgrades in 2025. Fitch November 2025 upgrade: Raised IDR to BBB from BBB- citing leading position in mobile gaming, increased scale ($11B+ gross spend across platforms), and stronger-than-expected operating performance; AXON AI enhancements driving improved ad performance for customers; Free cash flow margins expected to exceed 50%. S&P July 2025 upgrade: Raised issuer credit rating to BBB from BBB- reflecting improved credit metrics and consistent growth trajectory. Moody's assessment: Baa2/Stable reflecting strong profitability, cash generation, and conservative financial policy with EBITDA leverage remaining below 2x. Fitch expects AppLovin to maintain conservative financial policy despite strong cash generation capacity.​

Q3 2025 Record Financial Performance:
AppLovin reported exceptional Q3 2025 results (November 5, 2025): Revenue: $1.41 billion (+68% YoY)—significantly exceeding analyst forecasts of ~$1.34 billion. Profitability: Adjusted EBITDA $1.16 billion (+79% YoY) with 82% margin (vs 77.5% Q3 2024). Earnings: Net income from continuing operations $836 million (+92% YoY vs $433 million Q3 2024); EPS $2.45 (vs ~$1.34 consensus). Cash flow: Free cash flow $1.05 billion; Net cash from operations $2.66 billion (nine months). Nine-month performance: Revenue $3.82 billion (+72% YoY); Adjusted EBITDA margins expanded significantly. S&P 500 inclusion achieved reflecting strong business performance and broader investor expectations.​​

AXON AI Platform & E-Commerce Expansion:
AppLovin's growth is driven by AXON AI-based recommendation engine enhancements: AXON technology: Continuous improvements driving improved ad performance for customers, leading to increased spending on user acquisition and monetization platforms; Strengthened customer retention and expanded market share. Gaming dominance: Leading position in mobile gaming with over $11 billion gross spend across platforms; Revenue scale more than double Unity Software 
 ($5B+ vs ~$2B). E-commerce expansion: Annualized customer spend in e-commerce vertical already exceeding $1 billion (Q3 2025); Diversification reduces gaming concentration; Self-service platform and referral program launched October 2025 with rapid advertiser spend growth. Apps divestiture: Completed sale of Apps Business for $715.6 million (June 30, 2025), shifting focus to higher-margin advertising solutions. Advertising software revenues grew >65% in 2025, partially offset by divested Apps business negative revenue impact.​

Q4 2025 Guidance & Outlook:
AppLovin provided strong Q4 2025 guidance: Revenue: $1.57-1.60 billion (12-14% sequential growth). Adjusted EBITDA: $1.29-1.32 billion with 82-83% margins. Full-year 2025: Revenue expected to exceed $5 billion with low-20% growth driven by >65% advertising software growth; Free cash flow expected to exceed $3 billion; EBITDA margins projected in low-80% range. Guidance reflects optimism around e-commerce referral program, model enhancements, and normal holiday seasonality. Management expects continued investment in AI-powered advertising technology and expansion into new verticals including connected TV.​

Overall Assessment: AppLovin Corporation is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within technology/digital media 6-7% investment-grade corporate allocation. AppLovin represents strong investment-grade credit (BBB/Baa2) following recent upgrades, with exceptional growth, industry-leading margins, and substantial free cash flow generation. Strengths include: (a) double-upgrade to BBB from BBB- by both S&P and Fitch in 2025 reflecting credit improvement; (b) exceptional Q3 2025 growth (+68% revenue, +79% EBITDA) significantly beating consensus; (c) industry-leading 82% EBITDA margin (vs ~20% for peer Unity Software); (d) $1.05B quarterly free cash flow providing substantial financial flexibility; (e) AXON AI recommendation engine enhancements driving customer performance and retention; (f) e-commerce diversification ($1B+ annualized spend) reducing gaming concentration; (g) S&P 500 inclusion validating market position and investor confidence; (h) conservative financial policy with EBITDA leverage below 2x; (i) revenue scale exceeding $5B—more than double Unity Software; (j) Apps divestiture sharpening focus on higher-margin advertising solutions. Risks include: (a) mobile gaming concentration despite e-commerce expansion; (b) fragmented ad-tech market exposure with competitive pressures; (c) app-tracking regulatory risks (Apple 
 ATT, Google 
 Privacy Sandbox) limiting third-party data collection; (d) customer concentration risks in gaming vertical; (e) AI model enhancement uncertainty and technology obsolescence risk; (f) e-commerce expansion execution risk in competitive vertical; (g) connected TV market entry facing established competitors. RECOMMENDED: Overweight mobile advertising leader with exceptional margins, FCF generation, and recent ratings upgrades reflecting credit momentum.

Monitor: (1) EBITDA margin sustainability (82%+ margin maintenance amid reinvestment in AI and new verticals); (2) E-commerce revenue ($1B+ annualized continuing growth and market share gains); (3) AXON platform performance (AI enhancement impact on customer retention and ad performance); (4) Leverage maintenance (EBITDA leverage remaining below 2x per conservative financial policy); (5) Regulatory environment (Apple/Google privacy policy impacts on advertising targeting effectiveness).

issuers.name: APTARGROUP INC
issuers.creditComment:
APTARGROUP INC
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Upgraded to BBB; Fitch Affirmed June 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's Upgraded March 2025) – Drug Delivery & Packaging Leader; Pharma/Beauty Exposure; Conservative Leverage
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgraded by Moody's and S&P) – AptarGroup, Inc. [finance:AptarGroup, Inc.] has strengthened its credit profile. Moody's upgraded the rating to 'Baa2' (from Baa3) in March 2025 with a Stable outlook. S&P upgraded Aptar to 'BBB' (from BBB-) based on sustained low leverage. Fitch affirms the 'BBB' rating with a Stable outlook as of June 11, 2025. The upgrades reflect the company's conservative financial policy, strong profitability (EBITDA margins ~20%), and diversified portfolio across pharma, beauty, and food/beverage segments.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) market leadership in drug delivery systems and dispensing solutions; (b) high-margin pharmaceutical business providing stability; (c) geographic and end-market diversification; (d) conservative leverage policy (net debt/EBITDA target 1.0x-2.0x); (e) strong FCF generation. Moody's expects leverage to remain below 2.0x through 2025. S&P notes sustained leverage below 2.0x as a key driver for the BBB upgrade. Risks include beauty segment cyclicality and raw material cost inflation.
​

Financial Profile: Aptar maintains strong credit metrics with adjusted debt-to-EBITDA improved to 1.4x in 2024. Margins are stable in the 20% area. The company generates consistent positive free cash flow. Liquidity is robust with a $600 million revolver covering maturities. The company navigates softer demand in beauty/home segments with resilient pharma growth. Capital allocation prioritizes organic investment and dividend growth while maintaining a strong balance sheet.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Packaging/Healthcare IG 4-5% allocation. Solid IG credit (BBB/Baa2) with pharma resilience. Monitor: (1) Pharma segment growth (high margin driver); (2) Beauty/home segment demand recovery; (3) EBITDA margin stability (~20%); (4) M&A discipline (maintaining leverage <2.0x).

issuers.name: APTIV SWISS HOLDINGS LTD
issuers.creditComment:
APTIV SWISS HOLDINGS LIMITED
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P 2025) | Baa2/Negative (Moody's 2025) | BBB/Rating Watch Negative (Fitch 2025) – Automotive Technology Subsidiary Aptiv PLC; EDS Spin-Off Announced January 2025; Pro Forma $12.1B Revenue; High-Growth Advanced Safety & User Experience; Engineered Components Group; Separation Expected Q1 2026

Credit Momentum: Negative Watch with Spin-Off Transition – Aptiv Swiss Holdings Limited maintains investment-grade ratings (BBB/Baa2) as wholly-owned subsidiary of Aptiv PLC 
, with credit profile directly linked to parent undergoing strategic transformation. Aptiv announced EDS business spin-off in January 2025 (expected completion by March 31, 2026), creating New Aptiv focused on high-growth advanced safety/user experience and engineered components, and standalone EDS for electrical distribution systems. Moody's revised outlook to Negative in November 2024; Fitch placed ratings on Rating Watch Negative following spin-off announcement. S&P affirmed BBB/Stable in October 2025 following spin-off details:

Credit Ratings & Spin-Off Impact:
Aptiv Swiss Holdings Limited maintains BBB/Stable ratings from S&P, Baa2/Negative from Moody's, and BBB/Rating Watch Negative from Fitch. S&P October 2025: Affirmed BBB/Stable ratings on planned spin-off of EDS, reflecting expectation that post-separation Aptiv will maintain credit metrics consistent with current rating; Stable outlook reflects expectation that Aptiv will use financial policy flexibility appropriately. Moody's November 2024: Revised outlook to Negative and affirmed Baa2, citing spin-off will increase leverage initially while sharpening focus on growing, higher-margin technologies. Fitch January 2025: Placed ratings on Rating Watch Negative following EDS spin-off announcement; New Aptiv expected to focus on advanced automotive technologies with higher margins and growth prospects. Aptiv Swiss Holdings Limited is a Jersey-incorporated wholly-owned subsidiary serving as borrower under Aptiv's credit facilities.​

Separation Transaction Structure:
Aptiv's Board unanimously approved EDS spin-off plan (January 2025): Transaction structure: Tax-free spin-off to Aptiv shareholders who will retain Aptiv shares and receive pro-rata EDS stock dividend; Expected completion by March 31, 2026. New Aptiv profile: Advanced Safety & User Experience plus Engineered Components Group; Full sensor-to-cloud technology stack including ADAS and in-cabin user experience software platforms; Diversified end markets (aerospace & defense, telecommunications, automotive, commercial vehicle, industrial); Pro forma 2024 revenues $12.1 billion (including intercompany sales to EDS), $1.4 billion GAAP operating income, $2.3 billion Adjusted EBITDA. New EDS profile: Leading global supplier of low/high voltage signal, power, and data distribution solutions for automotive/commercial vehicle; Pro forma 2024 revenues $8.3 billion, $0.4 billion GAAP operating income, $0.8 billion Adjusted EBITDA. Medium-term targets: New Aptiv mid-to-high single digit revenue growth, low-to-mid teens GAAP operating margins, high-teens-to-low-twenties Adjusted EBITDA margins.​

Strategic Rationale & Value Creation:
Aptiv's separation creates focused entities with distinct investment profiles: New Aptiv advantages: (a) High growth, high margin portfolio aligned with EV, autonomous, and software-defined vehicle megatrends; (b) Robust cash flows supporting organic/inorganic investment and capital returns; (c) Adjacent market penetration in aerospace, defense, telecom, industrial; (d) Industry-leading open-architected ADAS platforms. EDS advantages: (a) 100-year legacy as leader in vehicle electrical architecture; (b) Global scale and localized capabilities; (c) Industry-leading cost structure; (d) Low and high voltage portfolio positioned for EV content growth. Analysts argue separation eliminates low-margin drag, mirroring strategies of Continental AG 
 and ABB 
 to boost valuations through specialization. Oppenheimer raised price target to $92-141 based on Q2 2025 outperformance.​

Leverage Profile & Credit Considerations:
Aptiv's credit profile reflects transition period: Current facilities: Amended and restated credit agreement (March 31, 2025) provides revolving credit facilities for Aptiv PLC and subsidiaries including Aptiv Swiss Holdings; Pricing grid tied to ratings (BBB/Baa2 = 0.125% facility fee, 1.125% Term Benchmark spread). Moody's concerns: Spin-off of EDS will reduce Aptiv's size and initially increase leverage, though sharpening strategic focus on growing, higher-margin technologies. S&P expectations: Aptiv expected to use financial policy flexibility appropriately to maintain credit metrics consistent with BBB rating post-separation. Fitch assessment: New Aptiv's focus on advanced automotive technologies with higher margins and growth prospects provides credit support, though Rating Watch Negative reflects execution uncertainty. Post-spin leverage trajectory will depend on debt allocation between entities and capital allocation priorities.​

Overall Assessment: Aptiv Swiss Holdings Limited is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within automotive/technology 5-6% investment-grade corporate allocation. Aptiv Swiss Holdings represents investment-grade credit (BBB/Baa2) undergoing strategic transformation through EDS spin-off, with credit profile directly linked to parent Aptiv PLC. Strengths include: (a) S&P BBB/Stable affirmation following spin-off announcement reflecting confidence in post-separation credit profile; (b) New Aptiv high-growth, high-margin positioning (mid-to-high single digit growth, high-teens-to-low-twenties EBITDA margins); (c) diversified end market exposure (aerospace, defense, telecom, automotive, industrial) reducing cyclicality; (d) industry-leading ADAS and user experience software platforms; (e) full sensor-to-cloud technology stack aligned with vehicle software-defined architecture trends; (f) $12.1B pro forma revenue scale providing competitive positioning; (g) focus on megatrends (EV, autonomous, connectivity) driving content growth. Risks include: (a) Moody's Baa2/Negative outlook and Fitch Rating Watch Negative reflecting execution uncertainty; (b) initial leverage increase from separation transaction before deleveraging; (c) automotive sector cyclicality affecting OEM customer demand; (d) EV adoption pace uncertainty affecting content growth assumptions; (e) technology disruption risks in rapidly evolving ADAS and autonomous driving markets; (f) spin-off execution risk and potential for transaction delays; (g) post-separation capital allocation priorities unclear (shareholder returns vs reinvestment vs debt reduction); (h) New EDS competitiveness as standalone entity affecting separation value realization. RECOMMENDED: Market weight automotive technology company undergoing strategic transformation with favorable long-term positioning but near-term execution uncertainty warranting caution.

Monitor: (1) Spin-off execution (Q1 2026 completion timeline and debt allocation between entities); (2) Rating agency actions (Moody's/Fitch negative outlook/watch resolution post-separation); (3) Post-spin leverage (New Aptiv credit metrics and deleveraging trajectory); (4) End market diversification (Aerospace, defense, telecom revenue contribution growth); (5) Automotive sector dynamics (EV adoption pace and ADAS content growth assumptions).

issuers.name: ARCELORMITTAL SA
issuers.creditComment:
ARCELORMITTAL SA
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Jun 2025) | Baa2/Positive (Moody's Feb 2024) – World's Largest Steel Producer; $8.0-8.2B 2025 EBITDA Target; India JV Growth; Liberia Iron Ore Expansion; 57.9Mt Crude Steel Production; FFO/Debt >30%

Credit Momentum: Positive/Upgraded – ArcelorMittal 
 achieved S&P upgrade to BBB (from BBB-) in June 2025 and Moody's Positive outlook (February 2024), reflecting structural improvements in business profile from strategic projects, acquisitions, and reduced European exposure. EBITDA expected to improve toward $8.0-8.2B in 2025 driven by India JV expansion (tripling capacity by 2030) and Liberia iron ore mine growth, with FFO-to-debt above 30% through forecast period:

Credit Ratings & Upgrade Rationale:
ArcelorMittal maintains BBB/Stable from S&P (upgraded June 2025) and Baa2/Positive from Moody's (outlook revised February 2024). S&P June 2025 upgrade: Raised to BBB/A-2 (from BBB-/A-3) reflecting improved business through strategic projects, acquisitions, and cost control; Structural enhancements underpin expectation for improved 2025 performance despite global steel oversupply. Moody's February 2024 assessment: Changed outlook to Positive citing capital allocation discipline, structural profitability improvements, and EBITDA progress. European de-risking: Divested Ilva (Italy) to Italian government (2024) and Kazakhstan facility (2023) reducing slower-growth European exposure.​

Strategic Growth Projects:
ArcelorMittal advancing transformational initiatives: AM/NS India JV: EBITDA contribution growing from $506M (2024) to $700M+ (2025); Phase 1 expansion to 15mt capacity on track; Targeting tripling capacity by 2030; Most significant JV providing substantial growth contribution. Liberia iron ore: Capacity expansion contributing to EBITDA growth; High-quality iron ore supporting margins. AM/NS Calvert (USA): New 1.5mt EAF completed and commissioned; Investments expected to contribute ~$1.9B EBITDA by 2027. Decarbonization: European investments more gradual, aligned with supportive regulations introduction. These projects position ArcelorMittal for structural EBITDA improvement despite challenging steel pricing environment.​

Financial Profile & Credit Metrics:
ArcelorMittal demonstrates strong financial position: EBITDA guidance: $8.0-8.2B expected for 2025 (improved from ~$7.5B-range 2024). FFO/debt: Expected above 30% through forecast period. Debt/EBITDA: 2.5x historical, improving toward lower levels with EBITDA growth. Liquidity: Assessed as strong with limited short-term maturities, committed lines, and positive FOCF; Sources/uses ratio exceeding 1.5x. Capital discipline: Funded organic investments and acquisitions without increasing gross debt. Production: 57.9mt crude steel production maintaining global leadership.​

Overall Assessment: ArcelorMittal SA is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within steel/materials 6-7% European industrials allocation. ArcelorMittal represents solid investment-grade credit (BBB/Baa2) with upgraded ratings, strategic project execution, and structural EBITDA improvement trajectory. Strengths include: (a) S&P upgrade to BBB (June 2025) reflecting improved business profile; (b) Moody's Baa2/Positive outlook signaling further upgrade potential; (c) world's largest steel producer with global scale; (d) AM/NS India JV tripling capacity by 2030 providing growth; (e) EBITDA improving toward $8.0-8.2B in 2025; (f) FFO/debt >30% through forecast period; (g) Liberia iron ore and Calvert USA contributing ~$1.9B EBITDA by 2027; (h) European exposure reduced through divestitures; (i) capital discipline funding growth without increasing debt. Risks include: (a) global steel oversupply pressuring prices; (b) steel industry cyclicality; (c) European decarbonization costs and regulation uncertainty; (d) India JV execution risk on capacity expansion; (e) iron ore price volatility; (f) trade policy/tariff risks affecting exports; (g) energy costs in European operations. RECOMMENDED: Overweight leading steel producer with upgraded ratings, strategic project execution, and structural improvements supporting credit trajectory.

Monitor: (1) AM/NS India (Capacity expansion and EBITDA contribution growth); (2) EBITDA trajectory ($8.0-8.2B 2025 achievement); (3) Steel prices (Global supply/demand and pricing); (4) Leverage metrics (FFO/debt >30% maintenance); (5) Moody's upgrade (Baa2/Positive conversion to Baa1).

issuers.name: ARCOS DORADOS BV
issuers.creditComment:
ARCOS DORADOS BV
Credit Rating: BBB-/Stable (S&P 2025) | BBB-/Stable (Fitch 2025) | Ba1/Stable (Moody's 2025) – McDonald's Latin America Franchisee; Full Investment-Grade Status Achieved; 20+ Countries; 1.4x Leverage; MFA Extended to 2044

Credit Momentum: Positive – Arcos Dorados B.V., the world's largest independent McDonald's franchisee operating across 21 Latin American countries, achieved full investment-grade status in 2025 following S&P's initial 'BBB-' rating (July 2025) and Fitch's upgrade to 'BBB-' (January 2025). This milestone reflects the company's market-leading position, exclusive Master Franchise Agreement (MFA) with McDonald's extended to 2044 (with 20-year renewal option), and disciplined financial management yielding 1.4x Net Debt-to-Adjusted EBITDA leverage. Moody's maintains Ba1/Stable (one notch below investment-grade), providing slight divergence. Q2 2025 results demonstrated strong performance with adjusted EBITDA growth and the addition of Saint Martin as the 21st market.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:

Arcos Dorados holds ratings of BBB- (S&P, Stable), BBB- (Fitch, Stable), and Ba1 (Moody's, Stable). The full investment-grade status from S&P/Fitch represents culmination of multi-year deleveraging and operational improvements. Leverage profile: 1.4x Net Debt-to-Adjusted EBITDA (June 2025), among the strongest in Latin American QSR sector and well within investment-grade thresholds.

Q2 2025 Financial Performance:

Arcos Dorados reported strong Q2 2025 results: Net Debt-to-Adjusted EBITDA: 1.4x (comfortable investment-grade levels). Restaurant Openings: 20 new EOTF restaurants in Q2; growth trajectory maintained. Market Expansion: Added Saint Martin as 21st territory, demonstrating geographic growth momentum. Labor Contingencies: Favorable Brazilian court judgment reducing liability provisions.

Strategic Initiatives:

Strategic Priorities: (a) Investment-Grade Maintenance – Sustaining leverage <2.0x and conservative financial profile; (b) Geographic Expansion – 21-market footprint with selective growth opportunities; (c) Restaurant Development – EOTF (experience of the future) format rollout; (d) MFA Extension – Leveraging 2044 MFA with 20-year renewal option; (e) Brand Strength – Exclusive McDonald's brand access across 2,400+ restaurants.

Competitive Position:

Market position: World's largest independent McDonald's franchisee; exclusive operator across 21 Latin American/Caribbean markets. The company's scale, brand access, and market leadership provide structural competitive advantages in region's QSR sector.

Capital Allocation:

Arcos Dorados prioritizes balanced capital allocation: modest dividends, growth capex on new restaurants, and balance sheet strength maintenance. The company targets leverage maintenance below 2.0x.

Overall Assessment: Arcos Dorados BV is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 5-7% Latin American QSR Investment-Grade. The premier Latin American franchisee with newly achieved full investment-grade status. Strengths: (a) BBB-/Stable from S&P/Fitch (full investment-grade); (b) 1.4x leverage (strong coverage); (c) MFA extended to 2044 with 20-year renewal option; (d) 21-market exclusive McDonald's franchise; (e) 2,400+ restaurants with growth momentum; (f) Strong liquidity and manageable debt maturity profile. Risks: (a) Latin America macro sensitivity (FX, inflation); (b) Brazil sovereign stress (major market exposure); (c) Consumer discretionary cyclicality; (d) Competition from local/regional QSR players.

Monitor: (1) Leverage maintenance <2.0x; (2) Same-store sales growth; (3) Restaurant development pace; (4) Brazil macro/sovereign stability; (5) Rating upgrade potential from Moody's (Ba1 → Baa3).

issuers.name: ARROW ELECTRONICS INC
issuers.creditComment:
ARROW ELECTRONICS INC
Credit Rating: BB+/Stable (S&P 2025) | Ba1/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BB+/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Global Technology Distributor; Q3 2025 Revenue $7.71B; EPS $2.41 Beat; Gradual Recovery 2026

Credit Momentum: Stabilizing – Arrow Electronics Inc. 
 maintains sub-investment-grade ratings (BB+/Ba1) with Stable outlooks following a challenging 2024 inventory correction. Q3 2025 revenue of $7.71B (-13% YoY but +5.24% beat vs. consensus) and EPS $2.41 (+5.24% beat vs. $2.29 consensus) demonstrate operational stabilization. The company generated $1.1B+ in operating cash flow for 2024 and successfully reduced inventory by $1.1B from peak levels, supporting gradual margin recovery. S&P, Moody's, and Fitch maintain Stable outlooks, reflecting confidence in cyclical recovery as component demand normalizes in 2026. The company projects Q4 2025 sales $7.8-8.4B with EPS guidance $3.44-3.64.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Arrow Electronics holds aligned sub-investment-grade ratings of BB+ (S&P), Ba1 (Moody's), and BB+ (Fitch), all with Stable outlooks. The company's ratings reflect technology distribution cyclicality but acknowledge improving inventory metrics and margin trajectory. Leverage profile: The company maintains moderate leverage estimated at 2.5x-3.0x net Debt-to-EBITDA, appropriate for technology distributors with cyclical demand.

Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
Arrow reported Q3 2025 results: Revenue: $7.71 billion (-13% YoY; +5.24% beat vs. $7.66B consensus). Diluted EPS: $2.41 (+5.24% beat vs. $2.29 consensus); Non-GAAP EPS: $2.41. Operating Cash Flow: >$1.1B generated in FY2024. Inventory Management: Reduced $1.1B from peak levels. Q4 2025 Guidance: Sales $7.8-8.4B; Non-GAAP EPS $3.44-3.64.

Strategic Initiatives & Full-Year Guidance:
Strategic Priorities: (a) Inventory Normalization – Completed major correction; (b) Margin Recovery – Focusing on gross margin improvement through product mix; (c) Value-Added Services – Hybrid cloud solutions, high-margin services; (d) 2026 Recovery – Gradual improvement anticipated from strategic initiatives.

Competitive Position:
Market position: #1 global technology distributor (Arrow, ScanSource, Tech Data provide market leadership). The company's scale and vendor relationships provide competitive advantages.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
Arrow returned $250M to shareholders through buybacks in FY2024, demonstrating confidence in cycle recovery. Capital allocation balanced toward debt management and shareholder returns.

Overall Assessment: Arrow Electronics Inc is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – Technology Distributor Emerging from Cycle. A leading global technology distributor navigating inventory normalization. Strengths: (a) BB+/Ba1 stable ratings; (b) Q3 revenue beat (-13% YoY but +5.24% better than consensus); (c) Inventory reduction $1.1B demonstrates cycle management; (d) Gradual recovery trajectory 2026. Risks: (a) Sub-investment-grade reflects cyclicality; (b) Component demand remains soft (tech downcycle); (c) Customer inventory normalization ongoing; (d) Gross margin pressure from product mix.

Monitor: (1) Q4 2025 revenue and EPS realization vs. guidance; (2) Gross margin recovery trajectory; (3. FY2026 growth inflection; (4) Inventory trends.

issuers.name: AS MILEAGE PLAN IP LTD
issuers.creditComment:
AS MILEAGE PLAN IP LTD (ALASKA AIRLINES LOYALTY)
Credit Rating: Baa2/Stable (Moody's Assigned Sep 2024) | BBB-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Sep 2025) – Loyalty Program Financing Vehicle for Alaska Air Group; Secured by Mileage Plan Assets
​

Credit Momentum: Stable (Structure) – AS Mileage Plan IP Ltd. is the special purpose vehicle holding the intellectual property and assets of Alaska Airlines' loyalty program ("Mileage Plan"). While the parent, Alaska Air Group, was downgraded to junk (Ba1/BB+) following the Hawaiian Airlines acquisition, the loyalty-backed notes maintained investment-grade ratings due to their secured nature and structural protections.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: The notes are rated Baa2 (Moody's) and BBB- (Fitch), reflecting: (a) the high value and sticky nature of the Mileage Plan program; (b) strong debt service coverage ratios; (c) bankruptcy-remote structure; (d) first-priority lien on loyalty program cash flows. The program is a critical asset for the airline, incentivizing continued performance.
​

Transaction: In 2025, the issuer launched new senior secured notes to refinance debt assumed from the Hawaiian Airlines merger. The structure allows Alaska to access lower-cost capital despite its high-yield corporate rating.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 2-3% within Aviation ABS/Loyalty 4-5% allocation. Structural alpha play: Investment-grade notes (Baa2/BBB-) backed by a high-value asset, offering a yield pickup over traditional corporate IG. Monitor: (1) Alaska Airlines' recovery/integration of Hawaiian; (2) Loyalty program cash flow performance; (3) DSCR covenants.

issuers.name: ASHTEAD CAPITAL INC
issuers.creditComment:
ASHTEAD GROUP PLC
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed June 2025) | Baa3/Positive (Moody's Affirmed with Positive Outlook) | BBB-/Stable (S&P Affirmed) – Global Equipment Rental Leader; Strong FCF; Leverage Discipline
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Moody's Outlook Upgraded) – Ashtead Group plc 
 has solidified its investment-grade status. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB' long-term rating with a Stable outlook in June 2025. Moody's affirmed the 'Baa3' long-term rating and revised the outlook to Positive (from Stable) in its most recent review, signaling potential for upgrade to Ba1. S&P affirms the 'BBB-' rating with a Stable outlook. The positive momentum reflects strong operational performance, disciplined leverage management, and solid free cash flow generation.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) market-leading position in equipment rental globally (US rental network and UK operations); (b) strong EBITDA generation; (c) disciplined leverage management (2.0x-2.5x target range, per S&P guidance in FY2024); (d) solid liquidity position ($3.6bn available as of April 30, 2025); (e) diversified customer base reducing concentration risk. Net debt was $10.3 billion as of July 31, 2025.
​

Financial Profile: Ashtead maintains healthy credit metrics with strong operating cash flow supporting debt reduction. The company generates robust EBITDA, which provides cushion for leverage management. Free cash flow is allocated toward debt paydown and modest shareholder returns. Liquidity is strong with ample revolver availability and solid cash generation. The company has a fixed charge coverage covenant requiring ≥1.0x when availability drops below $475m (substantial headroom at present).
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Global Equipment Rental 5-6% allocation. Solid IG credit (BBB/Baa3 Positive) with disciplined capital structure. Monitor: (1) Rental market utilization and pricing trends; (2) Leverage trending toward mid-2.0x; (3) Free cash flow allocation; (4) M&A execution (synergy realization); (5) Moody's upgrade timing.

issuers.name: ASTRAZENECA PLC
issuers.creditComment:
ASTRAZENECA PLC
Credit Rating: A1/Stable (Moody's Upgraded February 2025 from A2) | A+/Stable (S&P Upgraded August 2025 from A) | A+/Stable (Fitch Upgraded August 2025) – Global BioPharma Leader; FY2024 Revenue $54.1B +21% CER; Leverage 1.8x; Target $80B Revenue by 2030; 19 Phase III Pipeline Drugs​

Credit Momentum: Positive with Recent Upgrades – AstraZeneca PLC received multiple rating upgrades in 2025 reflecting exceptional organic growth, strengthening pipeline execution, and conservative financial policy. All three major agencies upgraded ratings with stable outlooks.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: AstraZeneca maintains A1/Stable from Moody's (upgraded February 2025), A+/Stable from S&P (upgraded August 2025), and A+/Stable from Fitch (upgraded August 2025).
