Upgrades reflect: (a) 21% revenue growth at CER in 2024; (b) leverage reduction to 1.8x from 2.3x (December 2023-2024); (c) strong diversified pipeline with 19 drugs in Phase III/approval stage; (d) conservative financial policy (modest dividend growth, limited acquisition need); (e) Farxiga leadership (+31% CER growth).​

Financial Profile & Growth: FY2024 revenue $54.1B (+21% CER), driven by BioPharmaceuticals and Oncology segments. Core operating profit +22% CER with 50 bps margin improvement. Management targeting $80B revenue by 2030 organically. Moody's-adjusted debt/EBITDA improved to 1.8x (December 2024) from 2.3x (December 2023). Excellent liquidity: $5.5B cash, $4.9B undrawn committed facilities (maturing April 2030).​

Risks & Monitoring: China anti-corruption investigations ongoing (three separate probes); Moody's expects outcomes manageable given AstraZeneca's resources. Patent expiry management required as key drugs approach cliff. Pipeline execution critical for sustained growth trajectory.

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within global healthcare/pharma 8-10% allocation. High-quality investment-grade credit with recent positive rating actions, exceptional organic growth, conservative leverage (1.8x), and strong pipeline visibility. Monitor: (1) China investigation outcomes; (2) Farxiga and Oncology growth sustainability; (3) Pipeline Phase III readouts; (4) $80B 2030 revenue target progress; (5) Leverage maintenance below 2.0x.

issuers.name: ATL TRANSMISION SUR
issuers.creditComment:
ABENGO 6.875% 30/04/2043 – Atlantica Transmisión Sur (Peru), BBB
Профиль бизнеса и регуляторика
Проектная компания, владеющая и оперирующая 900 км линий электропередачи 500/220 кВ в южном Перу по 30‑летней концессии.
Выручка availability‑based, с индексацией на инфляцию США/Перу, не зависит от объёма переданной энергии, что делает cash flow очень предсказуемым.

Кредитные метрики
DSCR по моделям S&P/Fitch около 1,4x минимум, в среднем выше 1,5x; леверидж естественно высок, но типичен для project finance transmission.
Capex низкий, операционные расходы невелики, поэтому свободный денежный поток после обслуживания долга стабилен.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды
S&P подняла SACP до a‑ на фоне лучшего CFADS, но выпуск остаётся на уровне BBB, ограниченном рейтингом Перу; outlook стабильный.
Длинный выпуск 2043 даёт YTM ~6,1%, dur ~7,2, OAS ~150–190 bps – типичный IG‑project‑bond‑уровень с премией к суверену Перу.

Ключевые риски
Суверенный риск Перу (политика/соцнапряжение) и регулирование тарифов: проект нельзя рейтингово поставить выше суверена.
Концентрация на одном активе и на одного регулятора/offtaker; технологический или форс‑мажорный сбой может резко ударить по DSCR.

Инвестидия
Для IG‑портфеля это “чистый” инфраструктурный play: защищённый cash flow, разумные ковенанты, умеренный спред.
Минус – длинная дюрация и жёсткая привязка к суверену Перу: нет смысла держать, если ты принципиально не хочешь его страновой риск.

issuers.name: AUSGRID FINANCE PTY LTD
issuers.creditComment:
AUSGRID FINANCE PTY LTD
Credit Rating: Baa1/Stable (Moody's Affirmed Dec 2025) | BBB/Stable (S&P) – Bond-Issuing Vehicle for Ausgrid Group; Notes Due 2028 (USQ0697CAD58); Critical NSW Electricity Distributor; Regulated Asset Base Model; Senior Secured Ranking
​
Credit Momentum: Stable with Recent Affirmation – Ausgrid Finance Pty Ltd serves as the funding vehicle for the Ausgrid Group. On December 17, 2025, Moody's affirmed the Baa1 senior secured rating with a stable outlook, citing predictable cash flows from regulated network operations and prudent financial management.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Ausgrid Finance maintains Baa1/Stable from Moody's and BBB/Stable from S&P. Ratings reflect: (a) monopoly position as the electricity distributor for Sydney, the Central Coast, and the Hunter region in NSW; (b) transparent regulatory framework under the Australian Energy Regulator (AER); (c) stable cash flow generation from regulated tariffs; (d) senior secured nature of the notes providing structural enhancement versus unsecured debt.
​
Bond Details (USQ0697CAD58): 4.35% Senior Secured Notes due August 1, 2028. Issued by Ausgrid Finance Pty Ltd. The notes are secured by a general security agreement over the assets of the Ausgrid obligor group, ranking pari passu with other senior secured debt. Proceeds used for refinancing and general corporate purposes, supporting the regulated asset base (RAB) investment.
​
Operational & Financial Strength: Ausgrid operates a critical infrastructure network serving ~1.8 million customers. The regulatory reset process provides revenue visibility for 5-year periods. Financial policy balances distributions to shareholders (AustralianSuper, IFM Investors, NSW Government) with maintenance of investment-grade metrics. Liquidity remains adequate with established MTN programs and bank facilities.
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 1-2% within Australian/Global Utilities 3-4% allocation. Solid investment-grade regulated utility credit (Baa1/BBB) with secured debt structure and essential infrastructure characteristics. Monitor: (1) Regulatory reset outcomes and tariff determinations; (2) Interest rate impacts on allowable returns; (3) Shareholder distribution levels vs. capex requirements; (4) Energy transition capex needs.

issuers.name: AUTO METRO PUERTO RICO
issuers.creditComment:
AUTO METRO PUERTO RICO
“Auto Metro Puerto Rico” corresponds to Autopistas Metropolitanas de Puerto Rico LLC, concessionaire for toll highways PR‑22 and PR‑5. S&P affirms a BBB rating with Stable outlook on the senior secured notes, citing robust post‑COVID traffic, strong revenue growth and projected minimum DSCR of about 1.9x by 2027. Key risks are Puerto Rico’s distressed sovereign profile (S&P D rating) and hurricane exposure, but the assets have shown good resilience.​

issuers.name: AUTOMATIC DATA PROCESSNG
issuers.creditComment:
AUTOMATIC DATA PROCESSING (ADP)
Credit Rating: AA-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed May 2025) | Aa3/Stable (Moody's estimated) | AA-/Stable (S&P estimated) – Global HCM Leader; Recurring Revenue; AAA-Quality Financial Structure
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Automatic Data Processing Inc. [finance:Automatic Data Processing] maintains a high-grade investment profile. Fitch affirmed the 'AA-' Long-Term IDR and 'F1+' Short-Term IDR with a Stable outlook on October 17, 2025. Fitch also rated ADP's May 2025 senior unsecured notes offering at 'AA-'. The ratings reflect ADP's dominant position in human capital management (HCM), high recurring revenue retention (>90%), and exceptionally strong financial flexibility.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) global market leadership in payroll and HCM solutions; (b) mission-critical nature of services with high client retention; (c) strong float income from client funds (~$35 billion average client funds balance); (d) robust free cash flow generation; (e) conservative capitalization. ADP is one of the few U.S. industrial companies with 'AA' category ratings. The "AAA-quality" financial structure refers to extremely low leverage and massive liquidity from client funds float.
​

Financial Profile: ADP generates substantial free cash flow and maintains minimal financial leverage. The company benefits from rising interest rates via its client funds portfolio (float income). Fitch notes the "very strong financial structure." ADP issued $1 billion in senior notes in May 2025 for general corporate purposes. Capital allocation includes dividends and share repurchases funded by FCF, preserving balance sheet strength. Liquidity is exceptional.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Tech/Services High Grade 5-6% allocation. "Blue Chip" High Grade credit (AA-/Aa3) offering safety. Monitor: (1) Employment levels (payslip volume driver); (2) Client retention rates; (3) Interest rate environment (float income impact); (4) Competitive dynamics in HCM software.

issuers.name: AUTORIDAD CANAL DE PANAM
issuers.creditComment:
AUTORIDAD DEL CANAL DE PANAMÁ (PANAMA CANAL AUTHORITY)
Credit Rating: A3/Stable (Moody's November 2023) | A-/Negative (S&P July 2024) | A/Stable (Fitch) – Strategic Global Waterway Operator; FY2024 Revenue B/.5.0B; 11,240 Transits; Direct Treasury Contribution B/.2.5B; Traffic Recovery Post-El Niño​

Credit Momentum: Stable with Operational Recovery – Autoridad del Canal de Panamá (ACP) maintains strong investment-grade ratings one to three notches above Panama sovereign, reflecting the Canal's strategic global importance, operational resilience, and institutional independence.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: ACP maintains A3/Stable from Moody's (November 2023), A-/Negative from S&P (outlook linked to sovereign), and A/Stable from Fitch. Moody's A3 rating is three notches above Panama sovereign (Baa3), reflecting: (a) very strong credit profile with substantial international revenue component; (b) low risk of moratorium restrictions; (c) strong cash flow generation; (d) relatively low leverage; (e) track record of institutional independence and successful governance. S&P's negative outlook reflects sovereign linkage.​

FY2024 Financial Performance: FY2024 total revenue B/.4.986B (+0.4% YoY despite El Niño impact). Transits: 11,240. PC/UMS tonnage: 423.1M. Direct National Treasury contributions: B/.2.471B. Total contribution to national economy: B/.3.598B. Canal's GDP contribution: 2.9%. Infrastructure investment: B/.106M across 176 projects. Maintenance spending: >B/.500M annually.​

FY2025 Outlook & Strategic Position: FY2025 budget: B/.5.624B. Projected toll revenues: B/.4.139B. Projected transits: 13,900. Treasury contributions: B/.2.789B. El Niño recovery underway with normal water levels returning. Water pricing model implemented to strengthen competitiveness and resource sustainability. Monthly revenues averaging $340M through May 2025 (above 2024 average).​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 1-2% within EM infrastructure 3-4% allocation. Strong investment-grade infrastructure credit (A3/A-) with strategic global trade position, institutional independence, and revenue recovery. Monitor: (1) Water resource management and El Niño/La Niña cycles; (2) Transit volumes and toll revenue; (3) Panama sovereign credit trajectory; (4) Global trade patterns and geopolitical risks; (5) Red Sea disruption impact on Canal traffic.

issuers.name: AUTOZONE INC
issuers.creditComment:
AUTOZONE INC
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Sept 2025) | BBB/Stable (S&P Affirmed March 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's estimated) – Auto Parts Retail Leader; Consistent Growth; Shareholder Return Focus
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – AutoZone Inc. [finance:AutoZone Inc.] maintains a consistent investment-grade profile. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB' IDR with a Stable outlook on September 8, 2025. S&P affirmed the 'BBB' rating with a Stable outlook on March 20, 2025. The company is the leading retailer of automotive replacement parts in the Americas, known for consistent financial performance and aggressive share repurchases funded by operating cash flow and debt issuance.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) dominant market position in DIY and growing DIFM (commercial) auto parts markets; (b) recession-resilient business model (aging vehicle fleet benefits aftermarket); (c) highly consistent operating performance and margin stability; (d) robust free cash flow generation. The primary constraint is the company's aggressive financial policy, targeting leverage of 2.5x EBITDAR (Fitch metric), which keeps ratings in the mid-BBB area despite business strength.
​

Financial Profile: AutoZone operates with a target leverage ratio (typically ~2.5x adjusted debt/EBITDAR) and systematically returns excess cash to shareholders via buybacks. S&P noted leverage was 3.4x (S&P adjusted) vs. management's 2.5x calculation as of Feb 2025. Liquidity is strong with stable cash flows and revolver availability. The company issued $600 million senior notes in Dec 2025 (maturing 2031) and other notes in 2025 consistent with its capital strategy. Profit margins are high (operating margin >19%).
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Automotive Retail 4-5% allocation. Core IG credit (BBB/Baa1) with consistent buyback leverage targeting. Monitor: (1) Same-store sales growth; (2) Commercial (DIFM) growth execution; (3) Leverage adherence to 2.5x target; (4) Impact of EV transition (long-term tail risk); (5) Supply chain/freight costs.

issuers.name: AVERY DENNISON CORP
issuers.creditComment:
AVERY DENNISON CORP
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Affirmed Sept 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's estimated) | BBB/Stable (Fitch estimated, implied) – Materials Science/RFID Leader; Intelligent Labels Growth; Disciplined Capital Allocation
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Avery Dennison Corp. [finance:Avery Dennison Corp.] maintains a solid investment-grade profile. S&P affirmed the 'BBB' issuer credit rating with a Stable outlook on September 19, 2025. S&P also assigned a 'BBB' rating to the company's proposed €500 million senior notes in September 2025. The company is a global leader in pressure-sensitive materials and intelligent labels (RFID), serving diverse packaging, apparel, and industrial markets.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) market leadership in labeling and graphic materials; (b) strong growth platform in Intelligent Labels (RFID) driven by logistics/retail adoption; (c) geographic and customer diversification; (d) solid operating margins (adjusted EBITDA ~16-17%); (e) disciplined capital allocation. Risks include raw material cost volatility and cyclical exposure in some industrial segments.
​

Financial Profile: Avery Dennison reported Q1 2025 net debt to adjusted EBITDA of 2.3x, within its target range. The company maintains a strong balance sheet with ample liquidity. S&P-adjusted debt to EBITDA is expected to remain supportive of the 'BBB' rating. The company balances capital allocation between capex/growth (25-30%), dividends (~20%), and buybacks/M&A (50-55%). The €500 million note issuance in 2025 supports general corporate purposes and refinancing.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Packaging/Materials 4-5% allocation. Solid IG credit (BBB/Baa2) with RFID growth upside. Monitor: (1) Intelligent Labels (RFID) adoption rates; (2) Raw material costs and pricing power; (3) Leverage maintenance (2.0x-2.5x range); (4) Retail/apparel end-market demand; (5) M&A integration.

issuers.name: AXIATA SPV5 LABUAN LTD
issuers.creditComment:
AXIATA SPV5 (LABUAN) LIMITED
Credit Rating: Baa2/Stable (Moody's Dec 2024) | BBB/Stable (S&P Mar 2025) – Financing Vehicle for Axiata Group Berhad; Southeast Asian Telecom Leader; ~150M Subscribers; CelcomDigi 55% Dividend Contribution; XL Axiata-Smartfren Merger Pending; Government-Related Entity Support

Credit Momentum: Stable – Axiata SPV5 (Labuan) Limited issues senior unsecured notes guaranteed by Axiata Group Berhad ; ratings are equalized to parent. Moody's affirmed Baa2/Stable (December 2024) and S&P affirmed BBB/Stable (March 2025), reflecting Axiata's strong liquidity, financial discipline, and diversified regional telecom portfolio. The ratings incorporate one-notch government-related issuer (GRI) uplift from the baa3 baseline credit assessment, recognizing ~72% ownership by entities linked to the Government of Malaysia:​

Credit Ratings & GRI Support:
Axiata SPV5 maintains Baa2/Stable from Moody's (December 2024), BBB/Stable from S&P (March 2025). Moody's structure: Baa2 rating reflects one-notch GRI uplift from baa3 baseline, recognizing majority ownership by Khazanah Nasional Berhad and other Government of Malaysia-linked entities (~72%). S&P March 2025: Affirmed BBB with Stable outlook; Expects debt/EBITDA below 3x in 2025, declining to 2.5-2.8x in 2026. Financial discipline: Strong liquidity and financial management enable rating maintenance despite complex corporate restructuring.​

Corporate Structure & Dividend Quality:
Axiata operates diversified Southeast Asian telecom platform: Key subsidiaries: CelcomDigi Berhad (Malaysia, combined entity), XL Axiata (Indonesia, 66.1%), edotco (regional towers), Robi Axiata (Bangladesh), Dialog Axiata (Sri Lanka). Dividend quality: CelcomDigi and Indonesia MergeCo (XL-Smartfren) expected to contribute 11% of EBITDA but 55% of dividends—predominantly investment-grade quality cash flows. XL-Smartfren merger: Proposed deconsolidation of XL Axiata adds complexity but approved under Indonesian regulations. Link Net acquisition: 66% stake in Indonesian fixed broadband provider completed, enhancing convergence capabilities.​

Indonesia Strategy & Market Position:
Axiata strengthening Indonesian positioning: XL-Smartfren combination: Creates stronger competitor in mobile market vs. Telkomsel and Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison. Convergence strategy: Link Net's 860,000 broadband subscribers and 17,000km fiber backbone enable bundled mobile/broadband/pay-TV offerings. Market dynamics: Indonesian mobile market remains developing, providing growth opportunities through consolidation and cross-selling. Portfolio diversification: While Indonesia/Malaysia represent majority of cash flows, exposure to Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, and Cambodia introduces emerging market volatility.​

Overall Assessment: Axiata SPV5 (Labuan) Limited is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within ASEAN telecom 5-6% EM IG allocation. Axiata represents solid investment-grade credit (Baa2/BBB) with government-related support and regional telecom diversification, though subject to complex corporate restructuring and emerging market execution risk. Strengths include: (a) Baa2/BBB ratings with Stable outlooks; (b) one-notch GRI uplift from Malaysian government linkage; (c) strong dividend quality from CelcomDigi and Indonesia operations; (d) S&P expects debt/EBITDA below 3x; (e) 150M+ subscriber base across 11 countries; (f) financial discipline and strong liquidity; (g) convergence strategy via Link Net acquisition. Risks include: (a) complex corporate structure increasing governance challenges; (b) XL-Smartfren deconsolidation execution; (c) emerging/frontier market regulatory and political risks; (d) CelcomDigi/XL dividend dependency; (e) Bangladesh and Sri Lanka currency/economic volatility. RECOMMENDED: Market weight ASEAN telecom with government support, accepting structural complexity for regional diversification.

Monitor: (1) XL-Smartfren integration (Merger completion and synergies); (2) Leverage trajectory (3x → 2.5-2.8x by 2026); (3) Dividend upstream (CelcomDigi and Indonesia contributions); (4) GRI support (Government ownership maintenance); (5) Emerging market performance (Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Cambodia).

issuers.name: BACARDI-MARTINI BV
issuers.creditComment:
BACARDI-MARTINI BV
Credit Rating: Baa3/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BBB-/Stable (S&P 2025) | NR (Fitch) – Bacardi Ltd. Financing Subsidiary; Diversified Spirits Portfolio; 4.1x Leverage; Concentrated US Exposure; Premium Spirits Focus

Credit Momentum: Stable – Bacardi-Martini B.V., a core financing subsidiary of Bacardi Ltd. , maintains investment-grade ratings of Baa3 (Moody's) and BBB- (S&P) with Stable outlooks. Moody's affirmed its Baa3/Stable rating in August 2025, reflecting Bacardi's strong global brand portfolio (200+ brands including Patrón, Dewar's, Grey Goose, Martini), good financial health, and disciplined financial management. However, leverage elevated at 4.1x reflects near-term pressure from volume headwinds in North America and premium spirits demand weakness. Moody's expects leverage to drop below 4.0x within 12 months, supported by operational improvements and the company's commitment to maintaining investment-grade status. S&P revised its outlook to Stable from Positive (June 2023), affirming BBB- rating with leverage targeting 3.5x-4.0x over the medium term.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Bacardi holds ratings of Baa3 (Moody's) and BBB- (S&P), both with Stable outlooks. The ratings reflect the company's strong brand portfolio and market position in premium spirits but acknowledge near-term pressure from volume softness in key markets. Leverage profile: Currently elevated at 4.1x (FY2025 ended March 31, 2025), with Moody's target of <4.0x within 12 months and S&P's 3.5x-4.0x medium-term range.

FY2025 Financial Performance:
Bacardi reported FY2025 (ended March 31, 2025) revenue of $5.3 billion. Leverage at 4.1x slightly above Moody's target; liquidity adequate ($1B+ access). Key Challenges: Volume headwinds in North America (-3% to -5% organic growth estimated); premium spirits demand moderation; North American market concentration (40%+ of revenue).

Strategic Initiatives:
Strategic Priorities: (a) Deleveraging – Targeting leverage <4.0x through EBITDA growth and FCF deployment; (b) Portfolio Optimization – Continuing focus on premium/super-premium spirits; (c) Pricing Discipline – Offsetting volume headwinds through price increases; (d) International Expansion – Growing emerging market presence to diversify from North America.

Competitive Position:
Market position: World's largest privately held international spirits company (Bacardi is ~96.5% family-owned). Competes with Diageo, Pernod Ricard, and others in premium spirits segments. The company's diversified brand portfolio and premium positioning provide competitive resilience.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
As a private company with family ownership, capital allocation is directed toward debt reduction, strategic acquisitions, and reinvestment. No public dividends; private equity returns managed internally.

Overall Assessment: Bacardi-Martini BV is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – Premium Spirits with Near-Term Headwinds. A quality, privately held spirits leader with temporary leverage elevation. Strengths: (a) Baa3/BBB- investment-grade ratings; (b) 200+ global brands with strong pricing power; (c) Premium spirits focus with long-term demand tailwinds; (d) Strong liquidity; (e) Commitment to investment-grade maintenance. Risks: (a) North American market concentration (40%+ exposure); (b) Leverage currently elevated at 4.1x; (c) Volume headwinds in North America; (d) Premium spirits demand cyclicality; (e) Health/wellness trend (reduced alcohol consumption).

Monitor: (1) Leverage trajectory toward <4.0x target; (2) North American volume trends; (3) Premium spirits demand stabilization; (4) Pricing realization; (5) International growth.

issuers.name: BAE SYSTEMS PLC
issuers.creditComment:
BAE SYSTEMS PLC
Credit Rating: Baa1/Positive (Moody's February 2025) | A-/Stable (S&P Upgraded April 2025 from BBB+) – UK Defence Leader; H1 2025 Sales +11% YoY; EBIT +13% YoY; Order Backlog £78B Record; FFO/Debt 45-55% Projected; Free Cash Flow £4B+ Cumulative 2025-2026​

Credit Momentum: Strong with Recent Upgrade – BAE Systems PLC received an S&P upgrade to A- in April 2025 reflecting record order backlogs, improved credit metrics, and strong execution on major defence programs.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: BAE Systems maintains Baa1/Positive from Moody's (outlook upgraded February 2025) and A-/Stable from S&P (upgraded April 2025 from BBB+). S&P's April 2025 upgrade cited: (a) record order backlog of £78B; (b) FFO/debt projected 45-55% in 2025-2026; (c) debt/EBITDA 1.5-2.0x expected; (d) cumulative FOCF >£4B over 2025-2026; (e) strong execution track record. Moody's positive outlook reflects potential upgrade if debt/EBITDA sustained below 2.5x.​

H1 2025 Financial Results: H1 2025 sales +11% YoY. EBIT +13% YoY. Order backlog £78B (record). Revenue guidance: £28.4B FY2025 (+8% YoY), ~£30B FY2026. Adjusted EBITDA margin 14.5-14.8% expected. Net debt expected ~£7B (2025), ~£6.7B (2026), from £7.2B (2024). Ball Aerospace integration proceeding well. All divisions showing solid growth.​

Balance Sheet & Outlook: S&P-adjusted net debt ~£7B. FFO/debt improving from 43% (2024) to 45%+ (2025), 50%+ (2026). Dividend ~£1.1B (2025). Share buyback £1.5B over three years. Liquidity: ~£2.8B cash, £2B undrawn RCF. Upgrade to A (S&P) possible if FFO/debt >60% sustained with debt/EBITDA <2.0x.​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within global aerospace/defence 8-10% allocation. Strong investment-grade defence credit (Baa1/A-) with record backlog, improving metrics, and structural defence spending tailwinds. Monitor: (1) FFO/debt trajectory toward 50%+; (2) Order intake and backlog sustainability; (3) Ball Aerospace integration; (4) Program execution on key contracts; (5) Moody's upgrade decision timeline.

issuers.name: BAIDU INC
issuers.creditComment:
BAIDU INC
Credit Rating: Unrated (Major Agencies); Estimated A-/Baa1 Equivalent (Based on China Macro Risk) – Chinese Internet Leader; AI-Transformed; Q3 2025 Revenue RMB 31.2B; AI Cloud Infra +33% YoY; Apollo Go Autonomous Driving Breakthrough

Credit Momentum: Turnaround – Baidu Inc. 
 is undergoing a fundamental transformation from a search-revenue-dependent company to an AI and autonomous driving leader. While the company is unrated by major credit agencies, implicit credit support from China's government (as a strategic tech champion) and strong balance sheet metrics suggest an A-/Baa1 equivalent credit standing. Q3 2025 results showed core challenges (revenues RMB 31.2B, -7% YoY; Baidu Core online marketing -18% YoY) offset by explosive AI Cloud growth (+33% YoY, reaching RMB 4.2B) and breakthrough autonomous driving milestones (Apollo Go received Dubai approval for driverless trials, issued 50 test licenses).

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Baidu is unrated by S&P, Moody's, and Fitch. An implicit credit profile (estimated A-/Baa1) reflects the company's strategic importance to China's AI/autonomous driving agenda, strong balance sheet position (RMB 11.2B cash equivalent), and cash-generative operations. Leverage profile: Baidu maintains minimal financial leverage, with cash positions exceeding debt, providing substantial financial flexibility.

Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
Baidu reported challenging topline with strategic positives: Total Revenue: RMB 31.2 billion (-7% YoY). Baidu Core Revenue: RMB 24.7 billion (-7% YoY); Online Marketing down 18% YoY to RMB 15.3B due to advertiser spending caution. Profitability: Non-GAAP Baidu Core margin 9% (vs. 10% prior year). AI-Driven Growth: AI Cloud Infra RMB 4.2B (+33% YoY); AI-native Marketing Services RMB 2.8B (+262% YoY, 18% of core marketing). Apollo Go: 3.1 million fully driverless operational rides (+212% YoY); 22 city footprint.

Strategic Initiatives & Full-Year Guidance:
Strategic Transformation: (a) "AI Native" architecture – Reorganizing entire company around AI monetization models; (b) Apollo Go Global Expansion – Dubai autonomous driving approval represents major regulatory breakthrough; partnerships with Uber (July) and Lyft (August) broadening geographic reach; (c) AI Cloud Infrastructure – Accelerating investment to become China's leading AI computation provider (targeting competing with Alibaba/Tencent); (d) ERNIE LLM Development – Competitive large language model cumulative investment RMB 100 billion since March 2023.

Competitive Position & Brand Portfolio:
Market position: Chinese internet leader transitioning from search revenue dependence to AI/autonomous driving. Key Assets: ERNIE LLM (competitive with ChatGPT), Apollo Go autonomous driving platform (among world's most advanced), AI Cloud Infra (emerging growth engine). Competition: Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance (via Jianying), other Chinese tech giants.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
Baidu maintains a buyback program (~$2.3B executed YTD) and is exploring dividend policies. Capital prioritized toward AI infrastructure investment (RMB 100B cumulative ERNIE/LLM spend). Free cash flow remains strong (~RMB 11.2B 9M YTD), supporting both buybacks and strategic capex.

Overall Assessment: Baidu Inc is recommended OVERWEIGHT – AI/Autonomous Driving Transformation Play. A strategic tech champion with uneven near-term performance but transformative long-term potential. Strengths: (a) AI Cloud Infra growth +33% (emerging growth engine); (b) Apollo Go autonomous driving breakthroughs (Dubai approval, Uber/Lyft partnerships); (c) Strong balance sheet (RMB 11.2B cash equivalents); (d) ERNIE LLM competitiveness with Western models; (e) Implicit government support for strategic tech. Risks: (a) Core online marketing revenue decline (18% YoY) reflecting sluggish Chinese advertiser spending; (b) AI monetization unproven at scale; (c) Regulatory risks (China government intervention); (d) Autonomous driving commercialization timeline uncertain; (e) Competition from larger tech rivals (Alibaba, Tencent).

Monitor: (1) Apollo Go commercial deployment and regulatory approvals; (2) AI Cloud Infra revenue ramp and margins; (3) Online marketing revenue stabilization (critical near-term); (4) ERNIE adoption and AI-native monetization success; (5) China macro advertising recovery.

issuers.name: BARRICK MINING CORP
issuers.creditComment:
BARRICK MINING CORP
Credit Rating: A3/Stable (Moody's Upgraded December 2022) | BBB+/Stable (S&P March 2025) – Global Gold/Copper Leader; Q3 2025 Net Earnings $1.3B +169% YoY; EPS $0.76 +171% YoY; Operating Cash Flow $2.4B; Free Cash Flow $1.5B; Record $3,457/oz Gold Realization​

Credit Momentum: Strong with Commodity Tailwinds – Barrick Mining Corporation maintains strong investment-grade ratings with exceptional earnings leverage to elevated gold prices. Conservative financial policy and balance sheet strength support rating stability through commodity cycles.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Barrick maintains A3/Stable from Moody's (upgraded December 2022) and BBB+/Stable from S&P (affirmed March 2025). Ratings reflect: (a) Tier-1 gold/copper asset portfolio with long mine lives; (b) excellent liquidity ($5.0B cash at Q3 2025); (c) conservative leverage (minimal net debt); (d) disciplined capital allocation balancing growth, returns, and debt management; (e) strong execution on cost reduction initiatives.​

Q3 2025 Exceptional Financial Results: Net earnings $1.3B ($0.76/share), up 169% YoY. Adjusted EPS $0.58, highest since 2013. Revenue $4.15B (+23% YoY). Operating cash flow $2.4B (+82% QoQ), free cash flow $1.5B. Average realized gold price $3,457/oz (+39% YoY). Gold production 829,000 oz (down 12% YoY on expected decline at certain mines). AISC $1,538/oz (+2% YoY). Cash and equivalents $5.0B, total debt $4.7B (minimal net debt).​

Strategic Outlook & Capital Returns: FY2025 gold production guidance 3.15-3.5M oz. Copper production 200,000-230,000 tons. $1B share buyback program active. Quarterly dividend $0.15/share (including $0.05 performance top-up). 30% organic growth target in gold/copper production by 2029. Fourmile project potentially largest/highest-grade gold discovery this century.​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within global mining/commodities 6-8% allocation. Strong investment-grade mining credit with exceptional earnings leverage to gold prices, conservative balance sheet (near net cash), and strong shareholder returns. Risks: (a) gold price volatility; (b) production decline at mature assets; (c) geopolitical exposure (Africa, LatAm). Monitor: (1) FY2025 production delivery; (2) Fourmile project development; (3) Gold/copper price environment; (4) Buyback and dividend sustainability.

issuers.name: BAT CAPITAL CORP
issuers.creditComment:
BAT CAPITAL CORP
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch 2025) – British American Tobacco PLC Financing Subsidiary; Dividend Aristocrat; 1.0x-1.5x Leverage; NGP Transformation

Credit Momentum: Stable – BAT Capital Corp., the financing vehicle for British American Tobacco PLC 
, maintains strong investment-grade ratings (BBB+/Baa1) with Stable outlooks across all three agencies. S&P affirmed BBB+/Stable in April 2025. The ratings reflect BAT's position as the world's leading tobacco company by market cap, with a strong dividend aristocrat track record (50+ years of dividend increases). The company's leverage is conservative at ~1.0x-1.5x (adjusted), providing substantial financial flexibility. Moody's maintains Baa1/Stable, acknowledging the company's strategic pivot toward next-generation products (NGP) and the challenges of declining combustible volumes partially offset by price increases and NGP growth (contributing 36% of net revenue by 2024 vs. 30% in 2022).

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
BAT Capital holds aligned investment-grade ratings of BBB+ (S&P), Baa1 (Moody's), and BBB+ (Fitch), all with Stable outlooks. The company's strong credit profile is anchored by stable cash generation, brand pricing power, and disciplined leverage management. Leverage profile: Adjusted Net Debt-to-EBITDA estimated at 1.0x-1.5x, among the lowest for large-cap industrial companies, reflecting disciplined capital allocation prioritizing dividends and shareholder returns.

Financial Profile:
BAT operates across 180+ countries with diversified geographic revenue base: Americas (48%), Europe, Middle East & Africa (32%), Asia-Pacific (20%). Segment mix: Tobacco combustibles, cigars, oral tobacco products, heated tobacco (glo), e-vapor (Vuse), modern oral nicotine products.

Strategic Initiatives:
Strategic Priorities: (a) NGP Transformation – Target NGP revenue contribution of 50% by 2030 (was 36% in 2024); (b) Combustible Optimization – Maintain pricing power and market share through premium positioning; (c) Dividend Growth – Continue annual dividend growth (50+ year track record); (d) Emerging Market Focus – Capitalize on growth in higher-margin, less regulated markets.

Competitive Position:
Market position: Global #1 tobacco company by market cap. Diversified portfolio of brands (Dunhill, Kent, Lucky Strike, Pall Mall, Rothmans). Structural moat: High barriers to entry (regulatory, marketing restrictions), inelastic demand, limited substitutes in core markets.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
BAT prioritizes shareholder returns through progressive dividend growth (50+ year track record) and opportunistic share repurchases. The company has authorized $2B+ in buyback programs. Conservative leverage policy supports sustainable dividend and buyback capacity.

Overall Assessment: BAT Capital Corp is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 8-10% Defensive Dividend Core. A world-leading tobacco company with fortress balance sheet and dividend aristocrat status. Strengths: (a) BBB+/Baa1 stable ratings (highest in industry); (b) 1.0x-1.5x leverage (conservative); (c) 50+ year dividend growth track record; (d) NGP transformation achieving momentum (36% of revenue 2024); (e) Global brand portfolio with pricing power. Risks: (a) Declining combustible volumes long-term; (b) NGP profitability still inferior to combustibles; (c) Regulatory/litigation risks; (d) Health/wellness trends; (e) ESG pressures.

Monitor: (1) NGP revenue contribution progress (targeting 50% by 2030); (2) Combustible volume trends and pricing; (3) Dividend sustainability; (4) Regulatory/litigation risks; (5) Leverage maintenance.

issuers.name: BAXTER INTERNATIONAL INC
issuers.creditComment:
BAXTER INTERNATIONAL INC
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BBB/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Global Medtech Leader; Q3 2025 Revenue $2.84B; Adjusted EPS $0.69; 2025 Guidance Raised

Credit Momentum: Stable – Baxter International Inc. 
 maintains solid investment-grade ratings (BBB/Baa1) with Stable outlooks reflecting strong operational execution and consistent dividend policy. Q3 2025 results ($2.84B revenue, +5% reported / +2% operational growth) and adjusted EPS $0.69 (+41% YoY) demonstrate resilience in medtech with core portfolio strength. The company raised FY2025 guidance to 4-5% reported sales growth and $2.35-2.40 adjusted EPS (from continuing operations), signaling confidence in cash generation and balance sheet strength. S&P, Moody's, and Fitch maintain Stable outlooks, acknowledging Baxter's market-leading positions in renal therapy, hospital products, and global infusion/compounding services.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Baxter holds aligned investment-grade ratings of BBB (S&P), Baa1 (Moody's), and BBB (Fitch), all with Stable outlooks. The company maintains strong financial discipline and consistent dividend policy. Leverage profile: Conservative estimated leverage at 1.5x-2.0x net Debt-to-EBITDA, providing substantial financial flexibility.

Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
Baxter reported strong Q3 2025 results: Continuing Operations Revenue: $2.84 billion (+5% reported, +2% operational). U.S. Sales: $1.54 billion (+3% reported, -1% operational). International Sales: $1.29 billion (+8% reported, +5% operational). Adjusted Diluted EPS: $0.69 (+41% YoY). Front Line Care strong growth; Pharmaceuticals segment +7% operational growth (Drug Compounding, Injectables strength).

Strategic Initiatives & 2025 Guidance Raised:
Updated FY2025 Guidance: Continuing Operations Sales Growth: 4-5% reported, 1-2% operational. Adjusted EPS: $2.35-2.40 per diluted share. Strategic Priorities: (a) Portfolio Focus – Exiting discontinued operations, focusing on core medtech; (b) Geographic Expansion – International market penetration (5% operational growth Q3); (c) Innovation – Drug Compounding, Injectables, Infusion platforms; (d) Dividend Growth – Consistent with cash generation (20+ year dividend growth track record).

Competitive Position & Brand Portfolio:
Market position: Global medtech leader with market-leading positions in renal therapy (RenalGuard), IV solutions, infusion systems, drug compounding. The company competes with Fresenius, DaVita, Cardinal Health in select niches but enjoys scale and brand advantages.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
Baxter prioritizes dividends (20+ year growth track record) and opportunistic share repurchases. Balance sheet strength supports sustainable distribution policy with consistent increases.

Overall Assessment: Baxter International Inc is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 5-7% Quality Medtech Dividend Core. A global medtech leader with strong cash generation and consistent dividend growth. Strengths: (a) BBB/Baa1 stable ratings; (b) Q3 revenue beat (+5% reported) and EPS +41% YoY; (c) FY2025 guidance raised to 4-5% growth; (d) International segment strength (+5% operational Q3); (e) 20+ year dividend growth track record; (f) Conservative leverage (1.5x-2.0x). Risks: (a) Healthcare regulatory/pricing pressure; (b) Supply chain disruptions; (c) Competition in core markets; (d) Macro recession sensitivity (hospital capex, elective procedures).

Monitor: (1) Quarterly revenue/EPS realization vs. raised FY2025 guidance; (2) International market growth sustainability; (3) Drug Compounding/Injectables growth momentum; (4) Dividend growth continuation.

issuers.name: BECLE SA DE CV
issuers.creditComment:
BECLE SA DE CV (JOSE CUERVO)
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Oct 2025) | BBB-/Stable (S&P Affirmed Nov 2024) – World's Largest Tequila Producer; Strong Brand Equity; Improving Margins
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Becle, S.A.B. de C.V. 
 (Jose Cuervo) retains its investment-grade status. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB' rating in October 2025, and S&P revised its outlook to Stable from Negative in late 2024, affirming 'BBB-'. The stabilization reflects improving credit metrics and resilience in its core tequila portfolio despite a normalization in spirits consumption.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are underpinned by: (a) leadership in the global tequila category (~30% market share); (b) vertical integration (agave ownership); (c) strong free cash flow generation; (d) conservative leverage (Net Debt/EBITDA expected <2.0x). The company is navigating a destocking cycle in the US but benefits from lower input costs (agave prices).
​

Financial Profile: Fitch projects Becle's EBITDA net leverage to remain below 2.0x over the next 12-24 months. Total debt decreased in 2025 as the company paid down senior notes. Liquidity is robust with cash balances exceeding short-term debt. FCF is positive due to reduced working capital needs.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Emerging Market Consumer 4-5% allocation. High-quality consumer staple credit (BBB/BBB-) with deleveraging tailwinds. Monitor: (1) US spirits demand/premiumization trends; (2) Agave price stability; (3) FX (MXN/USD) impact on margins; (4) M&A discipline.

issuers.name: BELL CANADA
issuers.creditComment:
BELL CANADA
Credit Rating: BBB/Negative (S&P May 2025) | Baa3/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BBB/Stable (DBRS May 2025) – Canada's Largest Telecom; BCE Inc. Operating Subsidiary; Q1 2025 FCF $798M; 3.56x Net Leverage (Improved); $900M Hybrid Notes Issued; Dividend Policy Pressuring Credit Profile

Credit Momentum: Negative/Pressured – Bell Canada , the major operating subsidiary of BCE Inc., faces credit pressure with S&P revising outlook to Negative (May 2025), affirming BBB. Moody's downgraded both BCE and Bell Canada in 2025, citing high debt levels and limited deleveraging commitment while maintaining dividend growth. DBRS downgraded Bell Canada to BBB (May 2025) from BBB (high). The company issued $900M hybrid notes in Q1 2025 with 50% equity credit to improve leverage optics, but underlying dividend payout exceeds free cash flow:​

Credit Ratings & Downgrade Dynamics:
Bell Canada maintains BBB/Negative from S&P (May 2025), Baa3/Stable from Moody's, BBB/Stable from DBRS (May 2025). S&P May 2025: Revised outlook to Negative, affirming BBB; Expects elevated leverage of 3.8x adjusted for 2025. Moody's 2025: Downgraded to last level above junk (Baa3), citing debt consistently increased since 2019 with no demonstrated deleveraging commitment. DBRS May 2025: Downgraded to BBB from BBB (high); Removed from Under Review with Negative Implications; All trends Stable. Governance concern: Moody's cited dividend growth commitment despite elevated leverage as governance risk driving downgrade.​

Q1 2025 Financial Performance:
Bell Canada delivered improved Q1 2025: Free cash flow: $798M vs. $85M in Q1 2024, significant improvement. Cash from operations: $1,571M (+38.8% YoY). Net leverage: Improved to 3.56x adjusted EBITDA (March 2025) from 3.81x (December 2024). Hybrid notes: Issued $900M Bell Canada Series B notes due 2055 with 7.00% initial rate; 50% equity treatment from rating agencies. Debt tender: Repurchased $844M USD ($1,205M CAD) of notes in March 2025, contributing to net debt reduction.​

Dividend Policy Challenge:
BCE's dividend strategy constrains credit improvement: Historical payout: Dividends exceeded FCF in 7 of past 9 years; 130% payout in 2023, 111% in 2023, 108% in 2022. Cumulative analysis: Generated $25.8B FCF since 2012 vs. $29B common dividends paid—$3.2B net shortfall. Dividend commitment: 16 consecutive years of dividend growth; CEO called it "unwavering commitment." Current yield: ~8.5% dividend yield, exceptionally high for telecom sector, attracting income investors but pressuring credit metrics. Moody's warning: Bell Canada must focus on deleveraging "in reasonable timeframe" to sustain revised rating.​

Overall Assessment: Bell Canada is recommended UNDERWEIGHT – 2-3% within Canadian telecom 4-5% North American IG allocation. Bell Canada faces credit deterioration (BBB/Negative) from persistent dividend commitment exceeding FCF generation, requiring deleveraging action to avoid further downgrades. Strengths include: (a) BBB/Baa3 ratings remaining investment grade; (b) Q1 2025 FCF $798M improvement; (c) net leverage improved to 3.56x from 3.81x; (d) hybrid notes providing equity credit; (e) Canada's largest telecom with scale advantages; (f) good liquidity through next 12 months. Risks include: (a) S&P Negative outlook with potential downgrade; (b) Moody's downgrade citing governance/dividend concerns; (c) dividend exceeding FCF structurally; (d) competitive pricing pressures in Canadian mobile; (e) elevated leverage limiting financial flexibility; (f) further downgrades could approach sub-investment grade. RECOMMENDED: Underweight given Negative outlook, structural dividend/FCF imbalance, and downgrade trajectory; Upgrade requires demonstrated deleveraging commitment.

Monitor: (1) S&P outlook (Negative resolution and potential downgrade); (2) Deleveraging (3.56x trajectory toward 3.0x); (3) Dividend policy (Payout reduction or FCF improvement); (4) Competitive dynamics (Rogers, Telus pricing); (5) Hybrid issuance (Additional equity-credited capital).

issuers.name: BERKSHIRE HATHAWAY
issuers.creditComment:
BERKSHIRE HATHAWAY INC
Credit Rating: Aa2/Stable (Moody's Affirmed May 2025) | AA/Stable (S&P Affirmed October 2024) – Global Diversified Conglomerate; Q1 2025 Operating Earnings $9.6B; Cash & Investments $277B+ Record; Insurance Leadership; BNSF, BHE, Manufacturing Diversification​

Credit Momentum: Exceptionally Strong – Berkshire Hathaway Inc. maintains premier investment-grade ratings reflecting extraordinary diversification, exceptional capital position, and conservative financial policy under Warren Buffett's leadership.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Berkshire maintains Aa2/Stable from Moody's (affirmed May 2025) and AA/Stable from S&P (affirmed October 2024). Ratings reflect: (a) extraordinarily well-capitalized insurance operations; (b) highly diversified earnings across insurance, railroad (BNSF), energy (BHE), manufacturing, and retail; (c) conservative financial policy with large liquidity pool ($277B+ cash/investments); (d) moderate financial leverage; (e) strong and predictable operating profitability.​

Q1 2025 Financial Performance: Q1 2025 net operating earnings $9.6B (from $11.2B YoY, impacted by California wildfire losses). Net earnings $4.6B (from $12.7B YoY, due to investment losses vs. prior year gains). Insurance investment income and BNSF/BHE earnings offset wildfire impacts. Cash and short-term investments at record $277B+. CEO succession: Greg Abel to become CEO effective January 2026.​

Balance Sheet & Enterprise Risk: S&P-adjusted leverage ~1x (vs. near-zero for Apple, Microsoft, Alphabet 
). Strong subsidiary credit profiles: GEICO, BNSF, BHE, Lubrizol, Precision Castparts. Pilot Travel Centers upgraded to investment grade (Baa3 Moody's) following 100% Berkshire ownership. Rating stability despite investment portfolio concentration in Apple and Bank of America 
.​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 5-6% within global diversified holdings 10-12% allocation. Premier investment-grade conglomerate (Aa2/AA) with exceptional diversification, fortress balance sheet, and conservative financial policy. Monitor: (1) CEO succession transition (January 2026); (2) Capital deployment and acquisition activity; (3) Insurance underwriting profitability; (4) BNSF and BHE performance; (5) Investment portfolio concentration.

issuers.name: BHARTI AIRTEL LTD
issuers.creditComment:
BHARTI AIRTEL LIMITED
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Nov 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's Nov 2025) | BBB-/Stable (Fitch 2025) – India's Second-Largest Telecom Operator; 624M Subscribers Across 17 Countries; ₹256 Industry-Leading ARPU; 33.5% Wireless Market Share; ₹52,145Cr Q2 FY26 Revenue (+26% YoY); 1.5-1.8x Target Leverage

Credit Momentum: Positive/Upgraded – Bharti Airtel 
 achieved significant credit improvement with S&P upgrading to BBB (from BBB-) in November 2025 and Moody's upgrading to Baa2 (from Baa3) in November 2025, reflecting substantial improvement in financial profile, steadily increasing market share, and structural changes in India's fast-growing mobile sector. The company now stands rated one notch above India's sovereign rating (BBB-/Baa3), reflecting established market position, solid leverage trajectory (trending toward 1.5x-1.8x), demonstrated capital markets access, and supportive shareholders:

Credit Ratings & Upgrade Rationale:
Bharti Airtel maintains BBB/Stable ratings from S&P (upgraded November 17, 2025) and Baa2/Stable from Moody's (upgraded November 3, 2025), with BBB-/Stable from Fitch. S&P upgrade rationale: Raised to BBB from BBB- reflecting strong operating performance in India, expectation of healthy earnings growth, robust subscriber additions, and industry-leading ARPU positioning; Ratings above India sovereign (BBB-) constrained to one notch maximum due to economic linkages; Priority debt ratio ~60% as of September 2025 above 50% threshold. Moody's upgrade rationale: Raised to Baa2 from Baa3 reflecting significant improvement in financial profile and steadily increasing market share supported by structural changes in India's fast-growing mobile sector; Moderating competition and relatively supportive regulatory environment contributed to upgrade; Leverage trending toward 1.5x-1.8x over next 12-18 months; Position well to be rated above India's sovereign rating. Both agencies noted settlement of Adjusted Gross Revenue (AGR) issue in 2021 created relatively stable Indian telecom environment supporting three-player market structure.​

Q2 FY2026 Record Financial Performance:
Bharti Airtel reported exceptional Q2 FY26 results (October-November 2025): Revenue: ₹52,145 crore (+25.7% YoY, +5.4% QoQ). India revenue: ₹38,690 crore (+22.6% YoY, +2.9% QoQ). Profitability: Net profit ₹8,651 crore (more than doubled vs ₹4,153 crore Q2 FY25 per some reports); Net income ₹6,791-6,792 crore (+89% YoY, +14.2% QoQ per other reports); EBITDA margin improved to 56.7% (from 56.3% Q1 FY26). ARPU: Industry-leading ₹256 (+9.9% YoY vs ₹233 Q2 FY25, +2.4% QoQ vs ₹250 Q1 FY26)—comfortably ahead of Reliance Jio 
 ₹211.4. Subscriber metrics: Total customer base 624 million across 17 countries (+10.7% YoY); Smartphone data customers up 22.2 million YoY and 5.1 million QoQ (78% of mobile base); Postpaid net adds 0.95 million; Mobile data consumption up 26.6% YoY to 28.3 GB/customer/month.​

Leverage Improvement & Balance Sheet Strength:
Bharti Airtel's deleveraging has been central to ratings upgrades: Debt reduction: Prepayment of ₹260 billion ($3 billion) in higher-cost deferred spectrum liabilities in FY2024-25 accelerated balance sheet improvement. Net debt: Declined 5% YoY to ₹1.94 lakh crore as of Q2 FY26 reflecting disciplined capital allocation. Leverage trajectory: Moody's expects adjusted debt-to-EBITDA to improve to 1.8x by FY2025-26 (from 2.3x FY2024-25) and 1.5x by FY2026-27. Capital expenditure: Q2 FY26 capex ₹11,362 crore (₹9,643 crore India—largely 5G and fibre expansion). Bharti Telecom debt: S&P highlighted Bharti Telecom (holding company) debt of ~₹40,000 crore as of September 2025 (>15% of Bharti Airtel's adjusted debt)—a monitoring point despite rise in dividend receipts. S&P forecasts 2-4% annual subscriber additions and 6-8% ARPU growth supporting continued deleveraging.​

Market Position & Competitive Dynamics:
Bharti Airtel maintains strong competitive positioning: India market: Second-largest mobile operator with 33.5% subscriber market share (up from 28% March 2020); Revenue market share likely even higher reflecting industry-leading ARPU; Extensive pan-India network coverage; Large spectrum holdings. Global scale: Among top three telecom service providers globally by subscribers (624 million total). Three-player market: Moody's expects regulator to remain supportive of three-player market structure which has stabilized competition; Vodafone Idea 
 continues losing share to Bharti. Africa operations: ~20% of consolidated earnings; Revenue grew 36% YoY in rupee terms Q2 FY26. Homes business: 951,000 net customer additions Q2 FY26 demonstrating fixed broadband success. Potential for further upgrade exists if ARPU strengthens to ~₹300, adjusted EBITDA margins improve to ~60%, and adjusted debt falls to ~₹2 trillion.​

Overall Assessment: Bharti Airtel Limited is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 5-6% within telecom/EM corporates 8-10% India allocation. Bharti Airtel represents strong investment-grade credit (BBB/Baa2) following dual upgrades in November 2025, with leading Indian telecom position, industry-leading ARPU, and deleveraging trajectory. Strengths include: (a) S&P and Moody's dual upgrades to BBB/Baa2 in November 2025 reflecting credit improvement; (b) ratings now one notch above India sovereign demonstrating premium credit profile; (c) industry-leading ARPU ₹256 comfortably ahead of Jio (₹211.4); (d) record Q2 FY26 revenue (+26% YoY) and profit (more than doubled YoY); (e) market share gains to 33.5% (from 28% in 2020) capturing Vodafone Idea weakness; (f) leverage trajectory toward 1.5x-1.8x by FY26-27 from deleveraging focus; (g) ₹260B spectrum liability prepayment accelerating balance sheet improvement; (h) 624 million subscribers across 17 countries providing scale; (i) three-player market structure reducing competitive intensity; (j) 5G and fibre investment strengthening network leadership. Risks include: (a) India sovereign rating constraint limiting upgrade potential to one notch above BBB-; (b) Bharti Telecom holding company debt (₹40,000 crore) requiring monitoring; (c) priority debt ratio ~60% above 50% threshold; (d) Africa operations (~20% earnings) exposed to emerging market risks; (e) regulatory uncertainty despite AGR settlement; (f) Jio competition and potential tariff pressures; (g) spectrum auction capital requirements; (h) currency volatility affecting Africa operations translation. RECOMMENDED: Overweight leading Indian telecom with dual rating upgrades, industry-leading ARPU, and deleveraging trajectory supporting further credit improvement.

Monitor: (1) Leverage trajectory (1.5x-1.8x debt-to-EBITDA achievement by FY26-27); (2) ARPU progression (₹256 trending toward ₹300 target for further upgrade potential); (3) Market share (33.5% maintenance and gains from Vodafone Idea); (4) Bharti Telecom debt (Holding company leverage monitoring per S&P); (5) India sovereign rating (BBB-/Baa3 maintenance supporting ratings above sovereign).

issuers.name: BHP BILLITON FIN USA LTD
issuers.creditComment:
BHP BILLITON FINANCE (USA) LTD
Credit Rating: A1/Stable (Moody's) | A-/Stable (S&P) | A/Stable (Fitch) – Bond-Issuing Vehicle of BHP Group Limited 
; World's Largest Diversified Miner; Multi-Billion USD Bond Program Outstanding; Full Parent Guarantee​

Credit Momentum: Stable with Strong Parental Support – BHP Billiton Finance (USA) Ltd serves as the U.S. dollar bond-issuing subsidiary of BHP Group Limited, the world's largest diversified mining company. All debt is fully, unconditionally, and irrevocably guaranteed by BHP Group Limited.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: BHP Billiton Finance maintains A1/Stable from Moody's and A-/Stable from S&P (August 2025), equivalent to parent BHP Group Limited ratings. Ratings reflect: (a) world-leading diversified mining portfolio (iron ore, copper, coal, potash); (b) Tier-1 low-cost assets in stable jurisdictions; (c) conservative financial policy; (d) strong liquidity and balance sheet; (e) disciplined capital allocation.​

Bond Program & Recent Issuances: Active multi-tranche USD bond program including: (a) 5.0% notes due 2030 ($1.0B, February 2025); (b) 5.125% notes due 2032 ($750M, February 2025); (c) 5.3% notes due 2035 ($1.25B, February 2025); (d) 5.0% notes due 2036 ($500M, September 2025). Notes are senior unsecured, pari passu with other BHP Group Limited senior unsecured obligations. Proceeds used for general corporate purposes including debt refinancing.​

Parent BHP Group Financial Strength: BHP maintains exceptional balance sheet with: (a) strong investment-grade ratings; (b) conservative leverage targets; (c) substantial liquidity headroom; (d) progressive dividend policy. Short-term rating P-1 (Moody's), F-1 (Fitch).​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within global mining/materials 5-6% allocation. High-quality investment-grade mining credit fully backed by BHP Group Limited guarantee. Notes offer diversified commodities exposure through world-class assets. Risks: (a) commodity price cyclicality; (b) China demand sensitivity; (c) ESG/decarbonization pressures on coal assets. Monitor: (1) Parent BHP Group rating actions; (2) Commodity price environment (iron ore, copper); (3) Capex execution on growth projects; (4) Dividend policy sustainability.

issuers.name: BIMBO BAKERIES USA INC
issuers.creditComment:
BIMBO BAKERIES USA INC
Bimbo Bakeries USA is the main U.S. subsidiary and funding vehicle of Grupo Bimbo. Moody’s assigns Baa1/Stable to BBU’s backed senior unsecured global notes, guaranteed by the Mexican parent. S&P rates Grupo Bimbo BBB+ with Negative outlook, reflecting rising capex and modest margin pressure, but the group remains a large, diversified global bakery with solid cash generation supporting U.S. credit quality.​

issuers.name: BLACKSTONE HOLDINGS
issuers.creditComment:
BLACKSTONE HOLDINGS
Credit Rating: A+/Stable (S&P October 2025) | A1/Stable (Moody's) – World's Largest Alternative Asset Manager; $1.2T+ Total AUM; Q2 2025 GAAP Net Income $1.3B; Fee-Related Earnings $1.8B; Strong Fundraising Momentum​

Credit Momentum: Strong with Sector Leadership – Blackstone Inc. maintains the highest credit ratings among alternative asset managers reflecting exceptional scale, diversified strategies, and strong cash generation.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Blackstone maintains A+/Stable from S&P (assigned October 2025 to new issuance) and A1/Stable from Moody's. Ratings reflect: (a) world's largest alternative asset manager ($1.2T+ AUM); (b) diversified strategies (real estate, private equity, credit, infrastructure); (c) strong fee-related earnings ($1.8B Q2 2025); (d) conservative leverage; (e) exceptional fundraising momentum; (f) industry-leading investment performance.​

Q2 2025 Financial Performance: Q2 2025 GAAP net income $1.3B. Fee-related earnings $1.8B. Distributable earnings $1.3B. Total AUM $1.2T+. Strong fundraising continues across flagship funds. Blackstone Secured Lending Fund (BXSL) upgraded to Baa2 from Baa3 (August 2025). Blackstone Private Credit Fund (BCRED) maintains BBB-/Positive from S&P and Baa2/Stable from Moody's—only non-traded BDC with such ratings.​

Strategic Positioning: Global investment strategies across real estate, private equity, credit, and infrastructure. Industry leadership in private credit ($190B+ following HPS-like platform development). Strong institutional relationships and perpetual capital vehicles providing fee stability. Conservative parent leverage with strong interest coverage.​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within global alternative asset managers 6-8% allocation. Premier alternative asset manager credit (A+/A1) with exceptional scale, diversified strategies, and conservative leverage. Monitor: (1) Fundraising momentum and AUM growth; (2) Fee-related earnings stability; (3) Investment performance across vintage years; (4) Credit fund performance and leverage; (5) Real estate market recovery.

issuers.name: BLOSSOM JOY LTD
issuers.creditComment:
BLOSSOM JOY LTD
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed October 2025) – Funding Vehicle for COFCO (Hong Kong) Limited; Guaranteed by Parent; Strategic Agribusiness Role
​
Credit Momentum: Stable with Strong Parent Support – Blossom Joy Limited is a special purpose funding vehicle for COFCO (Hong Kong) Limited, the primary offshore platform of China's state-owned agribusiness giant COFCO Corporation. Fitch affirmed the A- rating in October 2025, mirroring the rating of its guarantor, COFCO (HK), due to the unconditional and irrevocable guarantee.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Fitch rates the guaranteed notes A-/Stable. Ratings reflect: (a) status as a core subsidiary of COFCO Corporation (central SOE); (b) strategic importance to China's food security; (c) strong linkage between Blossom Joy (issuer), COFCO HK (guarantor), and the ultimate parent; (d) guarantee structure providing full credit substitution. The 3.10% perpetual securities (issued 2020) were redeemed in October 2025.
​
Bond Structure: Issued US$500 million 3.10% subordinated guaranteed perpetual securities (ISIN: XS2237806364). These were redeemed in full on October 21, 2025. Other senior notes may remain outstanding or be issued under similar guarantee structures, which would carry the A- rating.
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within China SOE/Agribusiness 4-5% allocation. High-quality central SOE credit (A-) benefitting from sovereign support. Monitor: (1) China food security policy; (2) COFCO Group leverage; (3) Commodity price volatility; (4) Strategic importance to the state.

BMW US Capital LLC - USU09513KS75

Bond: $500M, 5.20%, 2035 maturity, issued August 2025​
