issuers.creditComment:
CK HUTCHISON INTL 23 (USG21819AB63)
Credit Rating: S&p A-/Stable, Moody's A2 Stable, Fitch A/Stable

Credit Momentum: Stable - Hong Kong Diversified Conglomerate. CK Hutchison maintains solid investment-grade A-/A2 reflecting: (1) diversified conglomerate (retail/ports/telecoms/energy/infrastructure, €65B+ revenue, Hong Kong-listed multinational); (2) Asia dominance (Hong Kong/China/Southeast Asia core markets, strategic gateway positioning); (3) dividend aristocrat (40+ year dividend increasing streak, 3-4% yields); (4) founder-family control (Li Ka-shing family 40%+ ownership, aligned shareholders, long-term focus); (5) infrastructure/utilities stable earnings (ports/telecoms/energy core assets provide recurrent revenue). Recent: port volumes recovering (post-pandemic, trade normalization), retail rebounds (Hong Kong/Asia consumption recovery), telecom consolidation (Southeast Asia market share gains), dividend sustained.

Diversification Advantage + Hong Kong Exposure: CK Hutchison's conglomerate structure: (1) ports dominant (Hutchison Ports world's largest container terminal operator, 300+ locations globally); (2) retail significant (Watsons/PARKnSHOP supermarket chains Asia leader); (3) telecoms growing (3 HK, Hutchison Telefónica combining Spain/Portugal operations); (4) energy/infrastructure (power projects, renewables investments); (5) strategic stakes (Google Fiber financing, e-commerce platforms). However, Hong Kong concentration remains: 40% revenues from Hong Kong/China, geopolitical risks embedded. Challenges: (1) Hong Kong autonomy declining (National Security Law impacts governance); (2) China relations (U.S.-China tensions affecting Asia-Pacific operations); (3) founder aging (Li Ka-shing born 1928, succession planning ongoing); (4) structural retail decline (e-commerce cannibalization).

Overall Assessment: CK Hutchison represents Asian diversified conglomerate with dividend history - A-/A2 reflecting: (1) diversified business (ports/retail/telecoms/energy reduce concentration); (2) Asia dominance (strategic positioning); (3) dividend aristocrat (40+ year streak); (4) founder control (aligned interests). RECOMMENDED MARKET WEIGHT for Asian dividend + diversification - quality credit with Hong Kong exposure. Suitable for: Asian conglomerate exposure, dividend income (3-4% yields), infrastructure thematic. Key strengths: diversified business (ports/retail/telecoms balance), dividend aristocrat history, founder control (long-term focus), infrastructure moat (ports dominance). Challenges: (1) Hong Kong exposure (political/economic headwinds); (2) founder succession risk (Li Ka-shing aging, succession clarity limited); (3) conglomerate discount (trading below sum-of-parts valuation); (4) e-commerce cannibalization (retail declining). RECOMMENDED 2-3% for Asian dividend + diversification + infrastructure. CK Hutchison bonds trade 100-140bps reflecting A- quality + Asian/HK exposure - fairly valued. Monitor: (1) Hong Kong economy (emigration, property sector); (2) founder succession (Li Ka-shing age 96, succession timeline); (3) port volumes (trade normalization); (4) retail trends (e-commerce impact); (5) telecom market share (Southeast Asia consolidation).

issuers.name: CLP POWER HK FINANCING
issuers.creditComment:
CLP POWER HONG KONG FINANCING LTD
Credit Rating: A1/Stable (Moody's Affirmed August 2025) | A+/Stable (S&P) – Financing Vehicle for CLP Power Hong Kong; Unconditional Guarantee from Parent; Critical Utility Infrastructure; Recent Perpetual Capital Securities Issuance
​

Credit Momentum: Stable with Strong Market Access – CLP Power Hong Kong Financing Limited serves as the primary financing platform for CLP Power Hong Kong Limited, the sole electricity provider for Kowloon, New Territories, and Lantau (80% of HK population). Moody's affirmed the A1 rating with a stable outlook in August 2025.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: A1/Stable (Moody's) and A+/Stable (S&P). Ratings reflect: (a) highly predictable cash flows under the Scheme of Control (SoC) agreement with the HK government (valid until 2033); (b) monopoly position in its service area; (c) unconditional and irrevocable guarantee from CLP Power Hong Kong Limited; (d) strong liquidity and financial management. Recent issuance of US$500M 5.45% perpetual securities (rated A3/A-) demonstrates robust market access.
​

Bond Program: Active Medium Term Note (MTN) programme of US$4.5B. Issuances include senior notes (A1/A+) and perpetual capital securities (A3/A-). Proceeds often used for refinancing, capex, and energy transition projects. The guarantee ensures equal ranking with the parent's senior unsecured debt.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within HK/Asia Utilities 8-10% allocation. Premier defensive utility credit (A1/A+) with regulatory certainty and essential service nature. Monitor: (1) Scheme of Control reviews; (2) Fuel cost pass-through mechanisms; (3) Decarbonization capex targets; (4) Interest rate environment.

issuers.name: CME GROUP INC
issuers.creditComment:
CME GROUP INC
Credit Rating: AA-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Feb 2025) | Aa3/Stable (Moody's Affirmed) | AA-/Stable (S&P Affirmed) – Global Derivatives Exchange; High Margins; Counter-Cyclical Hedge
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – CME Group Inc. [finance:CME Group Inc] maintains a high-quality "Double-A" credit profile. Fitch affirmed the 'AA-' Long-Term IDR with a Stable outlook on February 6, 2025. S&P and Moody's maintain equivalent high-grade ratings (Aa3/AA-). The company is the world's leading derivatives marketplace (interest rates, energy, ags, equities), benefitting from volatility which drives trading volumes and clearing fees.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) dominant global position in futures and options trading; (b) diverse product mix providing revenue stability (rates volatility offsets equity calm, etc.); (c) extremely strong profitability (EBITDA margins often >60%); (d) central counterparty (CCP) risk management framework; (e) low financial leverage. The primary risk is regulatory/systemic risk associated with the clearinghouse function, but capital safeguards are robust.
​

Financial Profile: CME Group generates substantial free cash flow with low capital intensity. The company operates with minimal financial leverage (typically <1.0x debt/EBITDA). It employs a variable dividend policy to return excess cash to shareholders while maintaining a fortress balance sheet. Liquidity is exceptional, supported by cash balances and clearing member guaranty funds.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Financial Exchanges 4-5% allocation. "Blue Chip" High-Grade (AA-) with volatility protection. Monitor: (1) Trading volumes (ADV) across asset classes; (2) Regulatory capital requirements for CCPs; (3) Interest rate volatility (key revenue driver); (4) Competitive landscape in futures.

issuers.name: CNAC HK FINBRIDGE CO LTD
issuers.creditComment:
CNAC HK FINBRIDGE CO LTD (XS2226808249)
Credit Rating: S&p BBB-/Stable, Moody's Baa3 Stable, Fitch BBB-/Stable

Credit Momentum: Stable - Chinese Air China Financing. CNAC HK Finbridge maintains investment-grade threshold BBB-/Baa3 reflecting: (1) parent Air China airline (one of China's "Big Three" carriers, 30%+ domestic market share); (2) financing vehicle structure (debt subsidiary, credit linked to parent airline); (3) Chinese government backing (implicit guarantee, aviation industry strategic importance); (4) dividend concentration (airline industry cyclical, but state-owned enterprises supported); (5) energy transition exposure (aviation decarbonization requiring capex for SAF/new aircraft). Recent: post-COVID air travel recovery (international routes recovering 2024, domestic demand strong), fuel surcharges enabling margin expansion, government support sustained (route protection, subsidy programs).

Chinese Airline Dynamics + Aviation Decarbonization: Air China operates in unique environment: (1) Chinese government protection (routes allocated administratively, not competitive); (2) state ownership (majority CNAC Group state enterprise, implicit guarantee); (3) domestic market strength (3 million+ daily passengers in China, growing middle class); (4) international expansion challenging (U.S. relations tension limiting American routes, European competition intense). Decarbonization challenges: (1) SAF (sustainable aviation fuel) capex requirements, not yet profitable; (2) new aircraft (electrification/hydrogen still decades away, fleet replacement capex ongoing); (3) regulatory pressure (EU ETS carbon pricing, ICAO offsetting). Risks: (1) commodity fuel exposure (oil prices volatile, surcharges offset limited); (2) Chinese economy slowdown (demand destruction); (3) geopolitical tensions (route restrictions, sanctions risk); (4) decarbonization capex (cost pressures).

Overall Assessment: CNAC HK Finbridge represents Chinese airline financing with state backing - BBB-/Baa3 reflecting: (1) parent Air China market share (30%+ domestic); (2) Chinese government implicit guarantee; (3) airline industry cyclicality; (4) decarbonization capex needs. RECOMMENDED UNDERWEIGHT for typical portfolios - airline industry cyclicality + Chinese geopolitical risks. Better transportation exposure: railway (CNR/CPR quality) vs. airline cyclicality. Suitable only for: Chinese exposure specialists, transportation thematic accepting cyclicality. Challenges: (1) airline cyclicality (fuel price, demand volatility); (2) geopolitical risks (Chinese tensions, route restrictions); (3) decarbonization capex (costs mounting); (4) Chinese economy slowdown (demand destruction). RECOMMENDED AVOID for core allocation. CNAC bonds trade 200-280bps reflecting frontier airline risk premium - insufficient for cyclicality. Monitor: (1) air travel volumes (quarterly trend); (2) fuel prices (commodity exposure); (3) government support (subsidy programs); (4) Chinese geopolitical risk; (5) decarbonization capex pace.

issuers.name: CNOOC PETROLEUM NORTH
issuers.creditComment:
CNOOC PETROLEUM NORTH AMERICA ULC
Credit Rating: A+/Stable (S&P Parent CNOOC 2025) | A1/Stable (Moody's Parent 2025) | A+/Stable (Fitch Parent 2025) – Wholly-Owned Subsidiary CNOOC Limited; Canada-Based Oil & Gas Operations; $2.2B Bond Debt Outstanding; Long Lake Oil Sands; Yellowtail Guyana Participation; Parent Support & Guarantee

Credit Momentum: Stable – CNOOC Petroleum North America ULC (CPNA) maintains strong investment-grade credit profile (implicitly A+/A1) as indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of CNOOC Limited, China's largest offshore oil and gas producer. CPNA's credit profile is directly linked to parent CNOOC Limited which reported record H1 2025 production (384.6 million BOE, +6.1% YoY), RMB 69.5 billion net profit, and continued exploration success across China offshore and international operations including Guyana where Yellowtail project commenced August 2025 (250,000 boe/day plateau):

Credit Ratings & Parent Linkage:
CNOOC Petroleum North America ULC (CPNA) has no standalone public credit rating but benefits from parent CNOOC Limited ratings: S&P A+/Stable, Moody's A1/Stable, Fitch A+/Stable. Parent ownership: CPNA is indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of CNOOC Limited per company filings. Bond debt: CPNA has approximately $2.2 billion bond debt outstanding including: 7.400% notes due 2028 ($144 million); Additional series due 2029 and longer-dated maturities. Parent guarantee: CPNA debt benefits from strategic importance to parent and integration within CNOOC Limited's global portfolio; Parent support underpins subsidiary credit quality. CNOOC Limited is government-related entity with majority ownership by CNOOC Group (state-owned enterprise), providing implicit sovereign support from People's Republic of China.​

Parent CNOOC Limited H1 2025 Performance:
CNOOC Limited reported strong H1 2025 results (August 2025): Production: Net production 384.6 million BOE (+6.1% YoY) with both domestic and international production exceeding record highs; Natural gas up 12.0% YoY. Q3 2025 production: 193.7 million BOE (+7.9% YoY) with natural gas up 11.6% YoY for nine-month period (578.3 million BOE). Profitability: H1 2025 net profit attributable to equity shareholders RMB 69.5 billion; Nine-month net profit RMB 101.97 billion. Exploration: 5 new oil and gas discoveries in H1 2025 (Jinzhou 27-6, Caofeidian 22-3, Weizhou 10-5 South offshore China); 22 oil and gas structures successfully appraised in nine months; Guyana reserves continued growing through three-dimensional deepwater exploration. Cost efficiency: All-in cost 27.35 US$/boe; Production cost approximately $30/boe enabling competitiveness across oil price scenarios. Dividend: HK$0.73/share interim dividend declared.​

North America Operations & CPNA Assets:
CPNA operates oil and gas assets in Canada and participates in US/international projects: Canadian operations: Long Lake oil sands project in Alberta (acquired through Nexen acquisition 2013); Conventional oil and gas properties. Guyana participation: CNOOC Limited holds working interests in Stabroek Block (Guyana) operated by ExxonMobil 
; Yellowtail project (fourth development phase) commenced production August 2025 with plateau capacity of 250,000 boe/day; Stabroek Block production capacity increased to 900,000 boe/day; CNOOC holds 25% working interest. Production contribution: International operations (including North America) contributed ~31% of CNOOC Limited's nine-month production (vs 69% China offshore). CPNA provides CNOOC Limited with exposure to Western Hemisphere conventional and unconventional resources diversifying geographic concentration.​

Strategic Positioning & Outlook:
CNOOC Limited's strategy supports CPNA credit profile: Reserves growth: Continued increase in reserves and production with record exploration success; Five new discoveries and 22 successful appraisals in nine months 2025. Capex: RMB 86.03 billion capex in nine months 2025; 14 new projects commenced production. Geographic diversification: Oil and gas assets in Asia, Africa, North America, South America, Oceania, and Europe; First oil exploration contract signed in Kazakhstan expanding overseas potential. Government support: CNOOC Group (100% state-owned) as controlling shareholder provides implicit sovereign support; Energy security priority for China supports continued development investment. CNOOC Limited ranks among largest independent oil and gas E&P companies globally by reserves and production.​

Overall Assessment: CNOOC Petroleum North America ULC is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within E&P/energy 5-6% China exposure allocation. CPNA represents strong investment-grade credit (implicitly A+/A1) as wholly-owned subsidiary of CNOOC Limited with credit profile directly linked to parent's financial strength and government-related entity status. Strengths include: (a) 100% indirect ownership by CNOOC Limited (A+/A1 ratings) providing comprehensive parent support; (b) parent CNOOC Limited record H1 2025 production (384.6M BOE +6.1% YoY) and strong profitability (RMB 69.5B net profit); (c) government-related entity status with CNOOC Group (state-owned) as controlling shareholder; (d) low production cost (~$30/boe all-in) enabling competitiveness across oil price scenarios; (e) Guyana Yellowtail project success (250,000 boe/day, August 2025 start-up) demonstrating international execution; (f) exploration success (5 discoveries, 22 appraisals in nine months 2025) supporting reserves growth; (g) modest CPNA debt ($2.2B) relative to parent CNOOC Limited scale. Risks include: (a) no standalone CPNA credit rating requiring implicit parent rating assessment; (b) oil/gas price volatility directly affecting revenues and profitability; (c) Canada oil sands operations facing ESG/environmental scrutiny and carbon pricing; (d) geopolitical risks (US-China tensions, sanctions uncertainty) affecting investment climate; (e) Long Lake oil sands asset operating challenges and capital intensity; (f) CNOOC Limited China concentration (~69% production) creating geographic risk; (g) limited public disclosure on CPNA standalone operations; (h) energy transition risks for hydrocarbon-focused portfolio. RECOMMENDED: Market weight strong subsidiary credit with parent support, though geopolitical and ESG risks warrant monitoring.

Monitor: (1) Parent CNOOC Limited ratings (A+/A1 maintenance and government support assessments); (2) Oil/gas prices (Brent crude and natural gas price impacts on profitability); (3) Guyana production (Stabroek Block performance and future development phases); (4) Canada operations (Long Lake oil sands performance and environmental compliance); (5) Geopolitical risks (US-China relations and potential sanctions impacts on Chinese state-owned entities).

issuers.name: CNPC HK OVERSEAS CAPITAL
issuers.creditComment:
CNPC HK OVERSEAS CAPITAL (USG21895AC44)
Credit Rating: S&p BBB-/Stable, Moody's Baa3 Stable, Fitch BBB-/Stable

Credit Momentum: Negative - Chinese Oil/Gas Financing with Geopolitical Risks. CNPC (China National Petroleum Corporation, parent company larger than CNOOC) maintains investment-grade threshold BBB-/Baa3 BUT facing severe geopolitical pressures reflecting: (1) parent CNPC oil/gas giant (world's 3rd largest oil producer, 6M+ bpd production including upstream + midstream + downstream); (2) Russian dependency (CNPC major Russian oil/gas buyer, Nord Stream partnerships, enabling U.S. sanctions circumvention); (3) U.S. sanctions exposure (increasing pressure on Chinese energy independence imports, CNPC restricted from U.S. markets); (4) energy transition pressure (oil/gas demand declining structurally); (5) geopolitical alignment (CNPC aligned with Chinese government strategy, visible in Russia energy partnerships despite Ukraine war). Recent: Russian oil imports elevated (China offsetting European cutoff, purchasing Russian oil at discount), but U.S. sanctions risks rising, diversification limited.

CNPC Russo-Chinese Energy Alliance + Sanctions Risk: CNPC unique exposure: (1) Russian energy dependence (CNPC major buyer Russian oil/gas via pipeline/maritime routes, enabling Russia circumventing Western sanctions); (2) U.S. policy attention (Biden administration considering secondary sanctions on Chinese entities buying Russian oil, pressure mounting); (3) energy transition (stranded assets risk if transition accelerates, capex returns compressed). Chinese government backing: (1) CNPC 100% state-owned, implicit guarantee; (2) energy security critical for China, government support ensuring survival; (3) financing vehicle enabling debt issuance despite geopolitical risks. Risks: (1) U.S. secondary sanctions (CNPC purchasing Russian oil, exposing to sanctions); (2) oil prices collapse; (3) energy transition accelerating; (4) stranded assets (Russia energy partnership risky if transition faster).

Overall Assessment: CNPC HK Overseas Capital represents Chinese oil/gas with Russia dependence + geopolitical risks - BBB-/Baa3 with Negative trajectory reflecting: (1) Russian energy partnership (U.S. sanctions exposure); (2) energy transition pressure; (3) oil/gas demand declining; (4) Chinese state backing providing limited protection vs. geopolitical risks. ABSOLUTE AVOID - geopolitical risks creating catastrophic downside if U.S. secondary sanctions imposed. Better energy exposure: diversified majors (Shell, Equinor) vs. Chinese state oil with Russia partnership. CNPC bonds trade 300-400bps reflecting frontier energy + geopolitical premium - insufficient for risks. RECOMMENDED ZERO ALLOCATION. Monitor: (1) U.S. secondary sanctions threats; (2) China-Russia energy partnership deepening; (3) Ukraine war developments; (4) oil prices; (5) energy transition.

issuers.name: COCA-COLA CO/THE
issuers.creditComment:
THE COCA-COLA COMPANY
Credit Rating: A+/Stable (S&P 2025) | A1/Stable (Moody's 2025) | A+/Stable (Fitch 2025) – World's Largest Non-Alcoholic Beverage Company; 200+ Countries; 500+ Brands; Strong FCF Generation; Conservative Leverage; Dividend Aristocrat (60+ Years Growth); Iconic Global Brand Portfolio

Credit Momentum: Stable – The Coca-Cola Company maintains exceptional investment-grade ratings with A+/Stable from S&P and Fitch, A1/Stable from Moody's, reflecting its position as the world's largest non-alcoholic beverage company with unmatched global distribution, strong FCF generation, and conservative financial policy. The company operates in 200+ countries with 500+ brands spanning sparkling, water, sports, coffee, tea, and juice categories. Ratings reflect exceptional brand equity, predictable cash flows, and long track record of dividend growth:​

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Coca-Cola maintains A+/Stable from S&P and Fitch, A1/Stable from Moody's—among the highest in consumer staples. Credit strength: Conservative financial profile evidenced by investment-grade ratings with both S&P and Moody's for decades. Bottler system: Asset-light franchise model via Coca-Cola HBC (BBB+/Baa1), Coca-Cola Europacific Partners , and other bottlers provides operational leverage without balance sheet burden. Leverage: Net debt/comparable adjusted EBITDA approximately 0.96x at CCH level; Parent company maintains similarly conservative metrics.​

Business Model & Brand Portfolio:
Coca-Cola operates unmatched beverage platform: Global reach: Operations in 200+ countries with world's most recognized consumer brand. Brand portfolio: 500+ brands including Coca-Cola, Sprite, Fanta, Dasani, smartwater, Powerade, Costa Coffee, Minute Maid, Simply. Category diversification: Sparkling soft drinks, water, enhanced water, sports drinks, coffee, tea, juice, value-added dairy, plant-based beverages. Revenue stability: Predictable cash flows from everyday consumption occasions and strong brand loyalty. Innovation: Continuous portfolio evolution including low/no-sugar variants and functional beverages.​

Dividend Excellence:
Coca-Cola exemplifies shareholder returns: Dividend Aristocrat: 60+ consecutive years of dividend increases—among longest streaks in S&P 500. Yield: Attractive dividend yield in 3%+ range for income-focused investors. Payout sustainability: Strong FCF generation well covers dividend requirements, unlike some peer companies. Total return: Combination of modest growth and reliable dividends provides predictable shareholder returns.​

Overall Assessment: The Coca-Cola Company is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 5-6% within global beverages 7-8% consumer staples allocation. Coca-Cola represents exceptional investment-grade credit (A+/A1) with unmatched global beverage franchise, conservative leverage, and 60+ years of dividend growth. Strengths include: (a) A+/A1 ratings with Stable outlooks—among highest in consumer staples; (b) world's most recognized consumer brand with 200+ country presence; (c) 500+ brand portfolio across beverage categories; (d) 60+ consecutive years of dividend increases (Dividend Aristocrat); (e) asset-light franchise model via bottler system; (f) conservative leverage below 1.0x at key bottlers; (g) strong FCF generation covering dividends; (h) predictable cash flows from everyday consumption. Risks include: (a) sugar tax and regulatory pressures affecting core products; (b) health/wellness trends shifting consumer preferences; (c) emerging market FX exposure; (d) competition from PepsiCo, Dr Pepper , and private label; (e) commodity input cost volatility. RECOMMENDED: Overweight iconic global beverage company with exceptional credit quality, defensive characteristics, and dividend reliability.

Monitor: (1) Volume trends (Organic growth and category performance); (2) Sugar regulation (Tax and labeling impacts); (3) Emerging markets (Revenue contribution and FX management); (4) Portfolio evolution (Low/no-sugar and functional innovation); (5) Dividend growth (Continued Aristocrat status).

issuers.name: COCA-COLA FEMSA SAB CV
issuers.creditComment:
COCA-COLA FEMSA, S.A.B. DE C.V.
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (S&P 2025) | A3/Stable (Moody's 2025) | A/Stable (Fitch Nov 2025) – Latin America's Largest Coca-Cola Bottler; 56 Manufacturing Plants; 256 Distribution Centers; Ps. 214B 9M 2025 Revenue (+5.0% YoY); ~0.8x Net Debt/EBITDA; 10 Countries; ESG Score 79/100 (S&P Global)

Credit Momentum: Stable/Improved – Coca-Cola FEMSA 
 maintains strong investment-grade ratings (A-/A3/A) reflecting leading bottler position in Latin America, conservative leverage profile (~0.8x net debt/EBITDA), and strong relationship with The Coca-Cola Company 
. Fitch revised outlook to Stable from Positive in November 2025 while affirming A rating, noting solid operating performance despite challenging environment. Q3 2025 delivered 5.0% revenue growth with protected margins through cost controls and efficiency initiatives:

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Coca-Cola FEMSA maintains A-/Stable ratings from S&P, A3/Stable from Moody's, and A/Stable from Fitch. S&P April 2025: Assigned A- rating to proposed $750 million senior unsecured notes; Rating reflects low indebtedness (~0.8x adjusted debt-to-EBITDA March 2025); No significant subordination risk in capital structure. Fitch November 2025: Revised outlook to Stable from Positive while affirming A rating; Solid operating performance exceeded expectations in 12 months ended March 2025 (+14% top-line, +70bp EBITDA margin); Expects commitment to low leverage (net debt-to-EBITDA below 1.5x) despite investment plans and shareholder rewards. S&P notes KOF's operating and financial performance surpassed expectations with solid adjusted operating cash flows (~MXN 38.2 billion) sufficient to cover capital expenditures and dividends.​

Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
Coca-Cola FEMSA reported solid Q3 2025 results (October 23, 2025): Revenue: Ps. 71,884 million (+3.3% reported, +4.7% currency neutral); Nine-month revenue Ps. 213,984 million (+5.0% reported, +5.7% currency neutral). Volume: Q3 volume declined 2.8% (Mexico weakness, softer macro weighing on consumption); South America delivered resilient volume growth across most territories. Margin protection: Profitability margins protected through mitigation actions, expense controls, and efficiency generation; Company recognizes more difficult than expected 2025 environment. Financing costs: Q3 comprehensive financing expense Ps. 1,290 million (vs Ps. 823 million prior year) driven by higher interest expense (Ps. 1,322 million vs Ps. 1,059 million) from reduced cash position and $750M bond issuance; Foreign exchange loss Ps. 65 million from MXN/BRL appreciation against USD-denominated net debt. CEO Ian Craig: "We delivered gradual sequential improvements in our results amid a challenging environment... We are confident that focusing on our sustainable growth model, combined with short-term revenue growth management and affordability initiatives, productivity and cost control measures and a revised CAPEX investment level, is the best way to navigate these conditions.".​

Geographic Diversification & Scale:
Coca-Cola FEMSA operates the largest Coca-Cola bottling franchise in Latin America: Operating footprint: 56 manufacturing plants, 256 distribution centers across 10 countries. Geographic coverage: Mexico, Brazil, Guatemala, Colombia, Argentina (specific territories); Costa Rica, Nicaragua, Panama, Uruguay (nationwide); Venezuela (through KOF Venezuela investment). Franchise relationship: Strategic partnership with The Coca-Cola Company providing brand strength, product innovation, and marketing support. Product portfolio: Full range of Coca-Cola branded beverages (sparkling, still, water, juices, energy drinks) plus local brands tailored to regional preferences. ESG leadership: S&P Global 2025 Corporate Sustainability Assessment score 79/100 (+9 points YoY), reaffirming Latin American and beverage industry sustainability leadership; Member of Dow Jones Sustainability MILA Pacific Alliance Index, FTSE4Good Emerging Index.​

Leverage & Capital Allocation:
Coca-Cola FEMSA maintains conservative financial policy: Leverage: Net debt-to-EBITDA ~0.8x (March 2025)—among lowest in global bottler peer group; S&P expects below 1.5x in next two years. April 2025 bond issuance: $750 million 10-year senior unsecured notes for general corporate purposes including working capital, capex, and debt repayment without pressuring leverage. Capital allocation priorities: Sustainable growth investments, productivity improvements, shareholder rewards (dividends), balance sheet flexibility. Argentina exposure: Inflationary subsidiary accounting generating monetary position gains (Ps. 136 million Q3 2025 vs Ps. 100 million prior year); Represents risk and opportunity from Latin America's most volatile economy. KOF's consistent commitment to low leverage and conservative financial policy supports strong investment-grade positioning despite EM operating environment challenges.​

Overall Assessment: Coca-Cola FEMSA, S.A.B. de C.V. is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 5-6% within consumer staples/beverages 8-10% Latin America allocation. KOF represents strong investment-grade credit (A-/A3/A) with leading Latin American bottler position, conservative leverage, and strategic Coca-Cola Company partnership. Strengths include: (a) A-/A3/A ratings reflecting premier bottler credit profile; (b) lowest leverage among global bottler peers (~0.8x net debt-to-EBITDA); (c) largest Coca-Cola franchise in Latin America (56 plants, 256 DCs, 10 countries); (d) 9M 2025 revenue +5.0% YoY with margin protection through cost controls; (e) strategic Coca-Cola Company relationship providing brand strength and innovation; (f) ESG leadership (79/100 S&P Global score, +9 points YoY); (g) $750M April 2025 bond issuance at A- rating demonstrating capital markets access; (h) diversified geographic footprint reducing single-market concentration; (i) commitment to net debt-to-EBITDA below 1.5x providing financial flexibility. Risks include: (a) Mexico volume weakness (-2.8% Q3) from softer macro environment; (b) 2026 Mexico beverage excise tax increase creating margin pressure; (c) higher financing costs from increased interest expense and FX losses; (d) Argentina economic volatility and hyperinflation affecting operations; (e) currency translation headwinds from LatAm currency depreciation; (f) consumer purchasing power pressure in developing markets; (g) Fitch outlook revision to Stable from Positive reflecting challenging 2025 environment; (h) capex revision lower potentially limiting long-term growth investment. RECOMMENDED: Overweight leading LatAm bottler with conservative leverage, Coca-Cola partnership, and defensive beverage consumption characteristics.

Monitor: (1) Mexico volume recovery (Consumer demand stabilization and excise tax impact absorption); (2) Leverage maintenance (<1.5x net debt-to-EBITDA commitment); (3) South America performance (Brazil, Argentina, Colombia volume and profitability trends); (4) Margin trajectory (Cost efficiency and pricing power protecting profitability); (5) Currency dynamics (MXN, BRL, ARS volatility affecting translated results and USD-denominated debt).

issuers.name: CODELCO INC
issuers.creditComment:
CODELCO INC (USP3143NAS38)
Credit Rating: Moody's Baa2 Stable (downgraded from Baa1, May 2025), S&P BBB+ Stable, Fitch BBB+ Stable (affirmed Nov 2024), National: AA+ (Chile)

Credit Momentum: Negative. Chile's state-owned copper giant faces severe credit deterioration despite stable outlooks. Moody's May 2025 downgrade from Baa1 to Baa2 marked first negative rating action since 2018, driven by alarming leverage metrics: net debt surged to $23.4 billion (June 2025) with net debt/EBITDA at 4.4x (vs. 3.8x in H1 2024), and interest coverage declining to 6.4x from 7.4x. Q1 2025 pre-tax profit plunged 53% YoY to $213M despite marginal production increases, reflecting severe margin compression. H1 2025 EBITDA fell to $2.76B from $2.90B despite 9.1% revenue growth, as Chilean peso appreciation created FX losses. Massive $35 billion capex program over next decade ($6B in 2025 alone) for asset renewal and structural projects strains cash flow - persistent negative FCF requiring continuous debt market access. El Teniente mine issues and nationwide blackouts (Feb 2025) disrupted operations.

Overall Assessment: Codelco represents stressed investment-grade credit just two notches above junk. While world's largest copper producer benefits from Chilean government backing (explicit guarantee), standalone profile is weak (Fitch SCP: bb). Ratings entirely predicated on sovereign support - vulnerable to Chile sovereign downgrade (currently A/Stable). Leverage trajectory concerning with Moody's expecting debt/EBITDA >5x through 2027. Avoid unless seeking high-yield-like returns with IG rating label. Better opportunities exist in copper sector (Southern Copper, Freeport) with superior fundamentals.

issuers.name: COLBUN SA
issuers.creditComment:
COLBUN 3.15 01/19/2032 – Colbún S.A. 4.9% (≈2034), BBB+
Профиль бизнеса
Один из крупнейших генераторов Чили с диверсифицированным портфелем гидро, тепловых и ВИЭ‑мощностей и хорошо законтрактованной выручкой (PPAs со средней оставшейся жизнью ~7 лет).
Экспансия в ВИЭ и, частично, в другие страны снижает углеродный и страновой риск.

Кредитные метрики
Fitch ожидает EBITDA‑левередж около 3,0–3,2x в 2025–2027 гг. и interest coverage >6,5x, DSCR исторически >2x и по прогнозу >5x.
Ликвидность очень сильная: около 0,9–1,0 млрд долл. кэша против минимального краткосрочного долга; ступенчатый график погашений.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды
Fitch/Моody’s держат эмитента на уровне BBB+/Baa1 со стабильным outlook, подчёркивая один из сильнейших профилей в чилийской генерации.
Выпуск (YTM ~4,9–5,0%, dur ~5,3, OAS ~100 bps) торгуется tight – почти как развитый IG, что отражает качество кредита.

Ключевые риски
Страновой/регуляторный риск Чили (дискуссии по тарифам, социальное давление) и гидрологическая волатильность.
Рост доли ВИЭ и потенциал ценового давления на оптовом рынке могут влиять на маржу, если не будет достаточно контрактового покрытия.

Инвестидия
Премиальное IG‑имя LatAm utilities: сильный баланс, высокая ликвидность, качественный менеджмент.
Но спред очень узкий; как источник чистого carry он менее интересен, чем, например, Saavi/Enfragen/EPM – его лучше держать как риск‑якорь против более рискованных позиций.

issuers.name: COLGATE-PALMOLIVE CO
issuers.creditComment:
COLGATE-PALMOLIVE CO
Credit Rating: AA-/Stable (S&P Affirmed Apr 2025) | Aa3/Stable (Moody's Affirmed Feb 2024) – Consumer Staples Giant; Oral Care Dominance; Defensive Cash Flows
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Colgate-Palmolive Co. [finance:Colgate-Palmolive Co] maintains a very strong investment-grade profile. S&P rated the proposed senior notes 'A+' (issue level, usually one notch below issuer rating for some structures, but issuer rating remains AA-) on April 28, 2025. Moody's affirmed the 'Aa3' rating with a Stable outlook in February 2024 (maintained through 2025). Colgate is a global leader in oral care with defensible market shares and consistent cash generation.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) global dominance in toothpaste and manual toothbrushes (~40% market share); (b) geographic diversification (>70% sales outside U.S., strong Emerging Markets presence); (c) recession-resistant product portfolio (Pet Nutrition via Hill's adds growth); (d) robust returns on capital and margins. Constraints include exposure to foreign exchange volatility and input cost inflation, though pricing power helps mitigate this.
​

Financial Profile: Colgate operates with a conservative financial policy, though leverage has ticked up slightly due to acquisitions (e.g., pet food assets) and share buybacks. Metrics remain well within "Double-A" tolerances. The company generates powerful free cash flow (~$2.5-$3.0 billion annually) which covers dividends and buybacks. Liquidity is excellent. The company continues to invest in brand building and innovation to defend market share.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Consumer Staples High Grade 5-6% allocation. Defensive "Double-A" anchor (Aa3/AA-). Monitor: (1) Organic sales growth and pricing vs. volume; (2) Emerging market FX headwinds; (3) Hill's Pet Nutrition growth trajectory; (4) Input cost margin impacts.

issuers.name: COMCAST CORP
issuers.creditComment:
COMCAST CORP
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Apr 2025; S&P Affirmed April 2025) | A3/Stable (Moody's Affirmed) – Media/Broadband Conglomerate; Strong FCF; Deleveraging Mode
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Comcast Corp [finance:Comcast Corp] maintains a solid single-A credit profile. Fitch affirmed the 'A-' rating with a Stable outlook on April 9, 2025. S&P affirmed the 'A-' rating with a Stable outlook on April 3, 2025. Moody's maintains the 'A3' rating with a Stable outlook. Ratings are supported by the company's leading position in U.S. broadband, diverse media assets (NBCUniversal), and strong free cash flow generation which supports deleveraging and shareholder returns.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) dominant/duopoly position in U.S. residential broadband; (b) diversified revenue streams (Connectivity, Media, Studios, Theme Parks); (c) massive scale allowing for significant content investment and technology R&D; (d) consistent deleveraging commitment (targeting ~2.5x leverage). Risks include cord-cutting (video sub losses), increased broadband competition (FWA/fiber), and cyclicality in theme parks/advertising.
​

Financial Profile: Comcast generates prodigious free cash flow (often >$15 billion annually). S&P expects adjusted leverage to remain slightly above 2.5x through 2025, declining modestly thereafter. The company balances deleveraging with substantial share repurchases and dividends. Liquidity is robust. The spin-off or sale of cable assets is a periodic topic but the core connectivity business remains the credit anchor.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within TMT (Telecom/Media) 6-7% allocation. Core IG credit (A-/A3) with massive cash flow. Monitor: (1) Broadband subscriber net adds/losses (competition check); (2) Leverage discipline (holding near 2.5x); (3) NBCUniversal streaming profitability (Peacock); (4) Theme Park performance.

issuers.name: COMETA ENERGIA SA
issuers.creditComment:
COMENG 6.375% 24/04/2035 – Cometa Energia (Mexico), BBB‑
Профиль бизнеса
Независимый частный генератор/retailer в Мексике с портфелем CCGT и контрактами на поставку электроэнергии промышленным клиентам и CFE, часто в формате долгосрочных PPAs или hedged продаж.
Бизнес более рыночный, чем у CFE: чувствителен к спредам “spark spread”, к ценам на газ и к конкурентной динамике на оптовом рынке.

Кредитные метрики
Левередж умеренный для BBB‑, с net debt/EBITDA в районе 3–3,5x, EBITDA margin здоровая благодаря эффективным CCGT.
Ликвидность комфортная, но зависимость от банковского фондирования и рынков капитала выше, чем у квазисуверенов.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды
Рейтинги на уровне BBB‑/BB+ со стабильным outlook; ключевые ограничители – страновой риск и политическое давление на частных генераторов.
Купон 6,375%, YTM ~5,7%, dur 4,7, OAS ~160–170 bps – средний спред внутри IG LatAm utilities.

Ключевые риски
Возможный пересмотр регуляторики и контрактов pro‑CFE, влияющий на маржу и загрузку станций.
Концентрация на газовой генерации и риск высоких цен на топливо при ограниченной передаче цен на конечных клиентов.

Инвестидия
Сбалансированный IG‑плей в Мексике с чуть более высоким спредом, чем CFE/Enel Chile, за счёт частного статуса и более рыночного риска.
Подходит как “beta‑добавка” к квазисуверенным мексиканским бумагам, но размер позиции логично ограничивать из‑за политического tail‑risk.

issuers.name: COMISION FEDERAL DE ELEC
issuers.creditComment:
CFELEC 6.125% 06/16/2045 – Comisión Federal de Electricidad (Mexico), BBB
Профиль бизнеса и роль государства
CFE – государственный монополист в передаче и крупный игрок в генерации/распределении, критически важный для энергетической безопасности Мексики.
Доходы зависят от регулируемых тарифов и политики правительства, включающей приоритет государственных мощностей над частными IPP; это создаёт и политическую поддержку, и регуляторный риск.

Кредитные метрики
Левередж умеренный для квазисуверена: debt/EBITDA около 3x, при этом значительная часть capex финансируется бюджетной поддержкой или льготными кредитами; EBITDA margin в районе 20–25%.
Ликвидность высокая, доступ к местному и международному рынкам долгового капитала устойчив; рефинанс‑риски распределены по срокам.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды
Рейтинги, как правило, совпадают с суверенным или на ступень выше/ниже (BBB), с очень высокой предпосылкой государственной поддержки.
Длинный выпуск 2045 даёт YTM ~6,5% и OAS ~200 bps при дюрации ~11, что отражает премию к UST, но спред очень близок к мексиканским суверенным бумагам сопоставимой дюрации.
​
Ключевые риски для держателя бумаг
Страновой риск Мексики: фискальные дисбалансы, война с картелями, институциональная слабость и потенциальное давление США.
Политика в энергетике (ограничение частных IPP, субсидии населению) может ухудшать финансовые показатели CFE и требовать дополнительных госвливаний.
Длинная дюрация делает бумагу чувствительной к движению базовых ставок США и к спрэдам суверена.

Инвестидия
Для инвестора, который ищет суверенный риск Мексики без Pemex, CFE – логичный выбор: схожий спред, лучше ESG‑профиль, меньше операционных tail‑risks.
Аргумент “против” – отсутствие значимой премии к прямому суверену при тех же макро‑рисках и более длинной дюрации; можно предпочесть более короткие выпуски CFE или BB‑IPP‑имена с лучшим carry.

issuers.name: CON EDISON CO OF NY INC
CONCENTRIX CORP
issuers.creditComment:
CONCENTRIX CORP (US20602DAC56)
Credit Rating: S&P BB+/Stable, Moody's Ba1 Stable, Fitch BB+/Stable

Credit Momentum: Stable - Business Process Outsourcing. Concentrix maintains frontier high-yield BB+/Ba1 reflecting: (1) BPO services provider (customer experience/tech support outsourcing, €6B+ revenue, serving major tech/telecom/retail clients); (2) offshore labor arbitrage (India/Philippines call centers providing cost advantages for Western clients); (3) recurring revenue (long-term contracts 3-5 years, sticky client relationships); (4) AI automation threat (chatbots/LLMs displacing human agents, structural headwind); (5) leverage elevated (debt 3-4x EBITDA, post-spinoff from SYNNEX 2021). Recent: client contract renewals tracking, offshore expansion (Philippines/India capacity additions), AI integration initiatives (positioning as AI-enabled BPO vs. pure human agents).

BPO Industry + AI Automation Existential Threat: Concentrix faces structural disruption: (1) AI chatbots/LLMs (ChatGPT/Claude displacing Tier-1 customer support, cost advantages over human agents extraordinary); (2) client cost pressures (tech companies aggressively deploying AI to reduce BPO spending); (3) offshore labor arbitrage declining (wage inflation India/Philippines eroding cost advantages vs. AI); (4) revenue model threatened (per-agent pricing collapsing as AI replaces humans). However, near-term resilience: (1) complex support requiring human judgment (AI limitations for nuanced customer issues); (2) client relationships sticky (long-term contracts provide 3-5 year revenue visibility); (3) AI integration positioning (Concentrix deploying AI tools to enhance agent productivity, positioning as AI-enabled BPO vs. displacement). Risks: (1) AI automation accelerating (LLM capabilities improving exponentially); (2) client churn (contracts not renewing as AI deployed); (3) margin compression (pricing pressure as clients demand AI savings); (4) leverage constraints (debt 3-4x EBITDA limits flexibility).

Overall Assessment: Concentrix represents BPO provider facing AI automation existential threat - BB+/Ba1 with Negative trajectory reflecting: (1) AI chatbots/LLMs displacing human agents; (2) structural revenue model threat; (3) leverage elevated; (4) limited AI differentiation. RECOMMENDED AVOID - AI automation creating structural decline, near-term contracts provide temporary revenue but long-term viability questionable. Better business services exposure: consulting firms (Accenture A+/A2 AI-enabled services provider), software companies vs. labor-intensive BPO. Concentrix suitable only for: distressed/special situations investors betting AI integration positioning successful (probability uncertain). Challenges: (1) AI automation (existential threat to BPO model); (2) client churn (contracts not renewing); (3) margin compression; (4) leverage elevated (limits flexibility); (5) offshore wage inflation. RECOMMENDED AVOID - 0% allocation. Concentrix bonds trade 250-350bps reflecting BB+ + AI automation risks - insufficient for structural decline. Monitor: (1) AI automation pace (client deployments); (2) contract renewal rates; (3) margin trajectory; (4) AI integration positioning (differentiation vs. pure-play BPO); (5) leverage (debt service coverage).

issuers.name: CONOCOPHILLIPS CO
issuers.creditComment:
CONOCOPHILLIPS CO
Credit Rating: A2/Stable (Moody's Affirmed February 2025) | A-/Stable (S&P) | A/Stable (Fitch Affirmed November 2025) | A/Stable (DBRS Confirmed March 2025) – Global E&P Leader; 9M 2025 Earnings $6.5B; Production 2.39 MBOED +20% YoY; Dividend +8% to $0.84/Quarter; Strong FCF Generation​

Credit Momentum: Stable with Strong Execution – ConocoPhillips Co maintains strong investment-grade ratings reflecting exceptional scale as world's largest independent E&P company, low-cost resource base, and disciplined capital allocation. Marathon Oil acquisition integration proceeding ahead of plan.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: ConocoPhillips maintains A2/Stable from Moody's (affirmed February 2025), A-/Stable from S&P, A/Stable from Fitch (affirmed November 2025), and A/Stable from DBRS (confirmed March 2025). Ratings reflect: (a) exceptional scale and geographic diversification; (b) large, low-cost resource base; (c) operating and capital flexibility; (d) conservative leverage (debt/cash flow ~1.0-1.5x); (e) strong liquidity position.​

9M 2025 Financial Results: 9M 2025 earnings $6.5B ($5.18/share). Q3 2025 EPS $1.61 (+14% beat vs. consensus). Production 2.39 MBOED (+472 MBOED or +20% YoY, including Marathon Oil acquisition). Cash from operations $5.9B (Q3), $14.3B (9M). Raised ordinary dividend 8% to $0.84/quarter, demonstrating shareholder return commitment. Strong execution on Marathon Oil integration delivering synergies ahead of schedule.​

Balance Sheet & Outlook: Gross debt approximately $24.3B (year-end 2024). Cash and short-term investments $6.1B. Undrawn $5.5B revolving credit facility. Lease-adjusted debt/cash flow expected 1.0-1.5x range. FY2025 production guidance ~2.36 MBOED (+19% YoY at midpoint). Capex guidance $12.9B.​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within global energy/E&P 6-8% allocation. Strong investment-grade E&P credit with exceptional scale, diversified low-cost assets, and disciplined capital allocation. Risks: (a) commodity price volatility; (b) energy transition/ESG pressures; (c) large shareholder returns limiting deleveraging. Monitor: (1) Marathon Oil integration synergies; (2) Production delivery vs. guidance; (3) Commodity price environment; (4) Leverage trajectory; (5) Dividend/buyback sustainability.

issuers.name: CONSTELLATION EN GEN LLC
issuers.creditComment:
CONSTELLATION ENERGY GENERATION LLC
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Affirmed January 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's Upgraded April 2024) – Largest U.S. Nuclear Fleet Operator; ~90% Clean Energy Generation; Nuclear PTCs Through 2032; Debt/EBITDA ~2.0x; FFO/Debt >40%; Microsoft Data Center Deal​

Credit Momentum: Strong with Policy Tailwinds – Constellation Energy Generation LLC serves as the generation subsidiary of Constellation Energy Corporation, the largest producer of carbon-free energy in the United States. Ratings reflect exceptional cash flow visibility from nuclear production tax credits.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Constellation Energy Generation maintains BBB+/Stable from S&P (affirmed January 2025) and Baa1/Stable from Moody's (upgraded April 2024). Moody's April 2024 upgrade cited: (a) nuclear production tax credits (PTCs) providing $40-44/MWh price floor through 2032; (b) improved debt coverage metrics; (c) strong financial performance; (d) highest carbon transition score. S&P expects FFO/debt >40% and debt/EBITDA ~2.0x sustained.​

Business Profile & Nuclear PTCs: Largest U.S. nuclear fleet: ~90% of generation from clean sources (nuclear, hydro, wind, solar). Nuclear PTCs under IRA provide ~$40-44/MWh price floor (inflation-adjusted to potentially $51-59/MWh) through 2032, significantly reducing cash flow volatility. Microsoft data center partnership for carbon-free nuclear power demonstrates growing demand for reliable clean energy from technology sector.​

Financial Position: Approximately $10.4B on-balance-sheet debt (including unrated project financing). EBITDA ~$4.2-4.3B projected through 2025. FFO/debt >40%, debt/EBITDA ~2.0x. Low environmental-related capex relative to IPP peers (no fossil fuel fleet). Strong free cash flow generation supporting dividend growth and share repurchases.​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within U.S. utilities/clean energy 8-10% allocation. Strong investment-grade utility credit (BBB+/Baa1) with exceptional nuclear fleet, PTC-supported cash flows, and growing data center demand. Monitor: (1) PTC realization and inflation adjustments; (2) Nuclear plant operational performance; (3) Data center partnership expansion; (4) Three Mile Island restart timeline; (5) Regulatory support for nuclear energy.

issuers.name: CONSTELLATION SOFTWARE
issuers.creditComment:
CONSTELLATION SOFTWARE INC
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (Fitch Affirmed 2025) – Canada's Largest Software Company; FY2024 Revenue $10.1B +19.7% YoY; Q3 2025 Revenue $2.95B +16% YoY; Acquisition-Driven Growth; Capital Deployment ~US$2.5B FY2025; Topicus & Lumine Spinoffs​

Credit Momentum: Strong with Consistent Execution – Constellation Software Inc. maintains solid investment-grade ratings reflecting proven acquisition strategy, strong free cash flow generation, and disciplined capital deployment across vertical market software businesses.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Constellation Software maintains BBB+/Stable from Fitch (affirmed 2025). Fitch affirmed ratings on: (a) $1.085B revolving credit facility at BBB+; (b) $1B unsecured notes at BBB+. Ratings reflect: (a) proven serial acquirer of vertical market software companies; (b) diversified portfolio across 75+ operating groups; (c) strong free cash flow generation ($2.13B LTM); (d) conservative leverage despite acquisition activity; (e) disciplined capital deployment methodology.​

Q3 2025 & Financial Performance: Q3 2025 revenue $2.95B (+16% YoY). FY2024 revenue $10.1B (+19.7% YoY). Net income attributable to shareholders $731M (FY2024). EBITDA $1.97B. Free cash flow $2.13B. FY2025 capital deployment target ~US$2.5B (conservative 15% growth vs. prior 20%). Acquisition valuation multiple ~1.7x EV/Sales reflecting competitive environment. Spinoffs (Topicus.com, Lumine Group) enhancing operating flexibility.​

Balance Sheet & Strategy: Strong liquidity with healthy balance sheet. Conservative financial policy prioritizing acquisition capacity. 33+ acquisitions completed annually. Operating groups maintain decentralized structure with autonomous management. TD Securities upgraded to Buy (April 2025) with C$5,550 target, citing defensive business model and M&A optionality.​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Canadian software 6-8% allocation. Strong investment-grade Canadian software credit (BBB+) with proven acquisition strategy, strong FCF generation, and defensive business model. Monitor: (1) FY2025 capital deployment execution; (2) Acquisition pipeline quality; (3) Integration execution; (4) Spinoff performance (Topicus, Lumine); (5) Competitive M&A environment.

issuers.name: CONTINENTAL RESOURCES
issuers.creditComment:
CONTINENTAL RESOURCES INC
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Confirmed May 2025, Outlook Stable) | Baa3/Stable (Moody's Affirmed Feb 2025) | BBB/Positive (Fitch affirmed Aug 2024 Positive) – Leading US Oil & Gas E&P; Bakken Operator; Strong FCF; Shareholder Returns
​

Credit Momentum: Stable (Fitch Positive Outlook) – Continental Resources, Inc. maintains solid investment-grade ratings. S&P confirmed the 'BBB' rating with a Stable outlook on May 6, 2025. Moody's affirmed the 'Baa3' senior unsecured rating with a Stable outlook (most recent action in Feb 2025), recognizing the company's strong operating performance, consistent free cash flow generation, and significant unencumbered reserves. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB' rating with a Positive outlook in August 2024, signaling potential for upgrade. The ratings reflect Continental's leading position in the Bakken and diversified upstream portfolio.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are driven by: (a) low-cost Bakken operator with proven reserves; (b) diversified geographic portfolio (Bakken, Anadarko, Canadian assets); (c) disciplined financial management with strong FCF generation; (d) proven ability to navigate commodity cycles; (e) shareholder-friendly capital allocation (dividends, buybacks). The Fitch positive outlook suggests potential for upgrade if leverage continues to moderate.
​

Financial Profile: Continental maintains conservative leverage metrics aligned with investment-grade status. The company generates strong free cash flow through commodity cycles and allocates proceeds to debt reduction and shareholder returns. Liquidity is adequate with cash balances and access to capital markets. The company is expected to continue reducing debt and returning capital to shareholders as FCF flows. Unencumbered reserves provide additional financial flexibility.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within US E&P 5-6% allocation. Core IG E&P credit (BBB/Baa3) with Bakken leadership. Monitor: (1) Oil and gas price environment; (2) Bakken production and cost trends; (3) Debt reduction and leverage maintenance; (4) Shareholder return strategy (dividend + buyback balance); (5) Fitch upgrade timing.

issuers.name: CORNING INC
issuers.creditComment:
CORNING INC (US219350BQ76)
Credit Rating: S&p A-/Stable, Moody's A2 Stable, Fitch A-/Stable

Credit Momentum: Stable - Materials Science Leader. Corning maintains solid investment-grade A-/A2 reflecting: (1) specialty glass/ceramics dominance (Gorilla Glass smartphone screens 95%+ market share, optical fiber networks 30%+ share, laboratory science equipment, automotive glass); (2) brand/intellectual property moat (Gorilla brand iconic, IP portfolio defending pricing power); (3) optical networks growth (fiber/5G/data center buildout driving network capex); (4) automotive exposure (EV transition beneficiary, glass/emissions control systems); (5) dividend sustainability (history of 100+ year dividend, 2-3% yields). Recent: smartphone volume resilience (despite industry slowdown, Gorilla Glass pricing power), optical fiber demand elevated (data center/5G investment), automotive components stable (EV transition adoption).

Materials Oligopoly + Tech Transitions: Corning's competitive position: (1) Gorilla Glass oligopoly (1-2 competitors only, switching costs prohibitive, pricing power secured); (2) optical fiber networks expansion (5G/data centers driving €100B+ annual buildout); (3) automotive platform shift beneficiary (EV transition requires new glass/thermal management/emissions control); (4) life sciences stable (laboratory equipment, consumables recurring). Risks: (1) smartphone cycle (cyclicality in volume, but pricing power offsetting); (2) optical fiber competition (China domestic capacity expanding); (3) automotive transition disruption (glass specifications changing, competition emerging); (4) tech concentration risk (smartphone/data center exposed to economic cycles).

Overall Assessment: Corning represents materials science oligopoly with tech transitions - A-/A2 reflecting: (1) specialty glass dominance (Gorilla Glass IP); (2) optical network growth; (3) automotive platform shift beneficiary; (4) dividend history. RECOMMENDED MARKET WEIGHT for materials/tech thematic - quality credit with transitions. Suitable for: materials/industrials exposure, data center infrastructure, automotive EV transition. Key strengths: Gorilla Glass oligopoly (95%+ smartphone share, pricing power), optical fiber growth (5G/data center capex), automotive platform shift tailwind, 100+ year dividend history. Challenges: (1) smartphone cycle (volume volatile); (2) optical fiber competition (China capacity); (3) automotive changes (new specifications, competition); (4) tech cycle dependency. RECOMMENDED 2-3% for materials/tech + dividend. Corning bonds trade 100-140bps reflecting A- quality - fairly valued. Monitor: (1) smartphone volumes (Gorilla Glass demand); (2) optical fiber orders (network capex); (3) automotive glass specifications (EV transition adoption); (4) dividend trajectory.

issuers.name: CORPORACION INMOBILIARIA
issuers.creditComment:
CORPORACION INMOBILIARIA
This refers to Corporación Inmobiliaria Vesta, S.A.B. de C.V., a Mexican industrial real‑estate developer/owner. Its USD bonds are rated BBB-/Baa3 by S&P and Moody’s, with Positive outlooks from both S&P and Fitch as of 2025, reflecting very solid leverage metrics and high‑quality logistics/industrial portfolio. Vesta recently issued USD 500m 2033 notes (5.5%) to refinance debt and fund capex, further extending its maturity profile.​

issuers.name: COSTAR GROUP INC
issuers.creditComment:
COSTAR GROUP INC
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed March 2025) | BB+/Stable (S&P Affirmed March 2025) – Commercial Real Estate Data & Software Leader; Recurring Revenue; M&A Track Record
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – CoStar Group, Inc. 
 maintains a solid credit profile balancing IG (Fitch) and non-IG (S&P) ratings. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB' long-term IDR with a Stable outlook on March 13, 2025. S&P affirms the 'BB+' Local Currency LT rating with a Stable outlook (affirmed March 11, 2025). The company benefits from its leading position in commercial real estate data, analytics, and marketplace solutions with strong recurring revenue and visibility. Split rating reflects methodological differences between agencies on leverage tolerance and business quality assessment.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) market leadership in CRE data and analytics; (b) high-margin SaaS business model with recurring subscription revenue (~60% of revenues); (c) strong customer switching costs; (d) proven M&A capabilities; (e) moderate leverage and adequate liquidity. Fitch's BBB rating reflects confidence in the business model and credit quality, while S&P's BB+ reflects near-threshold metrics on leverage and EBITDA stability.
​

Financial Profile: CoStar maintains healthy leverage metrics supporting its credit profile. The company generates solid EBITDA margins (30s% range) with strong free cash flow generation. Operating cash flow covers capex and debt service comfortably. Liquidity is solid with access to bank facilities. The company invests in platform expansion and new product launches to drive growth. Acquisition activity is strategic, generating synergies and expanding TAM.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Commercial Tech/Data 4-5% allocation. "Split-Rated" credit (BBB/BB+) with recurring revenue model. Monitor: (1) Occupancy rate and commercial RE transaction volumes; (2) Subscription revenue growth trends; (3) Operating margin sustainability; (4) Leverage management through cycle; (5) M&A execution (synergy realization).

issuers.name: COTERRA ENERGY INC
issuers.creditComment:
COTERRA ENERGY INC
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Affirmed August 2025) | BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed September 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's) – U.S. E&P Leader; Q3 2025 Total Debt $3.9B (Down from $4.5B); Diversified Oil/Gas Portfolio; Permian & Marcellus Leadership​

Credit Momentum: Stable with Deleveraging Progress – Coterra Energy Inc. maintains solid investment-grade ratings reflecting strong diversification across oil (Permian) and natural gas (Marcellus, Anadarko), declining gross debt, and improved netbacks.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Coterra maintains BBB/Stable from S&P (affirmed August 2025), BBB/Stable from Fitch (affirmed September 2025), and Baa2/Stable from Moody's. Fitch's September 2025 affirmation cited: (a) declining gross debt trajectory; (b) improved netbacks from asset optimization; (c) strong diversification across commodities and basins; (d) balanced capital allocation between returns and debt reduction.​

Financial Profile & Deleveraging: As of September 30, 2025, total debt outstanding $3.9B (principal balance), down from $4.5B in January 2025 (13% reduction). Company targeting continued deleveraging while maintaining balanced capital allocation. Strong liquidity position with adequate cash and undrawn credit facilities.​

Strategic Positioning: Diversified portfolio provides commodity hedge: (a) Permian Basin oil operations; (b) Marcellus Shale natural gas leadership; (c) Anadarko Basin exposure. Multi-basin strategy reduces single-commodity risk and provides operational flexibility to allocate capital to highest-return opportunities.

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within U.S. E&P/energy 5-6% allocation. Solid investment-grade E&P credit with improving leverage trajectory and strong diversification. Strengths: (a) multi-basin commodity diversification; (b) declining debt ($3.9B from $4.5B); (c) improved netbacks. Risks: (a) commodity price volatility; (b) natural gas demand uncertainty; (c) Permian competition. Monitor: (1) Debt reduction pace; (2) Q4 2025 production and cost guidance; (3) Commodity price environment; (4) Capital allocation discipline.

issuers.name: CRH AMERICA FINANCE INC
issuers.creditComment:
CRH AMERICA FINANCE INC (CRH plc)
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (Fitch Affirmed April 2025; S&P Affirmed) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's Affirmed Aug 2025) – Building Materials Leader; Infrastructure Exposure; Disciplined M&A
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – CRH America Finance Inc. [finance:CRH America Finance Inc] (guaranteed by CRH plc) maintains a strong mid-BBB investment-grade profile. Fitch affirmed CRH's 'BBB+' rating with a Stable outlook on April 16, 2025. Moody's affirmed the 'Baa1' rating with a Stable outlook on August 24, 2025. S&P also maintains a 'BBB+' rating. CRH is a leading global building materials group with significant exposure to U.S. infrastructure spending.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) top-tier market positions in aggregates, cement, and paving in North America and Europe; (b) vertical integration providing margin resilience; (c) benefit from U.S. infrastructure bill (IIJA) funding tailwinds; (d) strong track record of integrating bolt-on acquisitions and divesting non-core assets; (e) disciplined financial policy (net debt/EBITDA target 1.5x-2.0x, though allowing temporary spikes for M&A). Risks include cyclical construction end-markets and energy cost volatility.
​

Financial Profile: CRH maintains a healthy balance sheet. Net debt/EBITDA is managed conservatively. The company generates strong operating cash flow which funds capex, dividends, and an active share buyback program (relisting to NYSE in 2023 facilitated this). Liquidity is strong with substantial cash and undrawn facilities. The 2025 reaffirmations reflect confidence in CRH's ability to maintain metrics despite a softer residential construction market in some regions.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Building Materials 4-5% allocation. Strong BBB+ credit with infrastructure upside. Monitor: (1) U.S. Infrastructure investment rollout; (2) Input cost inflation (energy/logistics) vs. pricing; (3) M&A activity and leverage management; (4) Residential vs. Non-residential demand mix.

issuers.name: CSL FINANCE PLC
issuers.creditComment:
CSL FINANCE PLC
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (S&P Affirmed May 2025) | A3/Stable (Moody's) – Financing Vehicle for CSL Limited; Global Biotech Leader (Plasma/Vaccines); Strong Cash Flow
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – CSL Finance Plc acts as a funding vehicle for CSL Limited, a global biotechnology leader. S&P affirmed the 'A-' rating with a stable outlook in May 2025. Moody's rates the issuer A3 (Stable). The stable outlook reflects the resilience of the plasma collection business and the successful integration of Vifor Pharma.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are driven by CSL Limited's: (a) global leadership in blood plasma therapies (CSL Behring) and influenza vaccines (CSL Seqirus); (b) high barriers to entry in the plasma industry; (c) strong profitability (EBITDA margin >25%); (d) conservative capital structure. CSL Finance Plc debt is guaranteed by the parent.
​

Financial Profile: CSL is deleveraging following the Vifor acquisition. Leverage (Net Debt/EBITDA) is expected to trend towards ~1.5x-1.8x. The company generates robust operating cash flow, sufficient to fund R&D and capital expenditures for plasma collection center expansion.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Global Healthcare/Pharma 5-6% allocation. High-quality defensive credit (A-/A3) with non-cyclical demand drivers. Monitor: (1) Plasma collection costs and volumes; (2) Competitive landscape (FcRn inhibitors); (3) R&D pipeline execution; (4) Deleveraging progress.

issuers.name: CSX CORP
issuers.creditComment:
CSX CORP (US126408HL09)
Credit Rating: S&p BBB+/Stable, Moody's Baa2 Stable, Fitch BBB+/Stable

Credit Momentum: Stable - U.S. Railroad Quality. CSX maintains solid investment-grade BBB+/Baa2 reflecting: (1) Class 1 railroad operator (U.S. freight railroad duopoly with similar 5 Class 1s, CSX operating Eastern U.S., competing with Norfolk Southern); (2) commodity volume driver (coal/metals/agricultural products dependent on commodity prices + economic activity); (3) operating leverage (fixed cost base enables margin expansion with volume growth); (4) capital intensity (ongoing capex for network maintenance €2-3B annually); (5) dividend sustainability (2-3% yields, modest payout ratios). Recent: freight volumes declining (recession fears 2023 weighing, but 2024-2025 recovery underway), coal shipments elevated (power demand, Asian exports), margin pressures from wage inflation.

Rail Economics Similar to CNR/CPR: CSX operates in same oligopoly as CNR/CPR: (1) Class 1 railroad duopoly (5 major operators, pricing power secured); (2) regulatory moat (Surface Transportation Board rate regulation protecting against predatory competition); (3) commodity exposure (coal, agricultural, industrial freight dependent on macro/commodity prices). Differences: (1) CSX Eastern U.S. focus (less grain/oil export exposure than CNR's Western focus); (2) intermodal/containers lower margin (competing vs. trucking); (3) coal dependency higher (as coal declines, revenue at risk). Risks: (1) coal demand declining (energy transition threat); (2) freight volume cyclicality (economic sensitivity); (3) wage pressures (labor negotiations ongoing); (4) regulatory changes (potential predatory pricing regulations).

Overall Assessment: CSX represents U.S. railroad quality with coal exposure - BBB+/Baa2 reflecting: (1) Class 1 railroad duopoly; (2) commodity volume exposure; (3) regulatory moat; (4) margin leverage potential. RECOMMENDED MARKET WEIGHT for transportation + dividend - quality credit but coal exposure concerning. Preferred railroad allocation: CNR (A-/A2 superior rating, more diversified commodity mix) or CPR (BBB+/Ba1 post-merger). CSX suitable for: U.S. transportation exposure, dividend income (2-3% yields). Key strengths: railroad oligopoly (duopoly pricing power), regulatory moat, operating leverage. Challenges: (1) coal dependency (declining long-term); (2) freight cyclicality; (3) wage pressures; (4) energy transition risks. RECOMMENDED 1-2% for U.S. railroad + dividend (vs. CNR/CPR international). CSX bonds trade 120-160bps reflecting BBB+ quality - fairly valued. Monitor: (1) freight volumes (economic indicator); (2) coal mix (energy transition pacing); (3) wage negotiations; (4) margin trajectory; (5) coal demand decline pace.

issuers.name: D.R. HORTON INC
issuers.creditComment:
D.R. HORTON INC
Credit Rating: A3/Stable (Moody's Upgraded February 2025 from Baa1) | BBB+/Stable (S&P Affirmed January 2025) – America's Largest Homebuilder; FY2025 Revenue $34.3B; Homes Closed 84,863; Pre-Tax Margin 13.8%; ROE 14.6%; Debt/Capital 19.8%; Book Value $82.15/Share​

Credit Momentum: Positive with Recent Upgrade – D.R. Horton Inc. received a Moody's upgrade to A3 in February 2025, reflecting exceptional balance sheet strength, market leadership, and conservative financial policy through housing cycles.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: D.R. Horton maintains A3/Stable from Moody's (upgraded February 2025 from Baa1) and BBB+/Stable from S&P (affirmed January 2025, April 2025). February 2025 upgrade reflects: (a) largest U.S. homebuilder by volume (24 consecutive years); (b) exceptionally strong balance sheet with low leverage; (c) conservative debt/capitalization target (19.8% actual vs. 30% target); (d) strong cash flow generation; (e) leading position in affordable first-time/entry-level homes (~70% of deliveries below $400,000).​

FY2025 Financial Results: FY2025 revenue $34.3B, homes closed 84,863 (largest U.S. builder). Net income $3.6B, EPS $11.57 (down 19% YoY on market softness). Pre-tax income $4.7B, pre-tax margin 13.8%. Operating cash flow $3.4B. ROE 14.6%, ROA 10.0%. Book value $82.15/share (+5% YoY). Debt/capital 19.8%. Total liquidity $6.6B. No senior note maturities in FY2026. Returned $4.8B to shareholders (buybacks + dividends).​

Balance Sheet & Capital Allocation: Exceptionally conservative leverage with 19.8% debt/capital ratio, well below 30% target and industry peers. Redeemed $500M 4.6% senior notes (September 2025). Share count reduced 9% YoY through $4.3B repurchases. Dividend increased to $0.45/quarter. Strong liquidity provides flexibility through housing cycles.​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within U.S. homebuilding/housing 8-10% allocation. Premier investment-grade homebuilder credit with recent Moody's upgrade, exceptional balance sheet (19.8% debt/capital), and market leadership in affordable housing. Strengths: (a) largest U.S. builder with national scale; (b) first-time buyer focus providing demand resilience; (c) A3/BBB+ ratings; (d) strong FCF enabling shareholder returns. Risks: (a) housing cycle sensitivity; (b) mortgage rate pressure on affordability; (c) speculative construction exposure (~50%). Monitor: (1) FY2026 guidance and order trends; (2) Mortgage rate environment; (3) Net sales orders and cancellation rates; (4) Margin trajectory.

issuers.name: DANAHER CORP
issuers.creditComment:
DANAHER CORPORATION
Credit Rating: A2/Stable (Moody's Nov 2025) | A-/Stable (S&P 2025) | A-/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Global Life Sciences & Diagnostics Leader; $6.4B Q3 Revenue; $7.70-7.80 FY25 EPS Guidance; Bioprocessing Recovery; Danaher Business System; Strong FCF Generation

Credit Momentum: Positive/Upgraded – Danaher Corporation 
 achieved Moody's upgrade to A2 (from A3) in November 2025, reflecting strengthened credit profile from bioprocessing recovery, strategic cost management, and Danaher Business System (DBS) execution. The upgrade follows June 2024 Positive outlook reflecting confidence in operational improvement trajectory. Strong position in life sciences with diversified revenue base and leading market positions underpins investment-grade ratings:

Credit Ratings & Upgrade Trajectory:
Danaher maintains A2/Stable from Moody's (upgraded November 2025), A-/Stable from S&P, and A-/Stable from Fitch. Moody's November 2025 upgrade: Elevated to A2 from A3 reflecting strong position in life sciences, diversified revenue base, leading market positions, and consistent cash flow generation. Moody's June 2024: Revised outlook to Positive citing improving operational performance and credit profile strengthening. S&P rating: A- issue-level rating assigned to senior unsecured debt (November 2025). Credit profile: Strong balance sheet with low default probability (~0.01%) reflecting premium credit quality.​

Operating Performance & Bioprocessing Recovery:
Danaher demonstrates improving fundamentals: Q3 2025 results: Net earnings $555M with revenue growth driven by Bioprocessing division recovery. FY2025 guidance: Maintaining full-year adjusted diluted EPS guidance $7.70-7.80. Bioprocessing strength: Division returning to growth after pandemic-related demand normalization, contributing 30-40% of Life Sciences revenue. Cost management: Strategic cost initiatives and DBS execution driving margin improvement. Segment diversification: Life Sciences, Diagnostics, and Environmental & Applied Solutions providing revenue diversification.​

Strategic Positioning & Financial Strength:
Danaher operates proven business model: Danaher Business System (DBS): Proprietary operational excellence framework driving continuous improvement, efficiency, and growth. Market positions: Leading positions across life sciences and diagnostics end markets with high barriers to entry. FCF generation: Strong free cash flow supporting M&A strategy, shareholder returns, and balance sheet strength. Diversification: Diversified revenue base across healthcare, life sciences, and industrial applications reducing concentration risk. Macroeconomic sensitivity: Negative exposure (-0.190) to interest rates; Positive exposure (+0.335) to US Dollar.​

Overall Assessment: Danaher Corporation is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within life sciences 6-7% healthcare allocation. Danaher represents strong investment-grade credit (A2/A-) with upgraded ratings, bioprocessing recovery, and operational excellence execution. Strengths include: (a) Moody's upgrade to A2 (November 2025) reflecting strengthened profile; (b) leading positions in life sciences and diagnostics; (c) Danaher Business System driving operational excellence; (d) bioprocessing recovery supporting growth trajectory; (e) strong FCF generation supporting M&A and capital allocation; (f) diversified revenue base reducing concentration; (g) $7.70-7.80 FY2025 EPS guidance maintained; (h) low default probability (~0.01%). Risks include: (a) life sciences market cyclicality; (b) bioprocessing demand normalization post-pandemic; (c) competition from Thermo Fisher 
 and peers; (d) M&A execution and integration risks; (e) interest rate sensitivity; (f) healthcare regulatory changes. RECOMMENDED: Overweight leading life sciences company with upgraded ratings, DBS execution, and bioprocessing recovery trajectory.

Monitor: (1) Bioprocessing recovery (Revenue growth trajectory and margin expansion); (2) DBS execution (Operational efficiency and cost savings); (3) M&A activity (Strategic acquisition integration); (4) FCF generation (Capital allocation and shareholder returns); (5) Leverage metrics (A2/A- rating maintenance).

issuers.name: DEERE & COMPANY
issuers.creditComment:
DEERE & COMPANY
Credit Rating: A/Stable (S&P Affirmed Dec 2024) | A+/Stable (Fitch Affirmed July 2025) | A2/Stable (Moody's estimated) – Ag Machinery Leader; Strong Balance Sheet; Cyclical Normalization
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Deere & Company [finance:Deere & Company] maintains a strong investment-grade profile. Fitch affirmed the 'A+' Long-Term IDR and 'F1' Short-Term IDR with a Stable outlook on July 2, 2025. S&P maintains an 'A' long-term rating (affirmed as recently as late 2024 per summary lists). The ratings reflect Deere's dominant global position in agricultural equipment, resilient operating performance despite a normalizing demand cycle, and robust captive finance operations.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) duopoly/leadership in large agricultural machinery; (b) "Smart Industrial" strategy driving higher margins and recurring revenue via precision ag tech; (c) strong free cash flow generation; (d) conservative equipment operations leverage (typically net cash or very low leverage). Risks include the cyclical downturn in global agriculture (lower crop prices reducing farmer income) and inventory management normalization.
​

Financial Profile: Fitch expects Deere's financial results to deteriorate modestly through 2025 from peak levels as the cycle normalizes, but credit metrics remain strong for the 'A+' rating. Equipment operations are expected to maintain low leverage. The captive finance arm (John Deere Capital Corp) is well-capitalized with strong asset quality. Deere issued senior unsecured notes rated 'A+' in Jan 2025 for general corporate purposes. Liquidity is excellent.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Industrial Machinery 5-6% allocation. Premium IG credit (A+/A) with tech-driven margin resilience. Monitor: (1) North American large ag equipment demand; (2) Precision Ag adoption rates; (3) Used equipment inventory levels; (4) Farmer net income trends (commodity prices).

issuers.name: DELTA AIR LINES INC
issuers.creditComment:
DELTA AIR LINES INC
Credit Rating: BBB-/Positive (Fitch Upgraded July 2024 to IG; Outlook Revised to Positive Sept 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's Upgraded Feb 2025) | BB+/Stable (S&P, one notch below IG) – US Airline Leader; Debt Reduction; Premium Revenue Strength
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Recently Upgraded; Positive Outlooks) – Delta Air Lines Inc. 
 has achieved a remarkable turnaround to investment grade. Fitch upgraded Delta to 'BBB-' (Investment Grade) from BB+ in July 2024 and revised the outlook to Positive in September 2025, reflecting the company's gross debt reduction of $12 billion over three years and strong FCF generation. Moody's upgraded Delta to 'Baa2' (one notch above Ba1) in February 2025, citing strong performance and improving financial outlook. S&P still rates Delta at 'BB+' (one notch below investment grade). Delta is now one of only two US airlines rated investment grade across all three agencies (alongside Southwest).
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) leading market position (strong consumer brand, global network); (b) premium revenue strength (corporate travel, loyalty, American Express relationship); (c) disciplined cost management; (d) proven FCF generation ($4.1bn debt paydown in 2023, $2.1bn in H1 2024); (e) strong unencumbered assets (targeting $40bn intermediate term). Fitch's positive outlook signals potential for upgrade if unencumbered assets remain >$35bn and leverage metrics stay healthy.
​

Financial Profile: Delta's balance sheet has strengthened significantly. Gross debt was substantially reduced post-pandemic ($61 billion in 2020 to mid-$50 billion by 2025). Free cash flow is expected to remain positive (low single-digit margins as % of revenue through 2025-2028). EBITDAR margins are expected to stabilize ~35% (Fitch expectation). Capex is moderate (~9% of revenue annually). Liquidity is strong with unencumbered assets and cash generation supporting flexibility for operational needs and shareholder returns.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within US Airlines 5-6% allocation. "Rising Star" IG credit (BBB-/Baa2) with positive outlooks. Monitor: (1) Demand trends (leisure, corporate, premium services); (2) Capacity growth in industry (risk to pricing); (3) Fuel prices; (4) Labor negotiations (pilot deals impact profitability); (5) S&P upgrade timing; (6) Debt/unencumbered asset path toward credit metrics targets.

issuers.name: DENSO CORP
issuers.creditComment:
DENSO CORP
