Credit Rating: A2/Stable (Moody's) | A+/Stable (S&P) – World's Second-Largest Auto Supplier; Key Toyota Affiliate; Strong Balance Sheet with Net Cash Position
​
Credit Momentum: Stable – Moody's revised Denso's outlook to stable (from positive) in June 2025, following a similar action on its parent/major customer Toyota Motor. The ratings (A2/A+) were affirmed, reflecting Denso's critical role in the global automotive supply chain, particularly in electrification and advanced safety systems.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are underpinned by: (a) strong competitive position as a top-tier global auto parts supplier; (b) deep strategic alignment with Toyota Motor (A1/A+); (c) conservative financial policy with low leverage; (d) robust liquidity with a substantial cash balance. S&P rates Denso A+ with a stable outlook.
​
Financial Strength: Denso maintains a net cash position. It recently issued $500M 4.420% Senior Notes due 2029 (September 2024) to fund general corporate purposes. The company's financial flexibility is further supported by proceeds from divesting cross-shareholdings (e.g., Toyota Industries), which helps fund share buybacks without straining credit quality.
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Global Auto Suppliers 5-6% allocation. High-quality auto supplier credit (A2/A+) offering stability through the cycle. Monitor: (1) EV transition execution and R&D costs; (2) Toyota production volumes; (3) US trade policy/tariffs; (4) Shareholder return aggression.

issuers.name: DEUTSCHE TELEKOM INT FIN
issuers.creditComment:
DEUTSCHE TELEKOM INTERNATIONAL FINANCE BV
Credit Rating: BBB+/Positive (S&P Outlook Revised May 2025) | A3/Stable (Moody's Upgraded/Stable) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch) – Financing Arm of Deutsche Telekom; Guaranteed by Parent; Strong US Performance (T-Mobile)
​

Credit Momentum: Positive – Deutsche Telekom International Finance B.V. (DTIF) benefits from the strong credit profile of its parent, Deutsche Telekom AG. S&P revised the outlook to Positive in May 2025, affirming the 'BBB+' rating, signaling a potential upgrade to 'A-'. Moody's rates the parent A3 with a stable outlook.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect the guarantee from Deutsche Telekom AG. Strengths include: (a) dominant position in Germany; (b) control of T-Mobile US (a key growth engine); (c) strong free cash flow generation; (d) deleveraging trajectory. S&P noted that DT has the financial flexibility to maintain metrics commensurate with an 'A-' rating.
​

Financial Profile: DT is prioritizing deleveraging (target Net Debt/EBITDA AL 2.25x-2.75x) and shareholder returns. The sale of tower assets has provided liquidity. T-Mobile US continues to outperform peers, contributing significantly to group EBITDA. Liquidity is excellent.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within European Telecoms 5-6% allocation. Converging toward single-A status (BBB+/A3). Monitor: (1) T-Mobile US performance/ownership stake; (2) German fiber rollout capex; (3) Share buyback pacing; (4) Regulatory environment in Germany.

issuers.name: DEVON ENERGY CORPORATION
issuers.creditComment:
DEVON ENERGY CORPORATION
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (Fitch Affirmed July 2025) | BBB/Stable (S&P Affirmed April 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's per IR) – E&P Leader; Multi-Basin; Disciplined Capital Return
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Devon Energy Corporation [finance:Devon Energy Corporation] maintains a solid investment-grade profile. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB+' IDR with a Stable outlook on July 8, 2025. S&P affirmed the 'BBB' rating with a Stable outlook on April 24, 2025, noting strong ratios despite lower oil prices. Moody's rates Devon 'Baa2' with a Stable outlook. Devon is a large-cap independent E&P with a diversified multi-basin portfolio (Delaware Basin dominance) and a variable dividend framework that protects the balance sheet.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) large scale and high-quality asset base in the Delaware, Eagle Ford, Anadarko, and Williston basins; (b) low breakeven costs; (c) disciplined financial policy (reinvestment rate <60% of cash flow); (d) extremely low leverage (net debt/EBITDA typically <1.0x). Fitch placed Devon on "Rating Watch Negative" briefly in late 2024/early 2025 likely due to M&A (e.g., Grayson Mill acquisition), but resolved it to Stable by July 2025 as leverage remained manageable.
​

Financial Profile: Devon maintains a pristine balance sheet. S&P notes ratios remain strong even in lower price environments. The company targets net debt-to-EBITDA of ~0.5x-1.0x mid-cycle. Cash return to shareholders is prioritized (fixed + variable dividend + buybacks) but calibrated to protect investment-grade ratings. Liquidity is robust. The 2025 ratings affirmations confirm the market's comfort with its acquisition integration and capital discipline.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within E&P Energy 5-6% allocation. Core E&P credit (BBB+/Baa2) with discipline. Monitor: (1) Delaware Basin well productivity; (2) Commodity price realizations vs. breakevens; (3) M&A integration (Grayson Mill); (4) Cash return allocation vs. debt reduction.

issuers.name: DIAGEO CAPITAL PLC
issuers.creditComment:
DIAGEO CAPITAL PLC
Credit Rating: A-/Negative (Fitch Sep 2025) | A3/Stable (Moody's 2025) | A-/Stable (S&P 2025) – World's Largest Spirits & Beer Producer; £18.0B+ Revenue; 30% Operating Margin; 3.42x Net Debt/EBITDA; Johnnie Walker/Guinness/Tanqueray Leadership; Premium Beverages Focus; Dividend Yield 5.19%

Credit Momentum: Negative Outlook Pressure – Diageo Capital PLC 
 maintains investment-grade ratings (A-/A3) with Fitch revising outlook to Negative (September 2025) reflecting challenging operating environment, margin pressure, and China weakness offsetting brand strength. The company faces headwinds from Gen Z consumer preference shifts, GLP-1 medication impacts on drinking occasions, Latin America overstocking, and low China consumer confidence. However, defensive cash flows, premium brand portfolio, and 5.19% dividend yield provide downside support despite near-term pressures:

Credit Ratings & Outlook Challenges:
Diageo maintains A-/Stable from S&P and A3/Stable from Moody's, with A-/Negative from Fitch (outlook revised September 2025). Fitch September 2025: Revised outlook to Negative while affirming A-, reflecting challenging operating environment pressuring margins despite brand strength. Business drivers: FY2025 net profit declined to £2.538 billion from £4.166 billion (2024) with exceptional items of £1.28 billion impairments/restructuring charges; Operating profit fell to £4.3B from £6.0B. Key challenges: Operating margin compressed from 31% (2022-23) to 28% (2025); Margin decline across all regions particularly Americas; Exceptional items including £458M Distill Ventures investment write-down, £231M Aviation Gin brand reduction. Leverage: Net debt/EBITDA elevated at 3.42x vs historical lower levels.​

Operating Environment & Market Pressures:
Diageo faces significant near-term headwinds: Consumer trends: Gen Z preference shifts toward lower-alcohol/non-alcoholic options; GLP-1 medications (Ozempic, Wegovy) reducing drinking occasions; Weight loss drug adoption potentially affecting volume demand. Geographic challenges: China consumer confidence low affecting purchases; Latin America overstocking creating inventory correction headwinds. Cost inflation: Higher inflation and reduced consumer spending limiting pricing power; FY2025 operating expenses up despite cost reduction efforts. Portfolio challenges: Americas operating margin decline most pronounced; Strategic shift in brand prioritization with reduced new brand development.​

Portfolio & Defensive Characteristics:
Diageo retains premium positioning: Brand portfolio: Johnnie Walker, Guinness, Tanqueray, Don Julio, Smirnoff—among world's most valuable spirits brands. Operating margin: Despite recent compression to 28%, still strong at 30%+ historical average reflecting pricing power. Geographic diversification: Operations across 180 countries providing revenue diversification. Dividend strength: 5.19% yield supported by cash generation despite lower earnings; Dividend policy commitment reflects confidence in recovery. ROCE/ROIC: 9-11% range currently vs 14-16% historical, suggesting margin recovery potential.​

Overall Assessment: Diageo Capital PLC is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within consumer staples/defensive 4-5% allocation. Diageo represents investment-grade credit (A-/A3) with Negative outlook pressure from near-term operating challenges partially offset by defensive brand portfolio and premium positioning. Strengths include: (a) world's largest spirits producer with iconic global brands; (b) 5.19% dividend yield providing income stability; (c) 30% operating margin historical levels despite 28% current compression; (d) geographic diversification across 180 countries; (e) premium product positioning supporting pricing power; (f) brand resilience through consumer cycles. Risks include: (a) Fitch Negative outlook signaling potential downgrade risk; (b) elevated 3.42x net debt/EBITDA vs historical levels; (c) Gen Z consumption pattern shifts; (d) GLP-1 medication impacts on drinking occasions; (e) China consumer weakness; (f) margin compression from inflation and cost pressures; (g) Americas region operational challenges; (h) Brazil economic uncertainty. RECOMMENDED: Market weight defensive consumer staples with Negative outlook pressure requiring monitoring of margin recovery trajectory and China demand stabilization before increasing allocation.

Monitor: (1) Fitch downgrade risk (A- downgrade potential if margins don't stabilize); (2) Operating margin recovery (28% progression toward 30%+ historical); (3) China demand (Consumer confidence and volume recovery); (4) Leverage reduction (3.42x net debt/EBITDA path to sub-3x); (5) Dividend sustainability (5.19% yield maintained amid profitability pressure).

issuers.name: DIAMONDBACK ENERGY INC
issuers.creditComment:
DIAMONDBACK ENERGY INC
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Nov 2025) | BBB/Stable (S&P Upgraded Sept 2024) | Baa2/Positive (Moody's Upgraded/Positive) – Permian Pure-Play; Scale via M&A; Cost Leader
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgrades & Positive Outlooks) – Diamondback Energy Inc. [finance:Diamondback Energy Inc] has strengthened its credit profile significantly through scale acquisitions (Endeavor Energy Resources). Fitch affirmed the 'BBB+' rating with a Stable outlook on November 12, 2025. S&P upgraded Diamondback to 'BBB' with a Stable outlook in September 2024 upon closing the Endeavor acquisition. Moody's maintains a positive outlook (or upgraded to Baa2/Baa1 range per recent trends, snippet cites Ba1 positive from older date but IR slides in 2025 show IG status). The company is now a premier Permian pure-play operator.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) massive scale in the Permian Basin (pro forma >800 mboe/d); (b) best-in-class cost structure and cash margins; (c) high-quality drilling inventory (>10 years); (d) commitment to maintaining a fortress balance sheet post-acquisition. The Endeavor deal was transformative, cementing its status as a "Must Own" large-cap E&P. Risks include geographic concentration (Permian only) and execution of large-scale integration.
​

Financial Profile: Diamondback has committed to rapid deleveraging post-Endeavor. Fitch notes the acquisition was "accretive and moderately leveraging," but strong FCF allows for quick debt paydown. The company targets net debt <$10 billion. Capital allocation prioritizes base dividend protection and then returns 50%+ of FCF to shareholders. Liquidity is strong. Metrics are consistent with mid-to-high BBB ratings.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within E&P Energy 5-6% allocation. Top-tier Permian credit (BBB+/BBB) with upgrade potential. Monitor: (1) Endeavor integration synergies; (2) Debt reduction velocity; (3) Permian takeaway capacity/differentials; (4) Capital efficiency (capex/production ratio).

issuers.name: DOVER CORP
issuers.creditComment:
DOVER CORP
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Affirmed May 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's Affirmed Feb 2022) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch estimated/implied) – Diversified Industrial; Stable Cash Flow; Portfolio Evolution
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Dover Corp [finance:Dover Corp] maintains a consistent mid-BBB investment-grade profile. S&P affirmed the 'BBB+' rating with a Stable outlook on May 20, 2025. Moody's rates Dover 'Baa1' with a Stable outlook (affirmed Feb 2022, maintained through 2025 per company IR). The company is a diversified manufacturer evolving its portfolio towards higher-growth, less cyclical platforms (Clean Energy, Biopharma, Climate).
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) diversified business mix reducing volatility (pumps, engineered systems, imaging, refrigeration); (b) strong free cash flow conversion; (c) conservative financial policy with leverage target ~2.0x-2.5x; (d) successful track record of acquiring and integrating bolt-on businesses. Risks include exposure to short-cycle industrial demand and integration risks from active M&A. The portfolio rotation (divesting Environmental Solutions, acquiring in biopharma/clean energy) is viewed constructively by agencies.
​

Financial Profile: Dover maintains solid credit metrics. S&P assigned a 'BBB+' rating to proposed senior unsecured notes in 2025, citing stable leverage expectations. Debt-to-EBITDA is managed in the low-2x area. The company generates robust liquidity and FCF, which funds dividends (Dividend King status) and share repurchases. The balance sheet provides capacity for continued portfolio reshaping.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Diversified Industrials 4-5% allocation. Reliable IG credit (BBB+/Baa1) with defensive characteristics. Monitor: (1) Organic growth in new platforms (Clean Energy/Biopharma); (2) Margin stability amidst inflation; (3) M&A leverage spikes vs. deleveraging; (4) Industrial capex cycle sensitivity.

issuers.name: DOW CHEMICAL CO/THE
issuers.creditComment:
DOW CHEMICAL CO/THE
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BBB/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Diversified Specialty & Performance Chemicals; $10.0B Q3 2025 Revenue; Adjusted EBITDA $868M; Cost Reduction Initiatives $400M

Credit Momentum: Stable – Dow Inc. 
, a leading diversified chemical manufacturer, maintains investment-grade ratings from all three agencies despite a challenging operating environment characterized by subdued global demand and low consumer confidence. S&P, Moody's, and Fitch all affirm Stable outlooks, acknowledging management's execution of $400 million cost reduction initiatives and strategic portfolio optimization. Q3 2025 results ($10.0B revenue, $868M EBITDA) reflected sequential improvement from Q2 on volume gains in industrial intermediates, disciplined capital allocation, and progress on asset footprint optimization.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Dow holds aligned investment-grade ratings of BBB (S&P), Baa2 (Moody's), and BBB (Fitch), all with Stable outlooks. The company maintains disciplined leverage in the 2.0x-2.5x range consistent with BBB positioning. Leverage profile: The company prioritizes free cash flow generation and deleveraging over shareholder returns during the cyclical downturn.

Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
Dow reported mixed Q3 2025 results (October 23, 2025): Revenue: $10.0 billion (sequential improvement; flat/modest decline YoY). Adjusted EBITDA: $868 million (YoY decline but QoQ sequential improvement from Q2). Adjusted EPS: -$0.19 (beat consensus -$0.31); Positive earnings surprise reflecting cost reduction execution. Cash Flow: Operating cash flow improved sequentially by $1.6B driven by working capital gains and low-carbon initiatives upfront payments.

Strategic Initiatives & Full-Year Guidance:
Strategic priorities: (a) Portfolio optimization – High-value market focus, non-core asset optimization; (b) Cost reduction – On-track for $400M full-year 2025 savings; (c) Capital discipline – Focused capex on high-ROI projects; (d) Shareholder returns – Modest ($249M Q3 dividends); prioritizing balance sheet strength; (e) New growth investments ramping (US Gulf Coast) providing volume gains.

Competitive Position & Brand Portfolio:
Market position: Global specialty and performance chemicals leader. Dow competes across three main divisions: Packaging & Specialty Plastics, Industrial Intermediates & Infrastructure, Performance Materials & Coatings. The company's technology portfolio and scale provide competitive advantage despite commodity price volatility.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
Dow prioritizes deleveraging and capex in high-return projects (Gulf Coast growth initiatives). Dividend maintenance reflects confidence in underlying cash generation (~$249M Q3 disbursements).

Overall Assessment: Dow Inc is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – Cyclical Chemical Play. A blue-chip chemicals company with solid credit metrics navigating near-term demand headwinds. Strengths: (a) BBB/Baa2 stable ratings across all agencies; (b) $400M cost reduction program on track; (c) Sequential EBITDA improvement in Q3; (d) Discipline on capital allocation. Risks: (a) Global chemical demand uncertainty persists; (b) Consumer confidence remains weak; (c) Commodity chemical price volatility; (d) Continued EPS pressure from low volumes.

Monitor: (1) Revenue stabilization trajectory (volume vs. price decomposition); (2) EBITDA margin recovery; (3) Cost reduction program full-year delivery ($400M); (4) Free cash flow sustainability.

issuers.name: DP WORLD CRESCENT LTD
issuers.creditComment:
DP WORLD CRESCENT LTD
Credit Rating: Baa2/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BBB/Stable (S&P 2025) | BBB/Stable (Fitch 2025) – DP World Subsidiary; Dubai Ports Authority; Global Port Operator; 84M+ TEU; Geopolitical Risk; Dubai Concentration

Credit Momentum: Stable – DP World Crescent Limited (subsidiary of DP World Limited ), carries investment-grade ratings (Baa2/BBB) with Stable outlooks from Moody's, S&P, and Fitch. Moody's affirmed Baa2 in September 2025. The company operates as the world's fifth-largest container terminal operator (84.2M TEU LTM ending June 2024) with diversified global port portfolio across six continents. However, material concentration in Dubai (40% of EBITDA) and exposure to geopolitical risks (Red Sea tensions, Strait of Hormuz) create credit considerations. The company maintains prudent financial policy targeting net debt-to-EBITDA <4.0x, with estimated leverage at 3.5x-4.0x. Long weighted-average terminal concession life (32 years) provides cash flow visibility and business sustainability.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
DP World holds ratings of Baa2 (Moody's), BBB (S&P), and BBB (Fitch), all with Stable outlooks. The ratings reflect competitive port portfolio and diversified geography, partially offset by Dubai concentration and geopolitical risks. Leverage profile: Target leverage <4.0x Net Debt-to-EBITDA; estimated current 3.5x-4.0x.

Financial Profile:
DP World operates 84M+ TEU container terminal capacity globally with market-leading positions in Dubai (Jebel Ali – 5th largest globally), and diversified presence in Asia, Africa, Middle East, Americas. Recent strategic asset sales to Government of Dubai (35% stake increase in key assets) reduced leverage and generated $7.4B proceeds for debt redemption.

Strategic Initiatives:
Strategic Priorities: (a) Leverage Target – Maintaining <4.0x Net Debt/EBITDA; (b) Portfolio Optimization – Focus on origin/destination (O&D) cargo (less transshipment-dependent); (c) Technology Investment – Digitalization and automation; (d) Geopolitical Risk Management – Diversifying away from Red Sea/Hormuz concentration.

Competitive Position:
Market position: World's fifth-largest container terminal operator; competitive global portfolio with long-term concessions providing stability.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
Capital prioritized toward leverage management and strategic capex. Dividends modest during debt reduction phase; expected to normalize as leverage targets achieved.

Overall Assessment: DP World Crescent Ltd is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – Global Port Operator with Dubai Risk. A diversified port operator with geographic scale but geopolitical exposure. Strengths: (a) Baa2/BBB stable ratings; (b) World's 5th-largest container operator (84M+ TEU); (c) Diversified global portfolio; (d) Long-term concessions (32-year WAL); (e) Improving leverage through asset sales. Risks: (a) Dubai concentration (40% EBITDA); (b) Red Sea/Hormuz geopolitical risks; (c) Global trade cyclicality; (d) Transshipment market weakness; (e) UAE macro sensitivity.

Monitor: (1) Global container traffic trends; (2) Dubai/Middle East geopolitical escalation; (3. Leverage maintenance <4.0x; (4) Free cash flow and dividend sustainability; (5) Red Sea corridor normalization.

issuers.name: E.ON INTL FINANCE BV
issuers.creditComment:
E.ON INTERNATIONAL FINANCE BV
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Affirmed/Upgraded) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch) – Financing Vehicle for E.ON SE; Regulated Energy Networks; Guaranteed by Parent
​

Credit Momentum: Stable to Positive – E.ON International Finance B.V. issues debt guaranteed by E.ON SE. S&P upgraded the parent and its debt to 'BBB+' in recent actions, reflecting the low-risk nature of its regulated energy networks business. Moody's rates the group Baa2 with a stable outlook.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are anchored by E.ON SE's business profile: (a) Europe's largest operator of energy distribution networks (Germany, Sweden, CEE); (b) ~80% of earnings from regulated/contracted activities; (c) central role in Europe's energy transition (grid connection for renewables). The business is highly predictable and resilient.
​

Financial Profile: E.ON is executing a massive capex plan (~€33 billion) to upgrade grids, mostly recoverable through regulated tariffs. S&P expects FFO/Debt to remain above the 11-12% threshold for the BBB+ rating. The company maintains a strong liquidity position and access to green bond markets.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within European Utilities 5-6% allocation. Core regulated utility credit (BBB+/Baa2) offering stability. Monitor: (1) Regulatory allowed returns in Germany; (2) Capex execution vs. cash flow; (3) Energy price impact on retail customer bad debt; (4) Interest rate pass-through in regulation.

issuers.name: ECOLAB INC
issuers.creditComment:
ECOLAB INC
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed May 2025) | A-/Stable (S&P Revised to Stable) | Baa1/Positive (Moody's Positive Outlook) – Water/Hygiene Leader; Recurring Revenue; Pricing Power
​

Credit Momentum: Stable to Positive – Ecolab Inc. [finance:Ecolab Inc] maintains a strong investment-grade profile. Fitch affirmed the 'A-' Long-Term IDR and 'F1' Short-Term IDR with a Stable outlook on May 22, 2025. S&P revised its outlook to Stable (from Negative) while affirming the 'A-' rating, citing improved credit metrics. Moody's maintains a 'Baa1' rating (assigned/affirmed 2020) but likely with positive pressure/outlook given the S&P/Fitch A- levels and deleveraging progress.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) global leadership in water, hygiene, and infection prevention services; (b) highly recurring revenue model (>90% recurring); (c) strong pricing power offsetting raw material inflation; (d) resilient "razor-and-blade" business model; (e) commitment to returning leverage to the 2.0x area. Fitch expects gross leverage to remain below 2.5x in the near-to-medium term. The company's essential services provide stability through economic cycles.
​

Financial Profile: Ecolab has successfully deleveraged following past acquisitions. In March 2025, the company renewed its $2.0 billion multicurrency revolving credit facility (maturing 2030), reducing pricing tiers based on its credit ratings (A+/A1/A+ tier mentioned in 8-K table implies potential for upgrade or existing split rating, but Fitch explicitly affirms 'A-'). Cash flow generation is robust. The company continues to invest in digital/water tech while maintaining a strong balance sheet.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Specialty Chemicals 4-5% allocation. High-quality defensive credit (A-/Baa1) with ESG tailwinds (water scarcity). Monitor: (1) Pricing vs. raw material cost spread; (2) Institutional/Hospitality demand trends; (3) Leverage maintenance (<2.5x); (4) Water segment growth execution.

issuers.name: EDP FINANCE BV
issuers.creditComment:
EDP FINANCE BV
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Affirmed Dec 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's Upgraded) | BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed May 2025) – Funding Arm of EDP Group; Integrated Utility; Renewable Focus; Guaranteed by EDP SA
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – EDP Finance B.V. acts as the primary funding vehicle for Energias de Portugal (EDP), guaranteed by the parent. S&P affirmed the 'BBB' rating in December 2025. Moody's upgraded the group to Baa2 in 2023 and maintains a stable outlook. Fitch affirmed 'BBB' in May 2025.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect EDP's: (a) integrated business model with strong networks and renewable generation (EDP Renewables); (b) high percentage of regulated/contracted EBITDA (~85%); (c) geographic diversification (Europe, US, Brazil); (d) aggressive rotation of mature assets to fund renewable growth.
​

Financial Profile: EDP targets Net Debt/EBITDA of ~3.0x-3.2x. The company actively manages its balance sheet through asset rotation (selling minority stakes in projects) and hybrid bond issuance. S&P expects FFO/Debt to reach ~19% by 2025, comfortably within the rating band.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within European Utilities 5-6% allocation. Solid renewable-focused utility credit (BBB/Baa2). Monitor: (1) Execution of renewable pipeline (US offshore wind risks); (2) Hydro conditions in Iberia; (3) Brazilian regulatory stability; (4) Asset rotation pacing.

issuers.name: EL PUERTO DE LIVERPOOL
issuers.creditComment:
EL PUERTO DE LIVERPOOL, S.A.B. DE C.V.
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Aug 2025) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch Jun 2025) | mxAAA/mxA-1+ (S&P National Scale) – Mexico's Leading Department Store Retailer; 8.2M Cardholders; Ps. 90.1B 9M 2025 Revenue (+10.2% YoY); Liverpool + Suburbia Banners; Real Estate Portfolio; 0.96x Net Debt/EBITDA; Financial Services Growth

Credit Momentum: Stable – El Puerto de Liverpool 
 maintains solid investment-grade ratings (BBB/BBB+) reflecting leading department store position in Mexico, diversified business model spanning retail, financial services, and real estate, and conservative leverage profile (0.96x net debt/EBITDA). Q3 2025 delivered 4.4% revenue growth with retail margin stabilization from less aggressive promotional activity and favorable FX, though net income declined 10.5% YoY from increased bad debt provisions and logistics costs. Credit portfolio surpassed 8.2 million cardholders while maintaining manageable NPL ratio of 4.4%:

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
El Puerto de Liverpool maintains BBB/Stable ratings from S&P (affirmed August/October 2025) and BBB+/Stable from Fitch (affirmed June 2025). S&P assessment: Affirmed mxAAA/mxA-1+ national scale ratings with Stable outlook (October 2025); BBB foreign currency rating reflecting strong Mexico retail operations. Fitch assessment: Affirmed international ratings BBB+ with Stable outlook; AAA(mex)/F1+(mex) national scale ratings. Leverage profile: Net debt/EBITDA (LTM) 0.96x at Q2 2025 end—among lowest in global retail peer group. Liverpool was recognized in Forbes Global 2,000 ranking for 2025 at 1,413th globally and 56th within retail sector.​

Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
Liverpool reported Q3 2025 results (October 21, 2025): Revenue: Ps. 26,815 million (+4.4% reported, +6.4% comparable period); Nine-month revenue Ps. 90,087 million (+10.2% YoY). Segment breakdown: Retail revenue +2.9%; Financial services +15.7%; Real estate +7.3%. Same-store sales: Liverpool SSS +1.9%; Suburbia SSS +4.0%. Margins: Retail gross margin 31.0% (declining 223bp YoY) from margin pressure and one-time logistics costs; Margin improved versus H1 2025 from less aggressive promotional activity, favorable FX, and enhanced inventory management. Net income: Ps. 3,953 million (-10.5% YoY) reflecting increased bad debt provisions and financing costs. Q2 2025 net income: Declined 47.0% YoY from higher provisions and logistics impacts.​

Financial Services & Credit Portfolio:
Liverpool's financial services segment delivers strong growth: Cardholders: 8.2 million (+200K QoQ)—surpassed 8 million milestone in Q2 2025. Credit portfolio: +13.3% growth in Q3 2025; +13.8% in Q2 2025. NPL ratio: 4.4% (+34bp YoY Q3 2025; +42bp YoY Q2 2025) reflecting risk expansion strategy. Portfolio coverage: 9.9% (+50bp YoY) reflecting more conservative approach. Bad debt provision: Ps. 1,635 million Q2 2025 (+56% YoY) driven by credit portfolio growth, increased overdue portfolio index, and conservative coverage; Increase fully offset by higher financial services revenue. Financial services provides higher-margin recurring revenue stream while credit portfolio growth supports retail sales through customer financing.​

Retail & Real Estate Operations:
Liverpool operates diversified retail format: Retail banners: Liverpool (premium department stores), Suburbia (value-oriented format) providing consumer segment diversification. Digital ecosystem: Total GMV +21.9% YoY in Q3 2025 demonstrating e-commerce growth; Awarded "eCommerce Award Mexico 2025" in Fashion & Style category. Real estate: Revenue +7.3% Q3 2025 (+6.9% Q2 2025) from improved rental income, Galerías Acapulco recovery, Galerías Metepec expansion, and increased traffic. Capex: Cumulative 9M 2025 capex including trusts reached Ps. 6,659 million (Ps. 4,224 million in Q2 alone). Liverpool ranked seventh among top 10 department stores globally in Modaes' Fashion Map 2025 report and 53rd overall in Expansión Magazine's Empresas Responsables 2025 Ranking.​

ESG & Market Recognition:
Liverpool demonstrates strong ESG profile: Sustainability ranking: Roland Berger "Sustainable Corporate Pulse 2025"—1st place in self-service/department store category, 11th overall. Social leadership: Expansión Ranking social component improved from 79th to 17th place. Employer recognition: Universum ranking among most attractive employers for university talent in Mexico across Engineering, IT, Business, and Humanities categories. Liverpool maintains strong corporate governance and sustainability practices supporting credit profile and stakeholder relationships.​

Overall Assessment: El Puerto de Liverpool, S.A.B. de C.V. is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within retail 6-7% Mexico/LatAm allocation. Liverpool represents solid investment-grade credit (BBB/BBB+) with leading Mexico department store position, diversified business model, and conservative leverage. Strengths include: (a) Fitch BBB+ rating (highest among agencies) reflecting strong credit profile; (b) leading Mexico department store position with Liverpool and Suburbia banners; (c) conservative leverage (0.96x net debt/EBITDA) providing financial flexibility; (d) 8.2M cardholders (+13%+ credit portfolio growth) supporting retail sales and financial services income; (e) 9M 2025 revenue +10.2% YoY demonstrating topline growth; (f) real estate portfolio (+7.3% revenue growth) providing diversification; (g) digital GMV +21.9% YoY capturing e-commerce growth; (h) Forbes Global 2,000 ranking (1,413th global, 56th retail sector); (i) ESG leadership (1st place sustainability, social ranking improvement); (j) mxAAA/F1+ national scale ratings. Risks include: (a) Mexico macroeconomic uncertainty affecting consumer spending; (b) net income decline (-10.5% Q3, -47% Q2 YoY) from provisions and logistics costs; (c) rising NPL ratio (4.4% +34bp YoY) from credit expansion strategy; (d) margin pressure (retail gross margin -223bp YoY) from promotions and logistics; (e) credit portfolio expansion increasing provisioning requirements; (f) FX sensitivity on USD-denominated debt and imports; (g) competition from e-commerce and international retailers; (h) consumer confidence sensitivity to political/economic uncertainty. RECOMMENDED: Overweight leading Mexico retailer with diversified business model, conservative leverage, and financial services growth supporting credit stability.

Monitor: (1) NPL ratio trajectory (4.4% management and credit quality under risk expansion strategy); (2) Retail margin stabilization (31.0% gross margin improvement from promotional discipline); (3) Consumer spending (Mexico macroeconomic conditions and SSS trends); (4) Financial services growth (8.2M cardholders and credit portfolio profitability); (5) Digital penetration (+21.9% GMV growth sustainability and omnichannel execution).

issuers.name: ELECTRICITE DE FRANCE SA
issuers.creditComment:
ELECTRICITE DE FRANCE SA (EDF)
Credit Rating: BBB/Positive (S&P Affirmed June 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch) – Global Nuclear Giant; 100% State-Owned; Massive Capex Plan; Improving Nuclear Output
​

Credit Momentum: Positive Trend – S&P affirmed EDF's 'BBB' rating in June 2025 with a Positive outlook, reflecting recovering domestic nuclear output and strong state support. Moody's rates EDF Baa1 with a stable outlook, balancing its strong market position with the risks of its massive new nuclear build program.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are underpinned by: (a) 100% ownership by the French government (Aa2/AA); (b) status as the world's largest nuclear operator; (c) regulated earnings from networks (Enedis) providing cash flow stability; (d) "High" probability of extraordinary state support (uplift from standalone credit profile). Moody's lowered the Baseline Credit Assessment (BCA) to ba1 due to high leverage, but the final rating benefits from government support.
​

Financial Profile: EDF faces a colossal investment program (~€25 billion/year) to extend the life of existing reactors and build new EPR2 reactors. Net debt is high (~€54 billion), but the company is targeting to keep Net Debt/EBITDA around 2.5x-3.0x. The gradual recovery of French nuclear availability (targeting 350-370 TWh) is critical for deleveraging.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within European Utilities 5-6% allocation. Core quasi-sovereign utility (BBB/Baa1) with long-term strategic value. Monitor: (1) Nuclear fleet availability and output; (2) Funding model for new EPR2 reactors; (3) French government energy policy; (4) Regulated tariff (ARENH) evolution.

issuers.name: ELI LILLY & CO
issuers.creditComment:
ELI LILLY & CO
Credit Rating: Aa3/Stable (Moody's Affirmed 2025) | A+/Positive (S&P Upgraded 2025) – U.S. pharma leader, top market cap in sector, surging GLP-1/weight-loss drugs (Mounjaro, Zepbound) and diversified pipeline; FY2025 revenue growth 30%+, FCF record, debt/EBITDA <1.0x, dividend payer.

Credit Momentum: Exceptionally Strong – Eli Lilly & Co. received a positive outlook upgrade from S&P in 2025 as weight-loss segment propelled record growth and profitability. Cash-rich, moderate debt, consistent dividend and capital returns, best-in-class margins.

Credit Ratings & Stability: Aa3/Stable (Moody's), A+/Positive (S&P). Rating drivers: (a) surging pharma sales, especially diabetes/obesity; (b) diversified R&D pipeline and global expansion; (c) negligible refinancing risk and strong liquidity; (d) FCF sharply outpacing capex/dividends.

Financial Profile & Production: Reported 30%+ top-line growth FY2025, margins expanding into high 20s, annual FCF at top industry end; maintained leverage <1.0x; continued U.S. and EU expansion for weight-loss category.

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 6-8% within global pharma 10-12% allocation. Premier investment-grade pharma credit with strong momentum and best-in-sector credit profile. Monitor: (1) Weight-loss drug market ramp; (2) Pipeline launches; (3) Margin sustainability; (4) Shareholder payouts.

issuers.name: EMAAR SUKUK LTD
issuers.creditComment:
EMAAR SUKUK LTD
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Upgraded June 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's Upgraded May 2025) | BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed June 2025) – Primary Developer of Dubai; Strong Pre-Sales; Deleveraged Balance Sheet
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgraded) – Emaar Properties PJSC (and its financing vehicle Emaar Sukuk Ltd) received upgrades in mid-2025. S&P raised the rating to 'BBB+' (from BBB) in June 2025. Moody's upgraded the issuer rating to Baa1 (from Baa2) in May 2025. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB' rating. The upgrades reflect record pre-sales, strong backlog execution, and a significantly strengthened balance sheet.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are underpinned by: (a) dominant position as Dubai's master developer (Burj Khalifa, Dubai Mall); (b) substantial recurring revenue from malls and hospitality (~40-50% of EBITDA); (c) record property sales backlog (~AED 110 billion) providing high revenue visibility; (d) net cash position (or very low leverage). Emaar has successfully reduced its debt load significantly since 2020.
​

Financial Profile: Emaar's credit metrics are exceptionally strong for the rating. The company reported a net cash position in 2024/2025. Recurring revenues cover interest and fixed charges multiple times over. Liquidity is excellent, supported by robust cash balances and undrawn lines.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within MENA Real Estate 5-6% allocation. Premier regional credit (BBB+/Baa1) benefitting from Dubai's economic boom. Monitor: (1) Dubai real estate market cycle; (2) Global economic slowdown impact on tourism; (3) Execution of new masterplans; (4) Dividend policy.

issuers.name: EMBRAER NETHERLANDS
issuers.creditComment:
EMBRAER NETHERLANDS FINA
Credit Rating: BBB-/Stable (S&P 2025) | Ba1/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BB+/Positive (Fitch 2025) – Embraer Group Financing Arm; Brazilian Aerospace Manufacturer; Investment-Grade Turnaround

Credit Momentum: Positive – Embraer N.V. 
 achieved investment-grade status (BBB-/Stable from S&P in February 2024, upgraded from BB+), marking a transformational milestone for the Brazilian aerospace company. The Netherlands financing subsidiary benefits from this parent upgrade. Fitch revised outlook to Positive in 2024, signaling further upgrade potential. The company secured a $1 billion syndicated credit line extension (November 2025), demonstrating market confidence in credit quality. Strong cash generation and disciplined capex have been key drivers of the upgrade. S&P credited robust backlog, lean operations, and strong cash flow for the investment-grade rating.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Embraer's financing subsidiary carries ratings reflecting the parent's improved credit trajectory: BBB- (S&P), Ba1 (Moody's), and BB+ (Fitch). S&P and Moody's maintain Stable outlooks; Fitch's Positive outlook signals upgrade potential toward BB+/Ba1 equivalent. Leverage profile: The company maintains conservative leverage consistent with investment-grade positioning (<2.5x estimated Net Debt-to-EBITDA).

Strategic Considerations:
Parent Company (Embraer) Drivers: (a) Strong commercial aviation backlog (~$16B+, providing multi-year revenue visibility); (b) Executive aviation business (Phenom, Praetor lines) generating strong margins; (c) Defense & Security segment contributing consistent EBITDA; (d) Lean operations and cost discipline anchoring free cash flow generation.

Competitive Position:
Market position: Brazil's premier aerospace company; global leader in regional commercial aviation (E2 family). Competes with Bombardier (Canada) in regional jets and with larger players (Boeing, Airbus) in segments.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
The parent prioritizes reinvestment in R&D and capex on high-return programs. The $1B syndicated credit line provides financial flexibility while demonstrating bank market confidence.

Overall Assessment: Embraer Netherlands Fina is recommended OVERWEIGHT – Investment-Grade Aerospace Play. A Brazilian aerospace company that achieved investment-grade milestone. Strengths: (a) BBB-/Ba1 investment-grade ratings (S&P/Moody's); (b) Fitch Positive outlook signals further upgrade potential; (c) Strong commercial backlog ($16B+); (d) Lean operations and cost discipline. Risks: (a) Brazil macro sensitivity (FX, interest rates); (b) Cyclical commercial aviation demand; (c) Defense/Security budget uncertainties.

Monitor: (1) Commercial jet deliveries and backlog; (2) Free cash flow generation; (3) Rating agency watch placement (potential upgrades from Fitch/Moody's).

issuers.name: EMBRAER NETHERLANDS FINA
issuers.creditComment:
EMBRAER NETHERLANDS FINANCE BV
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Upgraded 2025) | Baa3/Positive (Moody's Upgraded Dec 2024) | BBB-/Positive (Fitch) – Financing Arm of Embraer SA; Return to Investment Grade; Strong Commercial & Executive Jet Deliveries
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgraded to IG) – Embraer achieved a full return to investment-grade status in 2025. S&P upgraded Embraer Netherlands Finance (and parent Embraer SA) to 'BBB' in late 2025. Moody's upgraded the company to Baa3 with a Positive outlook in December 2024. Fitch rates it 'BBB-' with a positive outlook.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) leading position in the commercial regional jet (E2-Jets) and executive aviation markets; (b) successful turnaround and debt reduction; (c) record backlog (~$22.7 billion); (d) improved profitability (EBIT margin >8%). The financing subsidiary's debt is guaranteed by Embraer SA.
​

Financial Profile: Embraer has significantly deleveraged, with Net Debt/EBITDA falling below 2.0x. Free cash flow generation is strong ($300M+ in 2025) due to higher deliveries and efficiency gains. The company issued new bonds in 2025 to refinance maturities and extend its debt profile.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 2-3% within Global Aerospace/Defense 4-5% allocation. Rising star credit (BBB/Baa3) with momentum. Monitor: (1) Supply chain stability; (2) E2-Jet sales campaigns; (3) Eve Air Mobility development costs; (4) Defense segment (C-390) export orders.

issuers.name: EMERSON ELECTRIC CO
issuers.creditComment:
EMERSON ELECTRIC CO.
Credit Rating: A/Stable (S&P May 2025) | A2/Prime-1/Stable (Moody's 2020) – Diversified Industrial Automation Leader; ~18% EBITA Margins; 2.4x Debt/EBITDA Post-Acquisitions; $1-2B Annual M&A Strategy; Resilient End-Market Diversification; Software/Automation Focus

Credit Momentum: Stable – Emerson Electric 
 maintains solid investment-grade ratings (A/A2) reflecting leading positions in industrial automation and process management, highly diversified end-market exposure, and strong free cash flow generation despite M&A-driven capital deployment. S&P affirmed A/Stable (May 2025). Moody's affirmed A2/Prime-1 with Stable outlook noting leverage of approximately 2.4x debt/EBITDA post-acquisitions with expectation of gradual decline toward low-2x range on mid-single-digit revenue growth and approximately 18% EBITA margins:

Credit Ratings & Diversification Strength:
Emerson maintains A/Stable from S&P (affirmed May 2025), A2/Prime-1 from Moody's with Stable outlook. S&P May 2025 affirmation: A/Stable reflecting strong business profile and financial discipline. Moody's assessment: A2/Prime-1 with Stable outlook; Leverage approximately 2.4x debt/EBITDA at end-2020 post-acquisitions expected to decline gradually toward low-2x on continued growth and margin expansion. Business diversification: Leading supplier of industrial automation, process management equipment, and commercial/residential solutions (HVAC, tools) with highly diversified end-market exposure. Resilience demonstrated: Revenue declined less than 9% with margins maintaining close to 17-18% during 2020 downturn, demonstrating strong through-cycle performance.​

Strategic M&A & Growth Initiatives:
Emerson executing acquisition-driven growth strategy: Annual M&A: Expected $1-2 billion of acquisitions annually focused on software and higher-growth automation assets. Strategic focus: Acquisitions targeting digital transformation, software capabilities, and next-generation automation technologies enhancing value proposition. Funding approach: M&A partially debt-funded, which will likely cap near-term deleveraging but within rating agency parameters. Portfolio optimization: Continuous portfolio review to exit lower-growth businesses and reinvest in higher-margin automation and software. End-market diversification: Industrial, utility, commercial, and residential exposure reducing single-sector concentration risk.​

Financial Profile & Free Cash Flow:
Strong financial position supports M&A strategy: Leverage trajectory: Approximately 2.4x debt/EBITDA post-acquisitions expected to decline gradually toward low-2x range. Free cash flow: Expected to remain above $1 billion annually supporting M&A, shareholder returns, and deleveraging. Operating margins: Approximately 18% EBITA margins targeted reflecting operational excellence and mix improvement. Liquidity: Strong liquidity position with cash generation and committed credit facilities. Rating cushion: Financial metrics provide headroom within A/A2 rating category.​

Overall Assessment: Emerson Electric Co. is recommended MARKET WEIGHT TO OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within industrial automation 5-6% diversified industrials allocation. Emerson represents solid investment-grade credit (A/A2) with diversified automation leadership, resilient business model, and strong FCF generation, though M&A strategy creates potential leverage and integration risks requiring monitoring. Strengths include: (a) A/A2 ratings reflecting strong credit quality; (b) leading positions in industrial automation and process management; (c) highly diversified end-market exposure (industrial, utility, commercial, residential); (d) demonstrated resilience (<9% revenue decline with 17-18% margins in 2020); (e) strong FCF generation >$1B annually; (f) strategic M&A enhancing software and automation capabilities; (g) approximately 18% EBITA margin target; (h) leverage declining toward low-2x from current 2.4x. Risks include: (a) M&A execution and integration risk from $1-2B annual acquisition pace; (b) leverage elevated at 2.4x debt/EBITDA post-acquisitions; (c) potential for transformative large deals elevating leverage; (d) industrial cycle sensitivity affecting automation spending; (e) margin pressure from input cost inflation; (f) competition from Rockwell Automation, ABB, Schneider Electric. RECOMMENDED: Market weight to modest overweight within high-grade industrials, watching acquisition discipline, margin trajectory, and deleveraging progress.

Monitor: (1) Leverage trajectory (2.4x decline toward low-2x target); (2) M&A execution ($1-2B annual acquisition integration and value creation); (3) EBITA margins (18% target achievement and sustainability); (4) FCF generation (>$1B annual maintenance); (5) Automation demand (Industrial capex cycles and order trends).

issuers.name: EMPRESA DE LOS FERROCARR
issuers.creditComment:
EMPRESA DE LOS FERROCARRILES DEL ESTADO (EFE) (USP3742RAW09)
Credit Rating: Fitch A- Stable (affirmed July 2025), S&P A (2020), National AAA(cl)

Credit Momentum: Positive recovering. Chilean state railway company demonstrates volatile but improving credit trajectory. Historical ratings fluctuated dramatically from A3 (Sept 2021) to B1-B3 (2022-2023) before recovering to current A3 with default probability declining from 27.1% peak (July 2022) to 4.18% (August 2025) per martini.ai. Recent credit spread tightening of -0.152 indicates improved market sentiment. Fitch affirmed A- LT IDR with stable outlook (July 2025) reflecting government-related entity status and strategic importance. CAF approved second phase financing up to $700M (June 2025) following initial $500M (July 2024), supporting EFE's 2024-2031 Strategic Investment Plan exceeding $5B. Company successfully placed first international bond in August 2020 ($500M at 3.068%) with same rating as Chilean sovereign. Benefits from essential transport infrastructure role and government support.

Overall Assessment: EFE represents improving credit recovering from pandemic-era stress. A-/A ratings appropriate given government backing and infrastructure investments. Suitable for investors seeking Chilean infrastructure exposure with sovereign-linked support. Positive momentum from strategic investment program and multilateral financing. Monitor execution of $5B+ capex program and ridership recovery trends.

issuers.name: EMPRESA NACIONAL DEL PET
issuers.creditComment:
EMPRESA NACIONAL DEL PETROLEO (ENAP) (USP37110AT33)
Credit Rating: S&P BBB- Stable (upgraded from BB+, May 2024), Fitch A- Stable (affirmed July 2025), Moody's Baa3 Stable (affirmed July 2024), National AAA(cl)

Credit Momentum: Very Positive - Investment Grade Achievement. ENAP achieved landmark upgrade to investment-grade BBB- by S&P (May 2024), becoming only state-owned Latin American oil & gas company with investment-grade rating. S&P upgraded both corporate rating and Stand Alone Credit Profile (SACP) from 'b' to 'b+', reflecting strengthened financial risk profile and solid strategic plan implementation (2023-2027). Fitch maintains superior A- rating with stable outlook (July 2025 affirmation). Company successfully issued $600M 5.95% 2034 notes (July 2024), recognized by LatinFinance as "Quasi-Sovereign Liability Management Deal of the Year" for refinancing execution. Debt reduced dramatically by ~$900M over two years with leverage declining from distressed levels to sustainable metrics. Q1 2024 showed profit of $408.2M with debt reduction of $297M. CEO emphasized management focus producing "profitable, efficient, financially sustainable state-owned company."

Overall Assessment: ENAP represents transformed Chilean oil/gas credit with exceptional upgrade trajectory. The BBB-/Baa3/A- ratings reflect: (1) successful turnaround from near-distressed to solid investment-grade; (2) Chilean government backing (100% state-owned) with strategic importance; (3) aggressive deleveraging execution; (4) disciplined capital allocation. SACP upgrade to 'b+' demonstrates standalone improvement independent of sovereign support. Suitable for investors seeking: (1) rare LatAm oil/gas investment-grade credit; (2) Chilean sovereign-linked exposure (A-rated); (3) ESG-conscious state enterprise with improving governance. Recent $600M placement demonstrates strong market access at attractive pricing. Key strengths include refined product exports, petrochemical operations, and international E&P (Sipetrol subsidiary). Risks: (1) energy transition threatening long-term oil demand; (2) Chilean political changes potentially impacting management/strategy; (3) commodity price volatility; (4) refining margin cycles. Monitor SACP trajectory - continued improvement could drive parent rating higher toward 'BBB' if leverage sustainably below 3.0x. Recommended overweight - exceptional credit story with positive momentum and Chilean stability.

issuers.name: ENBRIDGE INC
issuers.creditComment:
ENBRIDGE INC
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BBB/Stable (Fitch 2025) – North American Midstream Infrastructure Leader; $4.3B Q3 2025 Adjusted EBITDA; Stable DCF Model; 4.8x Debt-to-EBITDA

Credit Momentum: Stable – Enbridge Inc. 
 maintains solid investment-grade ratings (BBB/Baa2) with Stable outlooks from all three agencies, reflecting the company's critical midstream infrastructure position, stable cash flows from long-term contracts, and disciplined capital allocation. Q3 2025 results demonstrated stable operations with $4.3B adjusted EBITDA (+0.2% YoY) and $2.6B distributable cash flow. Management reaffirmed 2025 guidance for $19.4-20.0B adjusted EBITDA and $5.50-5.90 DCF per share. Leverage stands at 4.8x debt-to-EBITDA, within the company's target 4.5x-5.0x range.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Enbridge holds aligned ratings of BBB (S&P/Fitch) and Baa2 (Moody's), all with Stable outlooks. The ratings reflect the company's essential infrastructure role (transport ~30% of North American crude oil and ~20% of natural gas) and contracted revenue model. Leverage profile: Net Debt/EBITDA of 4.8x is appropriate for a large-cap midstream utility with stable, contracted cash flows.

Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
Enbridge reported stable Q3 2025 results (November 6, 2025): Adjusted EBITDA: $4.3 billion (+0.2% YoY to prior Q3). Distributable Cash Flow: $2.6 billion (flat vs. prior year). Adjusted EPS: $0.46 per share (-8.2% YoY). GAAP EPS: $0.30 (vs. $0.59 prior year, impacted by unrealized derivative losses). Guidance Reaffirmed: Full-year EBITDA $19.4-20.0B; DCF per share $5.50-5.90.

Strategic Initiatives & Full-Year Guidance:
Strategic priorities: (a) Liquids Pipelines optimization – Placing Texas Eastern Venice Extension in service; (b) Gas Transmission – Rate case settlements supporting revenue; (c) Growth capex – Targeted investment in high-return projects; (d) DCF sustainability – Maintaining distribution/dividend growth trajectory of ~3% (post-2026 guidance).

Competitive Position & Brand Portfolio:
Market position: North America's leading midstream operator; essential crude oil and natural gas transport infrastructure. The company's long-term take-or-pay contracts provide revenue stability across commodity cycles.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
Enbridge prioritizes distribution sustainability. The company pays a monthly distribution (~4.5% yield) supported by stable DCF. Capital is directed toward organic capex on high-return projects.

Overall Assessment: Enbridge Inc is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 7-9% Defensive Core Holding. A essential midstream utility with stable cash flows and durable competitive advantage. Strengths: (a) BBB/Baa2 stable ratings; (b) Essential infrastructure role (transport 30% of North American crude); (c) Stable DCF model ($5.50-5.90 per share 2025); (d) Long-term contracted revenues. Risks: (a) Energy transition pressures (long-term crude demand uncertainty); (b) Regulatory/permitting risks; (c) Leverage at 4.8x moderately elevated; (d) Distribution growth constrained to ~3%.

Monitor: (1) Distributable cash flow sustainability; (2) Leverage trajectory (maintaining 4.5x-5.0x); (3) Growth capex returns; (4) Energy transition impact on crude transport volumes.

issuers.name: ENEL CHILE SA
issuers.creditComment:
ENELCH 4.875 06/12/2028 – Enel Chile 4.25% 18/07/2029, BBB
Профиль бизнеса
Дочерняя структура глобальной Enel, объединяющая генерацию и сбыт электроэнергии в Чили, с растущей долей ВИЭ и сокращением угольной генерации.
Высокая доля регулируемых/контрактованных доходов и поддержка со стороны Enel как стратегического акционера.

Кредитные метрики
Левередж умеренный (2,5–3,0x net debt/EBITDA), рентабельность стабильная, FFO/interest комфортный за счёт масштабов и интеграции с группой.
​Ликвидность сильная, доступ к рынкам капитала и внутригрупповому фондированию.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды
Рейтинг BBB со стабильным outlook, лимитированный суверенным потолком Чили и регулированием.
​Бумага 2029 торгуется с YTM ~4,2%, dur 2,0, OAS ~60–80 bps – по сути “quasi‑sovereign” IG с очень tight спредом.

Ключевые риски
Регуляторные решения по тарифам и поддержка ВИЭ, а также декарбонизация (закрытие угольных блоков) могут временно давить на метрики.
Зависимость от политики и финансового профиля материнской Enel.

Инвестидия
Отличный короткий IG‑якорь в LatAm utilities: минимальный кредитный риск и низкая дюрация.
Но carry слабый; логичнее использовать как “парковку” ликвидности или хедж против более рискованных LatAm‑позиций.

issuers.name: ENEL FINANCE INTL NV
issuers.creditComment:
ENEL FINANCE INTERNATIONAL NV
Credit Rating: BBB/Positive (S&P Outlook Revised July 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch) – Financing Vehicle for Enel SpA; Global Renewable Leader; Deleveraging via Asset Disposals
​

Credit Momentum: Positive – Enel Finance International NV benefits from the guarantee of Enel SpA. S&P revised the outlook to Positive in July 2025, affirming the 'BBB' rating. This follows Enel's successful execution of its disposal plan to reduce debt and simplify the group structure. Moody's maintains a Baa1 rating with a stable outlook.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are driven by Enel's: (a) position as the world's largest private renewable player; (b) extensive regulated networks (40% of EBITDA); (c) geographical diversification (Europe, Latin America, US); (d) solid liquidity. The positive outlook reflects improving credit metrics (FFO/Net Debt trending >20%).
​

Financial Profile: Enel has reduced net debt significantly through asset sales (e.g., Peru, Romania) and focuses capex on regulated grids and renewables. The target is to maintain Net Debt/EBITDA below 2.5x. The financing vehicle actively issues green and sustainability-linked bonds.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Global Utilities 5-6% allocation. Improving credit story (BBB/Baa1) with upgrade potential. Monitor: (1) Execution of remaining asset disposals; (2) Regulatory returns in Italy/Spain; (3) Interest rate impact on debt costs; (4) Hydrology in Latin America.

issuers.name: ENEL GENERACION CHILE SA
issuers.creditComment:
ENELGX 7.325% 02/01/2037 – Enel Generación Chile, BBB+
Профиль бизнеса и цикличность
Крупнейший генератор в Чили с диверсифицированным портфелем гидро, газовой и возобновляемой генерации, практически полностью работающий по долгосрочным PPAs с дистрибьюторами и крупными клиентами.
Является стратегической “core”‑дочкой Enel Chile (Enel SpA), что обеспечивает доступ к централизованной ликвидности и рынкам капитала.

Кредитные метрики
Fitch и S&P ожидают net debt/EBITDA около 2,5–3,0x и FFO/debt >20% в 2025–2026 гг., что соответствует верхней части IG‑диапазона для генерации.
Ликвидность подкреплена участием в cash‑pool Enel Chile и доступом к крупным револьверным линиям; график погашений сглажен, без крупных стен.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды
Долгосрочные рейтинги – BBB+/BBB со стабильным прогнозом; standalone‑профиль даже сильнее родителя, но ограничен linkage и суверенным потолком Чили.
Бумага 2037 торгуется с YTM 6,8%, дюрацией 7,5 и OAS 250–300 bps – заметная премия к короткой Enel Chile и Colbún, что отражает более долгий срок и генерационный риск.

Ключевые риски для держателя бумаг
Регуляторные изменения и социальное давление на тарифы могут снижать маржу по PPAs и увеличивать риск невозврата инвестиций в ВИЭ.
Гидрологическая волатильность и риск задержек в запуске новых ВИЭ‑проектов могут временно ухудшать метрики.

Инвестидия (IG взгляд)
Сильный IG‑кредит с поддержкой глобального Enel и большим запасом по метрикам; длинная дюрация компенсируется заметным спредом к UST и к чилийскому суверену.
Аргумент “за”: качественный high‑beta IG‑плей на чилийскую энергетику/ВИЭ с более высоким carry, чем у сетевых компаний.
Аргумент “против”: высокая дюрация и регуляторный риск Чили; в стресс‑сценарии (тарифное вмешательство, климатические шоки) спрэды могут расшириться сильнее, чем у transmission‑имён.

issuers.name: ENGIE ENERGIA CHILE SA
issuers.creditComment:
ECLCI 6.375% 04/17/2034 – Engie Energía Chile, BBB
Профиль бизнеса и цикличность
Дочерняя структура глобальной ENGIE, один из крупнейших генераторов в Северной Чили с фокусом на газе и ВИЭ; значительная часть портфеля – PPAs с горнодобывающими и дистрибуционными компаниями.
Бизнес пережил период стрессов из‑за short‑position по PPAs и высоких цен spot, но компания ускоренно развивает ВИЭ и storage, чтобы закрыть разрыв.

Кредитные метрики
После ухудшения в 2021–2022 гг. S&P/Fitch видят восстановление: ожидают постепенного возврата метрик к уровню net debt/EBITDA ~3–3,5x и улучшения FFO/interest по мере ввода ВИЭ‑проектов.
Краткосрочный долг рефинансируется за счёт новых выпусков и поддержки Engie S.A.; ликвидность достаточна, но была под давлением, что и вызвало CreditWatch в 2023.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды
Рейтинг BBB (S&P), BBB‑/BBB у других, со стабильным или нейтральным прогнозом; SACP – ‘bb’, но итоговый рейтинг подтянут связью с материнской Engie (BBB+).
Твой выпуск 2034 даёт YTM ~5,4%, dur ~6,1, OAS ~125–165 bps – чуть выше Enel Chile, отражая более слабый standalone‑профиль.

Ключевые риски
Задержки по реализации ВИЭ‑портфеля или сохранение неблагоприятной позиции по досрочно проданным PPAs могут дольше давить на EBITDA и леверидж.
Регуляторные и климатические риски чилийской энергетики.

Инвестидия
Интересный IG‑эмитент с поддержкой глобального Engie и небольшим премиумом к Enel/Colbún; подойдёт как часть IG‑баскета Чили.
Но его стоит считать чуть более рискованным: по сравнению с Enel/Colbún здесь меньше запаса прочности, поэтому размер позиции логично делать меньше.

issuers.name: ENGIE SA
issuers.creditComment:
ENGIE SA
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Affirmed April 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's Affirmed May 2025) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch Affirmed July 2025) – Global Integrated Utility; Strong Gas & Renewables; Balanced Business Mix
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Engie SA 
 maintains strong investment-grade ratings. S&P affirmed its 'BBB+' rating in April 2025, and Moody's affirmed Baa1 in May 2025. The outlooks are stable, reflecting the group's resilient performance and balanced mix of regulated infrastructures, renewables, and flexible generation.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) large scale and diversification; (b) high share of contracted/regulated EBITDA (~55%); (c) leadership in gas infrastructure and renewables; (d) solid financial profile (FFO/Net Debt ~22%). The company is managing the exit from nuclear in Belgium, which has been de-risked through an agreement with the Belgian state.
​

Financial Profile: Engie targets a Net Economic Debt/EBITDA ratio of <4.0x. The company generates strong operating cash flow to fund its €22-25 billion capex plan (2024-2026), focused on renewables and energy solutions. Liquidity is robust.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within European Utilities 5-6% allocation. Stable core utility credit (BBB+/Baa1). Monitor: (1) Gas and power price volatility; (2) Belgian nuclear phase-out execution; (3) Renewables capacity ramp-up; (4) Regulatory changes in France/Brazil.

issuers.name: ENI SPA
issuers.creditComment:
ENI SPA
Credit Rating: A-/Positive (S&P Outlook Revised) | A3/Stable (Moody's Upgraded Nov 2025) | A-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed July 2025) – Italy's Integrated Energy Major; Upgraded by Moody's; Strong Upstream & Gas Business
​

Credit Momentum: Positive – Eni SpA 
 received a rating upgrade from Moody's to A3 (from Baa1) in November 2025, following a sovereign upgrade for Italy. S&P also revised its outlook to Positive (on 'A-'). This reflects Eni's resilient operating performance and strong balance sheet.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) top-tier upstream portfolio with low breakeven prices; (b) successful dual-exploration model; (c) growing low-carbon businesses (Plenitude, Enilive); (d) conservative financial policy. Eni is rated two notches above the Italian sovereign by Moody's, reflecting its global diversification.
​

Financial Profile: Eni maintains a fortress balance sheet with leverage (Net Debt/Equity) around 0.33x. The company generates robust free cash flow, covering capex and shareholder distributions even at lower oil prices. It continues to issue sustainability-linked bonds to fund its transition.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within European Integrated Oil & Gas 5-6% allocation. High-quality energy credit (A-/A3) with improving trajectory. Monitor: (1) Oil/Gas price environment; (2) Plenitude IPO/stake sale; (3) Italy sovereign risk; (4) Bio-refining growth execution.

issuers.name: ENTERPRISE PRODUCTS OPER
issuers.creditComment:
ENTERPRISE PRODUCTS OPER
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BBB/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Largest Midstream MLP; $7.4B Distributable Cash Flow (2024); 6% Distribution Yield; Integrated Energy Infrastructure

Credit Momentum: Stable – Enterprise Products Partners L.P. 
 maintains solid investment-grade ratings (BBB/Baa2) with Stable outlooks, reflecting the company's position as North America's largest midstream master limited partnership. The company generated $7.4 billion in distributable cash flow in 2024 and maintains a 6%+ distribution yield, anchored by long-term contracts, fee-based revenue, and integrated assets spanning crude oil, natural gas, and petrochemical logistics. S&P, Moody's, and Fitch affirm Stable outlooks, acknowledging disciplined capital allocation and leverage management.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Enterprise Products holds aligned ratings of BBB (S&P/Fitch) and Baa2 (Moody's), all with Stable outlooks. The company maintains conservative leverage estimated at 2.8x-3.2x Net Debt-to-EBITDA, appropriate for a large-cap, low-beta infrastructure MLP. Leverage profile: Strong DCF generation supports both the distribution yield and deleveraging capacity.

Financial Profile:
Enterprise Products operates one of North America's most diversified midstream portfolios: crude oil transport (pipelines, barges, terminals), natural gas infrastructure, petrochemical logistics, and processing. The company's fee-based model limits commodity exposure.

Strategic Initiatives:
Strategic priorities: (a) Petrochemical logistics expansion – Adding processing and storage capacity; (b) LNG export support – Investing in supporting infrastructure for US LNG exports (Golden Pass, Lake Charles); (c) Energy transition – Evaluating hydrogen and renewable feedstock opportunities; (d) Distributions – Committed to 2-3% annual distribution growth supported by capex programs.

Competitive Position:
Market position: Largest US midstream MLP; unrivaled integrated asset base. The company's scale and diversity create competitive advantages in contract negotiations and project economics.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
Enterprise Products prioritizes distributions to unitholders. The company pays a sustainable 6%+ yield supported by $7B+ annual DCF generation. Capex is carefully managed to support long-term infrastructure growth.

Overall Assessment: Enterprise Products Oper is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 8-10% Core MLP Holding. A premier midstream infrastructure company with unmatched scale and stability. Strengths: (a) BBB/Baa2 stable ratings (rare for MLPs); (b) $7.4B distributable cash flow providing 6%+ distribution yield; (c) Integrated midstream portfolio reducing commodity volatility; (d) Long-term contract base providing revenue stability. Risks: (a) Energy transition uncertainty (long-term hydrocarbon demand); (b) Regulatory/permitting risks; (c) Interest rate sensitivity (debt refinancing); (d) MLP taxation/regulatory changes.

Monitor: (1) Distributable cash flow sustainability; (2) Distribution growth pace; (3) Major project execution (Golden Pass, petrochemical expansion); (4) Capital spending efficiency.

issuers.name: EQT CORP
issuers.creditComment:
EQT CORP
Credit Rating: BBB-/Stable (S&P Affirmed March 2022) | Baa3/Stable (Moody's Affirmed Aug 2025, Outlook Revised to Stable from Negative) | BBB-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed March 2025) – Largest US Natural Gas Producer; Vertical Integration; Zero-Scope Methane
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Outlook Upgraded by Moody's) – EQT Corporation 
 has solidified its triple-investment-grade status. Moody's affirmed the 'Baa3' senior unsecured rating but revised the outlook to Stable (from Negative) in August 2025, reflecting improved cash flow prospects and progress toward the $7.5 billion year-end 2025 debt target (debt was $8.3 billion as of June 30, 2025). S&P affirmed the 'BBB-' rating in March 2022 and maintains a stable outlook. Fitch affirms the 'BBB-' IDR with a Stable outlook. The rating stability reflects EQT's scale as North America's largest natural gas producer with advantaged cost structure and vertical integration through the July 2024 acquisition of Equitrans Midstream Corporation.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) market-leading position as the largest independent US natural gas producer; (b) advantaged cost structure enabling breakeven below peer levels (can generate FCF at $2/mcf NYMEX pricing); (c) vertical integration (gathering, processing, transportation control); (d) zero-scope methane emissions (attracting LNG/data center buyers requiring IG counterparties); (e) strong liquidity (~$4 billion available as of June 2025). Constraints include high debt levels and distributions to midstream JV partner (Blackstone receives 60% of distributions), limiting deleveraging flexibility.
​

Financial Profile: EQT maintains strong operational performance and cash flow generation. Net debt was $7.8 billion as of June 30, 2025 (target $7.5 billion YE2025). The company generates substantial free cash flow even in lower natural gas price environments. Strong liquidity supports financial flexibility. The company is investing in capacity expansion projects (2.5 Bcf/day of new demand expected from basin growth initiatives) generating attractive returns.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within US Natural Gas E&P 5-6% allocation. Core IG gas credit (BBB-/Baa3) with vertical integration. Monitor: (1) Natural gas prices (key leverage driver); (2) Debt trajectory toward $5 billion long-term target; (3) Midstream distribution obligations (Blackstone arrangement); (4) Expansion project ROIC realization.

issuers.name: EQUINOR ASA
issuers.creditComment:
EQUINOR ASA
Credit Rating: Aa2/Stable (Moody's) | AA-/Stable (S&P) – Norway's Largest Energy Company; Q3 2025 Adjusted Operating Income $6.21B; Production 2,130 MBOE/D (+7% YoY); Cash Flow from Ops $5.33B; $5B Share Buyback 2025; Strong Norwegian State Support (67% Ownership)​

Credit Momentum: Strong with Operational Excellence – Equinor ASA maintains premier investment-grade ratings reflecting global integrated energy leadership, Norwegian continental shelf dominance, and strong state ownership support, despite lower commodity price environment impacting reported results.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Equinor maintains Aa2/Stable from Moody's and AA-/Stable from S&P. Ratings reflect: (a) Norwegian state ownership (67%) providing implicit support; (b) dominant position on Norwegian Continental Shelf (NCS); (c) diversified integrated energy operations across Exploration & Production, Marketing/Midstream, and Renewables; (d) strong balance sheet and liquidity; (e) conservative financial policy; (f) leading position in European gas supply.​

Q3 2025 Financial Results: Q3 2025 adjusted operating income $6.21B, post-tax income $1.51B. Net operating income $5.27B (down from $6.91B YoY). Adjusted EPS $0.37 (vs. $0.79 YoY). Production 2,130 MBOE/D (+7% YoY), driven by Johan Sverdrup and Johan Castberg. Cash from operations after taxes $5.33B. Results impacted by net impairments $754M from updated price outlook. Renewables segment loss narrowed to $64M (from $115M YoY) with 50% cost reduction achieved.​

Balance Sheet & Capital Distribution: $5B share buyback program for 2025. Quarterly dividend $0.37/share. Strong balance sheet maintained through lower price environment. 2025 guidance reaffirmed: 4% YoY production growth, $13B organic capex. Strategic developments: Bacalhau field first oil (October 2025), Ørsted rights issue participation.​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within European integrated energy 6-8% allocation. Premier investment-grade energy credit (Aa2/AA-) with Norwegian state support, strong NCS position, and energy transition investments. Monitor: (1) Q4 2025 production guidance delivery; (2) Gas price environment; (3) Renewables segment cost trajectory; (4) Impairment reversal potential; (5) Capital distribution sustainability.

issuers.name: EXPAND ENERGY CORP
issuers.creditComment:
EXPAND ENERGY CORP
Credit Rating: BBB-/Stable (S&P Upgraded Oct 2024) | BBB-/Stable (Fitch Upgraded Oct 2024) | Baa3/Stable (Moody's Upgraded April 2025) – Largest Independent US Natural Gas Producer; Merger Integration; Strong Hedges
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Triple-Rated IG; Added to S&P 500) – Expand Energy Corporation 
 has achieved a major milestone as the newest company with triple-investment-grade ratings across all three agencies. S&P and Fitch upgraded the company to 'BBB-' in October 2024 (2 notches from BB+), following the October 1, 2024 completion of Chesapeake Energy's merger with Southwestern Energy. Moody's upgraded to 'Baa3' (from Ba1) in April 2025. The company was also added to the S&P 500 Index in March 2025. The upgrades reflect successful debt reduction post-merger, substantial hedge position, and benefits from consolidation of Appalachian and Haynesville production (largest independent US natural gas producer by volume).
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) largest independent US natural gas producer with diversified asset base across leading supply basins; (b) successful merger integration with Southwestern Energy, achieving synergies; (c) strong hedge position (~significant portion of 2025-26 natural gas production hedged via favorable priced contracts reducing volatility); (d) committed debt reduction toward $4.5 billion net debt target (from elevated post-merger levels); (e) zero-scope methane emissions positioning (attracting LNG/data center customers requiring IG suppliers). Leverage expected <1.0x net debt to EBITDAX at mid-cycle. Rating could be upgraded to Baa2 if sustained LFCR approaches 2.5x and RCF to debt exceeds 60%.
​

Financial Profile: Expand demonstrated strong Q3 2025 results with revenue up 15% YoY to $123.4 million and net income of $21.4 million. As of December 31, 2024, the company had $317 million cash (excluding $78m restricted) and $2.5 billion undrawn RCF. In September 2025, Expand amended and extended its credit facility, increasing commitments from $2.5 billion to $3.5 billion with maturity extended to 2030. The company is actively managing debt (repaid $389m of 2025 Notes matured in Jan 2025, redeemed $225m in senior notes via open market repurchases in current period). No near-term debt maturities until RCF maturity in Dec 2027.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within US Natural Gas E&P 5-6% allocation. "Rising Star" IG credit (BBB-/Baa3) with synergy upside. Monitor: (1) Merger synergy realization and integration progress; (2) Natural gas pricing and hedge effectiveness; (3) Debt reduction pace toward $4.5B target; (4) Potential upgrade timing to Baa2/BBB from Moody's; (5) Free cash flow allocation.

issuers.name: EXPEDIA GROUP INC
issuers.creditComment:
EXPEDIA GROUP INC
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch Upgraded/Affirmed 2025; S&P Upgraded Feb 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's per 10-Q) – Online Travel Leader; B2B Growth; Deleveraging Success
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgraded to solid BBB) – Expedia Group Inc [finance:Expedia Group Inc] has solidified its investment-grade status. Fitch upgraded the Long-Term IDR to 'BBB' (from BBB-) with a Stable outlook on January 17, 2025, and assigned a 'BBB' rating to new senior unsecured notes in February. S&P assigned/upgraded to 'BBB' in early 2025. Moody's rates the company 'Baa2' with a Stable outlook as of September 30, 2025. The upgrades reflect successful deleveraging, strong B2B segment growth, and margin expansion.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) position as a top-tier global online travel agency (OTA) with diverse brands (Expedia, Hotels.com, Vrbo); (b) rapid growth in B2B business providing higher-margin revenue; (c) disciplined financial policy targeting leverage ~2.0x; (d) successful technology platform unification. Risks include high competition (Booking Holdings, Google), potential regulatory headwinds, and sensitivity to consumer travel spending cycles.
​

Financial Profile: Expedia has materially improved its credit metrics. Leverage has declined to the low-2x area. The company redeemed $1 billion of 6.25% notes early in Feb 2025 and refinanced with lower-cost debt. Free cash flow generation is robust, supporting share buybacks while maintaining investment-grade leverage. Liquidity is strong with ~$4.5 billion in cash and undrawn revolver capacity.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Consumer Discretionary/Services 4-5% allocation. "Rising Star" cementing mid-BBB status. Monitor: (1) B2B revenue growth trajectory; (2) Vrbo market share vs. Airbnb; (3) Capital returns pacing vs. leverage targets; (4) Regulatory developments in digital markets (DMA in EU).

issuers.name: EXPERIAN FINANCE PLC
issuers.creditComment:
EXPERIAN FINANCE PLC
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (S&P Affirmed Oct 2025) | A3/Stable (Moody's) – Global Data Giant; Strong Margins; Resilient Cash Flow
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Experian Finance plc maintains strong investment-grade ratings. S&P rates the company 'A-' with a stable outlook (confirmed in Experian's October 2025 investor presentation). Moody's rates it A3 (Stable). The stable outlook reflects the company's leading market position, robust free cash flow, and disciplined capital allocation.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are underpinned by: (a) Experian's status as a top global credit bureau ("Big Three"); (b) high barriers to entry due to vast proprietary data; (c) diversification across geographies (North America, UK, Latin America) and B2B/Consumer segments; (d) exceptional liquidity and strong margins. Management is committed to maintaining a leverage ratio (Net Debt/EBITDA) of 2.0x-2.5x.
​

Financial Profile: Experian generates strong operating cash flows, which fund organic growth, bolt-on acquisitions, and shareholder returns. The company expects organic revenue growth of 6-8% in FY25. Leverage remains within the target range, supporting the 'A-/A3' rating level. The company has a robust liquidity position with undrawn credit facilities.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Global Business Services 5-6% allocation. High-quality defensive credit (A-/A3) with strong moat. Monitor: (1) Regulatory changes in data privacy; (2) Cybersecurity risks; (3) Economic cycle impact on credit volumes; (4) M&A integration.

issuers.name: EXXON MOBIL CORPORATION
issuers.creditComment:
EXXON MOBIL CORPORATION
Credit Rating: AA-/Stable (S&P Affirmed Sept 2025) | Aa2/Stable (Moody's Affirmed June 2024; maintained 2025) – Energy Supermajor; Fortress Balance Sheet; Pioneer Integration
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Exxon Mobil Corporation [finance:Exxon Mobil Corporation] maintains its elite high-grade status. S&P affirmed the 'AA-' rating with a Stable outlook on September 18, 2025. Moody's maintains the 'Aa2' rating with a Stable outlook. The company successfully integrated Pioneer Natural Resources (acquired May 2024), significantly boosting its Permian Basin production and inventory while preserving its pristine balance sheet.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) massive global scale and diversification across Upstream, Product Solutions (Downstream/Chemicals), and Low Carbon Solutions; (b) dominant position in the Permian Basin post-Pioneer acquisition (>1.3 MOEBD); (c) industry-leading breakeven costs; (d) extraordinarily strong balance sheet (net debt ratio often <5-10%); (e) counter-cyclical resilience. Risks include long-term energy transition and litigation risks, but the credit profile is among the strongest in the corporate universe.
​
