Financial Profile: ExxonMobil generates massive operating cash flow, easily funding capital expenditures (guided ~$20-$25B/year) and substantial shareholder returns (dividends + buybacks). The company issued floating rate notes due 2075 in late 2025, demonstrating unique access to ultra-long duration capital. Liquidity is exceptional with massive cash balances and credit facilities. Leverage metrics remain very low despite the large Pioneer acquisition.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within Global Energy 6-7% allocation. "Gold Standard" Energy credit (AA-/Aa2). Monitor: (1) Permian production optimization (Pioneer synergies); (2) Low Carbon Solutions project execution (lithium/CCS); (3) Refining margin volatility; (4) Litigation developments (climate suits).

issuers.name: FEDEX FREIGHT HOLDING CO
issuers.creditComment:
FedEx (FDX) сейчас – классический mid‑IG транспортный кредит (Baa2/BBB) с умеренным левериджем, хорошей генерацией FCF и ощутимым, но управляемым давлением на маржу; риск ближе к “среднему” в IG‑универсуме, но с цикличным и операционно сложным профилем.

Профиль бизнеса и цикличность
FedEx – глобальный интегратор экспресс‑доставки, наземной логистики и LTL, с выручкой около 87,9 млрд долл. в 2025 ф.г.; ключевые сегменты – Express, Ground и Freight.
Спрос чувствителен к мировой торговле, e‑commerce и промышленной активности: после пика ковида компания пережила несколько кварталов давления на объёмы и mix, но во 2 кв. 2025 г. выручка снова превысила 23 млрд долл. – впервые за три года.
Программа DRIVE/Network 2.0 нацелена на структурные сокращения затрат >4 млрд долл. к 2025 ф.г., включая оптимизацию авиасети, консолидацию наземных операций и сокращение персонала.

Кредитные метрики
В 2025 ф.г. FedEx сгенерировал операционный денежный поток 7,0 млрд долл. и capex 4,1 млрд долл., что дало FCF около 3,0 млрд долл. (FCF‑маржа ~3,4%).
Net debt после учёта кэша оценивается примерно в 17–18 млрд долл., что соответствует net debt/EBITDA около 2,0–2,5x, умеренно выше, чем у сильнейших IG‑транспортников, но в пределах комфортного диапазона для Baa2.
При этом компания активно возвращает капитал акционерам (байбэки ~3 млрд долл., дивиденды ~1,3 млрд долл. за 2025 г.), что замедляет deleveraging, но FCF покрывает эти выплаты.

Рейтинг, PD и спрэды
Moody’s в октябре 2025 г. подтвердила рейтинг FedEx на уровне Baa2 со Stable outlook, отмечая устойчивую FCF‑генерацию и реализацию программы DRIVE, но указывая на давление со стороны конкуренции и высоких затрат.
По модели martini.ai, FedEx имеет буквенный рейтинг B3 (по их шкале) и PD около 0,27% (топ‑79‑й перцентиль по качеству), а также спред около 2,5%; за последние 12 месяцев кредитный спред сузился примерно на 28%, что отражает улучшение настроений.
​PD немного вырос с минимума 0,15% в 2024 г. до 0,27% к концу 2025 г. на фоне роста издержек и опасений по марже, но остаётся в зоне “умеренного” кредитного риска.
​
Ключевые риски для кредитора
Операционная сложность и цикличность. Многослойная сеть (авиа, наземка, LTL) делает компанию чувствительной к снижению объёмов: при падении отгрузок фиксированные затраты быстро давят на прибыль и FCF.
Инфляция затрат. Рост зарплат, топлива и аэропортовых сборов сдерживает маржинальный эффект от DRIVE; если макро ухудшится, FedEx придётся пожертвовать частью ценовой власти для поддержания объёмов.
Акционерная политика. Агрессивные байбэки/дивиденды конкурируют с deleveraging’ом; при стресс‑сценарии менеджмент должен будет быстро переключиться на защиту рейтинга, но такого разворота ещё не тестировалось в текущем цикле.
Структурный риск спин‑оффа Freight. Выделение LTL‑бизнеса уменьшит диверсификацию на уровне FedEx Corp и может слегка изменить профиль кэша и капитальных затрат; регуляторы и рейтинговые агентства ещё оценивают итоговый эффект.

issuers.name: FERGUSON ENTERPRISES INC
issuers.creditComment:
FERGUSON ENTERPRISES INC
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Affirmed Oct 2023; maintained 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's implied/estimated) – Construction Distribution Leader; US Domicile; Resilient Cash Flow
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Ferguson Enterprises Inc [finance:Ferguson Enterprises Inc] (formerly Ferguson plc) maintains a strong investment-grade profile. S&P affirmed the 'BBB+' rating with a Stable outlook (note: PLC rating withdrawn due to US re-domicile, new Inc rating established at same level). Moody's typically rates the credit in the 'Baa1' area. The company completed its re-domicile to the US (August 2024) and entered the Fortune 500 in 2025, reflecting its dominant position in the North American construction distribution market.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) leading market position as the largest value-added distributor of plumbing and HVAC products in North America; (b) highly fragmented market offering consolidation runway; (c) diversified end-markets (Residential vs. Non-Res; Repair & Remodel vs. New Construction); (d) counter-cyclical cash flow (working capital release in downturns); (e) solid balance sheet with leverage target 1.0x-2.0x. Risks include sensitivity to US housing and construction cycles.
​

Financial Profile: Ferguson maintains a conservative financial profile. Net debt/EBITDA is managed within the 1x-2x range. The company generates strong free cash flow, which funds bolt-on M&A and dividends/buybacks. The re-domicile to the US aligns its corporate structure with its operational footprint (100% North America), simplifying governance and potentially enhancing index inclusion/capital access.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Industrials/Distributors 4-5% allocation. High-quality distributor (BBB+) with defensive R&R exposure. Monitor: (1) US Residential R&R spending trends; (2) Non-residential construction activity; (3) M&A integration pace; (4) Operating margin stability.

issuers.name: FIEMEX ENERGIA - BANC AC
issuers.creditComment:
FIEMEX ENERGIA – BANC AC
“Fiemex Energia – Banc AC” corresponds to Fiemex (Fideicomiso de Inversión en Energía México), a Mexican project bond backed by a portfolio of 12 combined‑cycle plants and one wind farm selling mainly to CFE. S&P and Fitch rate the senior secured notes BBB and BBB‑, respectively, both with Stable outlooks. Portfolio DSCRs average about 2.4x with a minimum around 1.27x in 2025, a strong cushion for BBB‑level risk, though exposure to CFE (Baa2/Negative) and Mexican policy remains the key constraint.​

issuers.name: FLOWSERVE CORPORATION
issuers.creditComment:
FLOWSERVE CORPORATION
Credit Rating: BBB-/Positive (Fitch Affirmed July 2025 with Positive Outlook) | Baa3/Stable (Moody's Affirmed June 2025) | BBB-/Stable (S&P revised to Stable from Positive Dec 2024) – Fluid Motion Control; Diversified Industrial End-Markets; Recurring Aftermarket
​

Credit Momentum: Mixed (Fitch Positive; S&P Stable) – Flowserve Corporation 
 maintains a solid IG credit profile with mixed momentum signals. Fitch affirms the 'BBB-' IDR with a Positive outlook on July 30, 2025, signaling potential for upgrade if execution on transformation initiatives continues. Moody's affirmed the 'Baa3' LT local currency rating with a Stable outlook on June 4, 2025. S&P affirmed the 'BBB-' rating but revised the outlook to Stable (from Positive) in December 2024 due to Chart Industries merger termination in June 2025 (Flowserve had proposed to acquire Chart Industries but transaction terminated). The company is refocusing on organic initiatives and improving operational execution.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) market leadership in fluid motion control (pumps, seals, valves) serving diversified end-markets; (b) high-recurring revenue from installed equipment base and aftermarket/MRO services (40-45% of sales); (c) diversified geographic and customer exposure (major oil & gas, petrochemicals, chemicals, power, utilities); (d) margin improvement initiatives through cost reductions and operational efficiency; (e) solid free cash flow generation. Fitch's positive outlook reflects confidence in transformation progress; S&P's stable outlook reflects post-merger termination repositioning. Challenges include capital intensity and end-market cyclicality (energy sector concentration ~40%).
​

Financial Profile: Flowserve maintains moderate leverage and adequate liquidity through strong operating cash flow. The termination of the Chart Industries acquisition in June 2025 does not materially change the credit profile. The company is investing in cost structure optimization and margin improvement initiatives to drive EBITDA growth. Free cash flow supports debt management and modest capital returns. The company has completed multi-year realignment initiatives, improving operational flexibility and cost efficiency.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Industrial Equipment 4-5% allocation. Solid IG credit (BBB-/Baa3) with transformation upside. Monitor: (1) Margin expansion progress from cost initiatives; (2) Energy sector capital spend cycles; (3) Fitch upgrade path toward BBB; (4) Free cash flow and debt reduction; (5) Operational leverage on volume growth.

issuers.name: FOMENTO ECONOMICO MEX
issuers.creditComment:
FOMENTO ECONOMICO MEXICANO S.A.B. DE C.V. (FEMSA)
Credit Rating: A2/Stable (Moody's, Q2 2025) | A-/Stable (S&P, Q2 2025) – Latin America’s largest beverage/retail conglomerate, owner of OXXO, Coca-Cola bottling, point-of-sale technology, and logistics businesses. Q2 2025 showed steady growth, moderate leverage, and strong capital allocation discipline.

Credit Momentum: Stable – Strong local market position, adequate liquidity, robust profitability, and proven management support high-grade rating.

Credit Ratings & Stability: A2/Stable (Moody’s), A-/Stable (S&P). Rating factors: (a) diversified retail/beverage footprint, (b) proven execution, (c) healthy leverage and solid FCF, (d) regional leadership and innovation.

Financial Profile & Production: Consistent mid/high single-digit revenue growth, margin improvement, debt/EBITDA below 2x, investments in logistics, fintech, and retail expansion (OXXO).

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within LatAm consumer/retail 6-8% allocation. Solid high-grade Mexican market leader with sustained performance and conservative balance sheet.

issuers.name: FONDO MIVIVIENDA SA
issuers.creditComment:
FONDO MIVIVIENDA SA
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Nov 2025) | BBB/Stable (S&P Affirmed) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's) – Peruvian State-Owned Development Bank; Strategic Role in Social Housing; Sovereign Guarantee Proxy
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Fondo Mivivienda S.A. (FMV) is a government-related entity (GRE) in Peru. Fitch affirmed its 'BBB' rating with a stable outlook in November 2025, equalizing it with the Peruvian sovereign rating. S&P also rates it 'BBB' (following the sovereign downgrade in previous years), and Moody's rates it Baa1.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) 100% ownership by the Peruvian state (Fonafe); (b) critical public policy role in reducing the housing deficit; (c) "high" probability of extraordinary government support; (d) strong capitalization. The rating is effectively a proxy for Peru's sovereign risk due to the explicit support framework and strategic importance.
​

Financial Profile: FMV is well-capitalized with a total funding to tangible equity ratio of ~2.3x. It acts as a second-floor bank, channelling funds through financial intermediaries, which minimizes direct credit risk. The issuance of social bonds (e.g., $600m in 2022) has diversified its funding sources. Profitability is stable but modest, driven by its social mandate.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Latin American Financials 4-5% allocation. Quasi-sovereign credit (BBB/Baa1) offering spread pickup over the sovereign. Monitor: (1) Peru sovereign rating trajectory; (2) Political stability; (3) Housing policy changes; (4) Asset quality of intermediary banks.

issuers.name: FREEPORT INDONESIA PT
issuers.creditComment:
PT FREEPORT INDONESIA (FREEPORT-MCMORAN INC.)
Credit Rating: BBB-/Stable (S&P May 2024) – Grasberg Copper-Gold Mine Operator; Freeport-McMoRan Parent Upgraded BBB-; 51.2% MIND ID Ownership; ~1.7B lbs Copper, ~1.8M oz Gold Production 2024; 1x Consolidated Leverage; IUPK to 2031 (Extension to 2041 Contingent); Low-Cost Premier Asset

Credit Momentum: Positive/Upgraded – PT Freeport Indonesia operates through parent Freeport-McMoRan 
, which was upgraded by S&P to BBB-/Stable (May 2024) as leverage fell to approximately 1x and Grasberg underground ramp-up boosted earnings. PTFI (51.2% owned by Indonesia's MIND ID state entity; 48.8% by Freeport-McMoRan) operates the Grasberg copper-gold complex in Papua—one of world's largest and lowest-cost operations. Independent credit analysis highlights clean capital structure, strong FX hedging, low leverage, and meaningful state linkage as key credit strengths, though concentrated single-asset and Indonesian jurisdiction exposure create structural risks:

Credit Ratings & Asset Quality:
Freeport-McMoRan parent upgraded by S&P to BBB-/Stable (May 2024). S&P May 2024 upgrade rationale: Raised to BBB-/Stable reflecting leverage declining to approximately 1x on Grasberg underground production ramp and strong copper/gold prices. Asset profile: Grasberg represents one of world's largest and lowest-cost copper-gold operations with substantial reserve base and long reserve life underpinning decades of cash generation. State linkage: 51.2% MIND ID (Indonesia state) ownership provides implicit government support and strategic importance. Capital structure: PTFI maintains clean capital structure with low leverage, strong FX hedging, and meaningful liquidity. Consolidated leverage: S&P expects Freeport-McMoRan consolidated leverage to remain approximately 1x even on proportionate PTFI consolidation, supporting investment-grade status.​

Underground Transition & Production Profile:
Grasberg underground ramp-up transforming production: Production growth: Copper output ramping from 0.6 billion pounds (2019) to approximately 1.7 billion pounds (2024); Gold production nearly doubling to approximately 1.8 million ounces. Underground transition: Essentially complete with underground block caves replacing open-pit operations, materially lifting profitability and extending reserve life. Cost position: Among world's lowest-cost copper-gold producers from large scale, grade advantages, and operational efficiency. Reserve life: Substantial proven and probable reserves supporting multi-decade production profile. 2024 guidance: Production targets consistent with ramp-up trajectory supporting strong cash generation.​

Jurisdiction & Regulatory Risks:
PTFI faces concentrated structural risks: Single-asset concentration: All production from Grasberg complex in Papua creating concentration risk; No geographic diversification unlike parent Freeport-McMoRan. Indonesian jurisdiction: Heavy regulatory and political exposure including permit renewal, ownership structure negotiations, and potential future divestment pressure. IUPK permit: Current mining permit (IUPK) extends to 2031; Extension to 2041 contingent on completion of Gresik smelter and further government negotiations creating renewal risk. Ownership evolution: State majority ownership (51.2% MIND ID) following 2018 divestment from Freeport-McMoRan creates governance complexity. Capex mandates: Government requirements for domestic smelting capacity and other infrastructure creating ongoing capital obligations. ESG concerns: Riverine tailings disposal creating environmental controversy and potential regulatory risk.​

Overall Assessment: PT Freeport Indonesia (Freeport-McMoRan) is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within copper/gold mining 6-7% EM commodities allocation. PTFI/Freeport represents investment-grade copper-gold exposure (BBB-/Stable) with premier low-cost Grasberg asset, state backing, and strong production growth trajectory, though significant single-asset and Indonesian jurisdiction risks require careful monitoring and position sizing. Strengths include: (a) S&P BBB-/Stable upgrade (May 2024) reflecting deleveraging to ~1x; (b) Grasberg among world's largest, lowest-cost copper-gold operations; (c) production ramping to ~1.7B lbs copper, ~1.8M oz gold from underground transition; (d) substantial reserve base supporting multi-decade production; (e) 51.2% MIND ID state ownership providing implicit government support; (f) clean capital structure with low leverage and strong FX hedging; (g) copper structural demand from electrification and AI infrastructure; (h) gold diversification reducing commodity concentration. Risks include: (a) single-asset concentration in Grasberg with no geographic diversification; (b) Indonesian jurisdiction creating regulatory, political, and ownership risks; (c) IUPK permit renewal uncertainty (current to 2031; extension to 2041 contingent); (d) potential future state divestment pressure on Freeport ownership; (e) ESG risks from riverine tailings disposal; (f) capex mandates for smelters and infrastructure; (g) copper/gold price volatility. RECOMMENDED: Overweight premier copper-gold asset with state backing and structural leverage improvement, accepting single-asset and Indonesian jurisdiction risks for high-quality, low-cost production profile.

Monitor: (1) IUPK permit (Renewal negotiations and extension to 2041); (2) Production ramp (1.7B lbs copper, 1.8M oz gold trajectory); (3) Leverage maintenance (~1x consolidated target); (4) Copper/gold prices (Commodity price realization); (5) Government relations (MIND ID ownership dynamics and regulatory environment).

issuers.name: FREEPORT-MCMORAN INC
issuers.creditComment:
FREEPORT-MCMORAN INC
Credit Rating: BBB-/Stable (S&P Affirmed April 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's Upgraded 2024, per search results) | BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed March 2025) – Leading Copper & Gold Producer; Leveraged to Copper Cycle; Strong FCF
​

Credit Momentum: Stable to Positive (Fitch Affirmed; S&P Stable; Moody's Stable) – Freeport-McMoRan Inc. 
 maintains a solid investment-grade credit profile. S&P affirmed the 'BBB-' Foreign Currency LT credit rating with a Stable outlook on April 28, 2025. Fitch affirms the 'BBB' rating with a Stable outlook as of March 2025 (upgraded in March 2024 from BB+). Moody's upgraded to 'Baa2' with a Stable outlook (per search result reference to upgrade). The company is a leading global producer of copper and gold with competitive cost positioning. Fitch expects FCX's consolidated EBITDA leverage to remain below 1.8x through 2028 based on copper price forecasts of $9/lb average.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) world-class copper production assets with cost-competitive operations; (b) leading cost profile among global copper producers; (c) strong FCF generation in elevated copper price environment; (d) diversified revenue from copper, gold, and molybdenum; (e) improving leverage metrics (sub-1.8x expected through 2028). Constraints include significant copper price exposure (cyclical commodity pricing) and leverage volatility during downturns. Moody's upgrade to Baa2 reflects improved leverage and financial flexibility.
​

Financial Profile: Freeport-McMoRan maintains strong liquidity and cash generation. The company has demonstrated ability to reduce debt rapidly in favorable copper price environments. Capex is disciplined and focused on maintaining cost competitiveness and production capacity. The company is well-positioned in the copper cycle given strong demand from electrification, renewables, and energy transition. Leverage metrics are expected to remain supportive through the forecast period with current copper price assumptions.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Global Metals & Mining 5-6% allocation. Core IG mining credit (BBB-/Baa2) leveraged to copper demand. Monitor: (1) Copper price environment (key leverage driver); (2) Global mine production and supply/demand balance; (3) EBITDA leverage path and debt reduction; (4) Energy transition copper demand tailwinds; (5) Operating cost trends.

issuers.name: FRESNILLO PLC
issuers.creditComment:
FRESNILLO PLC
Credit Rating: Baa2/Stable (Moody's Outlook Revised October 2025 from Negative) | BBB/Negative (S&P) – World's Largest Silver Producer; Production ~55M oz Ag, ~400K oz Au Annually; EBIT Margin >20%; Leverage <1.0x; $850M Notes Due 2050; Probe Gold Acquisition C$560M​

Credit Momentum: Improving with Outlook Revision – Fresnillo plc received a Moody's outlook revision to stable from negative (October 2025) reflecting easing Mexico regulatory pressures, strong credit metrics from high precious metals prices, and improved liquidity.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Fresnillo maintains Baa2/Stable from Moody's (outlook revised October 2025) and BBB/Negative from S&P (August 2024). Moody's October 2025 outlook revision cited: (a) easing pressures in Mexico's operating and regulatory environment; (b) strong credit metrics supported by high gold and silver prices; (c) solid liquidity position; (d) Probe Gold acquisition providing geographic diversification into Canada.​

Production & Operational Profile: World's largest silver producer with substantial gold production. 9M 2025 production on track for full-year guidance. San Julián DOB mining ceased (profitability marginal), zinc concentrate production ended Q3 2025. Gold and silver ore grades for 2025: 1.1-1.3 g/t Au and 130-150 g/t Ag. Strong liquidity: $691M cash (June 2024) + $350M RCF (5-year tenor). Debt: $850M senior unsecured notes due 2050.​

Probe Gold Acquisition: C$560M acquisition of Canada's Probe Gold Inc. announced October 2025. Novador project expected to produce 255K oz Au annually with 12+ year mine life and $1,038/oz AISC. Accretive to geographic diversification (reducing Mexico concentration) and provides access to stable Canadian regulatory environment. Construction phase won't contribute EBITDA until post-2029.​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within global precious metals mining 4-5% allocation. Solid investment-grade silver/gold credit (Baa2/BBB) with improved outlook trajectory, strong leverage metrics (<1.0x), and diversification benefits from Probe acquisition. Monitor: (1) Mexico regulatory environment stability; (2) Gold and silver price environment; (3) Production guidance delivery; (4) Probe Gold integration and construction execution; (5) S&P outlook stabilization.

issuers.name: GARTNER INC
issuers.creditComment:
GARTNER INC
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Oct 2025; Assigned to proposed senior unsecured notes Nov 2025) | BBB-/Stable (S&P Assigned Nov 2025 to notes) – Leading Research & Advisory Provider; High-Recurring Revenue; Pricing Power; AI/Tech Demand
​
Credit Momentum: Stable – Gartner, Inc. 
 maintains a solid investment-grade profile. Fitch affirms the 'BBB' Long-Term IDR with a Stable outlook as of October 3, 2025, with 'BBB' assigned to a new credit facility. S&P assigned the 'BBB-' issue-level rating to Gartner's proposed senior unsecured notes on November 13, 2025 (new financing). Gartner is a leading research and advisory provider with high-recurring SaaS-like revenue model from enterprise customers dependent on research, advisory, and access to proprietary data and insights in technology and business topics.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) market-leading position in research and advisory services with strong competitive moats; (b) high-recurring revenue model (>85% subscription/SaaS-like revenues with multi-year contracts); (c) strong customer switching costs and pricing power; (d) diversified customer base across enterprise IT, finance, HR, marketing, supply chain; (e) expanding TAM in areas like cybersecurity, AI, cloud, digital transformation. The BBB/BBB- ratings reflect solid business quality and financial performance, with Gartner expected to maintain stable EBITDA margins (mid-40s% range).
​
Financial Profile: Gartner maintains strong liquidity and profitability. The company generates solid free cash flow supporting debt service and strategic capital returns. The recent financing (proposed senior unsecured notes in Nov 2025) is likely for refinancing or strategic investment. Leverage metrics are expected to remain manageable with strong EBITDA and FCF generation supporting financial flexibility. The company invests in R&D and platform expansion to maintain competitive positioning and address AI/technology research demand.
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Enterprise Research/Data 4-5% allocation. Core IG credit (BBB/BBB-) with high-recurring revenue. Monitor: (1) Subscription revenue growth and churn; (2) Pricing power and margin expansion; (3) AI/emerging technology advisory attach rates; (4) Leverage management through cycle; (5) M&A strategy and integration.

issuers.name: GC TREASURY CENTRE CO
issuers.creditComment:
GC TREASURY CENTER CO LTD
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch) | Baa3/Stable (Moody's) | BBB-/Stable (S&P) – Treasury Vehicle for PTT Global Chemical; Guaranteed by Parent; Deleveraging Focus
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – GC Treasury Center Company Limited acts as the treasury vehicle for PTT Global Chemical Public Company Limited (GC), Thailand's largest integrated petrochemical company. Ratings are guaranteed by the parent. In September 2025, GC Treasury Center successfully issued US$1.1 billion in subordinated perpetual debentures to fund deleveraging.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect the parent's: (a) leading market position in petrochemicals; (b) strong support from PTT Group; (c) current elevated leverage (Debt/EBITDA ~6.0x in 2025), which constrains the rating to the low investment-grade tier. S&P affirmed the 'BBB-' rating in 2025 but noted deteriorated credit metrics.
​

Financial Activity: The recent $1.1B perpetual issuance (rated Ba2/BB) is part of a strategy to strengthen the balance sheet and manage hybrid equity credit. Proceeds are used to repurchase existing debt. Deleveraging remains a key priority to defend the investment-grade rating.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Asian Chemicals 5-6% allocation. Investment-grade credit (BBB/Baa3) under pressure from industry downcycle. Monitor: (1) Petrochemical spread recovery; (2) Deleveraging progress (target Net Debt/EBITDA <4x); (3) Strategic support from PTT PCL.

issuers.name: GCC SAB DE CV
issuers.creditComment:
GCC SAB DE CV
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Dec 2025) | BBB-/Stable (S&P Affirmed Oct 2025) – Leading Cement Player in Mexico/US; Solid Financial Profile
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – GCC, S.A.B. de C.V. 
 maintains a solid investment-grade profile. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB' rating with a stable outlook in December 2025. S&P affirmed the 'BBB-' rating with a stable outlook in October 2025. This reflects the company's strong market position in its core markets (Chihuahua, Mexico, and the U.S. "Mid-America" region) and robust financial health.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) cross-border diversification with significant USD revenues (~70% from the US); (b) vertical integration in cement, concrete, and aggregates; (c) leading market shares in land-locked markets providing pricing power; (d) very low leverage. Fitch projects revenue to remain flat in 2025 before growing in 2026-2027.
​

Financial Profile: GCC has a very strong balance sheet with Net Debt/EBITDA historically below 1.0x (often close to zero or net cash). The company generates solid free cash flow, which funds dividends and share buybacks while maintaining ample liquidity. Its debt profile is well-termed with no significant near-term maturities.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Latin American Materials 4-5% allocation. Defensive cement credit (BBB/BBB-) with strong USD cash flow. Monitor: (1) US construction activity (infrastructure/residential); (2) Energy costs (fuel/electricity); (3) Pricing power retention; (4) M&A discipline.

issuers.name: GENERAL ELECTRIC CO
issuers.creditComment:
GENERAL ELECTRIC CO
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (S&P Upgraded March 2025) | A3/Stable (Moody’s) – Global aerospace leader via GE Aerospace (jets, aftermarket, engines); significant backlog, >$30B revenue. Upgrade March 2025 on improved leverage (1.6-1.7x), durable commercial aviation demand, and strong cash flow.

Credit Momentum: Improving – Post-spinoff focused business, operating margins expanding, asset mix stabilized, predictable FCF.

Credit Ratings & Stability: A-/Stable (S&P), A3/Stable (Moody’s). Key factors: (a) dominant market share in aviation/jet engines; (b) recurring aftermarket revenue; (c) moderate leverage and robust liquidity.

Financial Profile & Production: Margin expansion in Aviation; FCF outpacing capex/dividends; investment in new engine platforms.

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within industrials/aerospace 8-10% allocation. Well-managed transformation and strong credit profile. Monitor: (1) Aerospace cycle demand; (2) aftersales revenue mix; (3) R&D capex discipline.

issuers.name: GENERAL MOTORS CO
issuers.creditComment:
GENERAL MOTORS CO
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Assigned/Affirmed May 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's Upgraded) | BBB/Stable (Fitch Assigned May 2025) – Auto OEM; EV Transition; Improving Margins
​

Credit Momentum: Positive to Stable – General Motors Co [finance:General Motors Co] has solidified its "mid-BBB" investment-grade status. Fitch assigned a 'BBB' rating to new senior unsecured notes on May 5, 2025. S&P likewise assigned a 'BBB' issue-level rating in May 2025. Moody's upgraded GM's senior unsecured notes to 'Baa2' (from Baa3) in recent actions. Morningstar DBRS confirmed the Issuer Rating in June 2025. The ratings reflect improved North American profitability, disciplined capital allocation, and progress in the EV transition despite industry headwinds.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) strong and profitable core internal combustion engine (ICE) truck/SUV franchise; (b) "winning with simplicity" strategy reducing complexity and fixed costs; (c) robust liquidity (~$30B+ total automotive liquidity); (d) captive finance arm (GM Financial) rated investment grade. Risks include the costly and uncertain EV transition, intense competition (especially from Chinese OEMs globally), and cyclical demand. Tariff risks (potential new US tariffs) are noted as a headwind for 2025 EBIT but manageable.
​

Financial Profile: GM's automotive balance sheet is strong, with net cash often positive. The company has aggressively bought back shares ($10B ASR executed previously) while maintaining IG metrics. Adjusted automotive leverage is low. GM Financial (the captive) is profitable and well-capitalized. The company faces tariff headwinds estimated at $3.5-$4.5 billion in 2025 but plans to offset a significant portion via efficiencies.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Autos 3-4% allocation. Solid BBB/Baa2 credit with strong liquidity. Monitor: (1) EV profitability/losses trajectory (Ultium ramp); (2) North American truck/SUV pricing and demand; (3) Cruise (autonomous) cash burn/regulatory status; (4) Tariff impacts and mitigation.

issuers.name: GERDAU SA
issuers.creditComment:
GERDAU S.A.
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Oct 2024) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's Oct 2024) | BBB/Stable (Fitch Jul 2025) – Leading Americas Steel Producer; R$2.6B Q2 2025 EBITDA (+6.6% QoQ); 61% North America EBITDA Contribution; 2.8M Tonnes Shipments; 0.85 tCO2e/t Steel (Record Low Emissions); <1.5x Net Debt/EBITDA Target

Credit Momentum: Stable – Gerdau 
 maintains solid investment-grade ratings (BBB/Baa2) reflecting diversified Americas steel operations, record North America EBITDA contribution (61%), and conservative financial policy. Q2 2025 delivered 14% QoQ net income growth to R$864 million despite record Brazil import penetration (26%), with North American operations benefiting from robust volumes and metal spread expansion following Section 232 tariff redefinition. Strategic investments (Miguel Burnier mining 72% complete, Midlothian expansion 51% complete) support long-term growth:

Credit Ratings & Financial Policy:
Gerdau maintains BBB/Stable ratings from S&P and Fitch, with Baa2/Stable from Moody's—all investment grade. Moody's upgrade: Upgraded to Baa2 from Baa3 reflecting improved credit profile and geographic diversification. Financial policy targets: Gross debt below R$12 billion; Net debt/EBITDA at or below 1.5x; Average debt term greater than 6 years. Q2 2025 debt activity: Issued US$650 million 10-year bonds (T+140bp) and R$1.4 billion 17-year debentures (CDI+0.65%), extending debt profile while bolstering cash reserves; Average debt term 7.2 years; Average cost BRL: CDI+0.49%, USD: 5.6%. National scale ratings brAAA from both S&P and Fitch.​

Q2 2025 Financial Performance:
Gerdau reported improved Q2 2025 results (August 2025): Adjusted EBITDA: R$2.6 billion (+6.6% QoQ) driven by North America strength. Net income: R$864 million (+14% QoQ from R$758 million Q1); EPS R$0.43 (vs R$0.37 Q1). Steel shipments: 2.8 million tonnes (+2.8% QoQ). Regional mix: North America contributed all-time high 61% of consolidated EBITDA (vs 48% Q1)—the standout performer benefiting from robust volumes, metal spread expansion, and decreased imports following Section 232 tariff redefinition. Dividends: Declared R$240 million (R$0.12/share) payable August 18, 2025. Share buyback: 68% program completion with R$686 million invested to repurchase 2.2% of outstanding shares.​

Regional Performance Dynamics:
Gerdau's geographic diversification proved effective: North America: Record margin expansion from robust volumes, metal spread expansion, and decreased imports; Section 232 tariff redefinition helping rebalance supply; All-time high EBITDA contribution (61%). Brazil: Significant challenges from record steel import penetration (26.0%, +3.9pp YoY)—highest since historical tracking began; Pressure on domestic pricing and margins; Company highlighting urgent need for enhanced trade defense measures including emergency safeguards and expedited anti-dumping action. South America (ex-Brazil): EBITDA margin declined to 11.2% (vs 13.8% Q1 and 16.2% Q2 2024) from weak demand despite slight volume upturn. Management expressed concerns that despite existing measures (quota + 25% tariff), Brazil imports continue at record pace requiring additional trade protection.​

Strategic Projects & Sustainability:
Gerdau advancing key strategic initiatives: Miguel Burnier Mining: 72% physical completion, on track for Q4 2025 start-up; Sustainable mining operation in pre-operational planning phase. Midlothian Expansion (North America): 51% physical progress, 59% financial progress; Phase 1 scheduled for H2 2025 operations start. Hot-Rolled Coil Expansion: Completed and commenced operations Q1 2025. Sustainability: Carbon emissions 0.85 tCO2e/tonne of steel—lowest level in company history; Workplace safety frequency rate 0.71, continuing long-term improvement trend. Management guidance indicates global capex reduction starting 2026 with focus on North American investments, and EBITDA growth expected from mining and HRC projects in 2026-2027.​

Overall Assessment: Gerdau S.A. is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within materials/steel 6-7% LatAm allocation. Gerdau represents solid investment-grade credit (BBB/Baa2) with diversified Americas steel operations, record North America performance, and conservative financial policy. Strengths include: (a) investment-grade ratings from all three agencies (BBB/Baa2) reflecting strong credit profile; (b) North America EBITDA contribution at all-time high 61%, providing diversification from Brazil challenges; (c) Q2 2025 net income +14% QoQ demonstrating operational resilience; (d) conservative leverage (<1.5x net debt/EBITDA target) with 7.2-year average debt term; (e) Section 232 tariff benefits supporting North America margins and competitiveness; (f) strategic project execution (Miguel Burnier Q4 2025, Midlothian H2 2025); (g) ESG leadership (0.85 tCO2e/t record low emissions, 0.71 safety rate); (h) active shareholder returns (R$240M dividend, 68% buyback completion); (i) US$650M 10-year bond issuance demonstrating capital markets access; (j) EBITDA growth expected 2026-2027 from mining and HRC projects. Risks include: (a) Brazil record import penetration (26%) pressuring domestic pricing and margins; (b) steel industry cyclicality affecting demand and pricing across regions; (c) South America ex-Brazil margin decline (11.2% vs 16.2% prior year) from weak demand; (d) potential US tariffs on Brazil steel affecting export competitiveness; (e) Iron ore price volatility affecting input costs; (f) BRL/USD currency volatility affecting translated results; (g) Brazil economic uncertainty impacting domestic steel demand; (h) trade policy unpredictability affecting regional competitiveness. RECOMMENDED: Overweight diversified Americas steelmaker with North America strength, conservative leverage, and ESG leadership offsetting Brazil import challenges.

Monitor: (1) North America performance (61%+ EBITDA contribution and margin expansion sustainability); (2) Brazil import dynamics (26% penetration trajectory and trade protection measures); (3) Strategic project execution (Miguel Burnier Q4 2025 and Midlothian H2 2025 start-ups); (4) Leverage maintenance (<1.5x net debt/EBITDA and debt reduction progress); (5) US trade policy (Section 232 tariff impacts and potential Brazil-specific measures).

issuers.name: GLAXOSMITHKLINE CAP INC
issuers.creditComment:
GLAXOSMITHKLINE CAPITAL INC (GSK)
Credit Rating: A2/Stable (Moody’s) | A/Stable (S&P) – UK-based global pharma company, strong vaccine and consumer health presence, reliable cash flow, and diversified product pipeline.

Credit Momentum: Stable – Outperformed sector on post-pandemic recovery, innovation, and cost controls; maintains moderate leverage.

Credit Ratings & Stability: A2/Stable (Moody’s), A/Stable (S&P). Considerations: (a) breadth in vaccines/consumer health; (b) successful pipeline launches; (c) stable margin profile; (d) consistent shareholder returns.

Financial Profile & Production: Margin resilience amid competitive pressures, prudent capital allocation, moderate capex.

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within global pharma 5-6% allocation. Maintain watch on U.S. regulatory, product cycle, M&A integration.

issuers.name: GLENCORE FUNDING LLC
issuers.creditComment:
GLENCORE FUNDING LLC
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P May 2025) | A3/Stable (Moody's 2025) – Diversified Commodities Producer; $5.4B H1 EBITDA (down 14%); $14.5B Net Debt; 1.08x Net Debt/EBITDA; Metals/Coal/Energy; ~$4B FCF Target; Shareholder Distributions

Credit Momentum: Stable – Glencore Funding LLC 
 maintains solid investment-grade ratings (BBB+/A3) reflecting diversified mining, energy, and marketing operations, strong liquidity position, and disciplined capital allocation. H1 2025 showed net debt at $14.5B ($14.0B ex-Viterra sale proceeds), representing 1.08x net debt/EBITDA (1.0x post-Viterra) with substantial financial headroom. The company targets meaningful deleveraging in H2 2025 from FCF generation (~$4B annualized at spot prices) and working capital unwind:

Credit Ratings & Financial Position:
Glencore maintains BBB+/Stable from S&P (affirmed May 2025) and A3/Stable from Moody's. S&P assessment: BBB+ reflects substantial financial headroom with comfortable leverage, adequate liquidity, and diversified commodity exposure. H1 2025 performance: Net debt $14.5B (up from $11.2B year-end 2024) primarily from $3.2B capex, $1.8B shareholder returns, and $1.1B working capital investment; EBITDA down 14% to $5.4B from weaker coal pricing and lower copper volumes. Leverage metrics: 1.08x net debt/EBITDA (1.0x ex-Viterra proceeds), providing significant rating headroom with BBB+ typically comfortable in 1.5-2.0x range. FCF guidance: Targets ~$4B annualized at spot prices reflecting healthy cash generation capacity.​

Operational Performance & Outlook:
Glencore demonstrated resilience amid challenging environment: Adjusted EBITDA: $5.4B H1 2025 reflecting: Metals operations 24% margins, steelmaking coal 35% margins, energy coal 18% margins. Marketing business: $1.4B EBITDA down 8% YoY despite US tariff uncertainty and Middle East tensions. Cash flow: FFO $3.2B H1 (down 22% from lower EBITDA); Operating cash generation strong supporting capex and shareholder distributions. H2 2025 outlook: Management expects "healthy cash flow generation and deleveraging" from: (a) 40/60 copper guidance split (H1 40%, H2 60%); (b) working capital unwind; (c) cost savings progress; (d) strength in marketing operations. Available liquidity: $12.6B committed liquidity with bond maturities capped ~$3B annually providing financial flexibility.​

Diversified Commodity Portfolio:
Glencore's operations span multiple commodities and regions: Metals: Copper, zinc, nickel, cobalt, molybdenum operations worldwide; 24% margins in metals. Coal: Steelmaking (35% margins) and energy coal (18% margins) with operational challenges in H1 2025. Energy: Oil/gas operations and power generation assets. Marketing: Global commodity trading and distribution business. Geographic reach: Operations across major producing regions worldwide. Diversification benefit: Commodity exposure spread reduces concentration risk on single commodity cycles.​

Overall Assessment: Glencore Funding LLC is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within diversified mining 6-7% commodities allocation. Glencore represents solid investment-grade credit (BBB+/A3) with diversified commodity operations, strong liquidity, and disciplined capital allocation. Strengths include: (a) BBB+/A3 ratings reflecting solid credit quality; (b) 1.08x net debt/EBITDA providing comfortable headroom; (c) diversified commodity exposure (metals, coal, energy, marketing); (d) strong marketing business providing profit stability; (e) $12.6B available liquidity; (f) expected deleveraging in H2 2025 from FCF generation; (g) capped ~$3B annual debt maturities; (h) shareholder return commitment demonstrating confidence. Risks include: (a) commodity price volatility affecting EBITDA and cash generation; (b) coal phase-out pressures from ESG transition; (c) working capital intensity creating leverage volatility; (d) emerging market exposure creating political/regulatory risks; (e) energy prices affecting operations; (f) leverage elevation if commodity cycle deteriorates. RECOMMENDED: Overweight diversified miner with solid ratings, strong liquidity, and expected deleveraging trajectory supporting credit stability.

Monitor: (1) Commodity prices (Copper, coal, nickel price realization); (2) Leverage trajectory (<1.0x net debt/EBITDA maintenance and deleveraging); (3) FCF generation (~$4B target achievement); (4) Coal operations (Phase-out pressures and profitability); (5) Viterra integration (Divestiture proceeds deployment and shareholder returns).

issuers.name: GOLD FIELDS / WINDFALL MINING
issuers.creditComment:
GOLD FIELDS / WINDFALL MINING
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P & Moody’s reaffirmed 2025 for Gold Fields Ltd.) – South Africa’s largest gold producer, expanding in Canada via Windfall project; targeting >3Moz output in 2028.

Credit Momentum: Stable – Sustained free cash flow, low leverage, industry-leading ESG scores, and healthy margins back rating.

Credit Ratings & Stability: BBB+/Stable (S&P/Moody’s). Core strengths: (a) quality assets across SA and Canada; (b) countercyclical investment; (c) robust balance sheet.

Financial Profile & Production: Strong earnings beat Q2 2025; capex on Windfall funded internally; healthy cash reserves.

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within global gold mining 4-5% allocation. Monitor commodity, asset ramp-up, sustaining capex, ESG.

issuers.name: GREAT WALL INTL
issuers.creditComment:
GREAT WALL INTERNATIONAL V LTD
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Reinstated June 2025) – Offshore Funding Vehicle for China Great Wall AMC; Guaranteed by Great Wall AMC (International); 2.875% Notes Due 2026; Strategic Importance in NPL Resolution
​

Credit Momentum: Stabilizing – China Great Wall International Holdings V Limited issues bonds guaranteed by China Great Wall AMC (International) Holdings, the offshore arm of China Great Wall Asset Management (one of the "Big Four" AMCs). S&P reinstated the BBB rating with a stable outlook in June 2025 following a period of delayed financial reporting which had caused a withdrawal.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: BBB/Stable (S&P). Ratings reflect: (a) Very high likelihood of government support for the parent AMC; (b) Keepwell deed and Deed of Equity Interest Purchase Undertaking from the ultimate parent; (c) Guarantee from the offshore platform. The rating is notched down from the sovereign/parent due to structural subordination, though support remains strong. Outstanding bond: US$300M 2.875% Guaranteed Notes due 2026.
​

Bond Details (2.875% Due 2026): Issued by Great Wall International Holdings V Limited. Guaranteed by China Great Wall AMC (International) Holdings. The structure relies on the credit strength of the central AMC parent flowing down to the offshore issuer. Proceeds used for refinancing and working capital.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within China Financials/AMCs 4-5% allocation. Investment-grade credit (BBB) with recovery in transparency after reporting delays. Monitor: (1) Timeliness of financial reporting; (2) Asset quality and impairment levels; (3) Government support for NPL managers; (4) Liquidity management.

issuers.name: GRUMA SAB DE CV
issuers.creditComment:
GRUMA SAB DE CV
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (Fitch Affirmed August 2025) | BBB+/Stable (S&P Upgraded 2025) – World's Largest Corn Flour & Tortilla Producer; LTM Sales ~$6.5B; LTM EBITDA ~$1.15B; Net Debt/EBITDA 1.2x; Strong Liquidity; Global Footprint Across 75 Plants​

Credit Momentum: Strong with Recent Upgrade – Gruma, S.A.B. de C.V. received an S&P upgrade to BBB+ in 2025 reflecting consistent strong operating performance and disciplined balance sheet management.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Gruma maintains BBB+/Stable from Fitch (affirmed August 2025) and BBB+/Stable from S&P (upgraded 2025). Fitch's August 2025 affirmation cited: (a) strong business position as world's leading tortilla and corn flour producer; (b) growth opportunities in US Hispanic community and mainstream tortilla popularity; (c) healthy liquidity ($353M cash, $122M short-term debt at June 2025); (d) consistent operational execution; (e) prudent financial management.​

Q3 2025 & Financial Profile: LTM sales ~$6.5B, LTM EBITDA ~$1.15B. Net debt ~$1.5B. Net debt/EBITDA 1.2x (down from 1.7x in 2022). Average debt cost 6.13%. Debt maturity profile: manageable with $800M notes due 2028+ as largest component. 68% of debt USD-denominated (backed by US operations). Strong shareholder returns: YTD 2025 ~$243M in buybacks (3.9% of shares).​

Business Segments: GIMSA (Mexico corn flour): 46% sales, 65% EBITDA. Gruma USA (tortillas): 28% sales, 17% EBITDA. Gruma Europe: 8% sales, 5% EBITDA. Mission Foods growing brand awareness driving improved volumes and profitability. Additional presence in Asia/Oceania (~$276M sales) and Central America (~$395M sales).​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within global consumer staples 4-5% allocation. Strong investment-grade food credit (BBB+) with dominant market position, conservative leverage, and strong cash generation. Monitor: (1) US tortilla market growth and brand penetration; (2) GIMSA Mexico performance; (3) Europe profitability improvement initiatives; (4) Corn price environment; (5) Geographic expansion in Asia.

issuers.name: GRUPO BIMBO SAB DE CV
issuers.creditComment:
GRUPO BIMBO SAB DE CV (USP4949BAP96)
Credit Rating: S&P BBB+ Negative (revised Mar 2025), Moody's Baa1 Stable (upgraded from Baa2, May 2023), Fitch BBB+ Stable (affirmed Aug 2025), National AAA(mex)

Credit Momentum: Stable with S&P caution. Latin America's largest baked goods producer with operations across Americas demonstrates solid fundamentals but divergent rating views. S&P revised outlook to negative (March 2025) while Fitch/Moody's maintain stable, creating rating tension. Moody's Baa1 reflects improved operating results with leverage dramatically compressed to 2.0x debt/EBITDA from 4.0x following 2017 East Balt acquisition, and EBITDA/interest coverage widening to 5.0x from 3.9x. Company benefits from global scale, very strong brand portfolio (Bimbo, Marinela, Takis, Sara Lee, Thomas', Entenmann's), and extensive distribution network. However, Moody's cautioned elevated capex in recent years resulted in negative FCF, though expected positive from 2025 onward as projects complete. High portfolio concentration in baked foods/snacks with lower margins versus other consumer products creates vulnerability to rising commodity costs. Geographic diversification across Americas (strong Mexico, U.S., LatAm presence) provides resilience.

Overall Assessment: Grupo Bimbo represents quality LatAm consumer staples credit with defensive characteristics. BBB+/Baa1 ratings appropriate for leading branded food producer with conservative 2.0x leverage. S&P negative outlook creates uncertainty but likely reflects growth investment cycle concerns rather than fundamental deterioration. Suitable for investment-grade LatAm consumer portfolios seeking established brands with pricing power. Fitch projects 5.5% annual revenue growth through 2027 supporting debt capacity. National AAA(mex) rating confirms highest domestic quality. Recommended hold - strong fundamentals offset by S&P caution warranting monitoring but not avoiding.

issuers.name: GRUPO ENERGIA BOGOTA
issuers.creditComment:
GEBCB 7.85% 11/09/2033 – Grupo Energía Bogotá, BBB
Профиль бизнеса и цикличность
Мульти‑utility холдинг, контролируемый городом Богота: магистральные газопроводы и электро‑сети в Колумбии и Перу, а также доли в распределительных и генерационных компаниях.
Большая часть cash flow – регулируемые тарифы transmission и стабильные дивиденды от дочерних обществ, что снижает цикличность.

Кредитные метрики
Fitch оценивает бизнес‑профиль как крепкий, при EBITDA‑левередже около 3,0–3,5x и адекватной ликвидности (cash ~0,5–0,6 млрд долл., стабильный OCF).
Однако агрессивная дивидендная политика и планы роста (M&A/ capex) ограничивают пространство для deleveraging и были причиной более волатильного PD в 2022–2025.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды
Fitch в 2025 г. подтвердила рейтинг BBB со негативным outlook, что отражает негативный outlook по городу‑акционеру Боготе и риски усиления дивидендного давления.
Облигация 2033 торгуется с YTM ~5,6%, dur ~5,7, OAS ~150–185 bps – небольшая премия к чилийским IG‑сетям за страновой и governance‑риск.

Ключевые риски
Зависимость от дивидендов дочерних компаний и от финансового состояния самого города Богота как мажоритарного владельца.
Политический риск и потенциальные изменения в регулировании газовых/электросетевых тарифов в Колумбии и Перу.

Инвестидия
Добротный IG‑эмитент с диверсифицированным regulated‑профилем и приличным спредом; хорош как carry внутри IG‑корзины.
Негативный outlook и зависимость от муниципального акционера требуют мониторинга; бумаги разумно держать с ограниченным весом, а не как крупнейший якорь.

issuers.name: GUARA NORTE SARL
issuers.creditComment:
GUARA NORTE SARL
Guara Norte S.à r.l., a special purpose vehicle owning the FPSO Cidade de Ilhabela (operating offshore Brazil), is rated Ba1 by Moody’s and BBB– by Fitch (affirmed November 2025), both with Stable outlooks. The ratings reflect the strong contractual structure with Petrobras (Ba1/BB) but are constrained by the single-asset nature and Brazilian sovereign risk. The senior secured notes amortize fully over the charter period.​

issuers.name: HARBOUR ENERGY PLC
issuers.creditComment:
HARBOUR ENERGY PLC
Credit Rating: BBB-/Stable (S&P Upgraded Sep 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's) | BBB-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed March 2025) – Largest UK Oil & Gas Producer; M&A Driven Growth; Solid Deleveraging
​
Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgraded) – Harbour Energy plc achieved a rating upgrade from S&P to 'BBB-' (from BB) in September 2025, solidifying its investment-grade status across all three agencies. Moody's rates it Baa2, and Fitch affirms 'BBB-'. The upgrades reflect the company's increased scale, diversification following the Wintershall Dea acquisition, and conservative financial policy.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) significantly enhanced scale (~473 kboepd production); (b) diversified portfolio beyond the UK North Sea (Norway, Mexico, Indonesia); (c) strong free cash flow generation; (d) low leverage (<1.0x). The acquisition of Wintershall Dea's non-Russian assets was a transformative credit positive event.
​
Financial Profile: Harbour Energy has a robust balance sheet with Net Debt reduced to $4.2 billion (pro forma) and significant liquidity. The company targets keeping leverage below 1.5x through the cycle. It actively manages its debt maturity profile, having pre-funded maturities out to 2028.
​
Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within European E&P 5-6% allocation. Improving credit story (Baa2/BBB-) with M&A execution upside. Monitor: (1) Integration of Wintershall Dea assets; (2) UK windfall tax (EPL) changes; (3) Oil/Gas price volatility; (4) Capex discipline.

issuers.name: HASBRO INC
issuers.creditComment:
HASBRO INC
Credit Rating: BBB (S&P 2025) | Baa2/Negative (Moody's 2025) | BBB-/Positive (Fitch 2025) – Global Play & Entertainment Leader; $1.4B Q3 2025 Revenue; $1.68 Adj. EPS; #1 Tabletop Gaming (Magic: The Gathering); ~25.6% Adj. Op Margin

Credit Momentum: Improving – Hasbro Inc 
 credit trajectory is turning positive, evidenced by Fitch revising its outlook to Positive in November 2025. This reflects the successful execution of its "turnaround" strategy, driven by the exceptional performance of Wizards of the Coast and Digital Gaming (+40% growth in Magic: The Gathering YTD), which offsets softness in the traditional consumer products segment. Q3 2025 results showed solid execution with 8% revenue growth and disciplined cost management, putting the company on track to achieve ~$1.25B adjusted EBITDA for the full year.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Hasbro is rated BBB by S&P, Baa2 by Moody's, and BBB- by Fitch. Divergence exists in outlooks: Fitch is Positive (citing deleveraging progress), S&P is Stable (following improved profitability), while Moody's maintains a Negative outlook (assigned May 2025) reflecting concerns over consumer discretionary spending, though recent performance may support stabilization. Leverage profile: Debt-to-EBITDA is trending toward the mid-2x range (Fitch expects ~2.9x by YE 2025), supported by strong free cash flow and debt repayment efforts ($120M repaid YTD).

Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
Hasbro delivered strong Q3 2025 results (October 23, 2025): Revenue: $1.4 billion (+8% YoY), beating consensus. Profitability: Adjusted Operating Profit $356 million (+8% YoY); Adjusted Operating Margin 25.6% (flat YoY). Segment Highlights: Wizards of the Coast & Digital Gaming revenue surged, driven by the "Magic: The Gathering" franchise; Consumer Products remained soft (-9% YTD) due to retailer caution and category weakness, partially offset by licensing strength. EPS: Adjusted EPS $1.68 vs $1.73 prior year (impacted by tax rate/FX).

Strategic Initiatives & Full-Year Guidance:
Hasbro raised its FY 2025 guidance: Revenue growth: High-single-digit (up from mid-single); Adjusted EBITDA: ~$1.25 billion. Strategic priorities: (a) "Blueprint 2.0" focusing on fewer, bigger, more profitable brands (Magic, D&D, Peppa Pig, Transformers); (b) Cost savings target of $750M (gross) by YE 2025 largely achieved; (c) Digital gaming expansion through internal studios and licensing partnerships (e.g., Monopoly Go!).

Competitive Position & Brand Portfolio:
Market position: A global leader in toys and games. Key Brands: Magic: The Gathering (premier hobby game), Dungeons & Dragons, Monopoly, Nerf, Play-Doh, Transformers, Peppa Pig. The portfolio is bifurcated into high-margin digital/hobby gaming (Wizards) and traditional toys (Consumer Products), with Wizards becoming the primary profit engine (approx. 85% of operating profit).

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
Hasbro prioritizes deleveraging and dividends. The company pays an annual dividend of ~$390 million (currently yielding ~4.5%). Share repurchases remain paused to prioritize debt reduction until leverage stabilizes comfortably below the 2.5x-3.0x target range.

Overall Assessment: Hasbro Inc is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 5-6% allocation. Hasbro represents a compelling "sum-of-the-parts" credit story where a high-growth, high-margin digital gaming business (Wizards) supports an investment-grade profile despite a cyclical traditional toy business. Strengths: (a) Wizards of the Coast remains a cash cow with >40% operating margins; (b) Strong liquidity and declining leverage (approaching <3.0x); (c) Rich IP library monetized through high-margin licensing. Risks: (a) Continued erosion in traditional toy sales; (b) Execution risk in video game development; (c) Consumer discretionary pullback.

Monitor: (1) Wizards of the Coast revenue sustainability; (2) Consumer Products segment stabilization; (3) Deleveraging pace toward 2.5x target.

issuers.name: HCA INC
issuers.creditComment:
HCA INC
Credit Rating: BB+/Stable (S&P 2025) | Ba1/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BB+/Stable (Fitch 2025) – US Healthcare Operator; $19.16B Q3 2025 Revenue; Adjusted EBITDA +$450M Upside; State Supplemental Programs Driving Growth

Credit Momentum: Positive – HCA Healthcare Inc. 
 delivered exceptional Q3 2025 results that exceed expectations and prompted significant full-year guidance raises. The healthcare operator reported $19.16B revenue (+9.6% YoY), Adjusted EBITDA $3.87B (+18.4% YoY), and Diluted EPS $6.96 (+42% YoY), beating consensus by 21.68%. The company raised FY2025 guidance significantly, including +$450M midpoint increase in Adjusted EBITDA guidance (split: $250M from state supplemental payment programs, $200M from operational outperformance). S&P, Moody's, and Fitch maintain Stable outlooks, acknowledging strong operational leverage and favorable healthcare industry dynamics.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
HCA holds aligned sub-investment-grade ratings of BB+ (S&P), Ba1 (Moody's), and BB+ (Fitch), all with Stable outlooks. The ratings reflect strong operational execution and steady leverage management. Leverage profile: The company maintains moderate leverage in the 2.8x-3.2x estimated Net Debt/EBITDA range, appropriate for healthcare operators with stable cash generation.

Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
HCA reported outstanding Q3 2025 results (October 24, 2025): Revenue: $19.16 billion (+9.6% YoY; beat consensus $18.56B). Adjusted EBITDA: $3.87 billion (+18.4% YoY). Diluted EPS: $6.96 (+42% YoY; beat consensus $5.72 by 21.68%). Adjusted EBITDA Margin: +140bps from strong labor/supply management and state supplemental programs (+$240M net benefit Q3 vs. prior year). Volume Growth: +2-3% same-facility volumes. Interest Margin: Strong pricing discipline maintained.

Strategic Initiatives & FY2025 Guidance Raised:
Updated FY 2025 Guidance: Revenue $75-76.5B; Net Income $6.50-6.72B; Adjusted EBITDA $15.25-15.65B (+$450M midpoint raise). Diluted EPS $27-28. Capital spending ~$5.0B. State Supplemental Program Benefits: $250-350M net favorable (vs. prior $200-250M). Strategic priorities: (a) Digital transformation – AI/advanced digital tools for operational efficiency; (b) Labor management – Contract labor stable (4.2% of total Q3); (c) Volume growth – Sustaining 2-3% same-facility growth; (d) Margin expansion – Operating leverage from strong pricing.

Competitive Position & Brand Portfolio:
Market position: US's largest for-profit healthcare operator; 186 hospitals across 21 states. Network spans acute care, oncology, cardiovascular specialties.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
HCA maintains disciplined capex (~$5B annually) while returning capital to shareholders through dividends and buybacks. Debt-to-Adjusted EBITDA leverage maintained in lower-half of guidance range.

Overall Assessment: HCA Inc is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 6-8% Core Healthcare Holding. A best-in-class healthcare operator with strong operational execution and favorable sector tailwinds. Strengths: (a) BB+/Ba1 stable ratings; (b) Exceptional Q3 execution (EPS beat 21.68%, revenue beat 9.6%); (c) FY2025 guidance +$450M midpoint (operational + supplemental programs); (d) Volume growth +2-3% sustained; (e) Margin expansion +140bps from operational leverage; (f) Strong liquidity and balanced capital allocation. Risks: (a) Sub-investment-grade rating reflects healthcare cyclicality; (b) Regulatory/payment policy changes (Medicare/Medicaid pressure); (c) Labor cost inflation (wage pressures); (d) Hurricane disruptions to operations (estimated $50M Q3 2024 impact).

Monitor: (1) Same-facility volume growth maintenance (2-3% target); (2) Adjusted EBITDA margin expansion trajectory; (3) State supplemental program funding certainty; (4) Capital allocation discipline; (5) Credit rating upgrade potential toward BBB-.

issuers.name: HEINEKEN NV
issuers.creditComment:
HEINEKEN NV
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P 2025) | A3/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch 2025) – #2 Global Brewer; €8.7B Q3 2025 Revenue; Premium Portfolio (Heineken, Amstel, Birra Moretti); ~15.5% Operating Margin target

Credit Momentum: Stable – Heineken N.V. 
 maintains a solid investment-grade profile (BBB+/A3) despite a "challenging" Q3 2025 characterized by softer volumes (-4.3% organic). The credit story rests on its global diversification, pricing power (+3.3% price/mix), and disciplined cost management (€0.5B savings target). While volumes face headwinds from consumer weakness in Europe and the Americas, the "Green Diamond" strategy continues to drive premiumization and digital efficiency, supporting stable credit metrics.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Heineken holds BBB+ (S&P), A3 (Moody's), and BBB+ (Fitch) ratings with Stable outlooks (affirmed S&P April 2025). Ratings reflect its position as the world's second-largest brewer, broad geographic footprint (strong in Europe, Mexico, Vietnam, Nigeria), and conservative financial policy. Leverage profile: Net Debt/EBITDA (beia) is expected to remain within the 2.5x-3.0x range for 2025, consistent with the target to stay below 2.5x long-term, although temporarily elevated due to softer EBITDA growth.

Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
Heineken reported a mixed Q3 2025 Trading Update (October 22, 2025): Revenue: €8.7 billion; Organic Revenue Growth: -0.3% (Pricing +3.3% offset by Volume -4.3%). Volume Trends: Beer volume declined 4.3% organically, underperforming expectations due to poor weather in Europe and macro headwinds in the Americas. Premium Beer: Declined 2.2% in Q3 (though +0.4% YTD), showing some resilience in the flagship Heineken brand (-0.6% Q3, +2.7% YTD). Guidance: Full-year organic operating profit growth expected at the lower end of the 4-8% range.

Strategic Initiatives & Full-Year Guidance:
Strategic priorities (EverGreen strategy): (a) Premiumization (Heineken® & Tiger brands); (b) Productivity (targeting €500M gross savings in 2025); (c) Digitalization of route-to-consumer (eB2B platforms). Acquisitions: Integration of Distell (South Africa) to create a regional beverage champion.

Competitive Position & Brand Portfolio:
Market position: #2 global brewer. Key Brands: Heineken (global flagship), Amstel, Tiger, Sol, Birra Moretti, Desperados, Lagunitas. Strong footprint in developing markets (Africa, Asia, LATAM) provides long-term growth potential superior to developed market peers, though with higher volatility.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
Heineken balances reinvestment with shareholder returns. The company is committed to a progressive dividend policy. Share buybacks are generally opportunistic or used to offset dilution, secondary to maintaining the Net Debt/EBITDA target of <2.5x.

Overall Assessment: Heineken NV is recommended MARKET WEIGHT. A defensively positioned consumer staple with temporary volume headwinds. Strengths: (a) Unrivaled global footprint with EM exposure; (b) Premium brand equity allowing for pricing power; (c) Family control ensures long-term strategic horizon. Risks: (a) Volume sensitivity to consumer wallet pressure; (b) Input cost volatility; (c) Currency headwinds in key emerging markets (Nigeria, Mexico).

Monitor: (1) European volume recovery; (2) Input cost deflation flow-through to margins; (3) Performance of the premium portfolio vs. mainstream.

issuers.name: HERSHEY COMPANY
issuers.creditComment:
HERSHEY COMPANY
Credit Rating: A/Negative (S&P Revised Outlook May 2025) | A1/Negative (Moody's Revised Outlook July 2025) – Confectionery Leader; Cocoa Cost Pressure; Leverage Spike
​

Credit Momentum: Negative (Outlook Revised to Negative) – The Hershey Company [finance:Hershey Company] is facing credit headwinds. S&P revised the outlook to Negative (from Stable) on May 21, 2025, while affirming the 'A' rating. Moody's affirmed the 'A1' rating but also changed the outlook to Negative in July 2025. The negative momentum is driven by historically high cocoa prices squeezing margins and increased leverage from acquisitions (e.g., LesserEvil) and buybacks.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) iconic brand portfolio and dominant US confectionery market share; (b) strong pricing power historically; (c) high margins relative to peers. However, the unprecedented spike in cocoa costs (doubling in 2024) is compressing EBITDA. Leverage is expected to rise to the mid-to-high 2x area (near 3.0x peak) in 2025, which is weak for the 'A' category. The negative outlook indicates a 1-in-3 chance of a downgrade if deleveraging is delayed.
​

Financial Profile: Hershey's leverage has deteriorated. Debt/EBITDA is projected to approach 3.0x by year-end 2025 before moderating. The company faces significant input cost inflation (cocoa/sugar) which pricing actions may not fully offset without hitting volume. Free cash flow remains positive but pressured. The company has paused significant M&A to focus on deleveraging, but the cocoa crisis is a major external shock.
​

Overall Assessment: UNDERWEIGHT – 1-2% within Consumer Staples IG 3-4% allocation. Credit Watch Negative on "Single-A" status. Monitor: (1) Cocoa spot/futures prices and hedging roll-offs; (2) Elasticity of demand to further price hikes; (3) Gross margin contraction magnitude; (4) Pace of debt repayment vs. shareholder returns.

issuers.name: HESS CORP
issuers.creditComment:
HESS CORP
Credit Rating: AA-/Stable (S&P Upgraded July 2025 from BBB-) | AA/Stable (Fitch Upgraded July 2025 from BBB-) | Aa3/Stable (Moody's July 2025) – Now Subsidiary of Chevron Corp; Ratings Equalized to Parent Following July 2025 Acquisition Closure​

Credit Momentum: Transformed via Chevron Acquisition – Hess Corporation received multi-notch rating upgrades in July 2025 following closure of its acquisition by Chevron Corporation, with ratings now equalized to the parent company's premier investment-grade profile.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Hess maintains AA-/Stable from S&P (upgraded July 2025 from BBB-), AA/Stable from Fitch (upgraded July 2025 from BBB-), and equivalent Aa3 treatment from Moody's. Upgrades followed Chevron's acquisition closure, with Hess' ratings equalized to Chevron based on: (a) full integration into Chevron's operations; (b) strategic importance of Hess' Guyana assets; (c) Chevron's strong balance sheet and cash generation; (d) elimination of standalone refinancing risk.​

Strategic Value to Chevron: Hess' primary value lies in its 30% interest in the Stabroek Block (Guyana), one of the world's most significant oil discoveries with 11+ billion barrels recoverable resources. Multiple FPSOs producing/under development. Low-cost production (~$35/bbl breakeven). Chevron's acquisition provides scale, financial strength, and downstream integration.​

Balance Sheet & Outlook: Post-acquisition, Hess debt is effectively consolidated into Chevron's capital structure. Chevron maintains net debt-to-capital well below 20-25% long-term target. Morningstar DBRS confirmed Chevron at AA rating, noting acquisition enhances Chevron's long-term growth profile through Guyana exposure.​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – included in Chevron allocation. Former standalone E&P now fully integrated into Chevron Corporation with AA-/Aa3 equivalent ratings. Hess bonds trade at tight spreads reflecting Chevron backing. Monitor: (1) Guyana production ramp and cost efficiency; (2) Chevron integration execution; (3) Parent Chevron rating actions; (4) Stabroek Block development timeline; (5) OPEC+ production quota impacts.

issuers.name: HF SINCLAIR CORP
issuers.creditComment:
HF SINCLAIR CORP
Credit Rating: BBB-/Stable (S&P Affirmed May 2025) | Ba1/Stable (Moody's, estimated) | BBB-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed April 2025) – Mid-Size US Refiner; Strong Liquidity; Stable Margins
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – HF Sinclair Corporation 
 maintains a solid investment-grade profile with multiple agency affirmations. S&P affirmed the 'BBB-' local currency LT credit rating with a Stable outlook on May 15, 2025. Fitch affirms the 'BBB-' IDR with a Stable outlook as of April 2, 2025. The company secured a $2 billion credit facility in April 2025 with initial maturity of April 3, 2030, featuring extension mechanisms to prolong commitments in one-year increments. The company operates as a mid-sized independent US refiner with disciplined financial management.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) competitive refining operations with stable margin generation; (b) strong liquidity position (current ratio of 1.65); (c) moderate leverage (debt-to-equity ratio of 0.34, total debt of $3.13 billion); (d) disciplined capital allocation; (e) diversified operational footprint. The BBB-/Stable ratings provide reasonable cushion despite commodity refining margin volatility. The company faces commodity refining spread risks and energy transition headwinds.
​

Financial Profile: HF Sinclair maintains strong liquidity with $2 billion revolving credit facility and adequate cash generation. Total debt of $3.13 billion is manageable relative to EBITDA generation. The company demonstrated Q4 2024 challenges (adjusted net loss of $191 million reflecting refining downturn), but remains well-positioned to benefit from improved refining margins. Liquidity cushion supports financial flexibility and debt management.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within US Refining 4-5% allocation. Solid IG refiner (BBB-) with stable margins. Monitor: (1) Refining crack spreads and utilization; (2) Global refining capacity additions; (3) Debt management and leverage; (4) Energy transition risks to long-term refining demand.

issuers.name: HONDA MOTOR CO LTD
issuers.creditComment:
HONDA MOTOR CO LTD
Credit Rating: A3/Stable (Moody's) | A-/Negative (S&P Revised May 2025) – Global Automaker; Strong Motorcycle Business; EV Transition Pressure; Strategic Alliance with Nissan
​
Credit Momentum: Mixed – S&P revised Honda's outlook to Negative in May 2025, affirming the 'A-' rating, citing potential impacts from U.S. tariffs and declining profitability relative to previous assumptions. Conversely, Moody's views the proposed integration with Nissan Motor as Credit Positive overall, potentially strengthening scale and EV competitiveness, though execution risks remain.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Moody's rates Honda A3/Stable. S&P rates it A-/Negative. Ratings reflect: (a) solid global market position; (b) highly profitable motorcycle segment providing cash flow stability; (c) robust balance sheet with net cash; (d) challenges in the auto segment's margin amid the EV shift. The potential alliance with Nissan is a key strategic pivot to address scale disadvantages.
​
Financial Strength: Honda issued U.S. dollar-denominated straight bonds rated 'A-' by S&P in June 2025. The company maintains strong liquidity, but S&P flags that profitability may be under pressure from U.S. tariff hikes and intense competition. The motorcycle business remains a unique credit strength, buffering auto volatility.
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Global Autos 4-5% allocation. Solid investment-grade credit (A3/A-) facing secular headwinds. Monitor: (1) Progress of Nissan alliance; (2) US tariff policy impact; (3) Auto segment margin recovery; (4) EV product launch success.

issuers.name: HONGKONG LAND FINANCE
issuers.creditComment:
HONGKONG LAND FINANCE (CAYMAN ISLANDS) COMPANY LTD
Credit Rating: A1/Stable (Moody's) | A/Stable (S&P) | A/Stable (Fitch) – Bond-Issuing Vehicle for Hongkong Land; Guaranteed by Hongkong Land Company Limited; Prime Commercial Landlord in Central HK
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Hongkong Land Finance serves as the primary funding vehicle for Hongkong Land Holdings Limited, one of Asia's premier property investment and development groups. The company maintains solid investment-grade ratings (A1/A) backed by its high-quality portfolio of prime office and retail assets in Hong Kong's Central district and Singapore's Marina Bay.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are underpinned by: (a) dominant market position as the largest landlord in Central, Hong Kong; (b) stable recurring rental income; (c) conservative financial management; (d) strong balance sheet with low leverage. The notes issued by Hongkong Land Finance are guaranteed by The Hongkong Land Company Limited.
​

Bond Details (e.g., XS2648476302, XS1915535196): Bonds such as the 5.25% notes due 2033 and 3.75% notes due 2028 are issued by the Cayman vehicle and guaranteed by the operating company. Proceeds are generally used for refinancing and general corporate purposes.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within HK Property 5-6% allocation. Premier property credit (A1/A) with defensive asset quality and strong sponsorship. Monitor: (1) HK office rental reversions; (2) Vacancy rates in Central; (3) Dividend policy sustainability; (4) Interest rate environment.

issuers.name: HOWMET AEROSPACE INC
issuers.creditComment:
HOWMET AEROSPACE INC
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (Fitch Upgraded March 2025; S&P Upgraded 2024/2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's Upgraded Aug 2024) – Aerospace Components Leader; Strong Deleveraging; Pricing Power
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgrades) – Howmet Aerospace Inc [finance:Howmet Aerospace Inc] has successfully migrated to solid investment-grade status. Fitch upgraded the IDR to 'BBB+' with a Stable outlook on March 31, 2025. S&P also upgraded Howmet to 'BBB+' from BBB (noted in related search context). Moody's upgraded the senior unsecured rating two notches to 'Baa1' in August 2024. The upgrades reflect robust commercial aerospace demand, successful pricing strategies to offset inflation, and significant balance sheet strengthening.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) dominant market positions in jet engine components (airfoils, rings) and fastening systems; (b) sole-source provider status on critical engine programs; (c) strong pricing power allowing for margin expansion; (d) rapid deleveraging (net debt/EBITDA declined below 2.0x); (e) disciplined capital allocation. Risks include supply chain constraints (titanium/labor) and Boeing production rate uncertainties, though Howmet is diversified across OEMs (Airbus/Boeing/Defense).
​

Financial Profile: Howmet has transformed its balance sheet since the Arconic spin-off. Leverage is now firmly in the mid-BBB+ area. Free cash flow conversion is strong, supporting shareholder returns (dividends and buybacks) without jeopardizing credit ratings. The company issued $500 million in senior notes rated 'BBB+' in November 2025, demonstrating strong market access. Liquidity is robust.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Aerospace & Defense 5-6% allocation. Strong momentum credit (BBB+/Baa1) benefitting from the aerospace super-cycle. Monitor: (1) Aircraft build rates (Boeing 737/787, Airbus A320neo); (2) Margin expansion sustainability; (3) Capital deployment vs. rating limits; (4) Titanium supply chain stability.

issuers.name: HP INC
issuers.creditComment:
HP INC
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Revised Outlook July 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's) | BBB/Stable (Fitch) – PC/Print Leader; Cash Cow; Secular Headwinds
​

Credit Momentum: Stabilizing – HP Inc [finance:HP Inc] has stabilized its credit outlook. S&P revised the outlook to Stable (from Negative) and affirmed the 'BBB' rating on July 7, 2025. This followed an earlier affirmation with a negative outlook in Feb 2025. Moody's maintains a 'Baa2' rating, recently updating its credit analysis in February 2025. The stability reflects the company's ability to maintain strong free cash flow and manage leverage despite secular challenges in printing and cyclicality in PCs.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) leading market shares in commercial PCs and printing; (b) massive recurring revenue stream from supplies (ink/toner), though structurally declining; (c) strong free cash flow generation (~$3-$4B annually); (d) commitment to returning 100% of FCF to shareholders over time while maintaining leverage <2.0x. Risks include the secular decline of home printing, intense price competition in PCs, and execution of the "Future Ready" cost-saving plan.
​

Financial Profile: HP maintains a shareholder-friendly but disciplined financial policy. Leverage (debt/EBITDA) is managed generally within the 1.5x-2.0x target range. S&P noted in July 2025 that operating performance had stabilized sufficiently to remove the negative outlook. The company continues to use its strong liquidity to fund share repurchases and dividends. Debt maturities are well-laddered.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Tech Hardware 4-5% allocation. "Cash Cow" BBB credit. Monitor: (1) PC market recovery/refresh cycle (AI PCs); (2) Print supplies revenue trajectory; (3) Cost reduction execution; (4) Shareholder return aggression vs. leverage.

issuers.name: HUBBELL INC
issuers.creditComment:
HUBBELL INC
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Aug 2025) | BBB+/Stable (S&P Assigned/Affirmed Oct 2025) – Electrical Grid/Utility Exposure; Strong Margins; Consistent Execution
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Hubbell Inc [finance:Hubbell Inc] maintains a strong investment-grade profile. Fitch affirmed the 'A-' Long-Term IDR with a Stable outlook on August 1, 2025. S&P assigned a 'BBB+' rating to proposed senior notes in October 2025, affirming the issuer rating with a stable outlook. The company benefits from secular tailwinds in grid modernization and electrification.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) leading position in utility electrical components (transmission & distribution); (b) high exposure to grid hardening and modernization spending; (c) consistent EBITDA margins (~23-24%); (d) strong free cash flow; (e) disciplined M&A strategy. Risks include utility capex cycles and raw material inflation, though Hubbell has demonstrated strong pricing power.
​

Financial Profile: Hubbell operates with a conservative balance sheet. Fitch notes the 'A-' rating reflects steady credit metrics. S&P expects leverage to remain moderate. The company recently issued senior notes due 2035 rated 'A-'/'BBB+', extending its maturity profile. Liquidity is solid.
