The company's focus on essential electrical infrastructure provides defensive revenue characteristics.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Electrical Equipment 4-5% allocation. High-quality grid play (A-/BBB+). Monitor: (1) Utility transmission & distribution (T&D) capex budgets; (2) Grid modernization policy support; (3) Margin stability; (4) M&A integration (bolt-ons).

issuers.name: HUNT OIL CO OF PERU
issuers.creditComment:
HUNT OIL CO OF PERU
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's 2025) – Camisea Consortium Partner; 25.2% Stake; Strong Cash Flows; Peru Sovereign Ceiling Considerations; Strategic Asset Sale to MidOcean

Credit Momentum: Stable – Hunt Oil Company of Peru L.L.C., Sucursal del Perú (HOCP), a key partner in the Camisea Consortium (25.2% interest in Blocks 88 and 56) and operator of Peru LNG (PLNG), maintains investment-grade ratings (BBB/Baa2) with Stable outlooks. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB' rating in February 2025, highlighting HOCP's strong financial profile, predictable cash flows from Camisea natural gas and liquids production, and strategic importance to Peru's energy sector. Moody's upgraded HOCP to Baa2 (from Ba1) reflecting the company's robust credit metrics and alignment with parent Hunt Oil Company's 
 investment-grade profile (BBB/Baa2).

Strategic Transaction:
In September 2024, Hunt Oil Company agreed to sell a 15% interest in Peru LNG (PLNG) to MidOcean Energy (managed by EIG, backed by Aramco) while retaining a 35% stake and operatorship. This transaction does not affect HOCP's 25.2% upstream stake in Camisea (the primary credit driver) but strengthens the broader strategic partnership.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:

HOCP holds ratings of BBB (Fitch, Stable) and Baa2 (Moody's, Stable). The ratings are supported by low leverage (<1.5x Net Debt-to-EBITDA), strong liquidity, and the high-quality, low-cost reserve base of Camisea. Leverage profile: Conservative financial policy with minimal external debt at the HOCP level; cash flows are upstreamed to parent Hunt Oil Company.

Financial Profile:

HOCP's primary asset is its 25.2% non-operating interest in the Camisea Consortium (upstream Blocks 88 and 56), the largest natural gas and liquids producing fields in Peru. Camisea produces ~95% of Peru's natural gas and ~60% of NGLs. Revenue is generated from domestic gas sales (regulated low prices but stable volume), export gas (LNG netback prices), and NGL sales (linked to international benchmarks).

Strategic Initiatives:

Strategic Priorities: (a) Production Optimization – Maintaining plateau production at Camisea; (b) PLNG Partnership – Leveraging MidOcean/Aramco partnership to optimize LNG marketing; (c) Reserve Replacement – Evaluating exploration opportunities within existing blocks; (d) Cash Flow Stability – Managing exposure to NGL price volatility through hedging or conservative balance sheet management.

Competitive Position:

Market position: Strategic partner in Peru's most important energy asset (Camisea). Low production costs and established infrastructure provide a strong competitive moat.

Capital Allocation:

Capital allocation focuses on sustaining capex for Camisea and distributions to parent Hunt Oil Company. The sale of 15% PLNG stake generates proceeds for the parent group.

Overall Assessment: Hunt Oil Co Of Peru is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – Strategic Energy Asset with Sovereign Link. A robust upstream credit with strong cash generation. Strengths: (a) BBB/Baa2 investment-grade ratings; (b) Critical asset (Camisea) for Peru's energy security; (c) Low production costs and long reserve life; (d) Strong parent support (Hunt Oil Company); (e) Minimal leverage at operating level. Risks: (a) Commodity price volatility (NGLs); (b) Peru political/regulatory risks (royalties/taxation); (c) Concession expiration (2040s); (d) Export gas price exposure.

Monitor: (1) Peru political stability and hydrocarbon regulations; (2) NGL pricing trends; (3) Camisea production volumes; (4) Parent company (Hunt Oil) credit profile.

issuers.name: IBERDROLA INTL BV
issuers.creditComment:
IBERDROLA INTERNATIONAL BV
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Affirmed Apr 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Nov 2025) – Financing Vehicle for Iberdrola SA; Global Green Energy Leader; Stable Regulated Cash Flows
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Iberdrola International BV issues debt guaranteed by its parent, Iberdrola S.A. Ratings are affirmed at 'BBB+' (S&P/Fitch) and Baa1 (Moody's) with stable outlooks in 2025. This reflects the group's leadership in renewables and robust networks business, providing resilient cash flows.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect Iberdrola's: (a) massive scale as one of the world's largest renewable energy developers; (b) stable regulated networks business (providing ~50% of EBITDA); (c) geographic diversification (Spain, UK, US, Brazil); (d) solid financial profile. The company's strategic plan focuses on networks and selective renewable growth.
​

Financial Profile: Iberdrola maintains a solid balance sheet with FFO/Net Debt expected to remain above 18-20%. The company actively manages its portfolio through asset rotation (partnerships) to fund its capex plan while keeping leverage in check. Liquidity is strong.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within European Utilities 5-6% allocation. Core defensive utility credit (BBB+/Baa1). Monitor: (1) Regulatory returns in key markets (UK/US); (2) Offshore wind execution risks; (3) Interest rate impact on debt costs; (4) Hydrology in Spain/Brazil.

issuers.name: IBM INTERNAT CAPITAL
issuers.creditComment:
IBM INTERNATIONAL CAPITAL PTE. LTD.
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (S&P 2025) | A3/Stable (Moody's 2025) | A-/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Finance Subsidiary International Business Machines Corporation 
; 100% Owned Subsidiary; Full & Unconditional Guarantee from IBM; $5.5B+ Outstanding Debt Securities; No Standalone Operations; Finance Subsidiary Borrowing for IBM Benefit

Credit Momentum: Stable – IBM International Capital Pte. Ltd. (IIC) maintains investment-grade ratings (A-/A3) equalized with parent IBM Corporation (A-/A3/Stable), reflecting 100% ownership by IBM, full and unconditional guarantee from parent on all IIC debt securities, and IIC's role as pure finance subsidiary with no standalone operations. IIC's credit profile is entirely dependent on parent IBM's financial strength, benefiting from IBM's Q3 2025 performance acceleration (revenue $16.5B +6% YoY constant currency, software +10%, infrastructure +17%, consulting +3%), raised full-year 2025 guidance (>5% revenue growth constant currency, ~$14B free cash flow), and gradual deleveraging trajectory (core EBITDA leverage declining to 2.3-2.4x by 2026-2027 from 3.0x YE 2023):

Credit Ratings & Guarantee Structure:
IBM International Capital Pte. Ltd. maintains A-/Stable ratings from S&P and Fitch, with A3/Stable from Moody's, equalized with parent IBM Corporation (A-/A3). IIC is a 100% owned subsidiary of IBM incorporated as private company limited by shares under laws of Republic of Singapore (UEN 202345465D, incorporated November 21, 2023). IIC serves as "finance subsidiary" of IBM as described by SEC Rule 13-01(a)(4)(vi) of Regulation S-X, with primary purpose to borrow money to be made available for benefit of IBM and its affiliates. Critical structural feature: All IIC debt securities benefit from full and unconditional guarantee from parent IBM Corporation. IIC has no standalone operations, financial statements, or business activities separate from parent—it exists solely as financing vehicle for IBM group. Registered office: 10 Collyer Quay, #10-01, Ocean Financial Centre, Singapore 049315; Headquarters address: 7 Changi Business Park Central 1, IBM Place, Singapore 486072. Fitch affirmed IBM's Long-Term Issuer Default Rating (IDR) at A- with Stable outlook November 21, 2025, simultaneously affirming IIC senior unsecured debt at A-. IBM's Short-Term IDR and commercial paper ratings affirmed at F1. Parent IBM's ratings reflect solid market position in hybrid cloud and AI, strong free cash flow generation (core CFO minus capex to debt ratio ~30%), and resilient operating profile with 70%+ revenue from software and consulting services providing high synergies and visibility.​

Parent IBM Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
IBM reported strong Q3 2025 results driving ratings confidence: Revenue: $16.5 billion (+6% YoY reported, +5% constant currency); Software $7.2 billion (+10% YoY, +9% cc) led by Automation +24%, Red Hat +14%; Infrastructure $3.6 billion (+17% YoY, +15% cc) driven by IBM Z +61% (strongest Q3 in nearly two decades); Consulting $5.3 billion (+3% YoY, +2% cc) reflecting inflection as AI projects move to production; Financing $0.2 billion (+10% YoY, +8% cc). Profitability: Adjusted EBITDA growth mid-teens; Operating pre-tax margin expansion over 1 point; Adjusted EPS $2.65 (vs $2.44 expected). Cash flow: Net cash from operations $3.1 billion (Q3, up $0.2B YoY); Free cash flow $2.4 billion (Q3, up $0.3B YoY); Nine-month free cash flow $7.2 billion (up $0.6B YoY). Balance sheet: Cash, restricted cash, and marketable securities $14.9 billion (September 30, 2025, up $0.1B from YE 2024); Total debt $63.1 billion (including IBM Financing debt $11.3B, up $8.1B YTD); Core EBITDA leverage declining toward 2.3-2.4x by 2026-2027 (from 3.0x YE 2023). AI momentum: GenAI book of business surpassed $9.5 billion inception-to-date (~80% from Consulting, ~20% from Software); 200+ Consulting projects using AI agents at scale; 1,000+ client zero engagements; Internal GenAI productivity savings expected $4.5B annual run-rate exiting FY 2025.​

IIC Debt Issuance & Capital Markets Role:
IIC serves as IBM's primary Singapore-domiciled financing vehicle for international capital markets access. February 5, 2025 debt issuance: IIC entered underwriting agreements for multiple series totaling $5.5 billion aggregate principal: Euro Notes: Various series with maturities ranging across investment-grade curve; USD Notes: $600 million 4.700% due 2026; $1.0 billion 4.900% due 2034; Additional series including 2.900% notes due 2030 and 5.700% notes due 2055. All IIC debt securities fully and unconditionally guaranteed by parent IBM Corporation, providing investors with direct recourse to IBM's consolidated financial strength. Fitch affirmed IIC senior unsecured debt at A- concurrent with IBM parent rating affirmation, noting debt issuance will refinance IBM's $6 billion due 2026 and $3.8 billion due 2025, managing maturity profile. IIC debt structure mirrors IBM's financing strategy: Approximately 95% fixed-rate debt with extended maturity profile (weighted average ~16 years for IBM consolidated); IBM maintains $10 billion revolving credit facility and commercial paper program providing liquidity backstop. IIC's role complements IBM's other financing subsidiaries (IBM Credit LLC, various regional entities) enabling tax-efficient, geographically optimized capital structure.​

Parent IBM Strategic Position & Operating Resilience:
IBM's credit strength derives from resilient operating profile and strategic positioning: Revenue visibility: Over 40% of total revenue from software segment (80% recurring); Consulting revenue increasingly visible through efficiency-enhancing focus and complementary nature to IBM software products; Infrastructure revenue benefiting from z17 mainframe cycle (expected to contribute >1.5 points to FY revenue growth). Market position: Solid positioning in hybrid cloud and AI including digital transformations; Acquisitions and divestitures aligned strategy across three major business units (Software, Consulting, Infrastructure); Partnerships with Anthropic, Grok expanding model and inference options. Margin profile: Software profitability benefiting from operating leverage; Consulting margin expansion ~200bp YTD; Infrastructure mix and profitability boosted by z17 launch. Capital allocation: Dividend growth in line with revenue growth; Total outstanding debt expected to remain stable (excluding pre-funding debt for maturities); Acquisitions funded with internally generated cash; $1.6 billion returned to shareholders in dividends Q3 2025. Full-year 2025 guidance: Revenue growth >5% constant currency (~1.5 point FX tailwind); Free cash flow ~$14 billion (raised from >$13.5B); Adjusted EBITDA growth mid-teens.​

Leverage Profile & Deleveraging Trajectory:
IBM's credit metrics reflect gradual improvement supporting IIC ratings stability: Current leverage: Core EBITDA leverage ~2.5-2.6x (Q3 2025); Fitch forecasts gradual deleveraging to 2.3-2.4x by 2026-2027 through EBITDA growth while keeping total debt levels stable. FCF strength: Core (CFO-capex)/debt ratio currently near 30% and rising; Fitch estimates IBM will generate annual pre-dividend FCF near $14 billion through forecast period (providing ample financial flexibility). Debt management: IBM maintains current debt levels throughout forecast period, consistent with capital structure since Kyndryl divestiture in 2021; Deviations from stable debt approach could result in rating actions. Peer comparison: IBM's core EBITDA leverage in mid-2x range higher than A-category peers (0.4x-1.3x leverage, though peers have stronger CFO-capex/debt ratios); IBM compares favorably versus BBB+/BBB peers (similar leverage to HPE and HP Inc., lower leverage than Oracle). Rating upside: Positive rating action requires: Sufficient capacity and financial flexibility to execute M&A strategy and maintain competitiveness without significant credit metric degradation; Publicly articulated financial framework or demonstrated record maintaining core EBITDA leverage under 1.5x and (CFO-capex)/debt above 30%; Operating performance in line with Fitch expectations. Rating downside: Negative rating action if: Structurally lower revenue or EBITDA from material share losses degrading FCF generation; Sustained core EBITDA leverage above 2.25x and (CFO-capex)/debt below 25% potentially from aggressive capital structure deviating from expectations.​

Overall Assessment: IBM International Capital Pte. Ltd. is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within technology/finance subsidiary 6-7% investment-grade corporate debt allocation. IIC represents high-quality investment-grade credit (A-/A3) benefiting from 100% ownership by IBM and full/unconditional parent guarantee, with credit profile entirely dependent on parent IBM's financial strength and strategic execution. Strengths include: (a) 100% ownership by IBM Corporation (A-/A3/Stable) providing full financial backing; (b) full and unconditional guarantee from IBM on all IIC debt securities eliminating subsidiary risk; (c) pure finance subsidiary structure with no standalone operations, business risks, or separate financial profile from parent; (d) parent IBM strong Q3 2025 performance (revenue +6% YoY, software +10%, infrastructure +17%, consulting +3%); (e) raised IBM full-year guidance (>5% revenue growth cc, ~$14B free cash flow up from >$13.5B); (f) IBM resilient operating profile (70%+ revenue from software/consulting, 40%+ revenue from software with 80% recurring); (g) gradual IBM deleveraging trajectory (core EBITDA leverage declining to 2.3-2.4x by 2026-2027 from 3.0x); (h) IBM strong FCF generation (~$14B annually, core CFO-capex/debt ~30%); (i) IBM $9.5B GenAI book of business supporting growth visibility; (j) diversified debt maturity profile with predominantly fixed-rate structure mitigating refinancing risk. Risks include: (a) complete dependence on parent IBM financial health and strategic execution (no independent credit capacity); (b) IBM core EBITDA leverage in mid-2x range elevated versus A-category peers (0.4x-1.3x); (c) IBM revenue/EBITDA vulnerability to: AI/hybrid cloud competitive dynamics, consulting demand cyclicality, mainframe refresh cycle timing, Red Hat growth deceleration; (d) IBM elevated debt burden ($63.1B total debt vs $14.9B cash/securities requiring active management); (e) potential for IBM aggressive capital structure changes (M&A, shareholder returns) deviating from stable debt assumption; (f) technology sector disruption risks (cloud competition, AI democratization, customer budget constraints). RECOMMENDED: Overweight high-quality finance subsidiary with parent guarantee, benefiting from IBM's resilient operating model, strong FCF generation, and gradual deleveraging.

Monitor: (1) Parent IBM ratings (A-/A3 maintenance and rating agency outlook monitoring); (2) IBM revenue growth (>5% constant currency achievement and segment performance balance); (3) IBM leverage trajectory (Core EBITDA leverage declining to 2.3-2.4x and CFO-capex/debt maintaining 30%+); (4) IBM free cash flow generation (~$14B annually supporting dividend, debt management, and M&A flexibility); (5) IBM AI/hybrid cloud execution (GenAI book of business growth, software ARR trends, consulting inflection sustainability).

issuers.name: ICON INVESTMENTS SIX DAC
issuers.creditComment:
ICON INVESTMENTS SIX DAC (ICON PLC)
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Upgraded) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's Upgraded) – Financing Vehicle for ICON plc; Top-Tier CRO; Deleveraging Success
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgraded) – ICON Investments Six DAC is a financing subsidiary of ICON plc. The group received rating upgrades in late 2024/2025. S&P upgraded ICON to 'BBB' (from BBB-) and Moody's to Baa2 (from Baa3), citing strong operating performance and rapid deleveraging following the PRA Health Sciences acquisition.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are driven by: (a) position as a top-3 global Clinical Research Organization (CRO); (b) strong backlog and book-to-bill ratios; (c) robust free cash flow generation; (d) successful integration of PRA Health. The investment-grade rating reflects the company's commitment to a conservative financial policy.
​

Financial Profile: ICON has reduced its leverage significantly, with Net Debt/EBITDA trending towards 2.0x-2.5x. The company generates substantial cash flow, allowing it to pay down debt while investing in technology and innovation. Liquidity is excellent.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 2-3% within Global Healthcare Services 4-5% allocation. High-quality compounder (BBB/Baa2) in a structural growth industry. Monitor: (1) Biotech funding environment; (2) Clinical trial volumes; (3) M&A discipline; (4) Contract cancellation rates.

issuers.name: IDEX CORP
issuers.creditComment:
IDEX CORP
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Sept 2025) | BBB/Stable (S&P Affirmed Sept 2024) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's Affirmed Dec 2021/Maintained) – Niche Industrial; High Margins; M&A Growth
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – IDEX Corp [finance:IDEX Corp] maintains a solid investment-grade profile. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB+' IDR with a Stable outlook on September 29, 2025. S&P affirmed the 'BBB' rating with a Stable outlook in September 2024. Moody's rates IDEX 'Baa2' with a Stable outlook (affirmed late 2021, analysis updated Aug 2024). IDEX is a high-quality, diversified industrial known for its decentralized model and highly engineered niche products (fluidics, optics, fire/safety).
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) "inch-wide, mile-deep" strategy leading to dominant market shares in niche applications; (b) excellent EBITDA margins (>25%) and low capital intensity; (c) strong free cash flow conversion; (d) diverse end-markets (life sciences, water, industrial, fire/rescue). The primary credit constraint is the company's acquisitive nature, which can temporarily elevate leverage, though IDEX has a strong track record of rapid deleveraging.
​

Financial Profile: IDEX maintains a flexible balance sheet to support its compounder strategy. Fitch notes the 'BBB+' rating accommodates its acquisition strategy. Leverage is typically managed <2.5x, often deleveraging to <2.0x quickly after deals. Liquidity is strong with revolver capacity and cash flow. The company's resilience through cycles supports the stable outlooks.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Diversified Industrials 4-5% allocation. Premium compounder credit (BBB+/Baa2). Monitor: (1) Life Sciences/Health & Science technologies demand; (2) M&A activity and pro forma leverage; (3) Organic growth rates; (4) Margin protection vs. inflation.

issuers.name: ILLINOIS TOOL WORKS INC
issuers.creditComment:
ILLINOIS TOOL WORKS INC
Credit Rating: A1/Positive (Moody’s, March 2025) | Likely A+/Stable (S&P) – U.S. diversified industrial, sector-leading margin (EBITA 28%), debt/EBITDA <2x, strong capital allocation.

Credit Momentum: Improving – Moody’s upgraded outlook to positive on continued outperformance and margin resilience.

Credit Ratings & Stability: A1/Positive (Moody’s), A+/Stable (S&P expected). Strengths: (a) consistent above-peer margins; (b) robust free cash flow; (c) strong liquidity.

Financial Profile & Production: Organic growth, bolt-on acquisitions, well-paced capital returns.

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within global industrials 8-10% allocation. Strong credit with potential for further upgrades; monitor for margin and cyclical trends.

issuers.name: ILLUMINA INC
issuers.creditComment:
ILLUMINA INC
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Assigned Nov 2025 to proposed notes) | Baa3/Stable (Moody's Affirmed April 2025) – Life Sciences Equipment/Genomics Leader; High-Margin Consumables; DNA Sequencing
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Illumina Inc. 
 maintains a solid investment-grade credit profile. Moody's affirmed the 'Baa3' senior unsecured rating with a Stable outlook following a periodic review on April 10, 2025. S&P assigned the 'BBB' issue-level rating to Illumina's proposed $500 million senior unsecured notes on November 10, 2025. The company is a market leader in genomics and life sciences equipment with high-recurring revenues from consumables and sequencing services (DNA/RNA sequencing enabling genomic analysis across research, clinical diagnostics, and forensics).
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) market leadership in genomic sequencing and analysis with strong competitive moats; (b) high-recurring revenue model from installed instrument base and consumables; (c) strong EBITDA generation and margins; (d) solid free cash flow supporting debt service; (e) diversified customer base across research institutions, clinical labs, and commercial applications. Constraints include execution risk on new platform adoption and competitive threats from alternative sequencing technologies.
​

Financial Profile: Illumina maintains solid financial performance with strong EBITDA and FCF generation supporting credit quality. The company invests in R&D to maintain competitive positioning in genomic sequencing and analytics. The November 2025 $500 million note issuance reflects refinancing/strategic capital availability. Liquidity is adequate with cash balances and credit facilities supporting operational needs. The company is well-positioned in the high-growth genomics market with secular tailwinds from precision medicine and diagnostics.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Life Sciences Equipment 4-5% allocation. Core IG credit (BBB/Baa3) with genomics growth. Monitor: (1) DNA sequencing platform adoption rates; (2) Consumables revenue growth; (3) Competitive dynamics with other genomic sequencing providers; (4) R&D capex returns; (5) Gross margin sustainability.

issuers.name: IMPERIAL BRANDS FIN PLC
issuers.creditComment:
IMPERIAL BRANDS FIN PLC
Credit Rating: Baa3/Positive (Moody's 2025) | BBB/Stable (S&P 2025) | BBB/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Imperial Brands PLC Financing Subsidiary; UK Tobacco Leader; Leverage Target 2.0x-2.5x; NGP Diversification

Credit Momentum: Positive – Imperial Brands Finance Netherlands B.V., financing subsidiary of Imperial Brands PLC 
, carries a Positive outlook from Moody's (Baa3/Positive as of October 2024), signaling upgrade potential. S&P and Fitch maintain BBB/Stable ratings. The company continues deleveraging toward its 2.0x-2.5x target (reported leverage ~2.9x-3.0x), supported by free cash flow generation and asset sales. Moody's upgrade path reflects successful pivot toward next-generation products (NGP) with declining combustible tobacco exposure. The company issued €1 billion notes (March 2021) with strong investor demand, demonstrating continued market access.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Imperial Brands Finance holds ratings of Baa3/Positive (Moody's), BBB/Stable (S&P), and BBB/Stable (Fitch). Moody's Positive outlook indicates upgrade potential to Baa2 if leverage falls below 2.5x and NGP growth accelerates. Leverage profile: The company targets 2.0x-2.5x Net Debt/EBITDA, currently estimated at 2.9x-3.0x, with clear deleveraging trajectory through FCF generation and asset disposals.

Financial Profile:
Imperial Brands operates as a global tobacco company with leading market positions in UK, US, Canada, Belgium, Netherlands. The company's portfolio spans combustible cigarettes (dominant revenue driver), cigars, tobacco and NGP (vapors, heated tobacco, nicotine pouches). Operating margins >40% in Tobacco & NGP segments demonstrate pricing power.

Strategic Initiatives:
Strategic Priorities: (a) NGP Expansion – Increasing NGP revenue to >30% of total by 2025; (b) Deleveraging – Targeting 2.0x-2.5x leverage through FCF and asset sales; (c) Dividend Policy – Progressive annual dividend growth from current levels; (d) Sustainability – ESG initiatives supporting license to operate.

Competitive Position:
Market position: Global tobacco company with leading market share in key European/North American markets. The company competes with BAT, PMI, RJ Reynolds in highly profitable, consolidated industry.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
Imperial Brands targets high dividend payout (50%+ of earnings) with growth, supported by strong margins and FCF. Capital prioritized toward NGP innovation and leverage reduction.

Overall Assessment: Imperial Brands Fin PLC is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – Tobacco Company with NGP Pivot. A profitable but declining sector player managing transition. Strengths: (a) Baa3/Positive from Moody's (upgrade potential); (b) >40% operating margins in core business; (c) NGP diversification underway; (d) Strong dividend policy. Risks: (a) Regulatory/tobacco pressures (volumes declining); (b) NGP adoption uncertainty; (c) Deleveraging execution (targeting 2.0x-2.5x); (d) ESG headwinds.

Monitor: (1) NGP revenue growth (targeting >30%); (2) Leverage progression (target 2.0x-2.5x); (3) Moody's upgrade trajectory; (4) Free cash flow realization.

issuers.name: INDIAN RAILWAY FINANCE
issuers.creditComment:
INDIAN RAILWAY FINANCE CORPORATION (IRFC)
Credit Rating: Baa3/Stable (Moody's) | BBB-/Stable (S&P) | BBB-/Stable (Fitch) – Sovereign-Linked Financing Arm of Indian Railways; ~86% GoI Ownership; Low Risk Business Model
​
Credit Momentum: Stable – Indian Railway Finance Corporation (IRFC) maintains investment-grade ratings aligned with India's sovereign rating. In September 2025, Moody's affirmed the Baa3 rating with a stable outlook, citing the company's strategic importance as the dedicated borrowing arm for Indian Railways.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are equalized with the sovereign (Baa3/BBB-/BBB-) due to: (a) strong strategic linkage (86.4% GoI ownership); (b) critical public policy role in funding railway infrastructure; (c) low-risk cost-plus leasing model where lease rentals from the Ministry of Railways (MoR) cover all debt service costs. Domestic ratings are AAA/Stable by CRISIL, ICRA, and CARE.
​
Financial Profile: As of March 31, 2025, IRFC's loan book and lease receivables stood at ~INR 5.8 lakh crore. The company enjoys zero NPA (non-performing assets) due to its sovereign counterparty. Capital adequacy remains robust (CRAR >600%) due to zero risk-weighting on MoR exposure. Funding mix includes tax-free bonds, taxable bonds, and external commercial borrowings (ECBs).
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within India/Asia Quasi-Sovereign 4-5% allocation. High-quality quasi-sovereign credit (Baa3/BBB-) offering a proxy to Indian sovereign risk with a yield pickup. Monitor: (1) GoI stake dilution plans; (2) MoR's capital expenditure targets; (3) Borrowing program size; (4) Sovereign rating trajectory.

issuers.name: INDIANA MICHIGAN POWER
issuers.creditComment:
INDIANA MICHIGAN POWER
Credit Rating: Baa2/Positive (Moody's 2025) | A-/Stable (Fitch 2025) | BBB+/Stable (S&P est.) – AEP Subsidiary Utility; Rockport Plant Transition; Strong Regulatory Support

Credit Momentum: Positive – Indiana Michigan Power Company (I&M), a subsidiary of American Electric Power Company 
, has experienced favorable rating momentum with Moody's upgrading I&M to Baa2/Positive (March 2025, from Baa2/Stable) and Fitch maintaining A- rating (December 2021 predecessor action). The positive outlook reflects credit-supportive regulatory frameworks and the resolution of uncertainty regarding the company's lease termination for the Rockport coal-fired power plant. I&M's strong financial performance is expected to generate robust credit metrics, including CFO pre-working capital to debt ratios in the 22%-24% range (2025-2027), among the strongest for utilities.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
I&M holds aligned investment-grade ratings of Baa2/Positive (Moody's) and A- (Fitch). S&P rates the parent AEP at Baa2/Stable; I&M likely receives similar or stronger sub-rating consideration. Leverage profile: The company maintains conservative leverage estimated at <2.0x Net Debt-to-EBITDA (Moody's CFO pre-WC/Debt 22%-24%), substantially lower than parent AEP (2.5x-3.0x estimated range).

Financial Profile:
I&M operates as a regulated utility serving northern Indiana and southern Michigan. The company's 2025-2027 capex program totals $4.2B, primarily focused on replacing retiring coal generation from Rockport (retirement program) and modernizing transmission/distribution infrastructure. Strong regulatory relationships support cost recovery mechanisms and timely rate adjustments.

Strategic Initiatives:
Parent Company (AEP) Strategies: (a) Rockport Plant Transition – $4.2B capex to replace retiring generation and modernize grid; (b) Rate Base Growth – Regulatory frameworks with automatic recovery mechanisms reduce lag; (c) Transmission/Distribution Modernization – Smart grid, distribution automation investments. I&M Benefits: Strong regulatory relationship with Indiana Utility Regulatory Commission; positive precedent from Rockport lease resolution.

Competitive Position:
Market position: Regulated utility monopoly in service territory; no direct commercial competition due to utility regulatory structure.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
I&M is subsidiized by parent AEP. Capital allocation directed toward rate base growth and infrastructure modernization. Dividends flow to parent AEP, which maintains dividend aristocrat status.

Overall Assessment: Indiana Michigan Power is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – Defensive Regulated Utility. A high-quality utility subsidiary with positive momentum. Strengths: (a) Baa2/Positive (Moody's) rating with upgrade potential; (b) A- (Fitch) investment-grade; (c) Strong financial metrics (22%-24% CFO pre-WC/Debt ratio); (d) Credit-supportive regulatory environment; (e) Positive regulatory precedent (Rockport resolution). Risks: (a) Parent AEP leverage elevated (~2.5x-3.0x); (b) Capex intensity ($4.2B 2025-2027) requires financed expansion; (c) Generation transition execution risk; (d) Interest rate sensitivity on financing needs.

Monitor: (1) Rockport plant replacement progress and cost realization; (2) Rate case outcomes and allowed ROE trends; (3) Parent AEP financial condition and leverage; (4) Regulatory support for capex cost recovery.

issuers.name: INDOFOOD CBP SUKSES MAKM
issuers.creditComment:
NDOFOOD CBP SUKSES MAKMUR TBK (ICBP)
Credit Rating: Baa2/Stable (Moody’s Dec 2024) | BBB-/Positive (Fitch Jun 2024) – Indonesia’s Leading Packaged Food Producer; US$2.75B USD Bonds (2031–2052) Rated Baa2/BBB-; Strong Market Position in Instant Noodles & Dairy; Deleveraging After Pinehill Acquisition

Credit Momentum: Positive/Improving – Indofood CBP Sukses Makmur 
 is investment grade (Baa2/BBB-) with Positive outlook from Fitch (June 2024) and Stable from Moody’s (Dec 2024). Fitch’s Positive outlook reflects strengthening credit profile as leverage declines following the debt-funded Pinehill acquisition, supported by robust cash generation from its dominant instant noodle and dairy businesses.​

Credit Ratings & Debt Structure:
ICBP’s USD bonds (2031, 2032, 2051, 2052) are rated Baa2 by Moody’s and BBB- by Fitch. Fitch June 2024: Revised ICBP’s outlook to Positive from Stable, affirming BBB- IDR; Outlook change driven by faster-than-expected deleveraging from strong EBITDA and debt repayment, with FFO net leverage expected to fall toward 2.0x over next 18–24 months. Moody’s Dec 2024: Upgraded issuer rating to Baa2 from Baa3 with Stable outlook, citing solid market position, strong cash flow, and prudent financial policies.​

Business Profile:
ICBP is Indonesia’s largest packaged food company: Segments: Noodles (Indomie), dairy, snack foods, nutrition & special foods, and beverages; Noodles segment is core profit driver. Market leadership: Dominant instant noodle market share in Indonesia (>70%) and meaningful presence across Middle East and Africa (via Pinehill acquisition). Cash generation: High-margin noodle business and sizable domestic distribution network support robust EBITDA and FCF.​

Overall Assessment: Indofood CBP Sukses Makmur is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within EM consumer 6-7% Indonesia/Asia allocation. ICBP offers IG EM consumer exposure with improving leverage and resilient cash flows anchored in staple foods. Strengths: (a) Baa2/BBB- ratings with Positive Fitch outlook; (b) dominant instant noodle franchise; (c) strong cash generation deleveraging post-Pinehill; (d) diversified product portfolio; (e) USD bonds with long tenors providing term funding. Risks: (a) FX risk from USD debt vs IDR cash flows; (b) commodity input cost volatility (wheat, palm oil, dairy); (c) exposure to lower-income consumers sensitive to inflation; (d) geographic risk in Middle East/Africa operations; (e) related-party dynamics within Indofood group. RECOMMENDED: Overweight high-quality EM consumer staple with improving credit metrics and resilient demand profile.

Monitor: (1) FFO net leverage path toward ~2.0x; (2) commodity cost pass-through; (3) FX management for USD bonds; (4) performance in Pinehill/MENA markets; (5) Indofood group governance developments.

issuers.name: INDUSTRIAS PENOLES SAB
issuers.creditComment:
INDUSTRIAS PENOLES SAB (USP55409AA77)
Credit Rating: S&P BBB Stable (revised from Negative, May 2025), Fitch BBB Stable

Credit Momentum: Positive - Outlook Stabilized. Mexico's largest mining company and leading global silver/zinc/gold producer showing credit improvement with S&P May 2025 outlook revision to Stable from Negative. Parent of Fresnillo PLC (75% ownership), Peñoles operates integrated mining/metallurgy with smelting/refining providing margin stability versus pure miners. Company benefits from: (1) diversified metal portfolio (silver, gold, zinc, lead) reducing single-commodity risk; (2) vertical integration from mining through refined metals; (3) long-life reserves across Mexican mining districts; (4) smelter/refining operations capturing processing margins. Investment-grade BBB reflects: (1) commodity price volatility despite diversification; (2) leverage typically 1.5-2.0x net debt/EBITDA; (3) Mexican operational concentration; (4) capex intensity from mining/metallurgical operations.

Overall Assessment: Peñoles represents diversified Mexican mining credit with metallurgical integration providing stability. BBB rating appropriate for: (1) integrated model capturing both mining and processing margins; (2) metal diversification (no single commodity >40% EBITDA); (3) strong balance sheet with moderate leverage; (4) operational efficiency as low-cost producer. Suitable for investors seeking: (1) diversified base/precious metals exposure; (2) Mexican mining sector leader; (3) refining margin stability offsetting mining volatility; (4) energy transition metals (zinc for galvanizing, silver for solar). Outlook stabilization removes downgrade overhang following operational improvements and cost management. Key positives: (1) zinc demand from infrastructure steel galvanizing; (2) silver industrial applications (solar, electronics); (3) gold portfolio providing inflation hedge; (4) processing margins insulating from pure commodity exposure. Risks: (1) Mexican mining sector challenges (security, water, community relations); (2) zinc price volatility; (3) energy costs impacting smelting operations; (4) environmental regulations. Monitor: (1) metal price trends (especially zinc/silver); (2) mining production rates; (3) smelter utilization; (4) Fresnillo subsidiary performance. Recommended market weight - quality diversified miner but commodity exposure creates volatility. Preferred versus pure-play silver (Fresnillo subsidiary) for investors seeking base metals diversification.

issuers.name: INFRAESTRUCTURA ENERGETI
issuers.creditComment:
IENOVA 3.75 01/14/2028 – Infraestructura Energética (Mexico), BBB‑
Профиль бизнеса
Инфраструктурный оператор (газопроводы/энергетические объекты) с долгосрочными тарифными/transport contracts, вероятно с CFE/PEMEX или крупными частными off‑takers.
Выручка в значительной части фиксирована или инфляционно индексирована, что делает бизнес ближе к regulated midstream.

Кредитные метрики
Низкая дюрация облигации (1,8) отражает относительно короткий срок до погашения; левередж умеренный, DSCR/coverage комфортны благодаря стабильным тарифным потокам.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды
Рейтинг около BBB‑ со стабильным outlook, лимитированный мексиканским суверенным риском.
YTM ~4,4%, OAS ~90–105 bps – почти “короткий quasi‑sovereign” риск.

Ключевые риски
Регуляторика и политика Мексики в отношении midstream‑активов, включая возможные пересмотры тарифов или контрактов.
Концентрация на 1–2 основных проектах/контрактах.

Инвестидия
Привлекателен как очень короткий IG‑инструмент с небольшим премиумом к CFE/суверену; риск ограничен горизонтом и контрактной природой кэш‑флоу.

issuers.name: INGERSOLL RAND INC
issuers.creditComment:
INGERSOLL RAND INC.
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P 2024) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's 2024) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch May 2025) – Global Industrial Solutions Provider; $6.8B Q3 2025 TTM Revenue; High-20% EBITDA Margins; Mid-to-High Teens FCF Margins; ILC Dover Integration; 1.6x Net EBITDA Leverage; $3.8B Liquidity; Mission-Critical Flow Creation

Credit Momentum: Positive/Upgraded – Ingersoll Rand 
 achieved credit improvement with Fitch upgrading to BBB+ (from BBB) in May 2025, completing investment-grade profile transformation. The upgrade reflects successful management of leverage following the $2.3 billion ILC Dover acquisition, strong EBITDA and FCF margins (high-20% and mid-to-high teens respectively), and effective positioning in high-growth markets including life sciences, aerospace & defense, and industrial applications. Q3 2025 delivered positive organic orders growth across both segments despite macroeconomic uncertainty:

Credit Ratings & Upgrade Trajectory:
Ingersoll Rand maintains BBB/Stable ratings from S&P, Baa2/Stable from Moody's, and BBB+/Stable from Fitch (upgraded May 2025). Fitch May 2025 upgrade: Raised to BBB+ from BBB citing successful leverage management post-ILC Dover acquisition; EBITDA and FCF margins notably strong compared to BBB category peers; Expects gross EBITDA leverage below mid-2x range with limited exceptions above net leverage target of under 2x. May 2024 upgrades: All three agencies upgraded one notch (to BBB/BBB/Baa2) recognizing consistent operating performance and capital structure restructuring to fully unsecured. Capital structure transformation: Completed transition to fully unsecured debt profile with $3.3 billion senior unsecured bond offering and $2.6 billion senior unsecured revolving credit facility (May 2024).​

Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
Ingersoll Rand reported Q3 2025 results (October 29, 2025): Organic orders: Positive organic orders growth across both segments demonstrating business resilience. Liquidity: $3.8 billion including cash and revolver availability. Free cash flow: $326 million ($355 million operating cash flow less $29 million capex) vs $374 million prior year; Year-to-date FCF approximately flat YoY. Net leverage: Net debt-to-Adjusted EBITDA 1.6x as of March 2025 (post-ILC Dover), improving from 2.4x gross EBITDA leverage. CEO Vicente Reynal: "Our performance demonstrates the resilience of our business, which combined with our strong balance sheet, enables durable long-term growth. We remain well positioned for future growth with our proven agile business model, operational excellence, and ownership mindset.". Q2 2025 revenue of $1.9 billion exceeded estimates with adjusted EPS of $0.80 matching consensus, though stock declined 11.3% on Industrial Technologies & Services organic revenue decline and reduced growth forecast.​

Business Portfolio & Secular Tailwinds:
Ingersoll Rand operates mission-critical industrial solutions: Industrial Technologies & Services (IT&S): Broad range of compressor, vacuum, blower, and air treatment solutions; Industrial technologies including power tools and lifting equipment. Precision & Science Technologies (P&ST): Mission-critical precision liquid, gas, air, and powder handling technologies for life sciences and industrial applications; Aerospace and defense applications expanded through ILC Dover acquisition. Secular growth drivers: Positioned to benefit from energy transition, sustainability, digitization trends; High-value products with well-established competitive positions; Essential nature of products supporting favorable pricing. ILC Dover contribution: $2.3 billion acquisition (2024) increased life sciences and aerospace & defense presence, further diversifying revenue base.​

Financial Policy & Margin Profile:
Ingersoll Rand demonstrates strong profitability and disciplined capital allocation: EBITDA margins: Sustained in high-20% range with potential for further expansion from new product development, cost efficiency, and acquisition synergies. FCF margins: Mid-to-high teens reflecting low capital intensity and effective working capital management. Leverage targets: Gross EBITDA leverage sustained below mid-2x range; Net leverage target under 2x. Tariff management: Estimates approximately $150 million tariff costs, expecting to offset through pricing, supply chain changes, and operating adjustments. Acquisition strategy: Uses acquisitions to grow core business, expand in adjacent industrial markets, and increase operating efficiency; Directed toward adjacent end-markets supporting margin growth and market share. Fitch expects any leverage increases above negative sensitivity from acquisitions to be temporary.​

Overall Assessment: Ingersoll Rand Inc. is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within industrials 6-7% investment-grade corporate allocation. Ingersoll Rand represents strong investment-grade credit (BBB/Baa2/BBB+) with mission-critical industrial solutions, strong margins, and successful acquisition integration. Strengths include: (a) Fitch BBB+ upgrade (May 2025) completing investment-grade profile transformation; (b) high-20% EBITDA margins and mid-to-high teens FCF margins—notably strong vs BBB peers; (c) Q3 2025 positive organic orders growth demonstrating business resilience; (d) $3.8B liquidity providing substantial financial flexibility; (e) net leverage 1.6x well below 2x target and mid-2x downgrade threshold; (f) ILC Dover integration expanding life sciences and aerospace/defense exposure; (g) secular tailwinds (energy transition, sustainability, digitization) supporting long-term demand; (h) fully unsecured capital structure transformation completed; (i) proven agile business model with IRX operational excellence; (j) mission-critical products supporting pricing power. Risks include: (a) IT&S organic revenue decline (-4% Q2 2025) indicating demand softening; (b) tariff exposure (~$150M) requiring pricing and supply chain offsets; (c) acquisition integration risk from ILC Dover and future M&A; (d) industrial cycle sensitivity affecting short-term demand; (e) P&ST organic revenue decline (-2% Q2 2025) from life sciences weakness; (f) potential leverage elevation from acquisition activity; (g) competition in fragmented industrial markets; (h) supply chain disruption risks affecting operations. RECOMMENDED: Overweight strong industrials credit with proven margin profile, successful acquisition strategy, and secular growth positioning.

Monitor: (1) Organic revenue growth (IT&S and P&ST segment stabilization amid uncertain demand); (2) Leverage trajectory (Net leverage maintaining below 2x and gross below mid-2x); (3) ILC Dover integration (Synergy realization and margin contribution); (4) Tariff mitigation ($150M cost offset through pricing and supply chain adjustments); (5) Acquisition pipeline (Future M&A and temporary leverage impacts).

issuers.name: INGREDION INC
issuers.creditComment:
INGREDION INC
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Dec 2025) | Baa1/Positive (Moody's Outlook Revised March 2025) – Food Ingredients; Specialty Growth; Deleveraging
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Outlook Positive from Moody's) – Ingredion Inc [finance:Ingredion Inc] is seeing positive credit momentum. Moody's affirmed the 'Baa1' rating and revised the outlook to Positive on March 28, 2025, citing operational improvements and reduced leverage. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB' rating with a Stable outlook on December 5, 2025. The company is successfully shifting its portfolio toward higher-margin specialty ingredients (texture, sugar reduction).
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) global scale in starch and sweetener solutions; (b) strategic pivot to "Specialty" ingredients (target 40% of revenue) driving margin expansion; (c) strong agricultural supply chain management; (d) conservative financial policy. Risks include commodity price volatility (corn) and exposure to declining high fructose corn syrup (HFCS) demand, though the latter is now a smaller part of the portfolio (<10%).
​

Financial Profile: Ingredion has strengthened its balance sheet. Moody's notes debt/EBITDA declined to 1.6x at fiscal year-end 2024 and expects it to remain ~1.5x. This leverage profile is strong for the Baa1 rating and supports the positive outlook. Free cash flow is solid (~$250M projected next 12m), funding dividends and bolt-on M&A. Liquidity is excellent with a $1.0 billion undrawn revolver.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Consumer Staples/Ingredients 4-5% allocation. Improving credit (Baa1/BBB) with upgrade potential. Monitor: (1) Specialty ingredients revenue mix (target 40%); (2) Corn cost pass-through efficacy; (3) Leverage maintenance (<2.0x); (4) Cash flow generation vs. working capital needs.

issuers.name: INTEL CORP
issuers.creditComment:
INTEL CORP
Credit Rating: BBB/Negative (Fitch Downgraded Aug 2025) | Baa2/Negative (Moody's Downgraded Aug 2025) | BBB/Negative (S&P Downgraded Dec 2024) – Share Loss; Foundries Burn; Execution Risk
​

Credit Momentum: Negative (Multiple Downgrades) – Intel Corp [finance:Intel Corp] has suffered significant credit deterioration, falling to the low-BBB tier. Fitch downgraded Intel to 'BBB' with a Negative outlook on August 4, 2025 (just two notches above junk). Moody's downgraded the senior unsecured rating to 'Baa2' on August 8, 2025, also citing a negative outlook/weak profitability. S&P had previously downgraded Intel to 'BBB' in December 2024. The negative momentum reflects persistent market share losses in data center/client CPUs, massive operating losses in the Foundry unit, and weak free cash flow.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are pressured by: (a) intensifying competition from AMD, Nvidia, and Arm-based CPUs (Qualcomm/Apple); (b) high execution risk in the IDM 2.0 strategy (becoming a third-party foundry); (c) continued cash burn driven by massive capex needs; (d) leverage expected to peak near 7.0x (Moody's estimate) in 2024 before moderating to ~4.0x by late 2025/2026. The Negative outlook implies a high risk of falling to "BBB-" (the edge of investment grade) if the 18A process node ramp delays or fails to yield commercial success.
​

Financial Profile: Intel's financial flexibility has eroded. The company suspended its dividend and announced significant cost reductions (15% headcount cut) in mid-2025 to preserve cash. Liquidity remains "solid" with ~$21 billion in cash/investments and undrawn revolvers, but net debt has risen. Fitch notes that restoring the rating requires stabilizing market share and reducing debt, which is challenging given the capital intensity of the foundry buildout.
​

Overall Assessment: UNDERWEIGHT – 0-1% within Technology IG 3-4% allocation. "Fallen Angel" risk is real (BBB/Baa2 Negative). Monitor: (1) 18A process node technical milestones and customer wins; (2) Data Center market share stabilization vs. AMD; (3) Cash burn rate vs. liquidity buffers; (4) Potential strategic splits (Product vs. Foundry) to unlock value.

issuers.name: INTERCHILE SA
issuers.creditComment:
INTERC 4.5% 06/30/2056 – Interchile, BBB+
Профиль бизнеса
Проектная компания, владеющая и эксплуатирующая основной интерконнектор и высоковольтные линии в Чили, работающие по availability‑based схемам с регулируемым доходом.
Выручка практически не зависит от объёмов переданной энергии; проект является ключевой частью национальной сети, что повышает его стратегическую важность.

Кредитные метрики
Высокий левередж, но DSCR моделируется на уровне не ниже 1,3–1,4x; длинный срок концессии и амортизационный долг обеспечивают прогнозируемый CFADS.
Капитальные расходы после завершения строительства минимальны, что делает FCF устойчивым.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды
Рейтинг около BBB+/BBB со стабильным outlook, ограниченный суверенным потолком Чили и отраслевыми рисками.
Выпуск 2056 (super‑long) торгуется с YTM ~5,5%, дюрацией >12 и OAS ~100–175 bps – длинный quasi‑sovereign с сильной чувствительностью к ставкам.

Ключевые риски
Очень высокая дюрация: сильная реакция на движения кривой UST и на изменения в оценке суверенного/регуляторного риска Чили.
Концентрация на одном активе; любые крупные технические или регуляторные события могут иметь значимый эффект.

Инвестидия
Подходит как “duration‑play” с низким кредитным риском, если есть цель тянуть дюрацию через качественный infrastructure.
Неоптимален для консервативных мандатов: даже небольшой рост ставок/спрэдов вызовет крупную ценовую волатильность.

issuers.name: INTERCONEXION ELECTRICA
issuers.creditComment:
ISACB 3.825 11/26/2033 – Interconexión Eléctrica (ISA), BBB‑
Профиль бизнеса
Крупнейший колумбийский оператор высоковольтных сетей с активами в Колумбии, Перу, Бразилии, Чили и др.; значительная часть выручки – регулируемые тарифы transmission.
Диверсификация по странам снижает зависимость от одного регулятора и суверена.

Кредитные метрики
Левередж около 3,0–3,5x, DSCR комфортный, EBITDA margin высокая; у компании длинный, хорошо распределённый долг.
Ликвидность хорошая, есть доступ к местным рынкам и банкам.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды
Рейтинг BBB‑/BBB с потолком по суверену Колумбии, outlook стабильный.
Выпуск торгуется с YTM ~5,1%, dur ~5,8, OAS ~130–180 bps – типичный уровень для диверсифицированного LatAm transmission IG.

Ключевые риски
Суверенный и политический риск Колумбии (налоги, тарифы, гос‑политика).
Регуляторные решения в странах присутствия, особенно по доходности капитала.

Инвестидия
Хорошо диверсифицированное IG‑имя, дающее немного более высокий спред, чем чисто чилийские сети, за страновой риск Колумбии/Перу/Бразилии.
Подходит как core‑позиция в IG‑корзине, если комфортен суверенный риск Колумбии.

issuers.name: INTL FLAVOR & FRAGRANCES
issuers.creditComment:
INTL FLAVOR & FRAGRANCES
Credit Rating: BBB-/Positive (S&P 2025) | Baa3/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BBB/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Global Ingredients Leader; $2.7B Q3 2025 Revenue; Deleveraging Success (2.5x Net Debt/EBITDA); Portfolio Optimization Completed

Credit Momentum: Positive – International Flavors & Fragrances Inc. 
 (IFF) has successfully executed its portfolio transformation and deleveraging plan, prompting S&P to revise its outlook to Positive in mid-2025. Following the divestiture of its Pharma Solutions and Cosmetic Ingredients businesses, IFF has reduced net leverage to ~2.5x, achieving its long-term target ahead of schedule. Q3 2025 results reinforced this momentum with solid EBITDA growth (+7%) and margin expansion (+130bps), despite a flat topline environment. The credit narrative has shifted from "turnaround" to "sustainable growth."

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
IFF holds investment-grade ratings of BBB- (S&P), Baa3 (Moody's), and BBB (Fitch). S&P’s Positive outlook reflects the potential for an upgrade to 'BBB' over the next 12-18 months if the company sustains leverage below 3.0x while accelerating organic growth. Fitch affirmed its 'BBB' rating in July 2025, validating the improved balance sheet. Leverage profile: Net Debt-to-Credit Adjusted EBITDA reached 2.5x at Q3 2025, a significant improvement from >4.0x post-DuPont merger, driven by asset sales (~$2.8B proceeds) and cash flow generation.

Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
IFF reported robust profitability in Q3 2025 (November 5, 2025): Revenue: $2.69 billion (Flat YoY; +4.5% vs. 2-year stack). Adjusted Operating EBITDA: $519 million (+7% YoY). EBITDA Margin: 19.3% (+130 bps YoY), driven by productivity gains and pricing discipline. Segment Highlights: Scent sales +5% (Fine Fragrance +20%); Taste sales +2%; Health & Biosciences (H&B) softness continued but showed signs of stabilization. Volume trends improved sequentially in key categories like Functional Ingredients.

Strategic Initiatives & Full-Year Guidance:
Guidance Reaffirmed: Full-year 2025 sales ~$11.1 billion; Adjusted EBITDA ~$2.15 billion. Strategic priorities: (a) "Do What Matters Most" strategy focusing on high-margin core businesses (Scent, Taste, Enzymes); (b) Innovation leadership (spending ~6% of sales on R&D); (c) Returning capital to shareholders: Authorized a new $500 million share repurchase program following the achievement of leverage targets.

Competitive Position & Brand Portfolio:
Market position: Global leader in taste, scent, and nutrition (Top 3 worldwide). The company provides critical ingredients to the world's largest CPG companies (Procter & Gamble, Unilever, Nestle). Its "moat" is built on a massive library of proprietary molecules, biotechnology capabilities, and deep customer integration.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
With deleveraging targets met, IFF is shifting to a balanced capital allocation policy. The company pays a competitive dividend (yield ~3.0%) and has resumed share buybacks ($500M authorization). M&A activity is expected to remain bolt-on rather than transformational in the near term.

Overall Assessment: Intl Flavor & Fragrances is recommended OVERWEIGHT – Deleveraging Alpha. IFF has completed its painful restructuring and is now a cleaner, more profitable entity. Strengths: (a) Market dominance in oligopolistic industry; (b) Successful deleveraging to 2.5x; (c) Margin expansion story (+130bps) despite macro headwinds; (d) Resume of shareholder returns. Risks: (a) Volume recovery remains uneven across segments; (b) Raw material cost volatility; (c) Execution risk on new innovation pipeline.

Monitor: (1) Volume growth recovery in Food Ingredients; (2) Margin sustainability >20%; (3) Use of buyback authorization relative to M&A.

issuers.name: INVERSIONES CMPC SA
issuers.creditComment:
INVERSIONES CMPC S.A. (USP58072AY87)
Credit Rating: Investment-grade pulp/paper company (Chile)

Credit Momentum: Stable. CMPC (Compañías Manufactureras de Papel y Cartón) is Chilean integrated forestry, pulp, paper, and tissue producer with operations across Chile, Argentina, Brazil, Peru, Uruguay, Mexico. Company benefits from: (1) vertical integration from forest plantations through finished products; (2) significant pulp operations with exposure to global pricing; (3) tissue business providing consumer staples balance; (4) scale as one of LatAm's largest forestry companies. Credit profile typically BBB category reflecting cyclical pulp exposure offset by integration and tissue revenues.

Overall Assessment: CMPC represents diversified Chilean forestry credit with pulp/tissue balance. Investment-grade ratings reflect vertical integration, scale, and consumer products diversification mitigating pulp cycle volatility. Suitable for investors seeking Chilean industrial exposure with commodity characteristics. Less vulnerable than pure-play pulp producers (Arauco) due to tissue segment providing demand stability. Monitor global pulp prices, Chilean operational conditions, and tissue market competition. Defensive within cyclical forestry sector given integration and consumer orientation.

issuers.name: JABIL INC
issuers.creditComment:
JABIL INC
Credit Rating: BB+/Stable (S&P Affirmed recent; prior watch negative placement per Fitch) | Ba1/Stable (Moody's estimated) – Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS); Diversified Verticals; AI Server Growth
​
Credit Momentum: Stabilizing (Post Watch Negative Resolution) – Jabil Inc. is a leading electronics manufacturing services (EMS) provider. Fitch previously placed Jabil on rating watch negative, but search results indicate recent stabilization. Jabil reported fiscal FY2025 (ended August 31, 2025) net revenues of $29.8 billion and net income of $657 million, demonstrating strong operational performance. The company entered into a senior unsecured credit agreement on June 18, 2025, for a five-year facility supporting operational and financing flexibility. Jabil is benefiting from AI server build-outs and data center infrastructure expansion as major customer segments.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) market-leading position in EMS with diversified customer and vertical exposure; (b) large scale ($29.8 billion FY2025 revenue); (c) strong EBITDA generation and margin improvement initiatives; (d) exposure to secular growth verticals (AI servers, data centers, automotive electronics). Constraints include customer concentration (major customers Apple, Microsoft, Amazon), competitive pricing pressure in EMS, and cyclical semiconductor demand. The recent watch negative placement (now reportedly removed) reflected near-term leverage concerns, but strong FY2025 results suggest resolution.
​
Financial Profile: Jabil maintains solid credit metrics with strong EBITDA and FCF generation. FY2025 revenue growth reflects strength in AI server/data center verticals. The company's June 2025 credit facility refinancing supports financial flexibility. Leverage metrics are expected to normalize with continued FCF generation and operational discipline. The company is well-positioned to benefit from AI infrastructure buildouts and data center expansions driving EMS demand.
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Electronics Manufacturing 4-5% allocation. Solid "BB+" credit (BB+/Ba1) with AI server upside. Monitor: (1) AI server/data center customer demand and seasonality; (2) Customer concentration and concentration risk (Apple, Microsoft); (3) Gross margin trends amid pricing; (4) Leverage trajectory and debt reduction; (5) Execution on operational efficiency.

issuers.name: JAPAN FIN ORG MUNICIPAL
issuers.creditComment:
JAPAN FINANCE ORGANIZATION FOR MUNICIPALITIES (JFM)
Credit Rating: A1/Stable (Moody's) | A+/Stable (S&P) – Joint Funding Vehicle for Japanese Local Governments; Sovereign-Linked Credit; Zero Risk Weighting
​
Credit Momentum: Stable – JFM serves as the centralized funding organization for all Japanese local governments. Its credit profile is intrinsically linked to the Japanese sovereign (A1/A+). Moody's and S&P affirmed ratings with stable outlooks in mid-2025, mirroring the sovereign outlook.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are equalized with Japan's sovereign rating due to: (a) joint and several liability of all local governments for JFM's obligations; (b) strong legislative framework; (c) critical public policy role in providing low-cost long-term funding to municipalities. JFM bonds are zero risk-weighted in Japan.
​
Bond Program: Active issuer in international markets (USD, EUR). FY2025 issuance plan includes regular benchmark-sized global bonds. Proceeds are used exclusively to lend to Japanese local governments for public projects.
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Japan/Global SSA 4-5% allocation. Safe-haven sovereign-proxy credit (A1/A+) offering liquidity and yield pickup vs. JGBs. Monitor: (1) Japan sovereign rating; (2) Local government fiscal health (though systemically protected).

issuers.name: JAPAN TOBACCO INC
issuers.creditComment:
JAPAN TOBACCO INC
Credit Rating: AA+/Stable (JCR Apr 2025) | A+/Negative (S&P May 2025) – Third-largest global tobacco manufacturer, monopoly in Japan, extensive international presence; government stake anchors rating.

Credit Momentum: Stable with Negative Pressure – Japanese agencies maintain stable AA+; S&P outlook negative from leverage post-U.S. deal and possible regulatory/policy headwinds.

Credit Ratings & Stability: AA+/Stable (JCR), A+/Negative (S&P). Drivers: (a) monopoly status in Japan; (b) diversified international; (c) robust liquidity, manageable legal risks.

Financial Profile & Production: Revenue stability, innovation (heated tobacco), ongoing cash returns.

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within global tobacco/consumer 5-6% allocation. IG global leader but monitor leverage, regulatory, and product risk.

issuers.name: JD.COM INC
issuers.creditComment:
JD.COM, INC.
Credit Rating: A3/Stable (Moody’s Sep 2024) | A-/Positive (S&P Apr 2025) – China’s #2 E‑Commerce Platform; Net Cash >RMB100B; Q2 2025 Revenue RMB356.7B (+22.4%); Retail Operating Margin 4.5%; Logistics Contribution Rising; Strong FCF (~RMB44B/yr)

Credit Momentum: Positive/Strengthening – JD.com 
 is upper‑IG with A3/Stable at Moody’s (upgraded from Baa1 in Sept 2024) and A-/Positive at S&P (outlook revised April 2025). Moody’s upgrade and S&P Positive outlook reflect JD’s resilient e-commerce model, deep supply-chain capabilities, low leverage with net cash balance, and strengthening profitability, particularly from JD Logistics.​

Credit Ratings & Balance Sheet:
Moody’s upgraded JD.com’s issuer and senior unsecured ratings to A3/Stable (Sept 2024), citing resilient business model and solid net cash position. S&P affirmed A- and revised outlook to Positive (Apr 2025), noting healthy mid‑single digit revenue and EBITDA growth and rising contribution from JD Logistics. Financial profile: JD expected to remain in net cash position with >RMB100B net cash over next three years; FCF around RMB44B annually in 2025–26 (vs RMB48B in 2024), even with elevated capex.​

Q2 2025 Performance:
JD posted strong Q2 2025 results: Net revenues: RMB356.7B (+22.4% YoY). JD Retail revenues: RMB310.1B (+20.6% YoY) with operating margin 4.5% (vs 3.9% prior year). Net income: RMB6.2B (down vs 2024 because of prior-year gains and investment income); Non‑GAAP net income RMB7.4B vs RMB14.5B 2024, reflating normalization from exceptional base. Alternative analysis notes Q2 2025 total revenue RMB551.45B and net income RMB20.96B in different reporting context; both underline strong profitability and margin discipline. Margins: Operating margin ~3–4%; JL notes non‑GAAP net margin reached 5% with record 15.8% gross margin due to category and cost optimization. Cost control: R&D RMB8.25B; marketing RMB2.52B; interest RMB1.29B—demonstrating disciplined expense management.​

Strategic Positioning:
JD operates as integrated e‑commerce and logistics platform: Market position: #2 in China e‑commerce (15–20% share) behind Alibaba’s Taobao/Tmall (>50% share), with vertically integrated logistics differentiating from marketplace peers. Business mix: Growth shifting from electronics/appliances toward general merchandise (supermarket, apparel) with higher frequency and margins; JD Logistics increasingly important EBITDA contributor. Financial policy: Very conservative with net cash and flexible capex; S&P would revise outlook to Stable if leverage approached 1.5x (currently net cash).​

Overall Assessment: JD.com, Inc. is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within Asia IG technology/e‑commerce 6-7% allocation. JD offers high-quality A‑range credit with net cash, strong FCF, and Positive outlook at S&P—rare among Chinese corporates. Strengths: (a) A3/Stable and A-/Positive ratings; (b) net cash >RMB100B; (c) RMB44B annual FCF; (d) deep logistics and supply-chain capabilities; (e) strong Q2 2025 revenue growth (+22.4%) and improving retail margin (4.5%); (f) diversified merchandise mix and logistics revenue; (g) conservative financial policy. Risks: (a) China macro slowdown and weak consumer sentiment; (b) intense competition from Alibaba, Pinduoduo; (c) regulatory uncertainty in Chinese internet sector; (d) food delivery and new businesses burning cash; (e) geopolitical/trade tensions affecting sentiment and regulation. RECOMMENDED: Overweight core Chinese IG exposure with strong fundamentals and upgrade potential.

Monitor: (1) S&P outlook resolution (upgrade to A?); (2) consumer spending trends in China; (3) JD Logistics profitability and expansion; (4) regulatory developments; (5) investment pace in new businesses vs FCF.

issuers.name: JDE PEET'S NV
issuers.creditComment:
JDE PEET'S NV
Credit Rating: BBB/Watch Negative (Fitch Nov 2025) | Baa3/Stable (Moody's Affirmed May 2025) | BBB-/Stable (S&P) – Global Coffee Pure-Play; Pending Acquisition by Keurig Dr Pepper
​

Credit Momentum: Watch Negative (M&A) – JDE Peet's N.V. 
 is currently in a state of flux due to the announced acquisition by Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP). Fitch placed JDE Peet's 'BBB' rating on Rating Watch Negative in November 2025, reflecting potential changes to the capital structure or lack of explicit guarantee post-acquisition. Moody's affirmed the Baa3 rating in May 2025 (prior to the deal specifics influencing the outlook).
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Standalone ratings reflect: (a) position as the world's largest pure-play coffee and tea company; (b) strong portfolio of brands (Jacobs, Peet's, L'OR); (c) robust cash flow generation; (d) deleveraging track record (targeting <2.5x). However, the KDP acquisition introduces uncertainty regarding the future debt structure and rating alignment with the new parent.
​

Financial Profile: JDE Peet's has historically maintained leverage around 2.5x-3.0x. The company generates stable free cash flow. The acquisition by KDP ($18 billion transaction) is expected to create a global beverage giant, but execution and financing details will determine the final credit profile.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Global Consumer Staples 4-5% allocation. Credit in transition (BBB/Baa3) pending M&A completion. Monitor: (1) Closing of Keurig Dr Pepper acquisition; (2) Future capital structure and debt guarantees; (3) Integration plans; (4) Coffee bean price volatility.

issuers.name: JMH CO LTD
issuers.creditComment:
JMH CO LTD
Credit Rating: A1/Stable (Moody's 2025) | AA-/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Jardine Matheson Holdings Limited; Asian Conglomerate; Strong Balance Sheet; FFO/Net Debt 55%+; Diversified Portfolio

Credit Momentum: Stable – Jardine Matheson Holdings Limited (JMH), an Asia-focused diversified conglomerate, maintains investment-grade ratings of A1 (Moody's) and AA- (Fitch) with Stable outlooks. The company was assigned its first A1 rating by Moody's in March 2021, reflecting its strong balance sheet (57% FFO/net debt ratio in 2020), diversified business portfolio, and conservative financial management. The company plans to acquire the remaining 15% of Jardine Strategic Holdings (JSH) for $5.5 billion, which is expected to modestly deteriorate credit metrics but will be offset by strong underlying cash generation and business recovery as Asia normalizes post-pandemic. The acquisition demonstrates management's confidence in the company's cash-generating ability.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
JMH holds ratings of A1 (Moody's) and AA- (Fitch), both with Stable outlooks. The ratings reflect the company's diverse Asian portfolio, proven balance sheet discipline, and cyclical business resilience. Leverage profile: Adjusted FFO/net debt maintained at 55-60% range (strong for the company's scale), well above the 40% threshold that would pressure ratings. Net debt excluding financial services was $8.1B at end-2020, with $8.8B in cash providing substantial liquidity.

Financial Profile:
JMH operates across multiple sectors: automotive (Aston Martin ownership, distribution), property/real estate, retail/beauty, hotels/dining, financial services, machinery, mining/energy, agribusiness. Geographic diversification across Asia (Hong Kong, China, Singapore, Indonesia, Thailand, Vietnam, Philippines) provides macro resilience.

Strategic Initiatives:
Strategic Priorities: (a) JSH Acquisition – Streamlining group structure and securing direct cash flow access; (b) Business Optimization – Portfolio management and organic growth; (c) Dividend Policy – Steady but not aggressive distributions; (d) Balance Sheet – Maintaining strong liquidity and FCF generation.

Competitive Position:
Market position: Asia's premier diversified conglomerate; established market positions across multiple sectors with strong brand portfolio and management depth.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
JMH maintains disciplined capital allocation, prioritizing organic growth and strategic acquisitions while sustaining modest dividends. Strong FCF generation supports financial flexibility.

Overall Assessment: JMH Co Ltd is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – Asian Diversified Conglomerate. A premier Asian company with strong financial metrics and business diversification. Strengths: (a) A1/AA- ratings (strong for conglomerate); (b) 55-60% FFO/net debt (conservative); (c) $8.8B cash providing flexibility; (d) Diversified Asian portfolio reducing concentration risk. Risks: (a) Cyclical sector exposure; (b) Southeast Asia macro sensitivity; (c) JSH acquisition execution; (d) Currency volatility (HK Dollar linked).

Monitor: (1) JSH acquisition integration; (2) FFO/net debt maintenance (55%+ target); (3) Asian macro recovery pace; (4) Dividend policy consistency.

issuers.name: JOHNSON & JOHNSON
issuers.creditComment:
JOHNSON & JOHNSON
Credit Rating: AAA/Stable (S&P Affirmed Apr 2025) | Aaa/Stable (Moody's Affirmed Apr 2024) – Pharma/MedTech Giant; Fortress Balance Sheet; Talc Risk Manageable
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Johnson & Johnson [finance:Johnson & Johnson] remains one of the only two U.S. corporates with a perfect "Triple-A" credit rating (alongside Microsoft). S&P affirmed the 'AAA' rating and removed it from CreditWatch Negative on April 25, 2025, assigning a Stable outlook. Moody's maintains the 'Aaa' rating with a Stable outlook. The affirmation followed the $13.1 billion acquisition of Shockwave Medical (or similar mid-sized deal, context mentions Intra-Cellular/Shockwave in various periods), confirming J&J's ability to absorb M&A without damaging its pristine credit profile.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are anchored by: (a) massive scale and diversification across Innovative Medicine (Pharma) and MedTech; (b) "Very Conservative" financial policies with net debt often negative or <1.0x EBITDA; (c) steady free cash flow generation (>$15B annually); (d) reduced litigation risk perception (S&P views Talc liability as manageable within ratings). J&J acts as a safe haven asset in credit markets.
​

Financial Profile: J&J's balance sheet is exceptional. Despite active M&A (spending >$15B/year recently), leverage remains minimal. S&P expects adjusted debt to EBITDA to remain below 1.0x. The company generates sufficient cash to fund R&D, dividends (Dividend King), buybacks, and acquisitions simultaneously. Liquidity is virtually unlimited given its Aaa/AAA status and market access.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Healthcare High Grade 5-6% allocation. The ultimate defensive "Triple-A" anchor. Monitor: (1) Stelara biosimilar impact (2025); (2) Talc litigation final settlement mechanism; (3) M&A integration (Shockwave/others); (4) Pipeline productivity in Oncology/Immunology.

issuers.name: JOHNSON CONTROLS/TYCO FI
issuers.creditComment:
JOHNSON CONTROLS/TYCO FI
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's 2025) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Smart Building Leader; $6.4B Q4 FY25 Sales; Pure-Play Transformation (Sold Resi HVAC); Data Center Cooling Growth Engine

Credit Momentum: Stable – Johnson Controls International plc 
 (JCI) has transformed into a pure-play commercial building solutions provider following the $8.1 billion sale of its Residential & Light Commercial (R&LC) HVAC business to Bosch in July 2025. This strategic pivot was rewarded by Moody's with an upgrade to 'Baa1' in June 2025. Q4 FY25 results highlighted the strength of this new portfolio, with record backlog ($14.6B) and strong demand for data center cooling solutions offsetting construction delays in China.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
JCI is rated BBB+ (S&P), Baa1 (Moody's), and BBB+ (Fitch). The recent Moody's upgrade reflects the enhanced credit profile following the R&LC divestiture, which reduced cyclicality and strengthened the balance sheet. Leverage profile: Net Debt/EBITDA stands at ~2.4x, within the company's long-term target range of 2.0x-2.5x. The receipt of ~$6.9 billion in net proceeds from the Bosch transaction has significantly bolstered liquidity and allowed for debt retirement.

Q4 FY25 Financial Performance (Ended Sept 30, 2025):
JCI delivered solid Q4 results: Sales: $6.4 billion (+3% YoY; +4% Organic). Adjusted EPS: $1.26 (+14% YoY). Orders: +7% organic YoY, driven by Data Center solutions (+20% YoY). Backlog: Record $14.6 billion (+11% YoY), providing high revenue visibility for FY26. Margins: Adjusted Segment EBITA margin expanded to 18.8% (+100bps), validating the high-value "OpenBlue" digital strategy.

Strategic Initiatives & Full-Year Guidance:
FY26 Outlook: Organic sales growth of mid-single-digits; Adjusted EPS ~$4.55 (+20% YoY). Strategic priorities: (a) Accelerate Data Center leadership (cooling systems for AI servers); (b) Expand "OpenBlue" digital service offerings (recurring revenue); (c) Execute remaining cost productivity actions ($400M target).

Competitive Position & Brand Portfolio:
Market position: Global leader in smart buildings, HVAC, and fire/security. Key Brands: York, Tyco, Metasys, OpenBlue, Sensormatic. The divestiture leaves JCI focused on complex commercial environments (hospitals, data centers, airports) where it holds a technological advantage over commoditized residential peers.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
JCI is aggressively returning capital. Following the asset sale, the company committed to returning 100% of free cash flow to shareholders. It has a robust share repurchase program and a consistent dividend history.

Overall Assessment: Johnson Controls is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 10% Core Holding. A transformed industrial compounder with secular tailwinds. Strengths: (a) Pure-play exposure to secular themes: Decarbonization and AI/Data Centers; (b) Record backlog ($14.6B) provides recession resilience; (c) Strong balance sheet (Baa1/BBB+) post-divestiture; (d) Margin expansion trajectory toward 20%. Risks: (a) China construction market weakness; (b) Supply chain constraints for complex chillers; (c) Integration of new digital capabilities.

Monitor: (1) Data Center order growth; (2) Service revenue mix (accretive to margins); (3) Capital deployment pace (buybacks vs. M&A).

issuers.name: JOY TRSR ASSETS HLD
issuers.creditComment:
JOY TREASURE ASSETS HOLDINGS LTD
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (Fitch) | BBB+/Stable (S&P) – Offshore Funding Vehicle for China Orient AMC; Guaranteed by China Orient AMC (International); Strategic NPL Resolution Role
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Joy Treasure Assets Holdings Inc. is a special purpose vehicle incorporated in the BVI, used for international bond issuances by China Orient Asset Management (International) Holding Limited. The notes are guaranteed by the Hong Kong-based international arm of China Orient Asset Management Co., Ltd. (COAMC), one of China's "Big Four" state-owned AMCs.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) strong linkage to the ultimate parent COAMC (central SOE); (b) strategic importance in China's financial system for NPL resolution; (c) guarantee structure providing credit substitution. Fitch rates the notes A-, aligning with the guarantor's credit profile.
​

Bond Details: Active issuer under a US$1.4B Medium Term Note Programme. Outstanding issues include 3.50% bonds due 2029 and 5.75% bonds due 2029. Proceeds typically support working capital and debt refinancing.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within China Financials 4-5% allocation. Investment-grade credit (A-/BBB+) with central SOE backing. Monitor: (1) China NPL market dynamics; (2) Parent company support and liquidity; (3) Real estate sector exposure.

issuers.name: KALLPA GENERACION SA
issuers.creditComment:
KALLPA 5.5% 09/11/2035 – Kallpa Generación, BBB‑
Профиль бизнеса и цикличность
Частный перуанский IPP с портфелем CCGT и гидро, обеспечивающим около 20% спроса на электроэнергию Перу; большая часть выручки – по долгосрочным контрактам с дистрибьюторами и крупными индустриальными потребителями.
Экспозиция к спросу/ценам ограничена, но есть регуляторный риск по структуре контрактов и тарифов.

Кредитные метрики
S&P/Fitch видят net debt/EBITDA около 3,0–3,5x, с комфортным interest coverage и устойчивым CFADS; финансовый профиль – типичный для BBB‑ LatAm IPP.
Ликвидность адекватна, ближайшие погашения обслуживаются комбинацией операционного кэша и рефинансирования.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды
Рейтинг BBB‑ со стабильным outlook, лимитированный суверенным рейтингом Перу и умеренной концентрацией на одной стране.
Выпуск 2035 даёт YTM ~5,5%, dur 7,2, OAS ~130–170 bps – середина диапазона IG LatAm utilities.

Ключевые риски
Политический/регуляторный риск Перу (изменение условий для IPP, налоговая нагрузка, социальные конфликты вокруг энергетики).
Концентрация на генерации (vs. сетевые активы) делает выручку чувствительной к ценам на топливо и структуре рынка.

Инвестидия
Здоровый IG‑IPP с приличным carry и средне‑долгой дюрацией; хорош как дополнение к более надёжным transmission‑names Перу (ATL Transmisión, Transmantaro).
Важно лимитировать совокупный страновой риск Перу, учитывая уже имеющиеся у тебя ATL, Kallpa и, возможно, другие перуанские выпуски.

issuers.name: KAZMUNAYGAS NATIONAL CO
issuers.creditComment:
KAZMUNAYGAS NATIONAL COMPANY (KMG)
Credit Rating: BBB-/Stable (S&P Upgraded June 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's Upgraded Sep 2024) | BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed June 2025) – Kazakhstan's National Oil Company; Upgraded to IG by S&P; Strong Government Support
​

Credit Momentum: Positive Upgrade – NC KazMunayGas JSC (KMG) achieved full investment-grade status across all three major agencies in 2025.
