S&P upgraded KMG to 'BBB-' from 'BB+' in June 2025, citing improved standalone credit profile and financial stability. Moody's upgraded KMG to Baa1 in late 2024.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are driven by: (a) status as Kazakhstan's vertically integrated national oil company; (b) state ownership (via Samruk-Kazyna); (c) strong government support; (d) significantly reduced debt burden; (e) stable oil production and pipeline operations. The S&P upgrade aligns KMG's rating with the sovereign.
​

Financial Profile: KMG has strengthened its balance sheet, reducing leverage and maintaining robust liquidity. The company is managing capital expenditures for new projects and petrochemical expansion while adhering to a balanced dividend policy.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Emerging Market Oil & Gas 5-6% allocation. Premier energy credit (BBB-/Baa1) with sovereign alignment and improving fundamentals. Monitor: (1) Oil price volatility; (2) TCO expansion project completion; (3) Dividend payouts to the state; (4) Geopolitical risks affecting export routes (CPC pipeline).

issuers.name: KELLANOVA
issuers.creditComment:
KELLANOVA (FORMERLY KELLOGG COMPANY SNACKS BUSINESS)
Credit Rating: BBB/Rating Watch Positive (Fitch Oct 2025) | BBB/Stable (S&P 2024) | Baa2/Stable (Moody’s 2024) – Global Snacks & Cereal Company; Pringles & Cheez-It Leadership; Being Acquired by Mars Inc. for $35.9B; Event Risk from LBO-Style Transaction; Standalone Profile Solid

Credit Momentum: Event‑Driven/Positive Watch – Kellanova 
 (snacks business spun from Kellogg in 2023) is rated BBB/Stable at S&P, Baa2/Stable at Moody’s, and BBB/Rating Watch Positive at Fitch following Mars Inc.’s agreement (Aug 2024) to acquire Kellanova for ~$35.9B including debt. Fitch’s RWP reflects expectation of improved business profile under Mars and potentially stronger support, but transaction introduces event risk for bondholders; simultaneously, Moody’s and S&P placed Mars’ A‑range ratings under review for downgrade due to deal leverage, not Kellanova’s CDS per se.​

Credit Ratings & Transaction Structure:
Fitch notes Kellanova’s ‘BBB’/RWP ratings reflect leading global snack share anchored by Pringles and Cheez-It brands and meaningful geographic diversification. Mars will pay $83.5 per share (33% premium) financed with $29B interim loan largely to be refinanced with long-term debt; transaction expected to close H1 2025 and Kellanova will become part of Mars Snacking division. Moody’s and S&P signaled possible downgrades of Mars from A1/A+ once transaction completes, implying the combined entity could settle around A2–A3/ A– territory if deleveraging plans proceed.​

Standalone Business Profile:
Kellanova specializes in snacks, cereals, noodles, and frozen foods, with portfolio heavily weighted toward snacks following spin-off of WK Kellogg cereal business in 2023. Brand strength: Cheez-It and Pringles provide strong category leadership; EM presence via noodles and cereals in LatAm and Asia adds diversification. Credit metrics (pre-deal): Mid-BBB leverage with stable FCF supported by resilient snack demand; Fitch views profitability and cash generation as solid within rating category.​

Overall Assessment: Kellanova is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within packaged foods 4-5% allocation, with emphasis on monitoring transaction completion and post-close capital structure. Strengths: (a) BBB/Baa2 ratings with Fitch RWP suggesting possible tightening under Mars umbrella; (b) strong global snack brands (Pringles, Cheez-It); (c) resilient cash flows from snacking category; (d) geographic diversification; (e) potential strategic benefits from Mars distribution and scale. Risks: (a) Event risk from Mars’ highly leveraged acquisition; (b) possible subordination or structural changes post-closing; (c) integration and synergy execution; (d) potential for more aggressive financial policy under private ownership; (e) slower growth in mature North American snacks markets. RECOMMENDED: Market weight until clarity on post-close capital structure; position as tactical IG snack exposure with event-driven spread opportunity.

Monitor: (1) Deal closing terms and timing; (2) New capital structure and guarantees for legacy Kellanova bonds; (3) Post-deal ratings of Mars and combined snack entity; (4) Deleveraging trajectory and synergy realization; (5) Category growth trends in snacks and emerging markets.

issuers.name: KENNAMETAL INC
issuers.creditComment:
KENNAMETAL INC
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed June 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's estimated) | BBB/Stable (S&P Affirmed with Stable Outlook) – Cutting Tools & Wear-Resistant Materials Leader; Industrial Cyclical; Margin Improvement
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Kennametal Inc. 
 maintains a solid investment-grade credit profile. Fitch affirms the 'BBB' ratings with a Stable outlook as of June 2, 2025. S&P affirmed the 'BBB' issuer credit rating and revised the outlook to Stable (from Negative) reflecting improved business conditions and financial performance. Moody's has Kennametal at investment-grade status (estimated Ba2 or Baa3). The company is a leading manufacturer of cutting tools, wear-resistant materials, and engineered components for diverse industrial applications (aerospace, automotive, energy, industrial machinery).
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) market leadership in engineered materials and cutting tools with strong competitive positioning; (b) diversified customer base across aerospace, automotive, energy sectors; (c) improving operational performance and margin recovery; (d) moderate leverage; (e) stable free cash flow generation. S&P's revision of outlook to Stable reflects confidence in business trajectory. Constraints include exposure to industrial cyclicality, especially aerospace and automotive capital spending cycles.
​

Financial Profile: Kennametal maintains healthy credit metrics with solid EBITDA generation supporting debt service. The company is executing on cost efficiency and margin improvement initiatives. S&P's positive outlook revision signals confidence in sustained business improvement. Liquidity is adequate with access to capital markets and credit facilities. The company invests in R&D for advanced cutting tool materials and digital solutions serving its industrial customer base.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Industrial Materials 4-5% allocation. Solid IG credit (BBB/Baa2) with margin expansion. Monitor: (1) Aerospace and automotive capital spending trends; (2) Cutting tool order rates and backlog; (3) EBITDA margin improvement progress; (4) Leverage maintenance; (5) Free cash flow allocation.

issuers.name: KENTUCKY UTILITIES CO
issuers.creditComment:
KENTUCKY UTILITIES CO
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's 2025) | A-/Stable (Fitch 2025) – LG&E/KU Utility Holding; Regulated Monopoly; Rate Base Growth; ~1.4M Customers

Credit Momentum: Stable – Kentucky Utilities Company (KU), part of the Louisville Gas and Electric Company/Kentucky Utilities holding, maintains solid investment-grade ratings (BBB+/Baa1/A-) with Stable outlooks. The company benefits from a favorable Kentucky regulatory environment with strong rate recovery mechanisms. Moody's affirmed Baa1/Stable (March 2025), citing disciplined capital investment in transmission/distribution modernization and cost recovery through regulatory proceedings. The company continues to execute its capex program (~$2+ billion annually) on rate base expansion, supporting earnings growth and credit metrics stability.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
KU holds ratings of BBB+ (S&P), Baa1 (Moody's), and A- (Fitch), all with Stable outlooks reflecting the regulated utility model and credit-supportive regulatory environment. Leverage profile: The company maintains conservative leverage (<2.0x estimated Net Debt-to-EBITDA), appropriate for a regulated utility with stable, cost-recovered cash flows.

Financial Profile:
KU operates as a regulated electric and natural gas utility serving ~1.4 million customers across Kentucky. The company's business model features cost-plus regulation, allowing recovery of authorized costs with an allowed return on equity (ROE). Rate base growth targets 4-5% annually through capex on infrastructure modernization (smart grid, distribution automation).

Strategic Initiatives:
Strategic Priorities: (a) Rate Base Expansion – $2B+ annual capex targeting 4-5% rate base growth; (b) Grid Modernization – Smart meter deployment, distribution automation, reliability improvements; (c) Renewable Transition – Renewable energy integration while managing stranded coal generation transition.

Competitive Position:
Market position: Regulated monopoly utility in Kentucky service territory; no direct competition due to state regulatory structure.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
KU prioritizes capex on rate base growth. Dividends supported by stable regulatory earnings; capital returned to parent holding company.

Overall Assessment: Kentucky Utilities Co is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – Defensive Regulated Utility. A high-quality utility with credit-supportive regulatory environment. Strengths: (a) BBB+/Baa1/A- stable ratings; (b) Regulated monopoly with cost recovery; (c) Steady rate base growth (4-5% target); (d) Strong regulatory relationships. Risks: (a) Regulatory/allowed ROE pressure; (b) Energy transition capex intensity; (c) Interest rate sensitivity.

Monitor: (1) Rate case outcomes and allowed ROE; (2) Capex execution and rate base growth; (3) Regulatory environment stability.

issuers.name: KEYSIGHT TECHNOLOGIES
issuers.creditComment:
KEYSIGHT TECHNOLOGIES
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (Fitch Affirmed April 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's Upgraded March 2025) | BBB/Positive (S&P Positive Outlook) – Test & Measurement Leader; 5G/6G Tailwinds; Conservative M&A
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgrades & Positive Outlook) – Keysight Technologies [finance:Keysight Technologies] is on an upward credit trajectory. Moody's upgraded the rating to 'Baa1' with a Stable outlook on March 26, 2025. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB+' rating with a Stable outlook in April 2025. S&P maintains a 'BBB' rating but with a Positive outlook (per Apr 2025 bond offering notes), signaling a potential upgrade to BBB+. The momentum is driven by strong profitability, secular growth in 5G/AI/Auto testing, and disciplined capital allocation.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) dominant market share in electronic design and test solutions (R&D focus); (b) high barriers to entry and sticky customer relationships; (c) strong free cash flow margins (>20%); (d) conservative leverage target (<2.0x, typically <1.5x). Recent acquisitions (e.g., Spirent - though faced regulatory delays) are strategic and funded prudently. Risks include cyclicality in semiconductor/electronics demand and trade tensions with China.
​

Financial Profile: Keysight has a very strong balance sheet. Pro forma leverage for acquisitions remains well within investment-grade limits (Moody's cites <2.5x even with deals). The company issued $750 million in senior notes in April 2025 to fund growth, which was well-received. Liquidity is robust with >$1.5 billion in cash and undrawn credit lines. The company manages its capital return program (buybacks) flexibly to protect ratings.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Technology Hardware 4-5% allocation. "Rising Star" to solid BBB+/Baa1. Monitor: (1) Spirent acquisition regulatory close/integration; (2) 5G/6G R&D spending cycle; (3) China export control impacts; (4) Automotive EV/AV testing demand.

issuers.name: KHAZANAH CAPITAL LTD
issuers.creditComment:
KHAZANAH CAPITAL LTD
Credit Rating: A3/Stable (Moody's Affirmed Sep 2025) | A-/Stable (S&P) – Funding Vehicle for Khazanah Nasional; Guaranteed by Malaysia's Sovereign Wealth Fund; Strong Sovereign Linkage
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Khazanah Capital Ltd is a special purpose vehicle for Khazanah Nasional Berhad, Malaysia's sovereign wealth fund. Moody's affirmed the A3 rating with a stable outlook in September 2025. S&P rates the issuer A-.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) Khazanah's status as the investment arm of the Malaysian government; (b) 100% state ownership; (c) high likelihood of extraordinary government support; (d) strong portfolio of strategic national assets (Tenaga, Telekom Malaysia, CIMB, Malaysia Airlines). The ratings typically trade tightly with the Malaysian sovereign (A3/A-).
​

Financial Profile: Khazanah Capital acts as the funding conduit for the group's US$10 billion Global Medium Term Note program. Proceeds are used for investments and general corporate purposes. Khazanah's portfolio value provides strong asset coverage, although leverage is managed within target levels (30-35% Market Value Leverage).
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Asian Sovereign/Quasi-Sovereign 5-6% allocation. High-quality sovereign wealth fund credit (A3/A-) offering diversification across Malaysian strategic sectors. Monitor: (1) Malaysian sovereign rating; (2) Portfolio valuation volatility; (3) Support required for investee companies (e.g., Malaysia Airlines).

issuers.name: KIMBERLY-CLARK CORP
issuers.creditComment:
KIMBERLY-CLARK CORP
Credit Rating: A/Stable (S&P Affirmed Oct 2025) | A2/Stable (Moody's) | A/Stable (Fitch presumed/implied) – Consumer Staples; Margin Recovery; Cost Savings
​

Credit Momentum: Stabilizing to Stable – Kimberly-Clark Corp [finance:Kimberly-Clark Corp] has stabilized its credit profile after a period of pressure. S&P affirmed the 'A' rating with a Stable outlook on October 15, 2025. Earlier in the year (around May 2025), S&P had revised the outlook to negative due to margin pressures, but the subsequent affirmation indicates operational improvements and effective pricing/cost-saving actions (part of the "Wireing the Organization" restructuring) restored metrics. Moody's maintains an 'A2' rating.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) strong brand portfolio (Huggies, Kleenex, Scott) with #1 or #2 positions globally; (b) essential nature of products (diapers, tissue) providing demand floor; (c) successful cost-savings programs boosting margins back to historical levels; (d) commitment to the single-A rating. Risks include input cost volatility (pulp/energy), private label competition, and FX headwinds.
​

Financial Profile: Kimberly-Clark operates with a leverage target of 1.5x-2.0x (Moody's adjusted). Recent restructuring efforts have improved free cash flow productivity. The company continues to pay a healthy dividend (Dividend King) and execute share buybacks, but within the constraints of the 'A' rating. Liquidity is strong. The stabilization of the outlook suggests the company has successfully navigated the post-COVID inflationary spike.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Consumer Staples 5-6% allocation. Core "Single-A" defensive credit. Monitor: (1) Pulp and energy input cost trends; (2) Market share vs. private label; (3) Restructuring savings realization; (4) Emerging markets growth/FX.

issuers.name: KIMBERLY-CLARK DE MEXICO
issuers.creditComment:
KIMBERLY-CLARK DE MEXICO
Credit Rating: A/Stable (Fitch Affirmed March 2025) | A-/Stable (S&P) | AAA(mex) (National Scale) – Market Leader in Consumer Products; Robust Cash Flow; Conservative Leverage
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Kimberly-Clark de México, S.A.B. de C.V. (KCM) maintains strong investment-grade ratings. Fitch affirmed its international IDR at 'A' and national scale rating at 'AAA(mex)' in March 2025. S&P rates the company 'A-' with a stable outlook. The stable outlook reflects KCM's dominant market position and consistent financial performance.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) undisputed leadership in Mexican personal care (diapers, tissue, feminine care); (b) strong brand equity; (c) highly efficient cost structure; (d) conservative financial policy with low leverage. The company benefits from its strategic association with Kimberly-Clark Corporation (USA), although it operates independently.
​

Financial Profile: KCM has a fortress balance sheet with Net Debt/EBITDA typically below 1.5x. It generates substantial free cash flow, covering dividends and capex. The company has successfully passed through cost inflation and implemented cost-savings programs to protect margins.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Mexican Corporate High Grade 5-6% allocation. Top-tier defensive credit (A/A-) with low volatility. Monitor: (1) Raw material prices (pulp/fiber); (2) FX volatility (MXN/USD); (3) Competitive environment; (4) Consumer purchasing power in Mexico.

issuers.name: KINROSS GOLD CORP
issuers.creditComment:
KINROSS GOLD CORP
Credit Rating: Baa3/Positive (Moody's Outlook Upgraded March 2025) | BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed June 2025) | BBB-/Positive (S&P) – Senior Gold Producer; Q3 2025 Production 530K Au oz; Free Cash Flow $535M; Cash $1.8B; Total Liquidity $3.4B; Net Debt Near Zero​

Credit Momentum: Strong with Upgrade Potential – Kinross Gold Corporation maintains solid investment-grade ratings with recent positive outlook actions reflecting strong operational execution, exceptional free cash flow generation, and near net-cash position.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Kinross maintains Baa3/Positive from Moody's (outlook upgraded March 2025), BBB/Stable from Fitch (affirmed June 2025), and implied BBB-/Positive from S&P. Moody's March 2025 outlook upgrade cited: (a) strong operational performance; (b) exceptional free cash flow generation; (c) near net-cash balance sheet; (d) disciplined capital allocation; (e) shareholder return programs. Potential upgrade if metrics sustained.​

Q3 2025 Exceptional Results: Q3 2025 production 530K Au oz. Revenue ~$1.5B. Attributable free cash flow $535M. Margins expanded on higher gold prices. AISC ~$1,500/oz. Cash and equivalents $1.8B. Total liquidity ~$3.4B. Term loan fully repaid ($200M in Q1). Share repurchases resumed (~$500M target for 2025). Quarterly dividend $0.03/share.​

Balance Sheet & Financial Strength: Near net-cash position with $1.8B cash, total liquidity $3.4B. Minimal debt following term loan repayment. Strong investment-grade metrics well below Baa3 upgrade thresholds. FY2025 production guidance: 2.0M Au oz. AISC guidance ~$1,500/oz.​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within global gold mining 4-5% allocation. Solid investment-grade gold credit with exceptional leverage to gold prices, conservative balance sheet (near net cash), and upgrade potential. Monitor: (1) FY2025 production guidance delivery (2.0M oz); (2) Gold price environment; (3) Share repurchase program execution ($500M target); (4) Moody's upgrade decision timeline; (5) Great Bear project development.

issuers.name: KLA CORP
issuers.creditComment:
KLA CORP
Credit Rating: A/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Dec 2025) | A-/Stable (S&P Affirmed March 2025) | A2/Stable (Moody's) – Semi Cap Equipment; Process Control Monopoly; Cash Machine
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – KLA Corp [finance:KLA Corp] maintains a strong single-A credit profile. Fitch affirmed the 'A' rating with a Stable outlook on December 8, 2025. S&P affirmed the 'A-' rating with a Stable outlook on March 3, 2025. Moody's rates the company 'A2'. KLA is the dominant player in semiconductor process control (inspection/metrology), a critical and high-margin segment of the wafer fab equipment (WFE) market.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are anchored by: (a) near-monopoly market share (~55-60%) in process control; (b) critical role in yield management for advanced nodes (AI/Logic/Memory); (c) exceptional profitability (Operating Margins >40%); (d) strong recurring revenue from services (~22% of sales); (e) robust investment-grade balance sheet. Risks include geopolitical restrictions (China is a large market ~30%) and WFE cyclicality, though KLA is less volatile than peers due to its yield-critical nature.
​

Financial Profile: KLA generates massive free cash flow (FCF margin ~34%). The company has a strong liquidity position with ~$4.5 billion in cash (as of Sept 2025) vs. ~$5.9 billion in debt. It renewed a $1.5 billion revolver in July 2025 with pricing tied to its credit ratings. Capital allocation is shareholder-friendly (targeting >85% FCF return) but disciplined, preserving the 'A' category ratings. Leverage is typically <1.5x.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Tech Hardware/Semis 4-5% allocation. Premium "Cash Machine" credit (A/A2). Monitor: (1) China export control tightening; (2) WFE spending outlook (Foundry/Logic vs. Memory); (3) Services revenue growth resilience; (4) Capital return aggression.

issuers.name: KONINKIJKE AHOLD DLHAIZE
issuers.creditComment:
KONINKIJKE AHOLD DLHAIZE
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's 2025) | NR (Fitch) – Transatlantic Retail Giant; €22.5B Q3 2025 Sales; Strong US Margins (4.6%); €1B Buyback Program

Credit Momentum: Stable – Koninklijke Ahold Delhaize N.V. 
 demonstrates exceptional resilience in a challenging consumer environment. S&P and Moody's maintain BBB+/Baa1 ratings, underscoring the company's robust free cash flow (€2.2B+ annually) and disciplined financial policy. Q3 2025 results beat expectations with a 4.1% operating margin, driven by operational efficiencies in the U.S. (Food Lion/Hannaford) which offset wage inflation. The company continues to balance aggressive shareholder returns (€1B buyback for 2026) with deleveraging.

Credit Ratings & Financial Profile:
Ahold Delhaize holds ratings of BBB+ (S&P) and Baa1 (Moody's). Fitch ratings are currently withdrawn (NR). The investment-grade profile is supported by its geographic diversification (US ~60% / Europe ~40%) and strong market positions. Leverage profile: Lease-adjusted Net Debt/EBITDA remains stable at ~2.4x, comfortably within the 2.5x threshold for the current rating.

Q3 2025 Financial Performance:
Ahold Delhaize reported strong Q3 results (November 5, 2025): Net Sales: €22.5 billion (+6.1% at constant rates). Comparable Sales (ex-gas): +2.9% (US +1.2%; Europe +6.4%). Operating Margin: Underlying margin 4.1% (+30bps), with US margin reaching an impressive 4.6%. EPS: Underlying EPS €0.67 (+8.7%). Performance was boosted by the "Save for Our Customers" cost-savings program and strong online sales growth (+12.2%).

Strategic Initiatives & Full-Year Guidance:
Guidance Reaffirmed: FY 2025 Underlying Operating Margin ~4.0%; Free Cash Flow >€2.2 billion. Strategic priorities: (a) Omnichannel growth (growing online capacity); (b) Health & Sustainability (targeting 55% healthy food sales by 2030); (c) Portfolio Pruning (completed closure of underperforming Stop & Shop stores).

Competitive Position & Brand Portfolio:
Market position: Leading supermarket operator on the US East Coast and in Benelux. Key Brands: Food Lion, Stop & Shop, Hannaford, Giant (USA); Albert Heijn, Delhaize (Europe); Bol.com (E-commerce). Food Lion continues to be the star performer with 40+ consecutive quarters of positive comp sales.

Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:
Ahold Delhaize is a "Cash Cow" for shareholders. It announced a new €1 billion share buyback program for 2026, continuing its tradition of returning excess cash. Dividend payout ratio targets 40-50% of underlying EPS.

Overall Assessment: Koninklijke Ahold Dlhaize is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – Defensive Income Play. A high-quality, low-beta retailer. Strengths: (a) Strong US dollar exposure (currency hedge for Euro investors); (b) Consistent margin delivery (~4%) despite inflation; (c) Shareholder-friendly capital return policy; (d) Market leadership in consolidated regions. Risks: (a) Highly competitive US grocery market (Walmart/Amazon); (b) Wage inflation pressure; (c) Potential regulatory changes in Europe.

Monitor: (1) US comparable sales momentum; (2) Stop & Shop revitalization progress; (3) Free cash flow conversion.

issuers.name: KONINKLIJKE PHILIPS NV
issuers.creditComment:
KONINKLIJKE PHILIPS NV
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Affirmed/Outlook Revised to Stable) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch Affirmed March 2025) – Global Health Technology Leader; Recovering from Respironics Recall; Improved Clarity on Litigation
​

Credit Momentum: Stabilizing – S&P revised its outlook on Philips to Stable from Negative in 2024/2025 and affirmed the 'BBB+' rating. This follows the settlement of the Respironics personal injury litigation for $1.1 billion, which removed a major overhang. Moody's rates the company Baa1, having maintained a stable outlook post-settlement visibility. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB+' rating with a stable outlook in March 2025.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) leading market positions in diagnostic imaging, image-guided therapy, and patient monitoring; (b) resilient order book; (c) recurring revenue streams (~40% of sales); (d) clarity on legal liabilities. The stable outlook reflects improved earnings visibility and the expectation that leverage will remain within rating thresholds.
​

Financial Profile: Philips is prioritizing deleveraging and cash flow generation. S&P expects adjusted Debt/EBITDA to improve to 2.5x-3.0x in 2025 as one-off costs subside and operating margins recover to 13-14%. Free operating cash flow is projected to be stable at ~€400-500 million in 2025 after settlement payments. Liquidity is solid with a €1 billion undrawn RCF.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Global MedTech 5-6% allocation. Recovery play (BBB+/Baa1) with reduced tail risk. Monitor: (1) Execution of productivity savings; (2) Supply chain stability; (3) Finalization of consent decree remediation; (4) China market demand.

issuers.name: KOREA EAST-WEST POWER CO
issuers.creditComment:
KOREA EAST-WEST POWER CO LTD
Credit Rating: Aa2/Stable (Moody's Affirmed Dec 2025) | AA/Stable (S&P) | AA-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Oct 2025) – State-Owned Generator; 100% Owned by KEPCO; Strategic Importance to Power Supply
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Korea East-West Power (EWP) continues to maintain high investment-grade ratings, closely aligned with its parent KEPCO and the Korean sovereign. Moody's affirmed the Aa2 rating with a stable outlook in December 2025, and Fitch assigned an 'AA-' rating to its USD notes in October 2025.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are equalized with KEPCO (and virtually the sovereign) due to: (a) 100% ownership by KEPCO; (b) critical role in national power generation; (c) high strategic incentive for government support. EWP operates key thermal power plants and is expanding into renewables.
​

Financial Profile: EWP benefits from the cost-based pool market structure which ensures recovery of fuel costs. While the energy transition requires capex, the strong parent linkage provides a safety net. Fitch highlights the "High" operational and strategic incentive for support.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Korea Quasi-Sovereign 4-5% allocation. Core utility credit (Aa2/AA/AA-) acting as a proxy for KEPCO/Korea sovereign risk. Monitor: (1) KEPCO's financial health; (2) Government energy policy (renewable targets); (3) Fuel price pass-through mechanisms.

issuers.name: KOREA ELECTRIC POWER
issuers.creditComment:
KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPCO)
Credit Rating: Aa2/Stable (Moody's) | AA/Stable (S&P Affirmed Oct 2025) | AA-/Stable (Fitch) – Korea's Integrated Power Utility; 51% Gov't Owned; Regulated Monopoly; Profitability Recovering
​

Credit Momentum: Stable with Improving Profitability – KEPCO 
 maintains ratings equalized with the South Korean sovereign. S&P affirmed the 'AA' rating in October 2025. Fitch forecasts continued profitability recovery in 2025, with operating profit expected to reach KRW 12-13 trillion, driven by tariff adjustments and stabilizing fuel costs.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) absolute monopoly in transmission and distribution; (b) dominant generation share (~70% via GENCOs); (c) legislative mandate for government support (KEPCO Act). The company is critical to Korea's industrial economy. S&P assigned an 'AA' rating to proposed USD senior unsecured notes in October 2025.
​

Financial Profile: KEPCO is recovering from massive deficits incurred during the energy crisis (2021-2023). Debt remains elevated (~KRW 200 trillion), but improved cash flows are stabilizing the balance sheet. The government's commitment to normalizing tariffs is key to deleveraging.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Asia Utilities 5-6% allocation. Premier quasi-sovereign utility (Aa2/AA) with improving fundamentals. Monitor: (1) Tariff hike implementation; (2) Global energy prices; (3) Political will for further rate normalization; (4) Sovereign rating stability.

issuers.name: KOREA GAS CORP
issuers.creditComment:
KOREA GAS CORPORATION (KOGAS)
Credit Rating: AA/Stable (S&P 2025) | Aa2/Stable (Moody's 2025) | AA-/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Government-Related Entity (GRE) Republic of Korea; World's Largest LNG Importer; 5 LNG Receiving Terminals (77 Storage Tanks); 4,945 km Pipeline Network; $16.3B Bond Debt Outstanding; ~78.8% Korea LNG Import Market Share

Credit Momentum: Stable – Korea Gas Corporation 
 maintains high investment-grade ratings (AA/Aa2/AA-) equalized or one notch below the Republic of Korea sovereign rating (AA/Aa2/AA-), reflecting government-related entity (GRE) status with "almost certain" likelihood of extraordinary government support under S&P's methodology, monopoly position in Korea's LNG import/wholesale market, and strategic importance to Korea's energy security. Despite structural challenges including elevated receivables from city gas utilities and KEPCO 
, significant leverage, and declining domestic LNG demand from energy transition policies, KOGAS benefits from regulatory tariff adjustments, diversified long-term LNG supply contracts (Qatar, Woodside, BP, TotalEnergies 
), and ongoing infrastructure investment supporting credit stability:

Credit Ratings & Government Support Structure:
Korea Gas Corporation maintains AA/Stable ratings from S&P, Aa2/Stable from Moody's, and AA-/Stable from Fitch, equalized with or one notch below Korea's sovereign rating (AA/Aa2/AA-). KOGAS is classified as government-related entity (GRE) under rating agency methodologies, benefiting from: (a) Ownership structure: Korean government holds ~54% direct ownership via Korea Development Bank (KDB) and other state entities, with additional shares publicly traded on Korea Exchange (KOSPI); (b) S&P GRE assessment: "Almost certain" likelihood of extraordinary government support reflecting critical role in national energy security and strategic policy objectives; (c) Moody's government-related issuer (GRI) assessment: High probability of government support based on strategic importance and legal framework; (d) Fitch GRE criteria: Government ownership exceeding 50% combined with strategic policy importance leading to equalized ratings. KOGAS was established under the Korea Gas Corporation Act in 1983 as statutory monopoly for LNG imports, regasification, and wholesale distribution, creating legal framework supporting government backing. $11 billion Global Medium-Term Note (MTN) program enables diversified international capital markets access.​

Q1 2025 Financial Performance & Operating Metrics:
KOGAS reported Q1 2025 results: Sales of KRW 106,404 million (vs KRW 90,660 million prior year +17.4%); Net income of KRW 367,482 million (vs KRW 406,356 million prior year -9.5%); Basic EPS KRW 4,199 (vs KRW 4,715 prior year -10.9%). Revenue increase reflects higher LNG import volumes and pricing dynamics, while net income decline reflects margin compression from regulated tariff constraints. Receivables/leverage profile: Rating agencies highlight sizeable receivables from downstream customers (city gas utilities, KEPCO power plants) and leverage as structural credit risks. KOGAS was able to raise tariffs for residential and general-use city gas by KRW 1/megajoule in 2024, with Fitch expecting gradual reduction in receivables over time supporting credit improvement. LNG sales trends: August 2025 gas sales decreased 10.8% YoY reflecting weakening domestic demand from declining power sector LNG consumption. Year-to-date (July 2025) LNG imports totaled 26.64 million mt (vs 26.48 million mt prior year +0.6%), with Australia (8.47 million mt), Qatar (4.33 million mt), and Malaysia (3.93 million mt) as top suppliers. KOGAS maintains ~78.8% share of Korea's LNG imports, with market liberalization scheduled from 2025 potentially introducing modest competition.​

LNG Supply Portfolio & Long-Term Contracts:
KOGAS maintains diversified long-term LNG supply portfolio securing supply through 2030s-2040s: Qatar: July 2021 contract with QatarEnergy (formerly Qatar Petroleum) for 2 MTPA over 20 years starting 2025; Woodside: February 2024 agreement for 5 MTPA over 10.5 years from 2026; BP: April 2024 agreement for up to 9.8 MT over 11 years starting mid-2026; TotalEnergies: September 2025 agreement for 1 MTPA over 10 years starting late 2027, increasing TotalEnergies supply to KOGAS to 3 MTPA from 2028; Cheniere: Long-term contract for 3.5 MTPA of U.S. LNG. This diversified portfolio provides supply security while exposing KOGAS to long-term pricing dynamics. Korea began importing U.S. LNG in 2017 following shale gas boom and Korea-U.S. Free Trade Agreement negotiations. U.S. Alaska LNG project participation remains under discussion with Korean government delegation visiting Alaska in June 2025, though economic viability concerns and declining domestic demand warrant caution.​

Infrastructure & Strategic Assets:
KOGAS operates Korea's largest LNG infrastructure: LNG receiving terminals: 5 terminals (Incheon, Pyeongtaek, Tongyeong, Samcheok, Aewol/Jeju) with 77 LNG storage tanks providing substantial regasification capacity; Dangjin terminal (under construction): Phase 1 completion expected May 2027, Phase 2 October 2028, featuring four 270,000 kl LNG tanks contracted with Doosan Heavy Industries; Pipeline network: 4,945 km natural gas transmission pipeline network serving Korean industrial, commercial, and residential customers; LNG bunkering: Korea LNG Bunkering subsidiary established January 2021, operating Korea's first dedicated LNG bunkering vessel since May 2023, targeting 1.4 MTPA LNG sales for LNG-fueled ships by 2030. KOGAS also holds international upstream investments including stake in Prelude floating LNG facility (Australia) with Shell 
, Inpex 
, and CPC Corporation, as well as Mozambique LNG development participation.​

Strategic Challenges & Energy Transition:
KOGAS faces structural challenges from Korea's energy transition policies: Declining LNG demand: Government climate targets project LNG-fired power generation declining from 26.8% (2023) to 11.1% by 2038; Natural gas demand for power generation estimated to decrease from 22.89 MTPA (2023) to 11.09 MTPA (2036) at 5.4% average annual decline; LNG terminal overinvestment risk: IEEFA November 2023 report highlighted 11 proposed LNG terminals (37 MTPA capacity, ~KRW 11.3 trillion cost) presenting high overinvestment risk given declining demand outlook; At least four projects with 11 MTPA combined capacity delayed/cancelled since early 2024; Receivables/leverage: Elevated receivables from city gas utilities and KEPCO (itself severely debt-burdened) create working capital constraints and leverage pressure; KEPCO financial stress: KEPCO continues incurring debt covering financial losses from regulated tariff constraints, creating potential counterparty risk for KOGAS wholesale gas sales. Despite these challenges, KOGAS's GRE status, sovereign-linked ratings, and strategic energy security role provide ratings stability.​

Overall Assessment: Korea Gas Corporation is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 5-6% within quasi-sovereign/GRE 8-10% sovereign-linked allocation. KOGAS represents high investment-grade credit (AA/Aa2/AA-) equalized with Korea sovereign ratings reflecting government-related entity status with "almost certain" support probability, monopoly position in Korea's LNG import/wholesale market, and strategic importance to national energy security. Strengths include: (a) GRE status with "almost certain" extraordinary government support under S&P methodology; (b) ratings equalized/linked to Korea sovereign (AA/Aa2/AA-) providing implicit government backing; (c) monopoly position in Korea LNG market (~78.8% import share) under statutory framework; (d) critical infrastructure (5 terminals, 77 tanks, 4,945 km pipeline) providing essential energy supply; (e) diversified long-term LNG supply contracts (Qatar, Woodside, BP, TotalEnergies, Cheniere) securing supply to 2040s; (f) world's largest LNG importer status providing counterparty leverage; (g) $11B MTN program enabling diversified capital markets access; (h) ongoing infrastructure investment (Dangjin terminal 2027-2028) supporting future capacity; (i) LNG bunkering business diversification. Risks include: (a) declining domestic LNG demand from energy transition policies (LNG power share 26.8% to 11.1% by 2038); (b) elevated receivables/leverage from city gas utilities and KEPCO counterparty exposure; (c) LNG terminal overinvestment risk given demand outlook creating potential stranded asset concerns; (d) regulatory tariff constraints limiting margin recovery and receivables reduction; (e) long-term LNG contract pricing exposure if spot prices decline below contracted levels; (f) market liberalization from 2025 potentially introducing competition to monopoly position; (g) KEPCO financial stress creating counterparty/credit risk for wholesale gas sales; (h) energy security tensions with U.S. Alaska LNG project creating political procurement pressures. RECOMMENDED: Overweight high-quality quasi-sovereign credit with GRE support, though structural challenges from energy transition and KEPCO counterparty stress warrant monitoring.

Monitor: (1) Receivables/leverage trajectory (Receivables reduction from city gas utilities/KEPCO and debt-to-EBITDA improvement from tariff adjustments); (2) LNG demand trends (Power sector LNG consumption and market liberalization competitive impacts); (3) Sovereign rating linkage (Korea AA/Aa2/AA- maintenance and GRE support probability assessments); (4) Long-term LNG contract performance (Supply security and pricing dynamics under existing/new contracts); (5) KEPCO counterparty health (Financial restructuring progress and wholesale gas payment performance).

issuers.name: KOREA HYDRO & NUCLEAR PO
issuers.creditComment:
KOREA HYDRO & NUCLEAR POWER CO., LTD.
Credit Rating: AA/Stable (S&P 2025) | Aa2/Stable (Moody's Jul 2025) | AA-/Stable (Fitch Jun 2025) – South Korea's Sole Nuclear Power Generator; ~28% National Electricity Generation; 24 Operating Reactors; Government-Related Entity KEPCO Subsidiary; Green Nuclear Bond Issuer (Asia's First); APR1400 Export Technology

Credit Momentum: Stable/Positive – Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power (KHNP) maintains high investment-grade ratings (AA/Aa2/AA-) equalized with parent Korea Electric Power Corporation 
 (KEPCO) and linked to Republic of Korea sovereign rating. As South Korea's sole nuclear power generator contributing ~28% of national electricity, KHNP benefits from strategic energy security importance and government commitment to increase nuclear share to 35% by 2036. The company issued Asia's first international green nuclear bond (HK$1.166 billion) in March 2025 for R&D investments enhancing nuclear safety and developing next-generation technologies:

Credit Ratings & Government Support:
KHNP maintains AA/Stable ratings from S&P, Aa2/Stable from Moody's (assigned July 2025), and AA-/Stable from Fitch (affirmed June 2025). S&P/Moody's/Fitch assessment: Ratings equalized with parent KEPCO and linked to Korea sovereign reflecting government-related entity (GRE) status with "almost certain" likelihood of extraordinary government support. KEPCO relationship: KHNP is wholly-owned subsidiary of KEPCO, which is majority-owned by Korean government (51.1% through Korea Development Bank and Ministry of Finance); Ratings benefit from comprehensive parent support framework. Fitch affirmed KEPCO and subsidiaries at AA- in June 2025; S&P affirmed AA rating on KEPCO and five power generating subsidiaries in October 2025.​

Green Nuclear Bond & Sustainable Finance:
KHNP issued landmark sustainable financing: Asia's first green nuclear bond: HK$1.166 billion international green nuclear bond issued March 2025 with proceeds for R&D investments enhancing nuclear plant safety and developing next-generation technologies. Green bond framework: Updated framework with eligibility criteria referencing Korean Green Taxonomy (K-Taxonomy) and EU Taxonomy technical thresholds for nuclear activities; Separate Nuclear Bond Register created for full traceability. Moody's SPO: SQS1 (Excellent) score—highest possible sustainability quality rating; Only ~9% of global issuers achieve SQS1, making KHNP's framework the first with nuclear-related use of proceeds to receive such recognition. Crédit Agricole CIB acted as Sole Dealer and Green Structuring Agent for this landmark transaction.​

Strategic Role in Korea's Energy Mix:
KHNP serves critical function in Korea's electricity generation: Market position: South Korea's sole nuclear generation company operating all 24 commercial nuclear reactors; Contributes approximately 28% of national electricity generation. Government energy policy: Nuclear power integral to Korea's energy transition with government plan to increase nuclear share from 28% to 35% by 2036; KHNP instrumental in facilitating this objective through continuous R&D strengthening core technologies and safety capabilities. APR1400 technology: Advanced reactor design for domestic deployment and export; UAE Barakah nuclear plant contract demonstrates international competitiveness. UN SDG alignment: Nuclear power recognized as critical for access to affordable, reliable, sustainable, and modern energy (SDG 7).​

Operational Profile & R&D Focus:
KHNP maintains strong operational capabilities: Reactor fleet: 24 operating commercial reactors across multiple sites providing baseload electricity. Safety investments: R&D investments focused on enhancing nuclear power plant safety, developing next-generation nuclear technologies, and ensuring long-term operational excellence. Next-generation technologies: Pursuing advanced reactor designs and small modular reactors (SMRs) for future deployment. KEPCO group integration: Benefits from KEPCO's vertically integrated electricity value chain spanning generation, transmission, and distribution. KHNP's strategic importance to Korea's energy security and carbon neutrality objectives ensures continued government support and investment.​

Overall Assessment: Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power Co., Ltd. is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 5-6% within quasi-sovereign/GRE 8-10% sovereign-linked allocation. KHNP represents high investment-grade credit (AA/Aa2/AA-) equalized with KEPCO parent and linked to Korea sovereign, reflecting GRE status, monopoly nuclear generation position, and strategic energy security role. Strengths include: (a) AA/Aa2/AA- ratings equalized with KEPCO and linked to Korea sovereign (AA/Aa2/AA-); (b) government-related entity status with "almost certain" extraordinary support likelihood; (c) South Korea's sole nuclear generator contributing ~28% of national electricity; (d) government commitment to increase nuclear share to 35% by 2036 ensuring strategic importance; (e) Asia's first green nuclear bond (HK$1.166B) demonstrating sustainable finance leadership; (f) Moody's SQS1 (Excellent) sustainability quality score—highest possible rating; (g) APR1400 export technology demonstrating international competitiveness (UAE Barakah); (h) 24 operating reactors providing critical baseload generation; (i) R&D investments strengthening safety and next-generation technologies; (j) 100% KEPCO ownership providing comprehensive parent support. Risks include: (a) KEPCO financial stress affecting dividend and capital support capacity; (b) nuclear safety incidents potentially affecting public acceptance and regulatory environment; (c) electricity tariff regulation constraining revenue recovery; (d) long-term nuclear waste management liabilities; (e) construction delays/cost overruns on new reactor projects; (f) energy transition uncertainty affecting long-term nuclear policy commitment; (g) competition from renewable energy for clean electricity generation; (h) geopolitical risks affecting regional energy security. RECOMMENDED: Overweight high-quality GRE credit with sovereign-linked ratings, monopoly nuclear position, and strategic role in Korea's energy transition.

Monitor: (1) KEPCO parent health (Financial restructuring progress and support capacity for subsidiaries); (2) Nuclear policy commitment (35% by 2036 target maintenance and new reactor approvals); (3) Operational performance (24 reactor fleet availability and safety record); (4) R&D progress (Next-generation technology development and safety enhancements); (5) Sovereign rating linkage (Korea AA/Aa2/AA- maintenance and GRE support assessments).

issuers.name: KOREA NATIONAL OIL CORP
issuers.creditComment:
KOREA NATIONAL OIL CORPORATION (KNOC)
Credit Rating: AA/Stable (S&P 2025) | Aa2/Stable (Moody's 2025) | A+/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Government-Related Entity Republic of Korea; National Oil & Gas Company; 135,600 bpd Oil Production (2023); 0.93 Billion BOE Proved Reserves; 98.32 Million Barrels Strategic Oil Stockpile; 32 Projects in 16 Countries; CCS/Hydrogen/Offshore Wind Initiatives

Credit Momentum: Stable with Elevated Leverage – Korea National Oil Corporation maintains high investment-grade ratings (AA/Aa2/A+) linked to Korea sovereign (AA/Aa2/AA-) reflecting government-related entity status, strategic role in Korea's energy security through oil development and strategic petroleum reserves, and ongoing diversification into low-carbon new energy (CCS, hydrogen/ammonia, floating offshore wind). However, KNOC remains highly levered with adjusted debt-to-EBITDA of 9.2x-10.5x (2024-2026E), reflecting historical asset impairments from oil price declines, aggressive international expansion, and ongoing capital-intensive upstream activities. Fitch downgraded KNOC to A+ (from AA-) in 2024 removing rating equalization with sovereign, reflecting standalone credit profile (SCP) considerations, though S&P/Moody's maintain sovereign-equalized ratings:

Credit Ratings & Government Support Structure:
Korea National Oil Corporation maintains AA/Stable ratings from S&P and Aa2/Stable from Moody's (equalized with Korea sovereign), while Fitch rates KNOC at A+/Stable (one notch below sovereign following August 2024 downgrade). S&P assessment: Rating equalized with Korea sovereign (AA) reflecting "almost certain" likelihood of extraordinary government support, critical role in national energy security, and statutory framework under KNOC Act; Moody's GRI assessment: Aa2 rating equalized with Korea sovereign based on strategic importance for oil supply security and government ownership; Fitch GRE criteria: A+ rating reflects SCP combined with GRE support score, rated one notch down from Korea sovereign (AA-) after removal of rating equalization due to standalone credit weaknesses. KNOC was established under the "Korea National Oil Development Corporation Act" March 3, 1979, to develop domestic/overseas oil resources, ensure stable oil supply, and contribute to national economy. 100% government ownership via Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy provides unambiguous sovereign backing. $9 billion Global MTN program supports capital markets access.​

Production, Reserves & Strategic Petroleum Reserve:
KNOC operates diversified upstream portfolio: Oil production: 135,600 barrels per day across 32 projects in 16 countries (as of December 2023); Proved reserves: 0.93 billion BOE as of December 2024; Historical peak reserves around 600 million barrels oil plus 10 billion m³ gas prior to 2015-2017 impairments; Geographic diversification: Operations in Vietnam (15-1 oil block extension to 2050), Peru, Indonesia, Nigeria, Yemen, Kazakhstan, Russia, China, Canada, Venezuela, UK, South Korea; Vietnam 15-1 block: Agreement signed June 2025 with Petrovietnam for exploration rights extension through September 2050, 11.4% stake in block generating ~34,000 bpd, cumulative production 430 million barrels with $1.4B profit since 2003; Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR): 98.32 million barrels stockpiled in 9 domestic storage facilities providing energy security during supply crises; UAE International Joint Stockpiling Agreement signed January 2023 enhancing reserve capacity. KNOC serves as Korea's frontline defense against oil supply disruptions through strategic stockpiling and domestic production capabilities.​

Leverage Profile & Financial Metrics:
KNOC remains highly levered despite operational improvements: S&P estimates: Adjusted debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 9.5x-10.5x in 2025-2026, compared with 9.2x in 2024 and 8.4x prior years; Historical impairments: KNOC recognized significant impairment losses 2015-2017: Oil/gas properties impairment KRW 2,362B (2015), KRW 434B (2016), KRW 320B (2017); Goodwill impairment KRW 559B (2015), KRW 32B (2016), KRW 87B (2017); Net losses of KRW 4,500B (2015), KRW 1,119B (2016), KRW 675B (2017) from forecasted oil price declines and reserve reductions; Acquisition-driven debt: 2009 Harvest Operations acquisition ($1.7B plus $2.2B assumed debt), 2010 Dana Petroleum acquisition ($2.9B hostile takeover) significantly increased leverage; Bond debt: $10.9B outstanding debt obligations. Capital expenditures remain substantial: Won 1,019B budget for 2018 exploration/development activities versus Won 923B (2017), with ongoing upstream investment requirements creating cash flow demands. KNOC's high leverage reflects capital-intensive upstream nature and historical expansion strategy during oil price upswing.​

Low-Carbon New Energy Diversification:
KNOC is actively diversifying into low-carbon energy to support Korea's carbon neutrality goals: Carbon Capture and Storage (CCS): Pursuing CCS projects leveraging offshore geological expertise from oil/gas operations; Hydrogen/Ammonia: Developing production, import, and infrastructure projects utilizing oil stockpiling capabilities; Clean hydrogen/ammonia projects to contribute to national greenhouse gas reduction goals; Floating Offshore Wind: Pursuing floating offshore wind projects utilizing East Sea gas field infrastructure and offshore expertise; Gwanggaeto Project: Korea's continental shelf exploration master plan established 2022 for East, West, and South Sea domestic development. June 2024 government announcement of potential 3.5-14 billion barrels oil/gas reserves under Yeongil Bay (East Sea) creates domestic exploration opportunity, with first exploratory drilling starting December 2024 and results expected H1 2025. KNOC leading domestic continental shelf exploration to address resource shortfall while transitioning toward low-carbon portfolio.​

Strategic Role & Energy Security:
KNOC serves as Korea's primary instrument for oil supply security: Energy security mandate: Securing Korea's crude oil supply during large price fluctuations or shortages under KNOC Act; Strategic petroleum reserves: 98.32 million barrels domestic stockpile providing supply crisis buffer; UAE joint stockpiling enhances regional reserve access; Resource diplomacy: Vietnam 15-1 block extension strengthens energy cooperation with key Southeast Asian partner; Domestic exploration: Gwanggaeto Project and East Sea potential reserves addressing Korea's minimal domestic oil/gas resources (over 92% energy import dependence). KNOC balances commercial oil development objectives with public policy considerations and national energy security priorities, occasionally taking actions in furtherance of government's broader objectives that may not align with standalone commercial optimization.​

Overall Assessment: Korea National Oil Corporation is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – 4-5% within quasi-sovereign/GRE 8-10% sovereign-linked allocation. KNOC represents high investment-grade credit (AA/Aa2/A+) benefiting from GRE status with sovereign-linked ratings (S&P/Moody's) or one-notch-down (Fitch) reflecting strategic role in Korea's energy security, though elevated leverage (9.5x-10.5x debt-to-EBITDA) and historical impairment record create standalone credit concerns. Strengths include: (a) GRE status with "almost certain" extraordinary government support (S&P) providing ratings equalization with Korea sovereign (AA/Aa2); (b) 100% government ownership under statutory KNOC Act framework creating unambiguous sovereign backing; (c) strategic petroleum reserve (98.32 million barrels) providing critical energy security function; (d) diversified upstream portfolio (32 projects, 16 countries, 135,600 bpd production); (e) proved reserves 0.93 billion BOE providing production runway; (f) Vietnam 15-1 block extension to 2050 securing long-term Asian production base; (g) low-carbon diversification (CCS, hydrogen/ammonia, offshore wind) positioning for energy transition; (h) domestic continental shelf exploration (Gwanggaeto Project, East Sea potential) addressing resource gap; (i) $9B MTN program supporting capital markets access. Risks include: (a) highly levered credit profile (9.5x-10.5x debt-to-EBITDA 2025-2026E) constraining financial flexibility; (b) Fitch A+ downgrade removing sovereign equalization reflecting standalone credit concerns; (c) historical impairments (KRW 4.5T+ losses 2015-2017) from oil price declines demonstrating commodity price sensitivity; (d) capital-intensive upstream business model requiring sustained investment; (e) oil/gas price volatility affecting revenues, reserves, and impairment risk; (f) geopolitical risks in operating countries (Venezuela, Yemen, Russia, Libya) affecting production stability; (g) exploration risk with East Sea drilling outcomes uncertain despite potential reserves; (h) energy transition risks for oil-focused portfolio despite low-carbon diversification; (i) public policy mandates potentially conflicting with commercial optimization. RECOMMENDED: Market weight quasi-sovereign credit with strong GRE support but elevated leverage and commodity exposure creating standalone credit concerns.

Monitor: (1) Leverage trajectory (Debt-to-EBITDA improvement toward 8x-9x range supporting credit stability); (2) Oil/gas price dynamics (Commodity price impacts on revenues, reserves, and impairment risk); (3) Production volumes (135,600 bpd maintenance and project execution across 32 projects); (4) East Sea exploration results (H1 2025 drilling outcomes potentially transformative for domestic production); (5) Sovereign rating linkage (Korea AA/Aa2/AA- maintenance and GRE support assessments, particularly Fitch reconsideration of rating equalization).

issuers.name: KT CORP
issuers.creditComment:
KT CORP
Credit Rating: A3/Stable (Moody's Affirmed May 2025) | A-/Stable (S&P Affirmed Sep 2024) | A/Stable (Fitch) – Leading Korean Telecom; Diversified into AI/Data Centers; Strong Cash Flow
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – KT Corporation 
 maintains solid investment-grade ratings. Moody's affirmed the A3 rating with a stable outlook in May 2025, citing stable operating performance and strong market position. S&P rates KT 'A-' with a stable outlook.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) dominant fixed-line and broadband market share; (b) #2 position in mobile; (c) diversification into non-telecom areas like AI, cloud, and media (digico strategy); (d) disciplined financial policy. The company generates stable EBITDA of ~KRW 5 trillion annually.
​

Financial Profile: Leverage remains low (Debt/EBITDA ~1.5x). While 5G and AI investments are ongoing, free cash flow is robust. The company recently issued USD bonds rated 'A-' by S&P. Management focus is on enhancing shareholder value and profitability in new growth engines.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Korea/Asia Telecom 4-5% allocation. Stable defensive credit (A3/A-) with digital growth optionality. Monitor: (1) 5G monetization and ARPU; (2) AI/Cloud business execution; (3) Regulatory environment (tariff cuts); (4) Shareholder return policy.

issuers.name: KYNDRYL HOLDINGS INC
issuers.creditComment:
KYNDRYL HOLDINGS INC
Credit Rating: BBB-/Positive (S&P Revised Outlook to Positive March 2025; Affirmed Rating) | BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Oct 2025) | Baa3/Stable (Moody's, estimated) – IT Services & Hybrid IT Infrastructure Leader; Cost Discipline; AI/Cloud Growth
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (S&P Outlook Upgraded) – Kyndryl Holdings Inc. 
 has achieved investment-grade status with improving credit momentum. S&P affirmed the 'BBB-' issuer credit rating but revised the outlook to Positive (from Stable) on March 10, 2025, signaling potential for upgrade. Fitch affirms the 'BBB' rating with a Stable outlook as of October 20, 2025. The company is rated investment grade by all three major rating agencies (Moody's, Fitch, S&P). Kyndryl is a leading provider of mission-critical technology services, including hybrid IT infrastructure, cloud migration, cybersecurity, and AI implementation.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) market-leading position in hybrid IT infrastructure and mission-critical services; (b) diversified customer base with strong stickiness (long-term service contracts); (c) strong FCF generation (net debt only $1.4 billion as of Q1 2025, maintaining net leverage <1.0x EBITDA); (d) cost discipline and operational efficiency; (e) growth exposure to AI and cloud migration (recurring revenue model >70% subscription/SaaS-like); (f) essential, non-discretionary services. S&P's positive outlook reflects potential for upgrade with continued FCF generation and debt reduction.
​

Financial Profile: Kyndryl maintains strong financial discipline with net debt at only $1.4 billion as of Q1 2025, well below the <1.0x net leverage target. Q1 2025 revenue grew 1.3% YoY in constant currency with improving profitability. The company has well-laddered debt maturities from late 2026 through 2041, providing financial flexibility. No borrowings outstanding on the revolver provide additional liquidity cushion. Strong free cash flow generation supports debt reduction and financial flexibility. The company is well-positioned in hybrid IT services with AI/cloud demand tailwinds.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within IT Services 5-6% allocation. "Rising Star" IG credit (BBB-/BBB Positive/Stable) with FCF discipline. Monitor: (1) Hybrid IT infrastructure demand trends and cloud migration cycles; (2) AI/ML advisory revenue attach rates; (3) Debt reduction pace toward $1.0b-$1.2b net debt targets; (4) Book-to-bill ratio and signing growth (Q1 2025 showed strong signings growth); (5) S&P upgrade timing.

issuers.name: LAFARGE SA
issuers.creditComment:
LAFARGE SA (NOW HOLCIM LTD)
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Mar 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's Nov 2025) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch Apr 2025) – Global Building Materials Leader; CHF 26B+ Revenue; #1 Cement/Ready-Mix Globally; Decarbonization Leadership; <1.5x Net Debt Leverage Commitment; North America Building Solutions Focus; Accelera Low-Carbon Materials

Credit Momentum: Stable/Upgraded – Lafarge SA merged with Holcim in 2015, creating Holcim Ltd 
 as the world's largest cement and building materials company. Holcim achieved investment-grade ratings upgrade with Fitch raising to BBB+ (from BBB) in April 2025 and S&P/Moody's affirming BBB+/Baa1 ratings. The upgrade reflects improved balance sheet strength with net debt leverage commitment below 1.5x through 2030, decarbonization leadership, and strategic transformation toward higher-margin solutions and products. The planned spinoff of North American building solutions business (projected 2025) demonstrates portfolio optimization:

Credit Ratings & Upgrade Trajectory:
Holcim (formerly Lafarge-Holcim) maintains BBB+/Stable ratings from S&P and Fitch, with Baa1/Stable from Moody's. Fitch April 2025 upgrade: Raised to BBB+ from BBB citing improved financial profile, disciplined capital allocation, and commitment to maintaining leverage below 1.5x. S&P March 2022 upgrade: Raised to BBB+ reflecting solid operating performance and strong first-quarter results. Moody's November 2025 affirmation: Affirmed Baa1/Stable citing continued robust operating performance and financial discipline. Fitch February 2025 Rating Watch Positive: Placed on RWP reflecting anticipated credit improvement from portfolio transformation. Short-term ratings A-2 (S&P) and P-2 (Moody's) support commercial paper and short-term funding access.​

Strategic Transformation & Portfolio Optimization:
Holcim is executing strategic transformation: North America spinoff: Planned separation of North American building solutions business to create focused entities with distinct investment profiles. S&P affirmed ratings in March 2025 noting Holcim's commitment to BBB+ rating with reported net debt leverage below 1.5x through 2025-2030. Solutions & Products segment: Growing higher-margin roofing, insulation, and building envelope products; Firestone Building Products, Malarkey Roofing Products acquisitions expanding presence. Accelera low-carbon materials: Leadership in sustainable building materials including ECOPact low-carbon concrete, ECOPlanet low-carbon cement, and circular construction solutions. Lafarge legacy brands: Lafarge brand maintained as key regional brand particularly in France, Africa, and Middle East within Holcim portfolio.​

Financial Profile & Leverage Commitment:
Holcim maintains conservative financial policy: Leverage commitment: Net debt leverage below 1.5x through 2025-2030 per management guidance and rating agency expectations. Credit metrics: S&P expects FFO-to-debt and RCF-to-debt metrics consistent with BBB+ rating; EBITDA margins among highest in global building materials sector. Balance sheet strength: Strong liquidity and manageable debt maturities support investment-grade profile. Holcim's commitment to maintaining BBB+ rating provides investor confidence in financial policy discipline despite ongoing portfolio transformation and M&A activity.​

Overall Assessment: Lafarge SA (now Holcim Ltd) is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 5-6% within building materials 8-10% European investment-grade allocation. Holcim represents strong investment-grade credit (BBB+/Baa1) with global building materials leadership, decarbonization transformation, and conservative leverage policy. Strengths include: (a) Fitch upgrade to BBB+ (April 2025) reflecting improved financial profile; (b) global #1 position in cement and ready-mix concrete with diversified geographic presence; (c) net debt leverage commitment below 1.5x through 2025-2030; (d) Accelera low-carbon materials leadership positioning for sustainability transition; (e) Solutions & Products segment providing higher-margin diversification; (f) North America spinoff optimizing portfolio composition; (g) Lafarge brand heritage providing regional market strength; (h) strong liquidity and manageable maturity profile. Risks include: (a) cement industry cyclicality affecting demand and pricing; (b) carbon pricing/emissions regulations increasing production costs; (c) energy price volatility affecting cement manufacturing costs; (d) emerging market exposure creating currency and political risks; (e) spinoff execution risk and portfolio complexity; (f) competition from regional cement producers and alternative building materials. RECOMMENDED: Overweight global building materials leader with upgraded ratings, decarbonization leadership, and conservative financial policy.

Monitor: (1) Leverage maintenance (<1.5x net debt commitment through 2030); (2) North America spinoff execution (Transaction completion and value realization); (3) Decarbonization progress (Accelera low-carbon products growth and emissions reduction); (4) Solutions & Products margins (Segment profitability and contribution growth); (5) Regional market dynamics (Cement demand and pricing across major markets).

issuers.name: LEAR CORP
issuers.creditComment:
LEAR CORP
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed June 2025; S&P Affirmed Aug 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's implied/estimated) – Auto Seating/E-Systems; Resilient Margins; Conservative Leverage
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Lear Corp [finance:Lear Corp] maintains a solid "mid-BBB" investment-grade profile. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB' rating with a Stable outlook on June 2, 2025. S&P affirmed the 'BBB' rating with a Stable outlook on August 18, 2025. The stability reflects Lear's leading market positions in Seating and E-Systems, combined with a conservative balance sheet that provides a cushion against cyclical auto production volumes.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are anchored by: (a) #1 or #2 global position in automotive seating (consolidated market); (b) growing E-Systems business aligned with vehicle electrification/connectivity; (c) vertically integrated manufacturing providing cost advantages; (d) consistent free cash flow generation; (e) low leverage target (typically 1.0x-2.0x). Risks include global auto production volatility and raw material cost inflation, though Lear has strong commercial recovery mechanisms.
​

Financial Profile: Fitch forecasts Lear's leverage to remain below 2.0x through 2025/2026. The company generates solid free cash flow, which funds dividends and share repurchases. Liquidity is robust with ~$3 billion in total available liquidity (cash + revolver). The company's disciplined capital allocation prioritizes maintaining investment-grade metrics over aggressive M&A.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Auto Components 4-5% allocation. Core IG auto supplier (BBB/Baa2). Monitor: (1) Global light vehicle production volumes (IHS forecast); (2) Margin performance in E-Systems; (3) Commodity cost recovery rates; (4) Capital return pacing.

issuers.name: LENNAR CORP
issuers.creditComment:
LENNAR CORP
Credit Rating: BBB+/Positive (S&P Revised Outlook July 2025; Fitch Affirmed/Positive June 2025) | Baa1/Positive (Moody's Estimated) – Homebuilding Leader; Fortress Balance Sheet; "Manufacturing" Model
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Outlook Revised to Positive) – Lennar Corp [finance:Lennar Corp] is on track for a potential upgrade to the 'A' category. S&P revised the outlook to Positive (from Stable) on July 16, 2025, affirming the 'BBB+' rating. Fitch likewise revised the outlook to Positive (from Stable) while affirming 'BBB+' in June 2025. The positive momentum is driven by Lennar's exceptionally strong credit metrics (debt/EBITDA <1.0x) and its successful transition to a land-light, manufacturing-oriented business model.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) status as one of the largest U.S. homebuilders; (b) "Everything's Included" manufacturing model driving efficiency; (c) substantial progress in reducing land duration (controlled vs. owned land ~75%/25% target); (d) massive liquidity and cash balance (~$4-$5B expected end 2025). S&P notes the potential for an upgrade if the company maintains gross margins >20% and debt/EBITDA <1.0x through the cycle.
​

Financial Profile: Lennar's balance sheet is pristine. As of May 2025, debt-to-EBITDA was 0.7x. S&P projects it could fall to 0.4x by year-end 2025 with ~$5 billion in cash. The company recently spun off Millrose Properties (land banking), further reducing asset intensity. This "fortress" balance sheet provides immense flexibility to navigate housing market fluctuations while continuing to return capital to shareholders.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within Homebuilders 5-6% allocation. "Rising Star" to Single-A potential. Monitor: (1) Gross margin sustainability (>20% threshold); (2) Land-light execution (optioned lot %); (3) Cash deployment (buybacks vs. debt vs. retained); (4) Mortgage rate impact on order volumes.

issuers.name: LENOVO GROUP LTD
issuers.creditComment:
LENOVO GROUP LTD
Credit Rating: Baa2/Stable (Moody's Affirmed July 2024) | BBB/Stable (S&P Upgraded September 2023) | BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed February 2024) – Global PC Leader; Diversified Non-PC Growth (SSG/ISG); Strong Cash Flow; Debt Reduction Progress; AI Opportunities
​
Credit Momentum: Positive Trend with IG Status – Lenovo Group Limited has solidified its investment-grade status with upgrades and affirmations from all three major agencies (Moody's, S&P, Fitch). The credit profile benefits from reduced leverage, growing non-PC services revenue, and AI-driven growth opportunities.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Lenovo maintains Baa2/Stable from Moody's (affirmed July 2024), BBB/Stable from S&P (upgraded September 2023), and BBB/Stable from Fitch (affirmed February 2024). Ratings reflect: (a) global leadership in PCs (22.7% market share); (b) successful diversification into Solutions & Services (SSG) and Infrastructure Solutions (ISG); (c) prudent financial policy with low leverage; (d) strong operating cash flow; (e) improved margin profile.
​
Financial Performance & Strategy: Record non-PC revenue mix (>40%). SSG driving profitability with high margins. ISG benefiting from AI server demand. Net cash position maintained. Commitment to innovation (AI PC launch, $1B AI investment). Resilience demonstrated through PC market cycles. Debt reduction and EBITDA growth supporting credit metrics.
​
Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within Global Tech Hardware 8-10% allocation. Strong investment-grade tech credit (Baa2/BBB) with improving business mix and conservative balance sheet. Monitor: (1) PC market recovery trajectory; (2) AI server/services growth; (3) Margin expansion sustainability; (4) Working capital management.

issuers.name: LG CHEM LTD
issuers.creditComment:
LG CHEM LTD
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Downgraded March 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's Downgraded Nov 2025) – Leading Chemical & Battery Player; Downgraded on High Capex and Weak Margins; Parent of LG Energy Solution
​

Credit Momentum: Negative Trend (Downgraded) – LG Chem 
 experienced rating downgrades in 2025 from both S&P (to BBB in March) and Moody's (to Baa2 in November). The downgrades reflect: (a) aggressive debt-funded capex for battery materials and biopharma; (b) cyclical downturn in petrochemicals; (c) slowing EV battery demand affecting its subsidiary LG Energy Solution.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Now rated Baa2/BBB. S&P cited that debt leverage will remain elevated (Adjusted Debt/EBITDA >3.0x) through 2025-2026. Moody's highlighted the rising debt burden from capacity expansions despite weaker profitability. However, the outlook is now stable, indicating ratings have likely troughed for this cycle.
​

Financial Profile: Adjusted net debt/EBITDA is expected to increase to 3.4x-3.7x in 2025-26 (Moody's estimate). The petrochemical division is suffering from oversupply, while the battery materials business faces EV market headwinds. Asset disposals (e.g., polarizing film business) are being used to manage leverage.
​

Overall Assessment: UNDERWEIGHT – 2-3% within Asian Chemicals 4-5% allocation. Cyclically challenged credit (Baa2/BBB) with elevated leverage. Monitor: (1) Petrochemical spread recovery; (2) Battery material sales growth; (3) Capex discipline and asset sales; (4) LG Energy Solution's share price (collateral value).

issuers.name: LG ELECTRONICS INC
issuers.creditComment:
LG ELECTRONICS INC
Credit Rating: BBB/Positive (S&P Outlook Revised Oct 2025) | Baa2/Positive (Moody's Outlook Revised Feb 2025) – Global Home Appliance Leader; Record Order Backlog in Vehicle Components; India IPO Inflow
​

Credit Momentum: Positive – LG Electronics 
 is on a positive trajectory. S&P revised its outlook to Positive (affirming BBB) in October 2025, following a similar move by Moody's (to Positive from Stable) in February 2025. This signals a potential upgrade to the 'A' category/Baa1 over the next 12-18 months.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: The positive momentum is driven by: (a) resilient profitability in home appliances despite macro headwinds; (b) strong growth and profitability in the Vehicle Component Solutions (VS) business (order backlog ~KRW 100 trillion); (c) cash inflow (~KRW 1.8 trillion) from the IPO of its Indian subsidiary; (d) turnaround at affiliate LG Display.
​

Financial Strength: LG Electronics is strengthening its balance sheet with IPO proceeds and robust operating cash flow. The VS business is becoming a key profit pillar. Diversification into B2B segments (HVAC, robots) is reducing earnings volatility. S&P expects financial metrics to improve further.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Global Tech/Consumer Electronics 5-6% allocation. Improving credit story (BBB/Baa2 Positive) with upgrade potential. Monitor: (1) Vehicle component margin expansion; (2) Global consumer demand recovery; (3) US tariff risks; (4) LG Display's continued recovery.

issuers.name: LGENERGYSOLUTION
issuers.creditComment:
LG ENERGY SOLUTION LTD
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Downgraded March 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's Downgraded Nov 2025) – Top Tier Global Battery Maker; Downgraded on Profitability Pressure; Strong Order Backlog
​

Credit Momentum: Negative Trend (Downgraded) – LG Energy Solution (LG EnSol) 
 faced successive downgrades in 2025: S&P lowered its rating to 'BBB' from 'BBB+' in March, and Moody's cut it to 'Baa2' from 'Baa1' in November. The outlooks are now stable. These actions reflect deteriorating profitability due to the global EV demand slowdown ("chasm") and aggressive capex increasing debt burdens.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) top-tier market position in EV batteries; (b) diverse customer base (GM, Hyundai, Tesla); (c) massive order backlog (~KRW 100 trillion+). However, leverage has spiked (Adjusted Net Debt/EBITDA >3.0x) due to rapid capacity expansion in the US and Europe amid weaker-than-expected sales volume.
​

Financial Profile: The company is adjusting its strategy by reducing capex (from >KRW 10 trillion to ~KRW 7 trillion) and focusing on cost efficiency. Profitability relies heavily on US IRA tax credits. Moody's expects leverage to remain elevated (3.4x-3.7x) through 2026. The stock price decline has also reduced the collateral value for parent LG Chem.
​

Overall Assessment: UNDERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Global EV/Battery Sector 5-6% allocation. Growth credit facing cyclical headwinds (Baa2/BBB). Monitor: (1) EV demand recovery in North America/Europe; (2) Capex discipline; (3) IRA policy continuity; (4) Operating margin stabilization.

issuers.name: LIMA METRO LINE 2 FIN LT
issuers.creditComment:
LIMA METRO LINE 2 FIN LT
Lima Metro Line 2 Finance Limited, the financing vehicle for the Lima Metro Line 2 concession, is rated Baa1 by Moody’s and BBB by S&P and Fitch. The rating is tied to the RPI-CAO (Retribución por Inversión - Certificado de Avance de Obra) mechanism, which represents an irrevocable payment obligation from the Government of Peru (Baa1/BBB) upon milestone completion, effectively mirroring sovereign risk.​

issuers.name: LOCKHEED MARTIN CORP
issuers.creditComment:
LOCKHEED MARTIN CORP
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (S&P Affirmed Dec 2024/2025) | A/Stable (Fitch Affirmed July 2025) | A3/Stable (Moody's Affirmed) – Defense Prime; F-35 Franchise; Predictable Cash Flow
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Lockheed Martin Corp [finance:Lockheed Martin Corp] maintains a strong single-A credit profile. Fitch affirmed the 'A' rating with a Stable outlook on July 23, 2025. S&P assigned an 'A-' rating to new senior notes in December 2024, implying the issuer rating remains stable at 'A-'. Moody's maintains an 'A3' rating (equivalent to A-). Lockheed is the world's largest defense contractor, underpinned by the F-35 program and broad portfolio.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) unrivaled position as the anchor of the U.S. defense industrial base; (b) massive backlog (>$160B) providing multi-year revenue visibility; (c) F-35 program (recurring revenue + sustainment); (d) strong alignment with U.S. DoD modernization priorities (hypersonics, space, missile defense). Risks include U.S. defense budget constraints and program execution/margin pressure on fixed-price contracts.
​

Financial Profile: Lockheed generates significant and predictable free cash flow (>$6B annually). Fitch notes the 'A' rating reflects this cash generation capability. The company balances shareholder returns (dividends + buybacks) with debt repayment and pension contributions. Leverage is typically managed around 1.5x-2.0x. The company continues to access capital markets efficiently, issuing senior notes rated 'A'/'A-' in late 2024/mid-2025.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within Aerospace & Defense 6-7% allocation. "Blue Chip" Defense Anchor (A/A-). Monitor: (1) U.S. Defense Budget top-line growth; (2) F-35 production lot negotiations and sustainment margins; (3) Hypersonics program wins/losses; (4) Classified program revenue growth.

issuers.name: L'OREAL SA
issuers.creditComment:
L'ORÉAL SA
Credit Rating: Aa1/Stable (Moody's) | AA/Stable (S&P Affirmed September 2025) – World's Largest Beauty Company; 9M 2025 Sales €32.8B +3.4% LFL; Professional Products +7.4% LFL; Services Revenue Record; Net Cash Position; Kering Beauty Partnership​

Credit Momentum: Exceptionally Strong – L'Oréal SA maintains premier investment-grade ratings reflecting global beauty market leadership, diversified portfolio across all price points, exceptional profitability, and fortress balance sheet.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: L'Oréal maintains Aa1/Stable from Moody's and AA/Stable from S&P (affirmed September 2025). Ratings reflect: (a) #1 global beauty company position with unrivaled brand portfolio; (b) diversified across Professional Products, Consumer Products, Luxe, and Dermatological Beauty; (c) strong geographic diversification (Europe, North America, North Asia, Emerging Markets); (d) exceptional margins and cash generation; (e) net cash position; (f) conservative financial policy.​

9M 2025 Financial Performance: 9M 2025 sales €32.8B (+1.2% reported, +3.4% LFL). Q3 LFL growth accelerated to +4.9%. All divisions contributed: Professional Products +7.4% LFL (haircare strength), Consumer Products +3.1%, Luxe +2.2%, Dermatological Beauty +3.7%. All regions positive: North America recovering (+1.8% LFL), mainland China improving, Europe solid (+3.6%), SAPMENA-SSA leading (+11.0%). Double-digit online growth ahead of market. Haircare and fragrances leading categories.​

Balance Sheet & Strategic Developments: €1.5B bond issuance (2024) rated AA (S&P) and Aa1 (Moody's). Kering strategic partnership announced (October 2025) for luxury beauty and wellness. Strong liquidity with net cash position. ESG ratings: MSCI AA, S&P Global 80/100, Sustainalytics 16.6 (Low Risk).​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within global consumer/beauty 8-10% allocation. Premier investment-grade beauty credit (Aa1/AA) with global leadership, diversified portfolio, and exceptional financial strength. Monitor: (1) Q4 2025 sales acceleration; (2) North America and China recovery trajectory; (3) Luxe division performance; (4) Kering partnership integration; (5) Online channel momentum.

issuers.name: MA'ADEN SUKUK LTD
issuers.creditComment:
MA'ADEN SUKUK LIMITED
