The company generates consistent free cash flow (~$600M avg), funding restructuring programs ($285M savings target) and modest buybacks. It recently issued $600 million in senior notes due 2028. The balance sheet is positioned to capture demand when the housing market turns.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Building Products 4-5% allocation. Resilient "BBB+" credit awaiting cycle turn. Monitor: (1) Residential Remodeling Index (RRI) and housing starts; (2) Input cost inflation (energy/materials) vs. pricing; (3) Restructuring savings realization; (4) LVT (Luxury Vinyl Tile) competitive dynamics.

issuers.name: MOLSON COORS BEVERAGE
issuers.creditComment:
MOLSON COORS BEVERAGE COMPANY
Credit Rating: BBB-/Stable (Fitch 2025) | BBB/Stable (S&P Aug 2024) | BBB (low)/Stable (DBRS 2023) – Global Brewer; $11.5B+ Revenue; Net Debt/EBITDA 2.3x; Q3 2025 Net Sales $2.97B (-2.3%); Large Non‑Cash Impairment; 2025 Guidance Reaffirmed at Low End

Credit Momentum: Stable/Restructuring – Molson Coors Beverage Company 
 maintains solid mid‑BBB credit ratings with Stable outlooks despite category softness and a large non‑cash impairment in Q3 2025. Fitch affirmed BBB‑ and revised outlook to Stable (2025) while S&P affirmed BBB/Stable (August 2024). Q3 2025 EPS and revenue modestly missed expectations, but underlying EBITDA slightly beat and management reaffirmed 2025 guidance (now guiding to the low end), signaling confidence in cash‑flow resilience during portfolio and cost restructuring.​

Credit Ratings & Capital Structure:
S&P affirms Molson Coors at BBB with Stable outlook; Fitch confirms BBB‑/Stable; DBRS rates issuer BBB (low)/Stable. Ratings reflect strong brands and steady free cash flow, partially offset by mature beer category exposure and moderate leverage. Net debt/underlying EBITDA increased to about 2.28x at September 30, 2025 from 2.10x a year earlier but remains compatible with current rating. Commercial paper is rated A‑3/P‑3/R‑2 (low).​

Q3 2025 Operating Performance:
Q3 2025 was dominated by impairment but underlying performance tracked expectations: Net sales: $2.97B, down 2.3% reported and 3.3% constant currency, modestly below consensus $3.01B. Underlying EPS: $1.67 (‑7.2% YoY), modest miss vs $1.70 consensus. Underlying EBITDA: ~$665–670M, slightly ahead of $653M consensus, indicating margin resilience despite volume softness. Impairments: $3.65B goodwill and $274M intangibles led to GAAP EPS loss of $14.79, but these are non‑cash and do not affect covenant metrics.​

Guidance & Category Backdrop:
Management reaffirmed FY 2025 guidance but now expects outcomes at the low end: Net sales: –3% to –4% constant currency. Underlying pretax income: –12% to –15% constant currency. Underlying EPS: –7% to –10%. Free cash flow: about $1.3B ±10%. CEO characterizes 2025 softness as cyclical rather than structural, citing healthy balance sheet, strong brands, and cost savings. However, Moody’s packaged alcoholic beverages sector commentary highlights weak demand and tariffs delaying spirits recovery, underscoring broader beverage pressure.​

Overall Assessment: Molson Coors Beverage Company is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within beverages 4-5% consumer staples allocation. Molson Coors offers mid‑BBB credit with resilient cash flow and manageable leverage, but faces category headwinds, volume declines, and restructuring execution risk. Strengths: (a) diversified brand portfolio across North America and Europe; (b) stable mid‑BBB ratings with Net debt/EBITDA ~2.3x; (c) strong underlying EBITDA vs expectations in Q3 2025; (d) reaffirmed FY guidance and ~$1.3B FCF target; (e) cost‑savings and mix improvement initiatives. Risks: (a) beer category softness, especially in North America; (b) large goodwill impairment signaling weaker long‑term growth expectations; (c) limited pricing power amid pressured consumers; (d) exposure to commodity and packaging cost inflation; (e) potential rating pressure if leverage drifts sustainably above mid‑2x or demand weakens further. RECOMMENDED: Maintain market‑weight exposure; consider as defensive BBB holding but monitor category trends, leverage trajectory, and progress on portfolio premiumization.

Monitor: (1) Volume and net sales trends vs guidance (–3% to –4%); (2) Net debt/EBITDA trajectory (targeting ≤2.5x); (3) Execution on cost savings and premiumization; (4) Sector demand signals from global packaged beverages; (5) Any further impairment or restructuring charges.

issuers.name: MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL
issuers.creditComment:
MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL INC.
Credit Rating: Baa1/Stable (Moody's Aug 2025) | BBB/Stable (S&P Nov 2024) – Global Snacks Leader; ~$36B Revenue; Oreo, Cadbury, Milka, Trident Brands; 2.9x S&P-Adjusted Leverage; Strong Organic Growth Despite Cocoa Headwinds; JDE Peet's Proceeds Deployed

Credit Momentum: Stable – Mondelez International 
 maintains Baa1/BBB investment-grade ratings with Stable outlooks from Moody's and S&P, reflecting global snacking leadership, strong organic revenue growth, and improved leverage around 3x. Moody's affirmed Baa1 (August 2025) with outlook changed to Stable from Positive, while S&P affirmed BBB (November 2024). Leverage improved to 2.9x from historical levels above 4x following JDE Peet's stake monetization and strong operating performance, though cocoa cost headwinds persist:​

Credit Ratings & Financial Policy:
Mondelez maintains Baa1/Stable from Moody's (August 2025), BBB/A-2/Stable from S&P (November 2024). Moody's August 2025: Affirmed Baa1, changed outlook to Stable from Positive; Upgrade potential requires sustained leverage below 3.5x and retained cash flow/net debt above 18%. S&P November 2024: Affirmed BBB, tightened downside trigger to 4x from low-4x following JDE Peet's monetization; Company no longer has sizable coffee assets for further divestitures. Leverage improvement: S&P-adjusted debt/EBITDA improved to 2.9x (12 months ended September 2024) from 3.3x prior year and historical levels above 4x.​

Organic Growth & Pricing Power:
Mondelez demonstrates strong category performance: Q3 2024 organic growth: 5.4% (price +5.1%, volume/mix +0.3%), exceeding packaged food peers. Volume stabilization: North America volume/mix improved to +1.7% after declines in prior quarters, demonstrating pricing power. Cocoa management: Implementing price increases to mitigate elevated cocoa costs; Hedge rolloffs expected to pressure margins into H1 2025 before improvement. Annual guidance: At least 3% organic revenue growth expected, outpacing overall packaged food industry.​

Capital Allocation & M&A:
Active capital deployment characterizes Mondelez: FOCF generation: At least $3.4 billion annually; $3 billion 2024 expected incorporating JDE Peet's sale proceeds. Shareholder returns: Over $2.3 billion annual dividends; At least $2 billion annual share repurchases. M&A history: Over $9 billion acquisitions since 2018, funded via coffee asset sales without increasing leverage; Good integration track record. Divestiture proceeds: JDE Peet's stake sale (~$6.3B value historically) utilized for share repurchases and debt reduction.​

Overall Assessment: Mondelez International Inc. is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within packaged foods 6-7% consumer staples allocation. Mondelez represents solid investment-grade credit (Baa1/BBB) with global snacking leadership, strong organic growth, and improved leverage positioning. Strengths include: (a) Baa1/BBB ratings with Stable outlooks; (b) world's largest snacking company with iconic brands (Oreo, Cadbury, Milka, Trident); (c) 5.4% Q3 organic growth exceeding packaged food peers; (d) improved leverage to 2.9x from historical >4x; (e) strong FOCF generation >$3.4B annually; (f) demonstrated pricing power with volume stabilization; (g) ~$36B revenue with 89% from snacks categories; (h) diversified emerging markets exposure. Risks include: (a) cocoa cost inflation pressuring near-term margins; (b) acquisitive financial policy limiting upgrade potential; (c) share repurchases limiting deleveraging capacity; (d) no remaining coffee assets for further monetization; (e) FX headwinds from international operations; (f) competition from Nestlé, PepsiCo, Hershey. RECOMMENDED: Overweight global snacking leader with improved leverage, strong brands, and pricing power.

Monitor: (1) Leverage trajectory (2.9x maintenance below 4x downside trigger); (2) Cocoa costs (Hedge rolloffs and pricing pass-through); (3) Organic growth (3%+ annual target achievement); (4) M&A activity (Acquisition-driven leverage elevation); (5) Volume trends (Pricing power and demand elasticity).

issuers.name: MOODY'S CORPORATION
issuers.creditComment:
MOODY'S CORPORATION
Credit Rating: A2/Stable (Moody's Self-Rating) | A3/Stable (S&P) – Global Credit Ratings & Analytics Leader; Q3 2025 Record Revenue $2.0B +11% YoY; Adjusted EPS $3.92 +22% YoY; MIS Revenue $1.03B +17%; MA Revenue $928M +5%; FY2025 Guidance Raised​

Credit Momentum: Exceptionally Strong – Moody's Corporation maintains strong investment-grade ratings reflecting duopoly position in credit ratings, diversified analytics business, and exceptional margin profile with strong free cash flow generation.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Moody's maintains A2/Stable (self-rating) and A3/Stable from S&P. Ratings reflect: (a) duopoly position with S&P Global 
 in credit ratings industry; (b) Moody's Investors Service (MIS) representing ~53% of revenue with high margins; (c) Moody's Analytics (MA) providing recurring revenue diversification; (d) exceptional EBITDA margins (50%+); (e) strong free cash flow generation; (f) conservative leverage profile.​

Q3 2025 Record Financial Results: Q3 2025 revenue $2.0B (record, +11% YoY). MIS revenue $1.03B (+17% YoY). MA revenue $928M (+5% YoY). Operating income $915M (+17%). Adjusted operating margin 48.9%. Diluted EPS $3.33 (+24%), adjusted EPS $3.92 (+22%). FY2025 guidance raised: revenue growth +12-14% (from +low double-digit), MIS revenue +18-21%, MA revenue +7-9%, adjusted EPS $13.50-$13.80.​

Business Momentum: Strong issuance environment driving MIS performance: leveraged finance, financial institutions, and structured finance segments particularly strong. MA recurring revenue ~95% with ARR growth trajectory. Decision Solutions and Data & Information segments driving MA growth. Digital investments in GenAI and analytics enhancing competitive positioning.​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within global financial services 8-10% allocation. Strong investment-grade credit ratings/analytics credit (A2/A3) with duopoly position, exceptional margins, and record revenue. Monitor: (1) FY2025 guidance delivery; (2) Issuance environment sustainability; (3) MA ARR growth trajectory; (4) GenAI competitive positioning; (5) Regulatory environment.

issuers.name: MOSAIC CO
issuers.creditComment:
MOSAIC CO
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P per IR as of 2021 baseline) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's per Nov 2025 assignment) | BBB-/Positive (Fitch per 2022) – Phosphate Fertilizer Leader; Cyclical Strength; Debt Management
​

Credit Momentum: Stable to Positive (Benefitting from Strong Ag Cycle) – Mosaic Company [finance:Mosaic Co] maintains an investment-grade profile anchored in strong phosphate market conditions. Moody's assigned a 'Baa2' rating to proposed senior unsecured notes in November 2025, citing increased phosphate demand and strong pricing that should support debt reduction and improved financial profile. S&P holds a 'BBB' rating (per investor relations, original date 2021 but maintained through cycle). Fitch rated at 'BBB-' with a Positive outlook (as of 2022, position likely improved given recent pricing strength). The company is the global leader in phosphate and potash production.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) dominant global market share in phosphate and potash; (b) high barriers to entry (capital intensity, resource scarcity); (c) essential product in global agriculture; (d) strong cash flow generation when commodity prices are elevated. Risks include cyclical fertilizer prices (linked to agricultural economics and commodity cycles) and environmental/permitting constraints. Moody's notes the upgrade was driven by elevated phosphate prices that are expected to support strong free cash flow in 2025/2026.
​

Financial Profile: Mosaic has strong liquidity and free cash flow generation when prices are favorable (current conditions). Leverage metrics are expected to improve significantly from the net debt reduction enabled by robust earnings. The company has committed cash to debt repayment and shareholder returns (dividends + buybacks). Liquidity is solid with access to capital markets.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Materials/Fertilizers 3-4% allocation. Cyclical BBB credit trading at peak earnings. Monitor: (1) Phosphate price trends (global supply/demand); (2) Agricultural commodity prices (corn/wheat); (3) Debt reduction targets; (4) Environmental/permitting headwinds.

issuers.name: MTR CORP CI LTD
issuers.creditComment:
MTR CORPORATION LIMITED
Credit Rating: Aa3/Stable (Moody's May 2025) | A+/Stable (S&P May 2025) | A+/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Hong Kong's Integrated Mass Transit & Property Developer; HK$27.4B 9M 2025 Revenue (+6.5% YoY); HK$3.4B Recurrent Profit (-15.7% YoY); HK$7.7B Net Profit (+27.5%); HK$100B+ Capital Works Program; Net Debt-to-Equity 18.8%

Credit Momentum: Stable – MTR Corporation 
 maintains high investment-grade ratings (Aa3/A+) reflecting Hong Kong's integrated rail operator monopoly, strategic importance to city connectivity, diversified revenue streams (transit operations + property development), and stable leverage profile. H1 2025 delivered HK$7.7 billion net profit (+27.5% YoY) including HK$5.54 billion property development gains, though recurrent operating profit declined 15.7% YoY to HK$3.39 billion due to higher operating costs. Moody's revised outlook to Stable from Positive (May 2025), mirroring Hong Kong government rating shift:

Credit Ratings & Hong Kong Linkage:
MTR maintains Aa3/Stable from Moody's, A+/Stable from S&P and Fitch, equalized with Hong Kong sovereign rating (Aa3/A+). Moody's May 2025: Affirmed Aa3/Stable, revising outlook from Positive to Stable reflecting shift in Hong Kong government rating outlook; Rating reflects MTR's dominant position in Hong Kong's public transportation network, essential infrastructure role, and integrated rail-property model generating economic benefits. S&P/Fitch: A+/Stable maintained reflecting strong business model, strategic importance, and solid financial position. Debt capital markets access: Exceptional investor confidence demonstrated by H1 2025 successful issuances: US$3 billion public senior notes (including 30-year tranche) and US$3 billion subordinated perpetual securities.​

H1 2025 Financial Performance:
MTR reported mixed H1 2025 results (August 2025): Net profit: HK$7.7 billion (+27.5% YoY including one-off property gains). Recurrent business profit: HK$3.39 billion (-15.7% YoY) from higher operating costs despite rising patronage. Property development profit: HK$5.54 billion (+218.5% vs HK$1.74 billion prior year H1) from two Ho Man Tin packages and The Southside mall near Wong Chuk Hang station. Total revenue: HK$27.4 billion (+6.5% YoY). Net debt-to-equity: 18.8% (+12.8pp vs December 2024 from capital works investment). Balance sheet: Total assets HK$402.6 billion (+9.6%); Net assets HK$211.2 billion (+13.5%). Interim dividend: HK$0.42/share (unchanged from prior year).​

Capital Works & Long-Term Growth:
MTR advancing massive capital expansion program: Total investment: Approximately HK$140 billion for new railway projects including Tung Chung Line Extension, Tuen Mun South Extension, Oyster Bay Station, Hung Shui Kiu Station, and Northern Link. Northern Metropolis projects: Kwu Tung Station on East Rail Line, Main Line and Spur Line of Northern Link targeted for 2034 commissioning. Funding flexibility: Robust financial strategy leveraging diverse fundraising options including capital markets issuances, creating headroom for investment. "Rail plus Property" model: Channels economic benefits from property development into railway expansion, supporting operational expenses and growth investments.​

Operating Challenges & Outlook:
Despite strong property gains, MTR faces operational headwinds: Operating costs: Rising expenses constraining recurrent profit growth despite higher patronage. Patronage recovery: Rising passenger volumes supportive of long-term revenue growth and ridership stability. CEO Jacob Kam noted business "eventful" in H1 2025 with company in "harvesting stage" of earlier property development projects, contributing substantial one-off gains.​

Overall Assessment: MTR Corporation Limited is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 5-6% within infrastructure/utilities 8-10% Asia-Pacific allocation. MTR represents high investment-grade credit (Aa3/A+) with Hong Kong public transit monopoly, strategic importance, and diversified revenue model. Strengths include: (a) Aa3/A+ ratings equalized with Hong Kong sovereign reflecting strategic infrastructure role; (b) H1 2025 net profit +27.5% (including property gains); (c) dominant Hong Kong rail network providing essential services; (d) property development model generating returns (HK$5.54B H1 gains) to fund rail investment; (e) HK$140B capital works program providing long-term growth visibility; (f) strong capital markets access demonstrated by US$6B successful issuances; (g) modest leverage (18.8% net debt-to-equity); (h) recurring property leasing revenue providing stable cash flows. Risks include: (a) Moody's outlook revision to Stable from Positive reflecting Hong Kong rating shift; (b) recurrent profit decline (-15.7%) from higher operating costs despite patronage growth; (c) reliance on Hong Kong property development cycle for earnings accretion; (d) massive capital intensity (HK$140B projects) requiring continued debt issuance; (e) political/regulatory risks in Hong Kong affecting operations and property policies; (f) post-COVID travel pattern changes potentially affecting long-term ridership assumptions. RECOMMENDED: Overweight Hong Kong infrastructure leader with strategic importance, property development returns, and capital markets confidence supporting rating stability.

Monitor: (1) Recurrent profit recovery (Operating cost management and patronage-revenue correlation); (2) Property development pipeline (New project delivery and profit realization); (3) Capital works execution (Northern Link and new line on-time/on-budget performance); (4) Hong Kong sovereign rating (Aa3/A+ maintenance affecting MTR rating linkage); (5) Leverage trajectory (Net debt-to-equity management amid HK$140B investment program).

issuers.name: NAKILAT INC
issuers.creditComment:
NAKILAT INC.
Credit Rating: AA-/Stable (Fitch 2024) | Aa3/Stable (Moody's 2025) | AA-/Stable (S&P 2025) – World's Largest LNG Shipping Fleet Operator; 69 LNG Carriers + Support Vessels; QAR 2.37B Cash Position; Fleet Expansion to 112 Vessels; 1H25 Net Profit QAR 860M (+3.7%); High-Quality LNG Charter Contracts

Credit Momentum: Stable – Nakilat Inc. 
 maintains AA-/Stable high investment-grade ratings from all three agencies (Fitch/Moody's/S&P), reflecting leading LNG shipping position, world's highest-rated shipping company status, strong balance sheet (QAR 2.37B cash), and strategic importance to Qatar energy sector. H1 2025 demonstrated resilience with net profit QAR 860 million (+3.7% YoY) despite lower interest income and joint venture headwinds, supported by strong LNG market fundamentals and fleet expansion strategy:

Credit Ratings & Shipping Leadership:
Nakilat maintains AA-/Stable from Fitch (affirmed 2024), Aa3/Stable from Moody's, and AA-/Stable from S&P—highest credit-rated shipping company globally. Fitch March 2024 upgrade: Raised two notches from A to AA- with Stable outlook reflecting improved credit profile from March 2024 bank debt refinancing; all Nakilat debt will be paid in full within firm charters period. Moody's rating: Aa3/Stable reflecting premier LNG shipping position. Market position: World's largest LNG fleet operator with 69 LNG carriers, 2 LPG carriers, 1 FSRU providing essential transport capacity for Qatar's LNG exports.​

H1 2025 Financial Performance:
Nakilat delivered solid H1 2025 results: Net profit: QAR 860 million (+3.7% YoY) demonstrating resilience despite headwinds. Total income: QAR 2.27 billion with EBITDA QAR 1.82 billion. Cost management: Finance charges down 14.2% from higher capitalized interest on newbuilds and lower refinancing margins; G&A expenses down 23.8%. Balance sheet: Current ratio 1.13; Return on equity 13.5%; Net borrowings decreased to QAR 18.9B (from QAR 19.4B year-end 2024). Cash position: QAR 2.37 billion providing financial flexibility for fleet expansion program.​

Fleet Expansion & Market Positioning:
Nakilat executing transformational fleet growth strategy: Current fleet: 69 LNG carriers, 2 LPG carriers, 1 FSRU. Expansion program: 36 additional LNG carriers and 4 LPG/ammonia carriers ordered, bringing total fleet to 112 vessels. Newbuild composition: 27 conventional LNG carriers (174,000 cbm), 9 QC-MAX carriers (271,000 cbm), 4 LPG/ammonia carriers. Delivery schedule: Accelerates 2027-2028 with 35-45% of capex due 2027. LNG market fundamentals: Liquefaction capacity projected to grow 64% (411 to 675 MTPA) by 2030 supporting long-term shipping demand. Charter rates: Q2 2025 spot rates ~$24,000/day (MEGI/XDF) recovering from Q1; Long-term contracts providing revenue stability.​

Overall Assessment: Nakilat Inc. is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within energy infrastructure/shipping 6-7% Qatar/GCC allocation. Nakilat represents premium investment-grade credit (AA-/Aa3) with world's highest-rated shipping status, strategic LNG positioning, and strong financial flexibility. Strengths include: (a) AA-/Aa3 ratings—highest among shipping companies globally; (b) world's largest LNG fleet (69 carriers) with 112 planned vessels; (c) QAR 2.37B cash position supporting fleet expansion; (d) long-term fixed-rate charter contracts providing revenue stability; (e) strategic importance to Qatar energy sector; (f) 1H25 net profit growth (+3.7%) demonstrating resilience; (g) LNG market growth (64% capacity expansion to 2030); (h) strong ROE 13.5% and improving debt ratios. Risks include: (a) LNG shipping market cyclicality from oversupply risk; (b) newbuild capex intensity creating leverage elevation; (c) scrapping of older steam turbine vessels reducing future supply support; (d) technology disruption (autonomous vessels); (e) geopolitical risks affecting Qatar operations; (f) interest rate impacts on floating-rate debt. RECOMMENDED: Overweight premier LNG shipping company with AA- ratings, strategic Qatar positioning, and long-term fleet expansion benefiting from projected 64% global LNG capacity growth through 2030.

Monitor: (1) Fleet expansion (Newbuild delivery schedule and capex payments); (2) LNG shipping rates ($24,000/day Q2 level sustainability); (3) Charter contract portfolio (Long-term fixed rates securing revenue); (4) Leverage trajectory (Net borrowings management through expansion); (5) LNG market (64% capacity growth realization).

issuers.name: NASDAQ INC
issuers.creditComment:
NASDAQ INC
Credit Rating: Baa1/Stable (Moody's Upgraded March 2025) | BBB+/Stable (S&P Upgraded Feb 2025) – Derivatives/Tech Exchange; Recurring Revenue; Deleveraging
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgraded) – NASDAQ Inc [finance:NASDAQ Inc] has successfully improved its credit profile through deleveraging and operational improvements. Moody's upgraded the rating to 'Baa1' with a Stable outlook on March 31, 2025, citing improved debt/EBITDA leverage to 3.9x (down from 4.6x year-end 2023 post-Adenza acquisition). S&P upgraded to 'BBB+' with a Stable outlook on February 6, 2025. The upgrades reflect Nasdaq's increasingly diversified revenue base (data services, trade technology via Adenza) beyond the volatile trading business.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) status as the world's leading technology exchange (with CME); (b) strong, recurring revenue from non-trading businesses (data, analytics, listings); (c) successful integration of Adenza (trade technology); (d) committed deleveraging plan (expected leverage path: 3.9x → 3.3x → 3.0x by end 2026). Risks include acquisition integration execution and leverage remaining elevated versus typical BBB+ standards, but Moody's and S&P expect improvement.
​

Financial Profile: Nasdaq has dramatically improved its balance sheet. The company is committed to reducing leverage below 3.5x through operating earnings growth and modest capex. Free cash flow is strong, and the company has seamless debt market access. Moody's notes any future acquisitions would be followed by timely deleveraging, which limits downside risk.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Financial Exchanges 4-5% allocation. "Rising Star" to solid mid-investment grade. Monitor: (1) Adenza integration progress and synergy realization; (2) Core exchange volumes and listing activity; (3) Leverage reduction path (3.0x target); (4) Regulatory/competitive pressures on tech listings.

issuers.name: NATIONAL FUEL GAS CO
issuers.creditComment:
NATIONAL FUEL GAS CO
Credit Rating: BBB-/Stable (S&P Affirmed July 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's estimated) | BBB/Stable (Fitch estimated) – Integrated Natural Gas Company; Upstream/Midstream/Utility; Dividend Aristocrat; Regulated Growth
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – National Fuel Gas Company 
 maintains a solid investment-grade credit profile with strong fundamentals. S&P affirmed the 'BBB-' ratings on July 6, 2025. The company is an integrated natural gas producer, midstream operator, and utility with businesses spanning exploration & production (Seneca), gathering (Seneca Resources gathering), pipeline transportation, and regulated utility distribution (National Fuel Gas Distribution). NFG is a 123-year dividend payer with 55 consecutive years of dividend increases (Dividend Aristocrat status).
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) integrated natural gas business model providing diversification and stable cash flows from regulated utility segments; (b) strong market position and balance sheet (net debt/adjusted EBITDA 2.27x FY24, improving to 2.0-2.1x FY25); (c) profitable upstream operations (Seneca breakeven <$2.25/MMBtu with 20-year drilling inventory); (d) rate base growth from regulated utility through three-year rate settlement and modernization programs; (e) consistent dividend policy and investor returns; (f) environmental progress (17% methane emissions reduction since 2020). The company has ample headroom below 3.5x downgrade threshold with disciplined capital allocation prioritizing rate base growth and balance sheet management.
​

Financial Profile: NFG projects strong growth with adjusted EPS CAGR >10% from FY24 to FY27. Q3 FY2025 results showed adjusted operating results +66% YoY and production guidance raised 8% to 420-425 Bcfe (reflecting operational efficiency in Eastern Development Area). The company expects net debt/adjusted EBITDA to improve to 2.0-2.1x in FY25. Capital priorities focus on regulated growth (rate base expansion through NY settlement and PA modernization), upstream production growth (mid-single digits), balance sheet management, and dividend increases. Paused buyback program to maintain balance sheet flexibility for growth investments.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Integrated Gas 5-6% allocation. Core IG credit (BBB-/Baa2) with Dividend Aristocrat status. Monitor: (1) Natural gas pricing (demand/supply); (2) Regulated utility rate case success; (3) Upstream production growth execution; (4) Leverage trajectory; (5) Dividend growth sustainability.

issuers.name: NATIONAL GRID PLC
issuers.creditComment:
NATIONAL GRID PLC
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Affirmed Apr 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's) | BBB/Stable (Fitch) – Transatlantic Utility Giant; Massive Capex Plan; Equity Raise Supported Balance Sheet
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – National Grid plc 
 maintains a solid investment-grade profile. S&P affirmed the 'BBB+' rating for National Grid North America in April 2025. Moody's rates the group Baa2 with a stable outlook. The stable outlooks reflect the successful execution of its £7 billion equity raise in 2024 to fund its expanded capital investment plan.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are anchored by: (a) regulated monopoly status in UK transmission (electricity/gas) and US networks (New York/Massachusetts); (b) inflation-linked regulatory asset base (RAV); (c) continued portfolio pruning (sale of ESO). The company's massive £60 billion capex plan (2025-2029) is the primary credit constraint, partially mitigated by the equity injection.
​

Financial Profile: National Grid targets keeping hybrid-adjusted net debt/EBITDA between 4.5x-5.5x. The recent equity raise was crucial to maintain credit metrics within this band while funding the "Great Grid Upgrade." Regulatory frameworks in the UK (RIIO-T2) and US provide predictable returns.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within Global Utilities 6-8% allocation. Defensive regulated utility (BBB+/Baa2) with strong asset backing. Monitor: (1) Execution of the £60bn capex plan; (2) Regulatory outcomes (RIIO-T3 preparations); (3) Interest rate pass-through; (4) US regulatory rate case filings.

issuers.name: NBN CO LTD
issuers.creditComment:
NBN CO LIMITED
Credit Rating: AA+/Stable (Fitch) | Aa3/Stable (Moody's) | A+/Stable (S&P, Implied) – Australia's National Broadband Network; 100% Government-Owned; Essential Infrastructure; Strong Liquidity
​
Credit Momentum: Stable – NBN Co Limited maintains high investment-grade ratings, supported by extraordinary government backing and the essential nature of broadband infrastructure to the Australian economy. Fitch rates NBN at 'AA+' with a stable outlook, while Moody's rates it 'Aa3' (both stable).
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are underpinned by: (a) 100% government ownership with statement of expectations (SoE) confirming public ownership "for the foreseeable future"; (b) essential infrastructure role with high bipartisan political support; (c) six-notch uplift above baseline credit assessment (Moody's) due to government support; (d) monopoly-like position as Australia's wholesale broadband provider. Moody's notes that NBN's A1 baseline assessment (ba1 BCA + 6-notch uplift) reflects exceptional support likelihood in times of need.
​
Financial Profile (FY25 Results): NBN Co delivered solid operational and financial performance with: (a) Revenue growth of 4% to AUD 5.7 billion (FY25); (b) EBITDA growth of 8% to AUD 4.2 billion; (c) Capex reduction of 7% to AUD 3.5 billion as the company completes majority of network infrastructure investments; (d) Operating expenses declined 5% to AUD 1.6 billion through cost efficiency initiatives. Residential ARPU rose to AUD 50/month (up AUD 3), driven by higher-speed tier adoption and SAU Variation tariff adjustments.
​
Network & Growth Drivers: Fibre Connect program is on track to complete initial five-year upgrade by December 2025, making 10+ million premises (90% of fixed-line network) eligible for near-gigabit wholesale speeds. Business and enterprise revenue grew 6% to AUD 1.2 billion. Data usage continues to accelerate (508 GB average per month; FTTP services averaging 606 GB, up 13% YoY).
​
Capital Structure & Debt Management: As of FY25, NBN Co had AUD 8.67 billion of outstanding Green and Sustainability Bonds. In FY25 alone, the company issued: (1) AUD 800M Green Bond (April 2024); (2) AUD 750M Green Bond (August 2024); (3) EUR 700M (~AUD 1.23B) Sustainability Bond (May 2025); (4) AUD 750M Green Bond (March 2025); (5) USD 650M (~AUD 988M) via US private placement (September 2025).
​
The September 2025 USD 650M bond placement was heavily oversubscribed (7.7x demand, achieving USD 4.8B in interest), achieving best-ever pricing for NBN's five-year USD bonds. Proceeds are being used to refinance existing debt and fund capex.
​
Investment-Grade Rating Path: While NBN currently carries high IG ratings (AA+/Aa3), a detailed financial analysis indicates the company is targeting transition to "standalone" investment-grade status over the medium term. This requires increasing revenue above the Annual Building Block Revenue Requirement (ABBRR), which NBN expects to achieve by 2031-32 under its baseline financial projections. However, with government support uplift embedded in current ratings, refinancing risk is minimal.
​
Sustainability & ESG Leadership: NBN Co is Australia's largest corporate issuer of Green and Sustainability Bonds (AUD 8.67B outstanding). The company has validated science-based emissions reduction targets with the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi), targeting Net Zero across all scopes by FY45. Climate Transition Plan approved by Board in December 2023, with 100% renewable electricity purchases by December 2025.
​
Liquidity & Funding Access: Robust market access. NBN has a Global Medium Term Note (GMTN) program providing diversified funding channels. Recent bond issuances across AUD, EUR, and USD markets with strong demand indicate stable, long-term funding access at favorable pricing. Total debt outstanding is approximately AUD 15.3 billion equivalent.
​
Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within Global Infrastructure/Utilities 6-8% allocation. Premier quasi-sovereign infrastructure credit (AA+/Aa3) offering: (1) government backing with explicit SoE support; (2) monopoly-like market position in wholesale broadband; (3) stable and growing cash flows as network matures; (4) strong ESG credentials; (5) low refinancing risk. Monitor: (1) ARPU trajectory vs. competitive threats; (2) Capex discipline as network completion approaches; (3) Government policy changes to SoE; (4) Regulatory decisions on wholesale pricing (SAU variations); (5) Debt trajectory toward investment-grade metric sustainability targets.

Key Risk/Opportunity: The primary upside catalyst is achieving standalone investment-grade status through organic cash flow generation, which would de-risk the credit from government policy shifts. Conversely, prolonged government funding constraints or major policy reversals (e.g., privatization plans) could theoretically constrain ratings, though this scenario remains low-probability given bipartisan support and essential infrastructure status.

issuers.name: NESTLE HOLDINGS INC
issuers.creditComment:
NESTLE HOLDINGS INC. (NESTLÉ S.A.)
Credit Rating: AA-/Negative (S&P Oct 2025) | Aa3/Stable (Moody's 2025) | A+/Stable (Fitch Sep 2025 - Withdrawn) – World's Largest Food & Beverage Company; CHF ~90B Revenue; 2.9-3.0x Leverage; CEO Change; CHF16K Headcount Reduction; Portfolio Review Underway

Credit Momentum: Negative/Pressure – Nestle Holdings Inc. issues senior unsecured notes guaranteed by Nestlé S.A. 
, with ratings equalized to parent. S&P revised outlook to Negative (October 2025), affirming AA-/A-1+ ratings, citing slower debt reduction and sustained leverage around 3x through 2027—not aligned with AA- rating. Moody's maintains Aa3/Stable. Fitch affirmed A+ before withdrawing (September 2025). The company faces multiple headwinds including restructuring costs, cocoa/coffee input inflation, and US tariffs on Swiss imports:​

Credit Ratings & Deleveraging Concerns:
Nestlé Holdings maintains AA-/Negative from S&P (October 2025), Aa3/Stable from Moody's, with Fitch A+ withdrawn (September 2025). S&P October 2025 outlook revision: Changed to Negative from Stable; Adjusted debt/EBITDA expected 2.9-3.0x through 2025-2027, approaching upper end of 2x-3x target and not aligned with AA- rating. Deleveraging constraints: Elevated dividend distributions absorbing majority of ~CHF9B annual FOCF; CHF2B one-off restructuring costs further limiting organic debt reduction. Downgrade trigger: Rating could be lowered if leverage remains around 3x beyond 2027 or strategic initiatives fail.​

Operating Challenges:
Nestlé faces multiple headwinds: Revenue contraction: 9M 2025 sales down 1.9% YoY, primarily from 5.4% FX headwinds; Organic growth 3.3% (price +2.8%). Margin pressure: Adjusted EBITDA margin expected ~20% in 2025 vs 21.6% in 2024 from higher advertising costs, cocoa/coffee input inflation, CHF appreciation, and 39% US tariffs on Switzerland. Restructuring: ~16,000 employee reduction announced over two years (~12,000 white-collar) driving CHF2B one-off costs. Leadership change: CEO succession (Laurent Freixe → Philipp Navratil in September 2025 following code of conduct violation); No material strategy changes expected.​

Portfolio Review & Potential Divestitures:
Nestlé pursuing strategic options: Water business review: Potential sale under consideration. VMS mainstream segment: Vitamins, Supplements and Minerals mainstream category review ongoing. Divestiture potential: Successful sales could generate additional resources for debt reduction. Froneri distribution: ~CHF2B extraordinary distribution received from ice cream JV with PAI Partners, partially offsetting debt. Debt reduction: S&P projects adjusted debt ~CHF54.5B end-2025 vs CHF57.5B in 2024, partly from USD depreciation on dollar-denominated debt.​

Overall Assessment: Nestle Holdings Inc. (Nestlé S.A.) is recommended MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within consumer staples 5-6% European IG allocation. Nestlé represents high-quality but pressured credit (AA-/Aa3) with Negative S&P outlook, facing multiple near-term headwinds requiring monitoring of deleveraging trajectory. Strengths include: (a) AA-/Aa3 ratings—among highest in consumer staples; (b) world's largest food & beverage company with iconic brands; (c) ~CHF90B revenue with global scale and diversification; (d) strong liquidity and market access; (e) portfolio review potentially unlocking divestiture proceeds; (f) Froneri distribution providing liquidity. Risks include: (a) S&P Negative outlook with potential downgrade if leverage persists at ~3x; (b) CHF2B restructuring costs constraining deleveraging; (c) 39% US tariffs on Swiss imports affecting margins; (d) cocoa/coffee input cost inflation; (e) CEO change creating execution uncertainty; (f) FX headwinds from strong CHF; (g) dividend policy limiting debt reduction capacity. RECOMMENDED: Market weight given high credit quality but near-term pressure; Monitor deleveraging and outlook resolution.

Monitor: (1) S&P outlook (Negative resolution and potential downgrade); (2) Leverage trajectory (2.9-3.0x sustainably below 3x by 2027); (3) Portfolio divestitures (Water, VMS proceeds and debt reduction); (4) Restructuring execution (CHF2B costs and margin recovery); (5) Tariff impacts (39% US tariff mitigation).

issuers.name: NETFLIX INC
issuers.creditComment:
NETFLIX INC
Credit Rating: Ba1/Positive (Moody's) | BB+/Positive (S&P) – Global Streaming Leader; Q3 2025 Revenue $11.5B +17% YoY; Net Income $2.55B +8% YoY; Operating Margin 28%; Free Cash Flow $9B FY2025E; Ad Revenue Doubling YoY​

Credit Momentum: Strong with Investment-Grade Potential – Netflix Inc. maintains strong high-yield ratings with positive outlook reflecting exceptional revenue growth, margin expansion, and free cash flow generation that positions the company for potential investment-grade upgrade.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Netflix maintains Ba1/Positive from Moody's and BB+/Positive from S&P, one notch below investment grade with positive outlooks signaling upgrade potential. Ratings reflect: (a) dominant global streaming position (largest subscriber base); (b) exceptional revenue growth (+17% YoY); (c) strong margin trajectory (~30% operating margin); (d) improving free cash flow generation ($9B FY2025E); (e) conservative leverage declining toward 2x.​

Q3 2025 Solid Financial Results: Q3 2025 revenue $11.51B (+17% YoY), in line with guidance. Net income $2.55B ($5.87 EPS), up from $2.36B prior year. Operating margin 28% (below 31.5% guidance due to Brazilian tax expense one-time charge). Free cash flow guidance raised to ~$9B FY2025 (from $8-8.5B). Ad-supported tier highest sales quarter ever; ad revenue expected to double in 2025 (~$1.1B). Regional growth: US/Canada +17%, EMEA +18%, Asia-Pacific +21%, LatAm +10%.​

FY2025 Outlook & Strategic Positioning: FY2025 revenue guidance $45.1B (+16% YoY). Operating margin ~29% (adjusted for Brazilian tax). Q4 2025 revenue guidance ~$11.96B (+17% YoY). Amazon Ads partnership launching Q4 2025 across 11 countries, potentially accelerating ad revenue. Gaming expansion through partnerships (GTA, Roblox). Content pipeline strong (Wednesday, Stranger Things upcoming).​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within global streaming/media 8-10% allocation. Strong high-yield credit on upgrade trajectory toward investment grade. Exceptional revenue growth (+17%), margin expansion, and $9B FCF support deleveraging. Monitor: (1) Investment-grade upgrade timeline; (2) Ad revenue trajectory (doubling target); (3) Q4 2025 revenue delivery; (4) Content engagement and churn metrics; (5) Competitive dynamics and pricing power.

issuers.name: NEWMARKET CORP
issuers.creditComment:
NEWMARKET CORP
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Dec 2025) | BBB/Stable (S&P per 2024) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's estimated) – Fuel & Lubricant Additives; Niche Specialty; Defensive
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – NewMarket Corporation [finance:Newmarket Corp] maintains a solid BBB investment-grade profile. Fitch affirmed the 'BBB' IDR with a Stable outlook on December 10, 2025, noting stable earnings profile, strong free cash flow generation, and conservative capital allocation. S&P rates the company 'BBB' (maintains through 2024/2025). The company is one of four leading producers of petroleum additives globally, benefiting from close customer relationships and high barriers to entry.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) niche market leadership in fuel and lubricant additives (Petroleum Additives + Specialty Materials); (b) barriers from long qualification processes and customer switching costs; (c) stable earnings with low sensitivity to oil price fluctuations (customers, not commodities, drive margins); (d) conservative leverage (EBITDA leverage ~1.5x); (e) strong FCF generation. Risks include limited diversification and long-term EV penetration threat (though miles driven still drive demand), but Fitch expects gradual shift toward higher-margin specialty materials.
​

Financial Profile: NewMarket maintains disciplined capital allocation. Fitch expects leverage to remain around 1.5x-1.6x through the forecast. The company generated strong FCF historically and continues modest dividends (~$100M/year) plus opportunistic buybacks. Recent acquisitions (AMPAC 2024, Calca 2025) are bolt-ons and immediately accretive. Liquidity is solid (~$900M available on revolver + cash).
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Specialty Chemicals 3-4% allocation. Defensive "BBB" compounder. Monitor: (1) EV penetration rates (long-term threat); (2) M&A integration (AMPAC/Calca); (3) Petroleum additive margins vs. competition; (4) Leverage discipline.

issuers.name: NEWMONT CORP
issuers.creditComment:
NEWMONT CORP
Credit Rating: A-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed June 2025) | A3/Stable (Moody's Upgraded Aug 2025) | A-/Stable (S&P estimated) – Gold Mining Giant; Fortress Balance Sheet; Rapid Deleveraging
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgraded to "A" Tier) – Newmont Corporation [finance:Newmont Corp] has achieved a significant credit upgrade. Moody's upgraded the issuer rating to 'A3' (from Baa1) on August 27, 2025, with a Stable outlook, citing strengthened balance sheet, improved credit profile, and excellent liquidity. Fitch affirmed the 'A-' rating with a Stable outlook in June 2025. The upgrades reflect Newmont's massive debt reduction ($6B+ paid down since 2023) driven by exceptional gold prices and disciplined capital management.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are anchored by: (a) status as the world's largest gold mining company; (b) diversified mine portfolio across Africa, Australia, Latin America, and North America; (c) strong free cash flow generation (gold price environment dependent); (d) rapid deleveraging (Moody's notes Debt/EBITDA fell to 0.7x as of Q2 2025 from 2.6x at year-end 2023). Risks include commodity price volatility and geopolitical exposure, but Newmont's scale and balance sheet strength provide resilience.
​

Financial Profile: Newmont's balance sheet is exceptional. The company ended Q2 2025 with $6.2 billion in cash and $10.2 billion in total liquidity. Capital allocation priorities are well-balanced: maintaining balance sheet strength, reinvesting in the business, and returning cash to shareholders (dividends + buybacks). The company has flagged robust free cash flow generation across a range of gold price environments.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Precious Metals Mining 4-5% allocation. High-quality "A" credit with fortress balance sheet. Monitor: (1) Gold price trajectory (primary earnings driver); (2) Mining jurisdictional risks (Africa/geopolitical); (3) Capital allocation discipline; (4) Copper/silver production scaling (portfolio diversification).

issuers.name: NEWS CORP
issuers.creditComment:
NEWS CORP
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch Upgraded May 2025 from BBB-) | BBB-/Stable (S&P estimated) | Ba1/Stable (Moody's, estimated) – Media and Publishing; Digital Growth; Diversified Revenue
​
Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgraded by Fitch) – News Corporation has achieved credit improvement. Fitch upgraded the Long-Term IDR to 'BBB' (from 'BBB-') on May 16, 2025, with a Stable outlook. News Corp is a global media and publishing company with diversified operations spanning newspapers, book publishing (Harper Collins), television, digital media, and other content/advertising platforms. The company operates across Australia, the UK, and the US with iconic brands (Wall Street Journal, Times, Financial Times stake, The Sun, etc.).​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) market leadership in news publishing and editorial content with strong brand portfolios; (b) diversified revenue streams (advertising, circulation, subscription, digital); (c) digital transition initiatives improving margin profile; (d) scale and global reach providing financial flexibility; (e) solid FCF generation from publishing operations. Fitch's upgrade from BBB- signals improved business fundamentals or financial metrics. Constraints include cyclical advertising demand, legacy print media structural headwinds (offset by digital growth), and operating challenges in competitive media market.
​
Financial Profile: News Corp maintains solid fundamentals with strong brand value and diversified revenue model. The company is investing in digital subscriptions and advertising technology to drive future growth. Liquidity is adequate with cash generation and capital markets access. The Fitch upgrade suggests improving leverage metrics or operational momentum. The company is well-positioned in digital news/publishing with strategic assets (WSJ, Times majority stake) benefiting from premium content subscription trends.
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Global Media 4-5% allocation. Upgraded IG credit (BBB/BBB- Stable) with digital media transition. Monitor: (1) Advertising cycle and demand trends; (2) Digital subscription revenue growth; (3) Print circulation declines mitigation; (4) M&A strategy; (5) Free cash flow and leverage management.

issuers.name: NEXA RESOURCES SA
issuers.creditComment:
NEXA RESOURCES SA (USL67359AB21)
Credit Rating: High-yield/crossover category (BB+/Ba1 range)

Credit Momentum: Stable to Positive - IG Crossover Potential. Nexa operates as Latin America's largest integrated zinc producer with mining/smelting operations spanning Peru, Brazil with exploration in Mexico. Company benefits from: (1) vertical integration from mining through refined zinc production; (2) diversified asset base (5 mines: Cerro Lindo, El Porvenir, Atacocha in Peru; Aripuanã, Vazante in Brazil); (3) by-product credits (copper, lead, silver); (4) smelting operations (Cajamarquilla Peru, Três Marias Brazil) capturing processing margins; (5) zinc structural demand from infrastructure steel galvanizing. Credit profile one notch below investment-grade (BB+/Ba1) reflects: (1) zinc price volatility with recent weakness; (2) operational challenges at specific assets; (3) leverage elevated at 2.5-3.5x net debt/EBITDA; (4) LatAm operational concentration.

Overall Assessment: Nexa represents LatAm zinc credit with investment-grade crossover potential following deleveraging. BB+/Ba1 ratings one notch below IG threshold reflect: (1) integrated model more stable than pure miners; (2) scale as regional leader; (3) improving operational performance; (4) zinc demand recovery pathway (infrastructure spending, Chinese stimulus). Suitable for high-yield investors seeking: (1) base metals exposure with IG upgrade catalyst; (2) zinc structural demand (post-pandemic infrastructure boom, steel galvanizing requirements); (3) LatAm mining diversification (both Peru/Brazil reduces single-country risk); (4) processing margin stability from smelters. Path to investment-grade requires: (1) sustained zinc prices above $3,000/ton; (2) leverage reduction below 2.5x net debt/EBITDA; (3) operational consistency across asset portfolio; (4) free cash flow generation enabling debt paydown. Key positives: (1) zinc supply-demand improving (mine closures creating deficit); (2) infrastructure spending supporting galvanized steel demand; (3) smelting margins resilient; (4) copper by-product credits valuable. Risks: (1) zinc price volatility (recent range $2,500-3,500/ton); (2) Peruvian political instability; (3) Brazilian mining regulations; (4) operational challenges (flooding, community relations); (5) energy costs at smelters. Monitor: (1) zinc LME pricing; (2) production guidance; (3) cost performance; (4) leverage trajectory; (5) smelter utilization rates. Recommended market weight for high-yield portfolios - quality miner with IG upgrade potential but zinc price volatility creates risk. Preferred zinc exposure versus smaller regional players given integration and scale.

issuers.name: NIAGARA ENERGY SAC
issuers.creditComment:
NIAENE 5.746% 03/10/2034 – Niagara Energy S.A.C. (Peru), BBB‑/Baa3
Профиль бизнеса
Холдинг генерационных активов в Перу (Orygen/Niagara), около 16–17% выработки страны, микс гидро/тепловых станций и контрактов с регулируемыми и нерегулируемыми клиентами.
Значительная часть генерации законтрактована по долгосрочным PPAs с кредитоспособными off‑takers, что обеспечивает видимость кэш‑флоу.

Кредитные метрики
Moody’s: Baa3 со стабильным outlook, при этом подчёркивает высокий леверидж и слабые метрики до 2027 г.; цель – FFO/debt >20% и FFO/interest >4,5x к концу горизонта.
​Fitch подтверждает BBB‑, ожидая постепенного улучшения метрик после волны инвестиций и переконтрактации PPAs.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды
IG‑уровень (BBB‑/Baa3) с поддержкой диверсифицированной базы активов и регуляторики; outlook в целом стабильный.
Выпуск 2034 (YTM ~5,5%, dur 6,5, OAS ~135–175 bps) платит стандартный спред для перуанских IG‑IPP с несколько более высоким левериджем.

Ключевые риски
Высокий долговой уровень и необходимость рефинансировать часть долга после 2027 г.; негативный сценарий по ценам/PPAs может замедлить deleveraging.
Конкурентный, частично избыточный рынок Перу, давление на цены и маржу при переконтрактации.

Инвестидия
Сильный IG‑IPP с хорошей франшизой и приемлемым спредом, но с очевидным риском execution по снижению левериджа.
В IG‑корзине Перу выглядит интереснее суверена, но чуть рискованнее ATL/Kallpa; позицию разумно держать, но мониторить динамику FFO/debt.

issuers.name: NOKIA OYJ
issuers.creditComment:
NOKIA OYJ
Credit Rating: BBB-/Positive (Moody's Outlook Revised Dec 2025) | BBB-/Stable (S&P Affirmed July 2025) | BBB-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Sept 2025) – Global Network Infrastructure Leader; Improving Profitability; Strong Balance Sheet
​

Credit Momentum: Positive – Nokia Oyj 
 is seeing improving credit sentiment. Moody's revised its outlook to Positive (affirming Ba1 - equivalent to BB+) in December 2025, citing strengthening profitability driven by network infrastructure. S&P and Fitch rate Nokia 'BBB-' (Investment Grade) with stable outlooks, affirmed in mid-to-late 2025.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) top-tier position in mobile and fixed network infrastructure (non-Chinese alternative); (b) strong intellectual property (IP) portfolio generating recurring royalties; (c) massive net cash position (€6.1bn); (d) disciplined cost management. The positive outlook from Moody's reflects confidence in the "Network Infrastructure" segment's growth.
​

Financial Profile: Nokia maintains a very conservative balance sheet with a substantial net cash position. Gross leverage is low. The company targets margins >14% by 2026. Free cash flow generation is positive, supporting shareholder returns while preserving credit quality. Liquidity is robust.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Global Tech/Telecom Equipment 5-6% allocation. Solid investment grade (BBB-) with net cash buffer. Monitor: (1) 5G spending cycle in India/US; (2) Enterprise private wireless growth; (3) Licensing renewal cycle; (4) Margins in Mobile Networks.

issuers.name: NORTHERN STAR RESOU
issuers.creditComment:
NORTHERN STAR RESOURCES LTD
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed December 2024) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's) – Australia's Second-Largest Gold Producer; FY2025 Net Income A$1.34B +110% YoY; Revenue A$6.41B +30%; Production 1.7-1.85M oz FY2026E; KCGM Mill Expansion On Track; Net Cash A$616M​

Credit Momentum: Strong with Gold Price Tailwinds – Northern Star Resources Ltd maintains solid investment-grade ratings reflecting large-scale Australian gold operations, strong cash generation from elevated gold prices, and conservative balance sheet positioning.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Northern Star maintains BBB/Stable from Fitch (affirmed December 2024) and Baa2/Stable from Moody's. Fitch's December 2024 affirmation followed announcement of A$5B asset acquisition program. Ratings reflect: (a) Australia's second-largest gold producer; (b) long-life, high-quality asset base (KCGM, Jundee, Pogo); (c) strong free cash flow generation; (d) net cash balance sheet; (e) disciplined capital allocation; (f) organic growth pipeline (KCGM Mill Expansion, Hemi Development).​

FY2025 & Q1 FY2026 Financial Performance: FY2025 (June 2025) net income A$1.34B (+110% YoY). Revenue A$6.41B (+30% YoY). EBITDA A$2.1B. Underlying NPAT benefited from 29% higher realized gold price (A$3,922/oz vs. A$3,031/oz). Record dividend 55 Australian cents per share for FY2025. Q1 FY2026: gold sold 381K oz, AISC A$2,522/oz (US$1,639/oz), net mine cash flow A$183M, net cash A$616M.​

Growth Projects & Outlook: FY2026 guidance: 1,700-1,850K oz gold sold, AISC A$2,300-2,700/oz. KCGM Mill Expansion on track for early FY2027 commissioning (Ministerial approval received). Hemi Development Project advancing. FY2026 growth capex A$2,125-2,270M. No hedge commitments added over past year, allowing full gold price exposure.​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within global gold mining 6-8% allocation. Solid investment-grade gold credit (BBB/Baa2) with strong cash generation, net cash position, and significant growth optionality. Monitor: (1) FY2026 production guidance delivery; (2) KCGM Mill Expansion commissioning; (3) Hemi Development progress; (4) Gold price environment; (5) AISC trajectory.

issuers.name: NOVARTIS CORP
issuers.creditComment:
NOVARTIS CAPITAL CORP
Credit Rating: Aa3/Stable (Moody's) | AA-/Stable (S&P Affirmed April 2025) – Debt-Issuing Vehicle of Novartis AG 
; Global Pharma Leader; Q3 2025 Net Sales $13.8B +10% CC; Core Operating Margin 39.3%; Core EPS $2.25 +10% CC; $10B Share Buyback Initiated​

Credit Momentum: Strong with Stable Outlook – Novartis Capital Corporation serves as the U.S. dollar debt-issuing subsidiary of Novartis AG, a global pharmaceutical leader. Credit ratings reflect exceptional operational performance, strong pipeline execution, and balanced capital allocation supporting investment-grade stability.​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Novartis Capital Corp maintains ratings equivalent to parent Novartis AG: Aa3/Stable from Moody's and AA-/Stable from S&P (affirmed April 2025). Ratings reflect: (a) leading positions across cardiovascular-renal-metabolic, immunology, neuroscience, and oncology; (b) strong organic growth driven by key franchises (Kisqali, Kesimpta, Pluvicto, Scemblix); (c) exceptional core operating margins (39.3%); (d) robust balance sheet and strong cash generation; (e) conservative financial policy.​

Q3 2025 Financial Performance: Q3 2025 net sales $13.8B (+10% CC, +7% USD). Core operating income $5.5B (+6%, +7% CC). Core operating margin 39.3% (stable CC). Core net income $4.3B (+5%, +6% CC). Core EPS $2.25 (+9%, +10% CC), benefiting from share count reduction. Key drivers: Kisqali +80%, Kesimpta +31%, Pluvicto +39%, Scemblix +95%. FY2025 guidance reaffirmed: sales +low double-digits CC, core operating income growing faster than sales.​

Balance Sheet & Capital Returns: Up to $10B share buyback announced (part of balanced capital allocation commitment). Strong liquidity with robust cash generation. Short-term ratings P-1 (Moody's), A-1+ (S&P). Multiple pipeline catalysts: FDA approval Rhapsido (CSU), positive Phase III for ianalumab (Sjogren's), CHMP positive opinion for Scemblix (CML all lines).​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within global pharma 6-8% allocation. Strong investment-grade pharma credit (Aa3/AA-) with exceptional organic growth, leading margins, and strong pipeline. Monitor: (1) FY2025 guidance delivery (low double-digit sales growth); (2) Key franchise momentum (Kisqali, Kesimpta); (3) Pipeline readouts and approvals; (4) Generic erosion management; (5) Capital allocation and $10B buyback execution.

issuers.name: NTPC LTD
issuers.creditComment:
NTPC LTD
Credit Rating: BBB-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed June 2025) | BBB-/Stable (S&P) | Baa3/Stable (Moody's) – India's Largest Power Generator; 51% GoI Owned; Regulated Cost-Plus Model; Transitioning to Renewables
​
Credit Momentum: Stable – NTPC Limited maintains investment-grade ratings supported by its dominant market position (24% of India's power generation) and regulated business model. Fitch affirmed the BBB- rating in June 2025. Domestic ratings are AAA/Stable.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) strategic importance to India's power sector; (b) majority GoI ownership (51.1%); (c) regulated tariff structure ensuring recovery of fixed costs and return on equity (15.5%); (d) high collection efficiency post-LPS scheme implementation. Moody's affirmed the Baa3 rating in September 2025 following a periodic review.
​
Operational & Financial Profile: Installed capacity ~76 GW (as of mid-2025). Aggressive renewable energy target (60 GW by 2032). FY2025 EBITDA ~INR 54,000 crore. Leverage (Debt/EBITDA) stood at ~4.6x, elevated due to capex but manageable given regulated cash flows. Liquidity remains strong with access to domestic and international markets.
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within India/Asia Utilities 5-6% allocation. Core utility credit (BBB-/Baa3) with stable regulated cash flows and government backing. Monitor: (1) Renewable energy execution risks; (2) Receivables from state discoms; (3) Coal availability and PLF levels; (4) Sovereign rating action.

issuers.name: NTT FINANCE CORP
issuers.creditComment:
NTT FINANCE CORP
Credit Rating: A3/Negative (Moody's Downgraded June 2025) | A-/Negative (S&P Downgraded June 2025) – Financing Arm of NTT Group; Downgraded Following NTT Data Acquisition; Elevated Leverage
​

Credit Momentum: Negative Trend – NTT Finance Corporation was downgraded by both Moody's (to A3 from A2) and S&P (to A- from A) in June 2025. The outlooks are Negative (S&P) and Stable (Moody's). The downgrades followed parent NTT Corp's debt-funded acquisition of the remaining stake in NTT Data Group, which significantly increased group leverage.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are inextricably linked to parent Nippon Telegraph and Telephone Corp (NTT). The acquisition pushed group debt/EBITDA to the mid-3x to mid-4x range, exceeding previous thresholds for the 'A/A2' ratings. Despite this, NTT Finance remains a core subsidiary with strong support.
​

Financial Impact: The acquisition cost ~JPY 2.4 trillion, funded largely by debt. S&P notes a "one-in-three chance" of further deterioration if NTT prioritizes growth over deleveraging. However, NTT's dominant market position in Japan telecom and growing global IT services business provide strong operating cash flow to service the debt.
​

Overall Assessment: UNDERWEIGHT – 2-3% within Japan Telecom/Tech 4-5% allocation. Weakening investment-grade credit (A3/A-) with elevated leverage overhang. Monitor: (1) Deleveraging progress (asset sales, profit growth); (2) Integration of NTT Data; (3) Further M&A activity; (4) Shareholder return policy.

issuers.name: NUCOR CORP
issuers.creditComment:
NUCOR CORP
Credit Rating: A3/Stable (Moody's Upgraded Sept 2025) | A-/Stable (S&P Assigned March 2025; Fitch Affirmed Feb 2025) – North American Steel Leader; Strongest Sector Credit; EAF Advantage
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgraded to "A" Tier) – Nucor Corp [finance:Nucor Corp] now holds the strongest credit rating in North American steel. Moody's upgraded the rating to 'A3' (from Baa1) in September 2025 with a Stable outlook. S&P assigned an 'A-' rating to senior unsecured notes on March 3, 2025. Fitch affirmed the 'A-' rating with a Stable outlook on February 28, 2025. The upgrades reflect Nucor's operational excellence, electric arc furnace (EAF) cost advantage, and fortress balance sheet management.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) leadership position as North America's largest steel producer; (b) low-cost EAF operations providing significant margin advantage vs. integrated mills in commodity downturns; (c) strong free cash flow and disciplined capital allocation; (d) fortress balance sheet (net debt/EBITDA expected <1.5x); (e) proven M&A integration capability. Risks include commodity price cyclicality and tariff policy changes, though recent U.S. tariff and trade policies support American manufacturing.
​

Financial Profile: Nucor maintains exceptional financial strength. As of Q3 2025, the company held $2.75 billion in cash and short-term investments with an undrawn $2.25 billion revolving credit facility. Leverage is expected to remain below 1.5x EBITDA through the forecast. The company generates substantial free cash flow, funding dividends, share repurchases, and strategic capital investments. Liquidity is world-class for the sector.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Steel & Metals 4-5% allocation. Premier "A" tier industrial credit (A3/A-). Monitor: (1) Steel pricing and mill utilization rates; (2) EAF cost advantage sustainability vs. scrap prices; (3) Capital returns discipline; (4) Trade policy/tariff impacts on pricing.

issuers.name: NVENT FINANCE SARL
issuers.creditComment:
NVENT ELECTRIC PLC
Credit Rating: BBB-/Stable (S&P 2025) | Ba1/Positive (Moody's 2025) | BBB-/Stable (Fitch 2025) – Global Electrical Connection & Protection Solutions; $1.1B Q3 2025 Revenue (+35% YoY, +16% Organic); $0.91 Adjusted EPS (+44% YoY); 27%+ Adjusted Operating Margin; Data Center & Power Utility Growth; 27-28% FY Sales Guidance

Credit Momentum: Positive – nVent Electric 
 maintains solid investment-grade ratings (BBB-/Ba1/BBB-) reflecting strong organic growth (16% Q3), exceptional free cash flow generation ($253M Q3 +77% YoY), and strategic positioning in high-growth infrastructure verticals (data centers, power utilities). Q3 2025 delivered record sales, orders, and backlog with 27% adjusted operating margin (+70bp YoY). Moody's Ba1 rating one notch below investment grade, though Positive outlook signals upgrade potential as operations scale:

Credit Ratings & Outlook Dynamics:
nVent maintains BBB-/Stable ratings from S&P and Fitch, with Ba1/Positive from Moody's. Fitch assessment: BBB-/Stable reflecting solid business model and growth in infrastructure markets. Moody's outlook: Ba1/Positive indicating potential upgrade path as revenue scaling and operational leverage improve credit metrics. Tariff mitigation: Expected tariff costs reduce to approximately $90 million (from $120 million prior estimate) as supply chain adjustments offset inflation. Acquisition integration playbook successfully executed through Trachte and EPG acquisitions bolstering capabilities.​

Q3 2025 Record Performance:
nVent reported exceptional Q3 2025 results (November 26, 2025): Revenue: $1.1 billion (+35% reported, +16% organic) marking record sales. EPS: $0.73 (+55% YoY); Adjusted EPS $0.91 (+44% YoY). Operating income: $166 million (+25% YoY); Adjusted operating income $213 million (+27%). Operating margin: 27%+ adjusted reflecting operational excellence and pricing power. Cash flow: Operating cash flow $272 million (+72% YoY); Free cash flow $253 million (+77%). Segments: Systems Protection net sales $716 million (+50%, +23% organic); Electrical Connections $338 million (+11%, +5% organic). Backlog: Record ending backlog reflecting strong demand pipeline.​

Strategic Growth Drivers:
nVent capitalizes on structural tailwinds: Infrastructure vertical: +20% organic sales growth with strength in data centers and power utilities driving segment growth. Data center solutions: Liquid cooling products and connection solutions for data center expansion; New product launches supporting growth. Power utilities: Electrical connection and protection solutions for grid modernization and renewable integration. Acquisition strategy: Trachte (manufacturing) and EPG (data solutions) acquisitions driving capabilities expansion; Integration success demonstrated in Q3 results. Geographic diversification: Americas and Asia Pacific each up high single digits; Europe up low single digits in Q2.​

FY2025 Guidance Raise:
nVent raised full-year 2025 guidance: Sales growth: 27-28% (from 24-26%) reflecting strong Q3 and improved data center/power utility visibility. Organic growth: 8-10% (from 5-7%) with FX providing 1pt tailwind. Adjusted EPS: $3.22-3.30 range (+29-33% YoY vs previous $3.03-3.13). Management confidence in execution and market positioning supports raising guidance mid-year.​

Overall Assessment: nVent Electric plc is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within industrials 6-7% infrastructure beneficiary allocation. nVent represents solid investment-grade credit (BBB-/Ba1/BBB-) with exceptional growth momentum, strong FCF generation, and strategic positioning in high-growth infrastructure markets. Strengths include: (a) Moody's Ba1/Positive outlook signaling upgrade potential to investment grade; (b) Q3 2025 record sales (+35% reported, +16% organic) demonstrating growth acceleration; (c) 27%+ adjusted operating margins reflecting operational excellence; (d) free cash flow exceptional ($253M Q3 +77%) providing deleveraging capacity; (e) data center and power utility verticals capturing secular infrastructure trends; (f) FY2025 guidance raised to 27-28% sales growth reflecting confidence; (g) successful acquisition integration (Trachte, EPG) demonstrating playbook execution; (h) strong backlog providing revenue visibility. Risks include: (a) Moody's Ba1 below investment grade (two notches below S&P/Fitch) pending upgrade; (b) tariff exposure ($90M estimated despite mitigation efforts); (c) rapid M&A-driven growth creating integration risks; (d) data center concentration if capex cycle moderates; (e) valuation metrics at premium levels reflecting growth; (f) supply chain execution risk scaling production. RECOMMENDED: Overweight infrastructure growth leader with record growth, exceptional FCF, and upgrade potential from Moody's Ba1/Positive outlook.

Monitor: (1) Moody's upgrade (Ba1 to Baa3 achievement on continued growth/margin delivery); (2) Organic growth sustainability (8-10% guidance maintenance amid infrastructure cycle); (3) Data center demand (Capital intensity and new product adoption); (4) Tariff costs ($90M mitigation and supply chain adjustments); (5) M&A integration (Trachte and EPG contribution to EBITDA/margins).

issuers.name: NVR INC
issuers.creditComment:
NVR INC
Credit Rating: A3/Stable (Moody's Upgraded March 2025) | A-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Aug 2025) | BBB+ (S&P as of 2024 baseline) – Premium Homebuilder; Conservative Model; Fortress Balance Sheet
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgraded to "A" Tier) – NVR Inc [finance:NVR Inc] has achieved strong credit upgrades. Moody's upgraded the senior unsecured rating to 'A3' (from Baa1) on March 10, 2025, with a Stable outlook. Fitch affirmed the 'A-' rating with a Stable outlook on August 11, 2025. The upgrades reflect NVR's distinctive "land-light" business model, strong execution, conservative financial policies, and fortress balance sheet management (net cash $1.7 billion as of December 2024).
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) unique optioned land/pre-sold construction model that dramatically reduces inventory risk and impairment exposure vs. industry peers; (b) position as the fourth-largest U.S. homebuilder by volume with strong geographic diversification (36 markets across 16 states, focused on higher-growth Mid-Atlantic region); (c) above-average gross margins (~24%+); (d) net cash position supporting flexibility; (e) proven resilience across housing cycles. Risks include industry cyclicality, regional concentration (Mid-Atlantic exposure), and expected aggressive share buyback activity, though Moody's views these as within acceptable parameters for the A3 rating.
​

Financial Profile: NVR maintains exceptional financial strength. The company carries minimal debt relative to large-cap peers and maintains substantial liquidity. Moody's expects NVR to continue conservative operations with low-risk business execution and prudent financial policies. The company generates strong free cash flow and disciplines capital allocation (historically reinvested in buybacks during strong cycles). Leverage is expected to remain low (debt-to-book capitalization <30%).
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Homebuilders 4-5% allocation. Strongest "A" tier homebuilder credit. Monitor: (1) Gross margin trends and order growth rates; (2) Housing starts and new home sales; (3) Buyback pacing and capital return discipline; (4) Mortgage rate trajectory.

issuers.name: NXP SEMICONDUCTORS NV
issuers.creditComment:
NXP SEMICONDUCTORS N.V. (NXP BV / NXP FUNDING LLC / NXP USA INC.)
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Nov 2024) | BBB+/F2/Stable (Fitch Aug 2025) | Baa3/Stable (Moody's 2025) – Global Automotive & Industrial Semiconductor Leader; $3.17B Q3 2025 Revenue; 57% Gross Margin; 1.8x Net Leverage; 17.4x Interest Coverage; Cyclical Recovery Underway

Credit Momentum: Stable – NXP Semiconductors 
 maintains solid investment-grade ratings (BBB+/Baa3) with Stable outlooks across all three agencies. Fitch affirmed BBB+/F2 with Stable outlook (August 2025), S&P affirmed BBB+ (November 2024), and Moody's maintains Baa3/Stable. Q3 2025 showed early signs of cyclical recovery with sequential improvement across all regions and end markets, though revenue declined 2% YoY. The company operates through co-issuers NXP B.V., NXP Funding LLC, and NXP USA Inc., all rated equally:​

Credit Ratings & Capital Structure:
NXP maintains BBB+/Stable from S&P and Fitch, Baa3/Stable from Moody's. Capital structure (2Q25): Total long/short-term debt $11.48B; Cash and equivalents $3.17B; Net debt $8.31B. Leverage metrics: Reported gross leverage 2.4x; Net leverage 1.8x; Interest coverage 17.4x (EBITDA/net interest). Debt cost: Weighted average cost of debt 3.98%, demonstrating efficient capital structure. Maturity profile: Well-spread maturities from 2026 through 2051 providing financial flexibility.​

Q3 2025 Operating Performance:
NXP reported resilient Q3 2025 results (October 27, 2025): Revenue: $3.17B, down 2% YoY but exceeding guidance midpoint; Up 8% sequentially. Segment performance: Automotive $1.8B (flat YoY); Industrial & IoT $579M (+3%); Mobile $430M (+6%); Communications Infrastructure $327M (-27%). Non-GAAP gross margin: 57.0% (vs 58.2% YoY) demonstrating pricing discipline. Non-GAAP operating margin: 33.8% (vs 35.5% YoY); Non-GAAP EPS $3.11 (down 10% YoY). Free cash flow: $509M non-GAAP FCF; Capital returns $310M (60.9% of FCF).​

Cyclical Recovery & Q4 Outlook:
NXP positioned for continued recovery: Q4 2025 guidance: Revenue $3.3B (+6% YoY, +4% sequential), indicating sustained recovery trajectory. Segment outlook: Auto up low-single digit; Industrial & IoT up ~10%; Mobile up mid-single digits; Comm & Infra flat. Channel normalization: Distribution weeks at 9, selectively staged to 10; Tier 1 auto inventory near consumption levels. Strategic initiatives: Investing in 300mm joint ventures (VSMC Singapore, ESMC Germany) targeting ~200bp gross margin uplift by 2028. Competitive positioning: NXP noted as gaining share vs. Intel in auto/industrial applications amid Intel's challenges.​

Overall Assessment: NXP Semiconductors is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within semiconductors 6-7% technology allocation. NXP represents solid investment-grade credit (BBB+/Baa3) with automotive/industrial semiconductor leadership, strong balance sheet, and early cyclical recovery signs. Strengths include: (a) BBB+/Baa3 ratings with Stable outlooks; (b) $3.17B Q3 revenue with sequential improvement across all segments; (c) conservative 1.8x net leverage with 17.4x interest coverage; (d) 57% gross margin demonstrating pricing power; (e) Q4 guidance showing +6% YoY growth trajectory; (f) strategic manufacturing investments targeting margin expansion; (g) strong FCF generation ($509M Q3); (h) automotive semiconductor leadership in growing EV/ADAS markets. Risks include: (a) automotive end-market cyclicality; (b) China revenue exposure and geopolitical risks; (c) competition from Texas Instruments, Infineon, STMicroelectronics; (d) margin pressure in industrial downcycle; (e) tariff impacts on semiconductor supply chains. RECOMMENDED: Overweight automotive/industrial semiconductor leader with conservative leverage and recovery positioning.

Monitor: (1) Revenue recovery (Q4 $3.3B guidance and beyond); (2) Automotive cycle (EV/ADAS content growth vs. unit volumes); (3) Gross margin (57%+ sustainability and 2028 uplift target); (4) Leverage maintenance (1.8x net below 2.5x threshold); (5) China exposure (Geopolitical and regulatory developments).

issuers.name: OIL&NATURAL GAS CORP (ONGC)
issuers.creditComment:
OIL & NATURAL GAS CORP (ONGC)
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Upgraded August 2025) | Baa3/Stable (Moody's) | BBB-/Stable (Fitch) – India's National Oil Company; Upgraded by S&P Following Sovereign Action; Strong Financial Profile; Domestic Production Dominance
​
Credit Momentum: Positive – ONGC received a rating upgrade from S&P to 'BBB' from 'BBB-' in August 2025, following S&P's upgrade of India's sovereign rating. The outlook is stable. Moody's and Fitch maintain Baa3 and BBB- ratings, respectively.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are underpinned by: (a) status as India's largest crude oil and natural gas producer (~70% domestic production); (b) strong government linkage (58.9% stake); (c) integrated operations through subsidiary HPCL; (d) solid financial metrics (FFO-to-debt >40%). S&P noted that ONGC's standalone credit profile (SACP) is 'bbb+'.
​
Financial Profile: Despite volatility in oil prices, ONGC maintains a robust balance sheet. The company's unutilized NCD program ratings were withdrawn at its request, but the active program retains AAA domestic ratings. International bonds are rated BBB/Baa3. ONGC Videsh (overseas arm) faces some geopolitical headwinds but remains a key contributor.
​
Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within India/Asia Oil & Gas 5-6% allocation. Premier energy credit (BBB/Baa3) with sovereign support and strong standalone fundamentals. Monitor: (1) Crude oil price realization (windfall taxes); (2) Production volume trajectory; (3) Capex on new fields (KG-98/2); (4) Sovereign rating ceiling.

issuers.name: OOREDOO INTL FINANCE
issuers.creditComment:
OOREDOO INTERNATIONAL FINANCE LTD
Credit Rating: A/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Feb 2025 - then Withdrawn) | A2/Stable (Moody's) | A/Stable (S&P) – Financing Vehicle for Ooredoo QPSC; Qatar's Telecom Champion; Strong Government Support
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Ooredoo International Finance Limited is the financing vehicle for Ooredoo Q.P.S.C. Ratings are aligned with the parent: A2 (Moody's), A (S&P), and A (Fitch). Fitch affirmed the rating at 'A' with a stable outlook in February 2025, though it subsequently withdrew the rating for commercial reasons. S&P and Moody's maintain stable outlooks, with S&P recently upgrading the standalone credit profile (SACP) to 'bbb' from 'bbb-'.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) dominant market position in Qatar (Aa2/AA); (b) diverse international footprint (Indonesia, Iraq, Kuwait, Algeria); (c) majority ownership (69%) by Qatar Investment Authority (QIA); (d) high likelihood of government support. The strong link to the Qatari state provides a multi-notch uplift to the rating.
​

Financial Profile: Ooredoo maintains a healthy financial profile with reported Net Debt/EBITDA of ~2.0x-2.4x. The company generates solid free cash flow, covering dividends and capex. Recent tower sales have provided liquidity for deleveraging. Liquidity is excellent, with cash balances covering near-term maturities.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within MENA Telecoms 5-6% allocation. Core GRE credit (A/A2) with sovereign support. Monitor: (1) Competition in Qatar and key international markets; (2) FX volatility in emerging markets (e.g., Iraq, Algeria); (3) Capex for 5G rollout; (4) Sovereign support stance.

issuers.name: ORANGE SA
issuers.creditComment:
ORANGE SA
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P Affirmed Dec 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's Outlook Revised May 2025) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch Affirmed Dec 2025) – Leading European Telco; Resilient Cash Flow; Strong African Growth
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Orange SA 
 maintains high investment-grade ratings. S&P and Fitch affirmed 'BBB+' with stable outlooks in December 2025. Moody's affirmed the Baa1 rating in May 2025 and revised the outlook to Stable from Positive, noting solid operating performance but limited near-term deleveraging capacity due to the MasOrange JV structure.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) incumbent position in France with premium network quality; (b) strong growth engine in Africa & Middle East (MEA); (c) disciplined M&A strategy (MasOrange JV in Spain); (d) solid free cash flow generation. The French government holds a 23% stake, providing a one-notch rating uplift from Moody's.
​

Financial Profile: Orange targets Net Debt/EBITDAaL of ~2.0x in the medium term. Actual leverage was ~2.5x (Moody's adjusted) as of year-end 2024. The company generates robust organic cash flow (~€3.3 billion target for 2025), supporting dividends and capex. Liquidity is excellent with €8.4 billion in cash.
​
