issuers.name: THAIOIL
issuers.creditComment:
THAIOIL TRSRY CENTER 
Credit Rating: BBB-/Negative (S&P June 2025 Downgrade) | Baa3/Negative (Moody's October 2025 Affirmed) | A+(tha)/Negative (Fitch May 2025 Affirmed National Scale) – Thailand Integrated Oil Refiner & Petrochemical Producer; Q3 2025 Net Profit THB 2.147bn Turnaround; EBITDA THB 3.897bn; Major Turnaround Recovery Q4 2025 Guidance

Credit Momentum: Negative with Stabilizing Operational Trends – Thai Oil faces elevated credit risk from above-investment-grade leverage and Clean Fuel Project (CFP) capex requirements, though recent debt reduction efforts and asset monetization initiatives provide stabilizing support:

Credit Ratings Under Pressure – Negative Outlooks:
Thai Oil operates under negative outlook ratings: S&P BBB-/Negative (June 2025 downgrade from BBB), Moody's Baa3/Negative (October 2025 affirmation), and Fitch A+(tha)/Negative (May 2025 national scale). The downgrade reflects S&P's concerns regarding weaker group profitability, elevated leverage above rating agency tolerance levels, and significant capital expenditure requirements for the Clean Fuel Project (CFP: US$1.538 billion through 2030). The consistent negative outlooks signal heightened credit stress and potential further downgrade risk if leverage and profitability metrics do not materially improve within 12–18 months.

Q3 2025 Operational Turnaround & EBITDA Recovery:
Thai Oil reported a sharp Q3 2025 net profit turnaround of THB 2.147 billion versus a Q3 2024 loss of THB 4.218 billion, driven by: (a) Major Turnaround refinery maintenance completion supporting gradual throughput recovery; (b) Higher Dubai crude pricing (Iran-Israel conflict supply tightness + Russia sanctions supporting upside); (c) Bond repurchase gains of THB 1.372 billion from US$633 million debenture redemption; (d) Joint venture profits from PT Chandra Asri Petrochemical (PT CAP) of THB 7.044 billion (acquisition in Singapore). Q3 EBITDA surged to THB 3.897 billion (+THB 2.619 billion QoQ). 9M 2025 net profit reached THB 12.126 billion (+68.6% vs 9M 2024), with EPS of 5.43 baht (+68.6% YoY).

Refinement Margin Pressures & Capacity Constraints:
Gross Refining Margin (GRM) declined to 3.5 US$/bbl in Q3 2025 (vs 5.2 US$/bbl Q2 2025), reflecting narrowing gasoline-fuel oil spreads and major turnaround impacts. Refinery utilization dropped to 82% in Q3 (from 113% Q2), as the scheduled Crude Distillation Unit 3 maintenance (July–August) reduced crude throughput. Accounting GRM stood at 4.1 US$/bbl (vs 5.8 US$/bbl in Q2). However, gross profit including oil stockpile gains improved to 7.4 US$/bbl (from 2.6 US$/bbl in Q2), benefiting from higher crude oil prices. Q4 2025 guidance suggests steady recovery as refinery operations reach maximum capacity, though commodity price volatility and product spread compression remain key tail risks.

Debt Reduction & Asset Monetization Strategy:
Thai Oil has aggressively reduced debt through: (a) Bond repurchases of THB 4.067 billion (9M 2025), with US$633 million debentures redeemed; (b) Asset Monetization initiatives via Lease & Leaseback structures for oil storage tanks, Single Buoy Mooring (SBM) facilities, and truck loading terminals—providing liquidity and reducing commodity exposure; (c) Joint venture optimization (PT CAP Singapore acquisition generating THB 7.044 billion profit). These measures have strengthened the balance sheet and provided cash flow cushion amid CFP capex demands. However, absolute debt levels remain elevated, and further deleveraging is required to achieve investment-grade leverage ratios and positive outlooks.

Clean Fuel Project (CFP) Capex & Long-Term Growth Investment:
Thai Oil has approved US$1.736 billion in capex for 2025–2030, with US$1.538 billion earmarked for CFP completion (hydrocracking, aromatic recovery units). CFP remains a strategic priority to enhance product quality compliance (meeting sulfur standards, cleaner fuels regulations) and unlock higher-margin revenue streams. Additional projects total over US$198 million. CFP's capital-intensive nature continues to constrain near-term leverage improvement but positions Thai Oil for structural margin improvement and regulatory compliance. Management guided Q4 2025 recovery with steady throughput, with FY 2025 expectations for operational normalization post-turnaround.

Overall Assessment: Thai Oil represents a MARKET WEIGHT to SLIGHT UNDERWEIGHT recommendation within emerging markets energy/refining sectors. The company faces material credit stress (BBB-/Baa3 with negative outlooks) from elevated leverage, significant capex obligations (CFP), and commodity margin pressures. However, recent operational improvements (Q3 turnaround net profit, EBITDA recovery), aggressive debt reduction (bond repurchases, asset monetization), and stable refining margins provide near-term stabilization. Key risks include: (a) crude price volatility impacting refining spreads; (b) CFP capex delays or cost overruns; (c) further credit downgrades if leverage rises above 3.0x; (d) macroeconomic slowdown reducing fuel demand. RECOMMENDED ALLOCATION: 1-2% within emerging markets energy exposure 2-3% speculative/high-yield category (credit stress near-term refinement margin recovery near-term tactical opportunity, downgrade risk medium-term limiting exposure).

Monitor: (1) Leverage ratio trajectory (debt reduction US$633m bond redemptions 9M 2025 sustaining below 3.0x target achieving); (2) Q4 2025 refinery recovery (maximum capacity throughput guidance achieving margin stabilization); (3) CFP execution (US$1.538bn capex 2025–2030 timeline progress capex discipline demonstrating); (4) Crude price/spread environment (Dubai crude pricing GRM 3.5–5.0 US$/bbl range sustaining commodity margin volatility managing); (5) Rating agency action risk (Negative outlooks downgrade trigger if leverage above 3.0x debt/EBITDA 12–18 months monitoring required).

issuers.name: THOMSON REUTERS CORP
issuers.creditComment:
THOMSON REUTERS CORP
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P May 2024 Upgrade) | Baa1/Positive (Moody's November 2025 Outlook Revised to Positive) | A-/Stable (Fitch September 2025 Upgrade) – Global Information & Technology Leader; Q3 FY2025 Revenue $1.78B +3% YoY (Organic +7%); Adjusted EBITDA Margin 37.7%; "Big 3" Segments 82% of Revenue at 9% Organic Growth; FY2025 Guidance Reaffirmed at 7.0-7.5% Organic Revenue Growth, ~39% EBITDA Margin​

Credit Momentum: Positive Trajectory – Thomson Reuters 
 maintains strong investment-grade ratings with recent positive rating actions reflecting consistent operating performance, margin improvements, and conservative leverage. S&P upgraded to BBB+ in May 2024 on strong operating performance, while Fitch upgraded to A- in September 2025, and Moody's revised outlook to positive in November 2025 affirming Baa1 ratings:​

Credit Ratings & Stability:
Thomson Reuters maintains BBB+/Stable ratings from S&P (upgraded May 2024) and equivalent Baa1 from Moody's (outlook revised positive November 2025), with Fitch upgrading to A- in September 2025. S&P's BBB+ reflects the company's well-recognized brand, leading market positions in Legal Professionals, Corporates, and Tax & Accounting Professionals segments (especially in the U.S.), high revenue visibility (~80% recurring revenue), client retention rates of 91%, and high switching costs for client-integrated solutions. The stable outlook incorporates expectations of maintaining sustainable organic revenue growth with debt-to-EBITDA below 3x. S&P noted the company's adjusted EBITDA margins improved to ~35% at year-end 2023 from ~20% in 2018 following the spinoff of its Financial & Risk business. Moody's positive outlook revision reflects potential for upgrade if strong execution continues while maintaining conservative leverage.​

Q3 FY2025 Solid Performance & AI-Driven Growth:
Q3 FY2025 (ended September 30, 2025) revenues reached $1.78 billion (+3% YoY), with organic revenue growth of 7% driven by 9% growth in recurring revenues. Operating profit surged 43% to $593 million, with adjusted EBITDA increasing 10% to $672 million. The "Big 3" segments (Legal Professionals, Corporates, and Tax & Accounting Professionals) delivered 9% organic revenue growth and comprised 82% of total revenues. Adjusted EPS reached $0.85, up 6% YoY. Free cash flow totaled $526 million in Q3, with approximately $1.4 billion generated in the first nine months of 2025. The company completed a $1.0 billion share repurchase program announced in August 2025, demonstrating confidence in cash generation and capital allocation discipline.​

FY2025 Guidance & 2026 Framework:
Management reaffirmed FY2025 guidance for: organic revenue growth of 7.0-7.5% (including ~9% for "Big 3" segments); adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 39%; and free cash flow of $1.8-1.9 billion. The updated 2026 financial framework raises expectations for adjusted EBITDA margin expansion (50+ bps) and free cash flow ($2.0-2.1 billion), with organic revenue growth targeted at 7.5-8.0% driven by approximately 9.5% growth in "Big 3" segments. Full-year 2024 results demonstrated consistent execution with revenue of $7.3 billion (+7% organic), adjusted EBITDA of $2.78 billion (38.2% margin), adjusted EPS of $3.77, and free cash flow of $1.83 billion.​

AI Innovation Strategy & CoCounsel Platform:
Thomson Reuters is executing an AI-driven innovation strategy with CoCounsel Legal (featuring Deep Research on Westlaw) and CoCounsel for Tax, Audit and Accounting representing professional-grade agentic AI solutions. Approximately 15% of total contract value (~$6 billion annual underlying contract value) now derives from generative AI. The company has invested over $200 million in AI initiatives in 2024 (up from ~$100 million in 2023) and announced new agentic AI solutions including ONESOURCE+ (intelligent compliance network), expanded CoCounsel capabilities, and bulk legal document review processing up to 10,000 documents. Management maintains $10 billion earmarked for potential acquisitions through 2027, with recent purchases including SafeSend ($600 million, January 2025), Safe Sign Technologies, and Materia to accelerate AI capabilities.​

Balance Sheet Strength & Conservative Leverage:
Thomson Reuters maintains exceptional financial flexibility with conservative leverage. Total debt stood at approximately C$2.9 billion as of mid-2025, down from C$4.4 billion at year-end 2024, following the repayment of C$1.4 billion notes (US$1.0 billion) with cash on hand in May 2025. S&P Global Ratings-adjusted debt-to-EBITDA remains in the low- to mid-1x area, well below the 3x downgrade threshold and supporting the stable outlook. The company's strong balance sheet enables continued shareholder returns (10% annual dividend increase marking 32nd consecutive year), strategic acquisitions, and $1.0 billion share repurchases while maintaining investment-grade credit metrics.​

Overall Assessment: Thomson Reuters is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within global information services/technology 8-10% defensive quality allocation. The company represents a high-quality investment-grade credit (BBB+/Baa1/A-) with improving credit momentum, AI-driven revenue acceleration, and conservative financial policy. Strengths include: (a) dominant market positions in legal, tax, and accounting professional segments with 91% client retention; (b) high recurring revenue model (~80%) providing cash flow predictability; (c) AI leadership with CoCounsel platform driving ~15% of contract value; (d) conservative leverage (debt/EBITDA low-1x) with significant headroom below 3x threshold; (e) consistent margin expansion from ~20% (2018) to ~38-39% (2024-2025); (f) strong free cash flow conversion supporting acquisitions and shareholder returns. Risks include: (a) AI competition from emerging legal tech providers; (b) potential for large debt-financed acquisitions (management target leverage of 2.5x S&P-adjusted); (c) Global Print revenue decline (~4-7% annually); (d) geographic concentration primarily in U.S. markets; (e) economic sensitivity in corporate segment. RECOMMENDED: Overweight quality global information services leader with AI-driven growth, conservative leverage, and investment-grade stability.

Monitor: (1) FY2025 guidance delivery (7.0-7.5% organic revenue, ~39% EBITDA margin); (2) AI revenue contribution trajectory (currently ~15% of contract value); (3) "Big 3" segment performance (targeting 9% organic growth); (4) Leverage maintenance (low-1x debt/EBITDA ensuring rating stability); (5) M&A activity and integration execution.

issuers.name: THREE GORGES FINANCE
issuers.creditComment:
CHINA THREE GORGES 
Credit Rating: A+/Stable (S&P April 2025 Upgrade SACP revised 'a') | Aa3/Stable (Moody's Implied) | A+/Stable (Fitch) – World's Largest Hydropower Producer; 78 GW Installed Capacity; 52 GW Wind & Solar; Q1-Q2 2025 Stable Operations; FFO/Debt 13%; Government Support Very High

Credit Momentum: Positive – China Three Gorges upgraded by S&P to A+ in April 2025, with SACP upgraded to 'a' (from 'bbb+'), reflecting improved operational efficiency, enhanced market competitiveness in clean energy, and stable hydropower cash flows supporting energy transition:

Credit Upgrade & Stand-Alone Profile Improvement:
S&P Global Ratings upgraded China Three Gorges (CTG) to A+/Stable on April 14, 2025 (from A/Stable), with SACP upgraded to 'a' from 'bbb+'. The upgrade reflects CTG's strengthened market position in clean energy development, improved operating efficiency from six-dam joint dispatching coordination, and essential role in China's energy transition and decarbonization. CTG maintains an extremely high likelihood of receiving extraordinary government support (100% state-owned, central government entity). The stable outlook reflects expectation of sustained competitive positioning and stable cash flow generation while discipline is maintained in debt growth. Moody's and Fitch maintain equivalent ratings (Aa3 and A+ respectively).

Hydropower & Renewable Energy Portfolio:
CTG operates 78 GW of installed hydropower capacity (world's largest by installed capacity and power generation), with large-hydro generation contributing over 60% of EBITDA through long-term power purchase agreements at relatively stable prices. In addition, CTG holds approximately 52 GW of onshore wind and solar capacity and operates over 7 GW of offshore wind farms (largest in China). The diversified clean energy portfolio—anchored by stable hydropower baseload—provides resilient, predictable cash flows while offering growth through renewable energy expansion aligned with China's net-zero 2060 commitments.

Financial Metrics & Cash Flow Stability:
Funds from operations (FFO) cash interest coverage is expected to maintain 5.0x–5.5x over 2024–2026 forecast period, reflecting strong earnings relative to interest obligations. FFO-to-debt ratio is forecast around 13% over the same horizon, supported by cash flow from large hydro and new renewable projects. While material deleveraging is not expected over the next 2–3 years (given elevated capex requirements for renewable energy infrastructure development), the consistent FFO coverage ratios demonstrate financial resilience and adequate debt servicing capacity. China's low interest rate environment and government preference for renewable energy reduce CTG's financing costs and facilitate refinancing.

Capital Expenditure & Asset Development:
CTG maintains elevated capex over 2025–2027 (primarily for large-scale renewable energy generation asset development and environmental protection initiatives). While average returns on renewable projects are lower than for mature large hydro plants, the capex is strategically aligned with government priorities and long-term energy transition requirements. The company benefits from preferential financing terms via state-owned banks and is expected to maintain financial discipline in debt growth despite high capex, supported by stable hydropower cash flows.

Government Support & Energy Transition Role:
CTG plays an essential, irreplaceable role to the central government: (a) flood control on the Yangtze River; (b) provision of navigation services on China's most important waterway; (c) acceleration of energy transition through large-scale hydropower and renewable energy generation. The company's environmental contribution is material—78 GW hydropower capacity accounts for ~20% of China's hydropower generation and contributes significantly to decarbonization. S&P's rating is directly linked to the Chinese sovereign rating (A+/Stable), with the assumption of very high likelihood of extraordinary support. Downgrade risk is primarily contingent on deterioration in China's sovereign rating.

Overall Assessment: China Three Gorges is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 5-6% within APAC utilities/energy infrastructure 8-10% ESG/green energy allocation. CTG represents an exceptional credit quality entity (A+/Stable) with government backing, stable hydropower cash flows, and strategic positioning in China's energy transition. The April 2025 upgrade and SACP improvement validate operational execution. Key strengths include: (a) world-largest hydropower platform with essential national role; (b) diversified renewable energy portfolio (52 GW wind/solar, 7 GW offshore wind); (c) extremely high government support likelihood; (d) stable FCF from contracted power sales; (e) favorable financing terms and low interest rate environment. Risks are limited but include: (a) Chinese sovereign rating downgrade (rating moves in tandem); (b) hydrological/geological changes reducing hydropower output (low probability); (c) regulatory changes affecting long-term power contract stability. RECOMMENDED: Overweight high-quality Chinese infrastructure/energy leader with government support and essential societal role.

Monitor: (1) Capex discipline (Debt growth restraint renewable energy spending proportionate to plan); (2) FFO metrics (13% FFO/debt 5.0-5.5x coverage sustaining forecast); (3) Hydropower utilization (Stable output from 78GW portfolio basin conditions adequate); (4) China sovereign rating (A+/Stable maintained CTG rating trajectory aligned); (5) Renewable asset returns (Wind/solar projects new additions average returns improving profitability trajectory).

issuers.name: TIERRA MOJADA LUX II SRL
issuers.creditComment:
TIEMOD 5.75% 01/12/2040 – Tierra Mojada Lux II, BBB‑ (project finance, Mexico)
Профиль бизнеса

SPV‑владелец CCGT Tierra Mojada (874 МВт) в штате Халиско, с долгосрочными контрактами поставки мощности/энергии для CFE и индустриальных потребителей.
​

Кэш‑флоу преимущественно контрактован, exposure к спот‑рынку ограничен.

Кредитные метрики

Типичный PF‑профиль: высокий леверидж, DSCR в районе 1,3–1,4x при базовом сценарии, амортизационный долг.

Пост‑строительный риск largely behind; остаётся операционный и регуляторный.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды

Оценка вокруг BBB‑/BB+ со стабильной перспективой; лимитировано страновым риском Мексики и контрактным риском CFE.

Выпуск 2040 (YTM ~5,9%, dur 5,9, OAS ~180–250 bps) даёт ощутимую премию к CFE и другим IG‑мексиканцам.

Ключевые риски

Возможные изменения в политике Мексики к частным IPP и риски пересмотра/оспаривания контрактов.

Длинная дюрация и единичный актив.

Инвестидия

Интересный high‑beta IG‑PF с хорошей доходностью; разумно использовать как приправу к суверенному/квазисуверенному Мексике.

Стоит внимательно следить за политикой в энергетике и новостями по CFE/IPP.

issuers.name: T-MOBILE USA INC
issuers.creditComment:
T-MOBILE USA INC 
Credit Rating: Baa1/Stable (Moody's September 2025 Upgrade) | BBB/Stable (S&P August 2022 Affirmed) | BBB-/Positive (Fitch April 2024) – US Wireless Leader; Q3 2025 Record Postpaid Net Adds 2.3m; Service Revenue +9% YoY; 5G Spectrum Leadership; Postpaid ARPA +2.7% YoY

Credit Momentum: Positive – T-Mobile upgraded to Baa1/Stable by Moody's in September 2025, reflecting strong operational execution, industry-leading postpaid customer growth, superior free cash flow generation, and expanding profitability supporting investment-grade convergence:

Credit Rating Upgrade & Multi-Agency Convergence:
Moody's upgraded T-Mobile's senior unsecured ratings to Baa1/Stable in September 2025 (from Baa2/Positive), recognizing consistent execution, rising profitability, and expanding free cash flow. This upgrade, combined with S&P's BBB/Stable (affirmed 2022) and Fitch's BBB-/Positive (2024), now constitutes T-Mobile's "first-ever full investment-grade rating" across all three major agencies. Baa1/BBB/BBB- convergence reflects institutional confidence in T-Mobile's Un-Carrier strategy, market share gains in a competitive wireless market, and superior cash generation relative to peers AT&T and Verizon. Moody's positive outlook change reflects stabilization following the 2024 upgrades surge.

Q3 2025 Record Customer Growth & Market Leadership:
T-Mobile reported unprecedented Q3 2025 postpaid customer additions of 2.3 million (industry-leading best-ever total), including 1.0 million postpaid phone net adds (highest Q3 in over a decade, best in industry). Postpaid net account additions reached 396,000 (+26% YoY, best-ever), while 5G broadband added 506,000 net customers. Prepaid saw 43,000 net adds. Total postpaid churn improved to 0.89%, and prepaid churn declined to 2.77% (down 1 basis point). Postpaid ARPA rose to $149.44 (from $145.60 YoY), up +2.7% demonstrating pricing power. Full-year 2025 guidance raised for both customers and financial metrics reflecting confidence.

Service Revenue & Profitability Growth:
Total service revenues reached $18.2 billion in Q3 2025 (+9% YoY, best in industry), with postpaid service revenues at $14.9 billion (+12% YoY, best in industry). Net income reached $2.7 billion (diluted EPS $2.41), and adjusted EPS of $2.59 exceeded estimates. The company's consistent execution translates into durable and profitable revenue growth, with strong ARPA growth and industry-leading churn rates indicating customer stickiness and competitive advantage. Operating leverage from scale and 5G network efficiency drives margin expansion.

Free Cash Flow & Financial Capacity:
Moody's projects T-Mobile's free cash flow (after dividends) will exceed $13 billion by end-2025, approximately 1.5x higher than AT&T and Verizon projections. T-Mobile's FCF-to-debt ratio is expected around 11% (double major competitors), highlighting superior cash generation abilities. Retained cash flow-to-net debt is projected ~25% (vs ~20% for AT&T/Verizon). Management maintains a 2.5x net debt-to-EBITDA target (Moody's-adjusted 3.0x net debt-to-EBITDA, 3.2x total debt-to-EBITDA), which translates to approximately $19 billion in additional financial capacity for capital allocation by year-end 2027. This capacity exists even after ~$50 billion in planned shareholder returns and ~$10 billion in committed M&A capex through 2027.

Strategic Positioning & Capital Allocation:
T-Mobile's valuable spectrum holdings (600 MHz, 1.9 GHz, 2.5 GHz bands), extensive 5G coverage (nationwide deployment), and demonstrated market share gains in a saturated market provide competitive moats. The company raised 2025 guidance for total postpaid net adds to 7.2–7.4 million (from 6.1–6.4 million), including postpaid phone adds of 3.3 million (from 2.95–3.10 million prior guidance). Fiber net customer guidance raised to 130,000 (from 100,000). Liquidity is strong: $10.3 billion cash, full $7.5 billion revolving credit facility availability (expires Oct 2027), and projected >$13 billion free cash flow post-dividends 2025. The company balances shareholder returns (dividends, buybacks), M&A, and debt reduction.

Overall Assessment: T-Mobile is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within US telecom/telecommunications 6-8% dividend/infrastructure allocation. The company represents a compelling investment-grade story with industry-leading operational momentum, record customer growth, superior cash flow generation, and consistent execution supporting the September 2025 Moody's upgrade to Baa1/Stable. Baa1/BBB/BBB- ratings are well-supported by: (a) market share gains in saturated wireless market; (b) pricing power (ARPA +2.7% YoY); (c) superior FCF generation vs AT&T/Verizon; (d) strong 5G spectrum/coverage; (e) disciplined leverage management (2.5x target). Key risks include: (a) intensified wireless competition/pricing pressure; (b) macro sensitivity to consumer discretionary spending; (c) spectrum auction participation capex; (d) integration risks from M&A (UScellular pending). RECOMMENDED: Overweight market leader with proven Un-Carrier execution, superior growth trajectory, and investment-grade credit quality with upside rating path.

Monitor: (1) 2025 postpaid guidance (7.2–7.4m adds 3.3m phone adds achieving raising consensus trajectory); (2) Postpaid ARPA stability ($149+ sustained pricing power validating competitive position); (3) Free cash flow delivery (>$13bn after-dividend 2025E generating capacity demonstrating leverage reducing); (4) Leverage target maintenance (2.5x net debt/EBITDA disciplined capital allocation balancing returns M&A); (5) Rating upgrade potential (Baa1/BBB to A3/BBB+ operating momentum sustained FCF delivery accelerating upgrade pathway opening).

issuers.name: TOTALENERGIES CAPITAL SA
issuers.creditComment:
TOTALENERGIES CAPITAL SA 
Credit Rating: A+/Stable (S&P July 2025 Affirmed) | Aa3/Stable (Moody's December 2024) | AA-/Stable (Fitch August 2025 Affirmed before Withdrawal) – European Oil & Gas Supermajor Energy Transition Leader; Q3 2025 Production 2.5 Mboe/d +4% YoY; Free Cash Flow +7% QoQ; Integrated Power +9% Net Generation

Credit Momentum: Stable – TotalEnergies Capital benefits from parent TotalEnergies' investment-grade ratings, strong operational performance, disciplined capital allocation, and strategic energy transition positioning as a bridge between legacy and renewable energy systems:

Credit Ratings & Financing Vehicle Structure:
TotalEnergies Capital S.A. (and TotalEnergies Capital International) serves as the primary financing subsidiaries for TotalEnergies SE, inheriting the parent's A+/Stable (S&P), Aa3/Stable (Moody's), and formerly AA-/Stable (Fitch prior to withdrawal August 2025) ratings. The credit support agreement structure with parent TotalEnergies ensures full linkage to parent credit quality. Bonds issued by TotalEnergies Capital are rated at the parent issuer level (A+/Aa3), reflecting the unconditional nature of the parent's financial support and strategic importance of these financing vehicles in TotalEnergies' capital structure.

Q3 2025 Operational Performance & Production Growth:
TotalEnergies reported Q3 2025 total hydrocarbon production of 2.5 million barrels of oil equivalent per day (Mboe/d), exceeding guidance with +4% year-over-year growth despite planned Ichthys LNG turnaround in Australia. Upstream production benefited from "accretive new barrels" with average upstream cash flow per barrel roughly 2x higher than the base portfolio, demonstrating superior project economics. Production growth exceeded prior guidance of 3% annually through 2030, with more than 95% of 2030 production already online or under construction, significantly derisking future cash flow trajectories and reducing commodity price sensitivity.

Cash Flow Resilience & Capital Discipline:
Despite refining margin compression (European margins fell 65% QoQ to $15.4/t from $44.9/t Q2, reflecting seasonal softness), TotalEnergies reported +7% quarterly cash flow increase with adjusted net income up +11%, demonstrating operational leverage across integrated business model. Free cash flow is expected to increase substantially, with management targeting $10 billion of additional free cash generation from all segments over the next five years. Net investments decreased $3.5 billion QoQ, reflecting capital discipline, while disposals ($2 billion expected Q4 2025) and working capital releases ($1.3 billion Q3) continue to strengthen balance sheet. Payout ratio expected to remain stable around 56% FY 2025.

Energy Transition & Integrated Power Growth:
Integrated Power segment generated 12.6 TWh in Q3 2025 (+9% QoQ), driven by flexible European generation capacity deployment. TotalEnergies is strategically repositioning toward 35 GW renewable generation targets by 2030 (from 14.8 GW currently), with €160 million battery storage investments and renewable portfolio sales (50% of 1.4 GW North America and 270 MW France portfolios) structured as capital recycling generating ~$1.5 billion cash while retaining 50% operating stakes. This business model evolution—balancing traditional E&P with low-carbon integrated power—positions TotalEnergies as a transition energy provider aligned with regulatory decarbonization pathways.

Strategic Project Pipeline & Financial Capacity:
TotalEnergies sanctioned Phase Two Ratawi redevelopment (Iraq GGIP projects) and achieved first oil at Begonia and closed Phase Three Ekofisk (Norway) fields Q3 2025, demonstrating project execution excellence. Net investment guidance maintained at €17–17.5 billion FY 2025, with disciplined sanctioning criteria (less than $20/bbl technical cost, $30/bbl breakeven) ensuring high-margin, low-cost production additions. The company maintains financial flexibility with strong cash generation supporting €2 billion disposal proceeds anticipated Q4 2025 and net acquisition cash flows ($1.5 billion) offsetting capex, enabling continued shareholder returns (buybacks, ADR conversions) while maintaining investment-grade leverage.

Overall Assessment: TotalEnergies Capital is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within European energy/oil & gas 6-8% transition/ESG allocation. The company represents a compelling investment-grade credit (A+/Aa3) with exceptional strategic positioning as a "bridge fuel" provider navigating energy transition. Strengths include: (a) world-class, low-cost production (+3% to 4% annual growth 2025–2030); (b) disciplined capital framework ensuring high-margin project economics; (c) expanding integrated power/renewable platforms reducing energy volatility; (d) strong FCF generation ($10 billion incremental 5-year target) supporting dividends, buybacks; (e) geographic and product diversification (upstream, downstream, power, renewables). Risks include: (a) commodity price downturns reducing cash flow (partially mitigated by low breakeven costs); (b) macroeconomic slowdown reducing refining/power demand; (c) regulatory/climate risks limiting future fossil fuel development. RECOMMENDED: Overweight quality energy transition leader with investment-grade stability and superior long-term cash generation.

Monitor: (1) Free cash flow trajectory ($10bn incremental 5-year delivery executing); (2) Integrated Power expansion (35 GW renewables 2030 target capital recycling sustaining); (3) Production growth delivery (3–4% annually through 2030 on-budget execution); (4) Leverage maintenance (Net investment discipline capital discipline investment-grade metrics); (5) Energy transition pace (Renewable/low-carbon mix expanding profitability sustaining).

issuers.name: TOYOTA INDUSTRIES CORP
issuers.creditComment:
TOYOTA INDUSTRIES CORP
Credit Rating: A2/Review for Upgrade (Moody's June 2025) | A/Watch Positive (S&P June 2025) – Key Toyota Group Company; Largest Forklift Maker; Proposed Delisting by Toyota Motor
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Potential Upgrade) – In June 2025, both Moody's and S&P placed Toyota Industries Corporation (TICO) on review for upgrade/CreditWatch positive following Toyota Motor's announcement of a tender offer to potentially delist the company or strengthen capital ties. This reflects the potential for stronger integration with the higher-rated parent.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Currently rated A2 (Moody's) and A (S&P). Ratings reflect: (a) global leadership in material handling equipment (Toyota Material Handling); (b) core supplier role for Toyota Motor (compressors, engines, electronics); (c) strong balance sheet with net cash; (d) vast investment portfolio (Toyota Motor shares). Strategic alignment with Toyota Motor is a key rating anchor.
​

Corporate Action: Toyota Motor's plan to acquire TICO shares (announced June 2025) aims to deepen collaboration in electrification and software. A successful transaction would likely lead to rating equalization or closer alignment with Toyota Motor (A1/A+).
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 2-3% within Japan Industrials 4-5% allocation. Strong credit (A2/A) with upside potential from parent integration. Monitor: (1) Outcome of Toyota Motor's tender offer; (2) Material handling market cycle; (3) Shareholder structure changes.

issuers.name: TOYOTA MOTOR CORP
issuers.creditComment:
TOYOTA MOTOR CORP
Credit Rating: A1/Stable (Moody's Outlook Revised June 2025) | A+/Stable (S&P) | AAA (JCR/R&I) – World's Largest Automaker; Fortress Balance Sheet; Strong Hybrid/EV Portfolio
​

Credit Momentum: Stable – Moody's revised Toyota Motor's outlook to Stable (from Positive) in June 2025, while affirming the A1 rating. The outlook revision reflects macroeconomic risks (US tariffs) and cash usage for the Toyota Industries tender offer. S&P maintains an A+ rating.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are underpinned by: (a) global market leadership and diversification; (b) industry-leading margins driven by hybrid dominance; (c) massive liquidity (~JPY 16.7 trillion); (d) extremely conservative financial policy. Domestic agencies rate Toyota AAA.
​

Financial Strength: Toyota maintains a net cash position in its automotive segment. Despite investments in electrification and the TICO tender offer, liquidity remains exceptional. Profitability is robust, supported by strong sales of high-margin hybrids in the US and Europe.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within Global Autos 6-8% allocation. Premier global industrial credit (A1/A+) offering safety and stability. Monitor: (1) US trade policy/tariffs; (2) EV transition speed vs. Hybrid demand; (3) FX volatility (JPY); (4) Supply chain stability.

issuers.name: TRANSCONT GAS PIPELINE
issuers.creditComment:
TRANSCONTINENTAL GAS PIPELINE
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (S&P March 2025 Upgrade) | Baa1/Positive (Moody's April 2025 Outlook Revised to Positive) | BBB+/Stable (Fitch January 2025 Affirmed) – Premier U.S. Interstate Natural Gas Pipeline; Q3 2025 Segment Modified EBITDA Record Highs; Transco Pipeline Serving Eastern Seaboard & Gulf Coast; Power Express Project Targeting Data Center Demand; Parent Williams 
 EBITDA +13% YoY​

Credit Momentum: Positive with Upgrade Potential – Transcontinental Gas Pipe Line Company, LLC (Transco), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Williams Companies, maintains strong investment-grade ratings with recent positive rating actions reflecting growth execution and strong credit metrics:​

Credit Ratings & Stability:
Transco maintains BBB+/Stable ratings from S&P (upgraded March 2025 from BBB), Baa1/Positive outlook from Moody's (outlook revised positive April 2025), and BBB+/Stable from Fitch (affirmed January 2025). S&P's March 2025 upgrade reflects parent Williams maintaining strong credit metrics while expanding size and scale both organically and through acquisitions. S&P Global Ratings-adjusted debt-to-EBITDA of approximately 4.0x in 2025 is expected to improve further in 2026. Moody's Baa1 rating (one notch above parent Williams' Baa2) reflects the regulated nature of Transco's operations, supply diversity, and growth potential. Moody's noted potential upgrade to Baa1 for Williams if consolidated adjusted debt/EBITDA remains below 3.75x while maintaining dividend coverage greater than 1.5x. Transco's pipelines do not guarantee Williams' debts, providing structural subordination protection.​

Q3 2025 Outstanding Financial Performance:
Parent Williams reported Q3 2025 adjusted EBITDA of $1.92 billion, up 13% YoY, with nine-month adjusted EBITDA of $5.72 billion, up 8%. The Transmission & Gulf of America segment (primarily Transco) achieved record Modified EBITDA driven by expansion projects coming online and higher revenues. Williams' Q3 2025 net income attributable to common stockholders rose 8% YoY, with adjusted EPS of $0.49, up 14% YoY. Cash flow from operations increased to $4.32 billion for nine months 2025, up from $3.76 billion in prior year. Dividend coverage ratio of 2.37x demonstrates strong cash generation relative to shareholder distributions.​

FY2025 Guidance & Growth Investment:
Williams reaffirmed FY2025 guidance with adjusted EBITDA midpoint of $7.75 billion, representing 9% growth over 2024 and a 9% five-year CAGR. Adjusted diluted EPS guidance of $2.01-$2.19, up 9% YoY at midpoint. Growth capital expenditures raised to $3.95-$4.25 billion for 2025 (up $875 million) to support power innovation projects. Leverage guidance maintained at approximately 3.7x debt-to-EBITDA, demonstrating disciplined capital allocation despite accelerated growth investment. Williams achieved 9% growth in adjusted EBITDA for full-year 2024 to approximately $7.0 billion.​

Transco Infrastructure Expansion & Data Center Strategy:
Transco operates the nation's largest natural gas pipeline network, extending 10,000+ miles from the Gulf Coast to New York City, serving approximately one-third of U.S. natural gas demand. Major expansion projects include:​

Power Express Project (950 MMcf/d, Q3 2030 in-service): Expansion targeting Virginia data center market, the world's largest, with returns expected similar to Southeast Supply Enhancement project (sub-4.0x build multiple)​

Southeast Supply Enhancement (1.6 Bcf/d, proposed 2027): Major expansion providing reliable natural gas deliveries to Virginia, Carolinas, Georgia, and Alabama​

Regional Energy Access: FERC reinstated authorization January 2025 for 829,400 dekatherms/day expansion serving New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, and Maryland​

Northeast Supply Enhancement ($1B, 400 Mcf/d): Approved permits clearing path for completion​

Williams announced $5.1 billion total commitment to "Power Innovation" portfolio, including $3.1 billion in two new gas-fired power projects announced October 2025 to support AI data centers and industrial demand. Projects backed by 10-year, primarily fixed-price power purchase agreements with completion targeted first half 2027.​

Balance Sheet & Capital Structure:
Williams ended 2024 with S&P Global Ratings-adjusted EBITDA of approximately $7.0 billion and leverage of about 4.2x. Leverage expected to remain near 4.0x at end of 2025 despite elevated growth spending, with improvement expected in 2026 as projects come online. Williams maintains excellent liquidity with full availability under committed revolving credit facility expiring October 2027. The company raised its annual dividend to $2.00 per share (9 consecutive years of increases), with dividend coverage ratio of approximately 2.4x demonstrating sustainable payout. Fitch expects 2026 leverage of around 3.6x, lower than previous forecast and below the 4.0x level commensurate with a strong 'BBB' rating.​

Overall Assessment: Transcontinental Gas Pipeline is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 4-5% within U.S. midstream/infrastructure 8-10% defensive allocation. The company represents a high-quality investment-grade credit (BBB+/Baa1) with positive rating momentum and exceptional strategic positioning for data center-driven demand growth. Strengths include: (a) premier U.S. natural gas pipeline serving critical Eastern Seaboard and Gulf Coast markets; (b) predominantly fee-based/take-or-pay contracts providing revenue stability; (c) strategic positioning for data center/AI demand with Power Express and Power Innovation projects; (d) strong parent Williams credit metrics improving toward 3.75x upgrade threshold; (e) structural subordination protection (Transco not guaranteeing parent debt); (f) demonstrated expansion execution with multiple projects coming online. Risks include: (a) regulatory/permitting delays for large expansion projects; (b) elevated near-term capital spending pressure on leverage; (c) interest rate sensitivity; (d) energy transition risks to long-term natural gas demand; (e) exposure to volume risk in gathering and processing. RECOMMENDED: Overweight premier U.S. gas infrastructure with data center demand catalyst, improving credit metrics, and regulated cash flow stability.

Monitor: (1) Williams leverage trajectory toward 3.75x Moody's upgrade threshold; (2) Power Express project development and permitting progress; (3) Southeast Supply Enhancement FERC approval (expected year-end 2025); (4) Data center power demand materialization; (5) FY2025 EBITDA delivery ($7.75B midpoint guidance).

issuers.name: TRANSELEC SA
issuers.creditComment:
TSELEC 3.875 01/12/2029 – Transelec 4.5%, BBB
Профиль бизнеса
Крупнейший частный оператор систем передачи в Чили, с монопольным положением на большинстве высоковольтных линий и полностью регулируемой, availability‑based выручкой.
Бизнес‑риск один из самых низких в Латинской Америке: regulated asset base, предсказуемые тарифы, долгосрочные концессии.

Кредитные метрики
Левередж умеренный, FFO/debt и DSCR в комфортных для BBB диапазонах; capex высокий, но компенсируется регуляторной доходностью на вложенный капитал.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды
Рейтинг BBB со стабильным outlook, ограниченный суверенным потолком Чили.
​Облигация 2029: YTM ~4,5%, dur 2,7, OAS ~90–110 bps – quasi‑sovereign‑pricing.

Ключевые риски
Потенциальная политизация тарифов (давление на снижение доходности капитала в сетях).
Регуляторные изменения по структуре оплаты услуг передачи.

Инвестидия
Очень качественное IG‑имя для короткой части кривой; можно использовать как низкорисковый якорь против более рискованных LatAm utilities.
Минус – ограниченный carry: спред уже близок к минимуму для Чили.

issuers.name: TRANSMANTARO
issuers.creditComment:
TRANSM 5.2% 11/04/2038 – Transmantaro (Peru), BBB‑
Профиль бизнеса

Крупный перуанский оператор передачных линий (часть группы ISA/Interconexion Electrica), работающий по availability‑based концессиям.

Бизнес очень похож на Atlántica/Interchile: фиксированная, индексируемая тарифная выручка, независимая от объёмов.

Кредитные метрики

Левередж высокий в абсолюте, но устойчивый: DSCR >1,4x, capex невелик, FCF стабилен.

Связь с ISA помогает ликвидности и governance.

Рейтинг, рынок и спрэды

Рейтинг BBB‑ со стабильным outlook, ограниченный суверенным рейтингом Перу.

Облигация 2038 (YTM ~5,3%, dur 7,8, OAS ~100–150 bps) торгуется как классический перуанский transmission IG.

Ключевые риски

Суверенный/политический риск Перу и регуляторные изменения в электроэнергетике.

Концентрация на одной стране и на transmission‑регулировании.

Инвестидия

Хороший “чистый” infrastructure‑IG с невысоким кредитным риском и умеренным спредом – логичен как core‑позиция в перуанском bucket вместе с ATL/Kallpa/Niagara.

Основной лимит – общий страновой risk‑budget на Перу.

issuers.name: TRANSPORT DE GAS PERU
issuers.creditComment:
TRANSPORT DE GAS PERU
Transportadora de Gas del Perú (TGP), operator of the Camisea pipelines, maintains ratings of Baa1 (Moody’s) and BBB+ (S&P/Fitch), though Fitch has a Negative outlook mirroring the sovereign. TGP benefits from an exceptionally strong competitive position (transporting 95%+ of Peru’s gas) and stable ship-or-pay contracts, but is exposed to Peruvian political and regulatory risks.​

issuers.name: TRANSPRTDRA DE GAS INTL
issuers.creditComment:
TRANSPRTDRA DE GAS INTL
Transportadora de Gas Internacional S.A. ESP (TGI), a subsidiary of Grupo Energía Bogotá, is rated BBB by Fitch (Negative outlook) and Baa3 by Moody’s (Stable). As Colombia’s largest gas transporter, TGI has strong regulated cash flows, but faces re-contracting risks as reserves decline and regulatory uncertainty in Colombia’s energy sector persists. The Negative outlook aligns with Colombia’s sovereign outlook.​

issuers.name: TSMC GLOBAL LTD
issuers.creditComment:
TSMC GLOBAL LTD
Credit Rating: Aa3/Stable (Moody's, linked to parent TSMC August 2025) | AA-/Stable (S&P, linked to parent Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited September 2025)
Key Role: Bond-issuing vehicle of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited 
 (TSMC), the world's largest contract semiconductor foundry; registered in British Virgin Islands; global multi-currency debt program ($3+ billion outstanding).

Credit Momentum: Exceptional with Upside Potential – TSMC Global Ltd's credit ratings are fully linked to parent Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited 's superb standalone financial strength, with recent rating stability affirmations amid record earnings growth driven by artificial intelligence (AI) chip demand acceleration.​

Credit Ratings & Stability:
TSMC Global Ltd maintains Aa3/Stable ratings from Moody's (affirmed August 2025) and AA-/Stable from S&P (affirmed September 2025, global scale). The bond-issuing vehicle is fully, unconditionally, and irrevocably guaranteed by parent TSMC, with guarantees ranking senior unsecured and pari passu with other senior unsecured parent obligations. Ratings reflect TSMC's: (a) world-leading 54% foundry market share; (b) technology leadership in advanced process nodes (3nm/2nm production commenced 2023-2024); (c) exceptional profitability (50%+ operating margins); (d) net cash position (NT$2.5+ trillion or US$80+ billion); (e) dominant positioning in AI/HPC chip supply chain; (f) fortress balance sheet with minimal financial leverage.​

Q3 2025 Exceptional Financial Results & AI Tailwinds:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited reported record Q3 2025 results with: (a) consolidated revenue of NT$989.92 billion (US$33.1 billion), up 10.1% sequentially and 30.3% YoY, driven by 74% of wafer revenue from advanced technologies (3nm, 5nm, 7nm); (b) net income of NT$452.30 billion (US$14.78 billion), up 13.6% QoQ and 39.1% YoY, reflecting operational leverage and pricing power; (c) gross margin of 59.5% (up 90 bps sequentially); (d) operating margin of 50.6% (up 100 bps sequentially); (e) net profit margin of 45.7%; (f) return on equity (ROE) of 37.8%.​

Advanced process technologies accounted for 74% of wafer revenue, with AI, smartphone, IoT, automotive, and platform customers driving demand. Nine-month 2025 net profit reached NT$1.21 trillion (+51.8% YoY), with EPS of NT$46.75.​

FY2025 Full-Year Guidance & Capital Allocation:
Management upgraded FY2025 revenue guidance to expect growth of almost 35% in USD terms (from prior 30% estimate), driven by continued strong AI demand. Q4 2025 guidance: revenue between US$32.2-33.4 billion (1% sequential decrease but 22% YoY growth at midpoint); gross margin 59-61%; operating margin 49-51%. Full-year 2025 capex raised to US$40-42 billion (from US$38-42 billion prior), with 70% allocated to advanced process development (3nm and beyond), 10-20% to specialty processes, and 10% to assembly/testing/other. Q3 capex reached US$9.7 billion, representing 51.5% increase YoY, demonstrating aggressive capacity expansion to support AI infrastructure demand.​

Strategic Expansion & Geopolitical Positioning:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited is executing a global capacity expansion strategy: (a) Taiwan fabs: continued investment in advanced nodes; (b) Arizona (USA): US$20+ billion committed to 3nm and advanced-node fabs (on schedule, under-budget); (c) Japan: Kumamoto fab ramping production (reducing overseas margin dilution from 2-3% to 1-2% in 2025 due to scale efficiencies); (d) Europe (Germany): foundry fab in development stage. Gross margin dilution from overseas fabs is now expected at 1-2% for 2025 (better than prior 2-3% forecast) and 2-3% in early ramp stages, improving over time as scale increases.​

Balance Sheet & Liquidity Excellence:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited maintains an exceptional financial position: (a) cash and marketable securities of NT$2.8 trillion (US$90 billion) at Q3 2025, up NT$106 billion sequentially; (b) total debt of approximately NT$1.05 trillion (US$35 billion, primarily long-term notes issued by TSMC Global Ltd); (c) net cash position of approximately NT$1.75 trillion (US$55 billion); (d) debt-to-equity ratio of ~0.20; (e) interest coverage of 125x+ (based on operating income vs. interest expense); (f) free cash flow of NT$139.38 billion (US$4.7 billion) in Q3 2025 alone.​

Operating cash flow in Q3 totaled NT$427 billion, while capex consumed NT$287 billion. Nine-month 2025 capex reached US$29.39 billion (up 58.6% YoY). Dividend payout remains conservative at ~30% of earnings (dividend yield 1.57%), supporting both shareholder returns and reinvestment in R&D/capex cycles.​

TSMC Global Ltd Bond Program & Funding Strategy:
TSMC Global Ltd has issued multi-tranche USD and TWD notes maturing 2025-2051, including: (a) 0.75% notes due 2025 (US$1.0B); (b) 1.00% notes due 2027 (US$750M); (c) 1.375% notes due 2030 (US$1.25B); (d) 2.25% notes due 2031 (US$1.0B); (e) 4.625% notes due 2032 (US$600M); (f) additional tranches in JPY and other currencies. All notes are fully guaranteed by parent TSMC. The funding vehicle efficiently manages: (a) foreign currency exposure (USD revenues matched to USD debt); (b) refinancing risk through staggered maturity profile; (c) market access across multiple investor bases (institutional, retail, geographic diversification).​

Risks & Sensitivities:

Geopolitical: U.S.-China tensions, Taiwan Strait volatility, or restrictive export controls on advanced nodes could impact return-on-investment for overseas fabs or customer demand.

Capex Execution: Overseas fab investments (Arizona, Japan, Germany) are exposed to cost inflation, regulatory delays, or macroeconomic headwinds that could depress near-term return metrics (though not solvency).

Cyclicality: Semiconductor demand is inherently cyclical; AI momentum may moderate as inventory normalization occurs, but long-term secular AI/data center trends support sustained capex.

Competition: Samsung foundry expansion and emerging Chinese competitors (SMIC, Huawei HiSilicon) pose long-term competitive pressure, though TSMC's technology lead (3nm/2nm) remains substantial.

Currency Risk: NTD appreciation vs. USD would reduce reported revenues/earnings (mitigated through USD debt and hedging).

Overall Assessment: TSMC Global Ltd is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 5-6% within global semiconductor/AI infrastructure 10-12% growth allocation. The company represents a world-class investment-grade credit (Aa3/AA-) with exceptional operational momentum, strategic positioning in AI/HPC supply chains, and fortress financial metrics. Strengths include: (a) 54% foundry market share with 3nm/2nm technology leadership; (b) AI-driven demand acceleration (9% sequential revenue growth Q3); (c) record profitability (50.6% operating margin, 45.7% net margin); (d) fortress balance sheet (net cash NT$1.75T, debt/equity 0.20); (e) globally scaled capex execution (Arizona, Japan on-schedule/under-budget); (f) conservative dividend policy (30% payout) supporting reinvestment; (g) multiple growth vectors (AI chips, smartphone recovery, automotive/IoT); (h) rating upgrade potential if leverage declines further or overseas fab ramp accelerates cost structure improvements. Risks include: (a) geopolitical/export control escalation impacting capex ROI; (b) semiconductor cyclicality and inventory normalization; (c) capex intensity ($40-42B 2025) creating near-term margin headwinds from overseas fabs; (d) competitive pressure from Samsung/Chinese fabs. RECOMMENDED: Overweight world-leading foundry with AI demand tailwinds, exceptional balance sheet, and fortress credit metrics.

Monitor: (1) FY2025 revenue delivery (35% growth guidance); (2) Gross margin trajectory (overseas fab dilution 1-2% vs. 2-3%); (3) Capex execution and Arizona/Japan ramp cost structure; (4) AI chip demand sustainability and customer concentration; (5) Geopolitical developments and export control impacts; (6) Dividend policy and share buyback activity.

issuers.name: TYCO ELECTRONICS GROUP
issuers.creditComment:
TYCO ELECTRONICS GROUP (TE Connectivity Ltd.)
Credit Rating: A3/Stable (Moody's Affirmed January 2025) | A-/Stable (Fitch Affirmed February 2025) | Implied A-/Stable (S&P equivalent based on comparable metrics)
Key Role: Tyco Electronics Group S.A. (Luxembourg-registered holding company and debt-issuing subsidiary); principal financing vehicle for TE Connectivity Ltd. 
, the global leader in engineered connectors, sensors, and components with market-leading positions in Industrial and Transportation segments.

Credit Momentum: Stable with Upside Potential – Tyco Electronics maintains solid investment-grade ratings with stable outlook reflecting TE Connectivity's exceptional operational execution, record financial results, strong cash generation, and conservative leverage profile. The business model's resilience is demonstrated through 6% organic sales growth and 20% adjusted operating margin in FY2025 amid uneven macro conditions.​

Credit Ratings & Stability:
Tyco Electronics Group maintains A3/Stable ratings from Moody's (affirmed January 2025) and A-/Stable from Fitch (affirmed February 2025). Both ratings reflect: (a) TE Connectivity's leading 40%+ market share in engineered electrical connectors and advanced sensor solutions; (b) diversified end-market exposure across automotive, industrial, IoT, data center, and aerospace sectors; (c) strong profitability (50%+ gross margin historically); (d) exceptional cash generation ($3.2 billion free cash flow in FY2025); (e) conservative leverage (net debt/EBITDA ~1.3x); (f) geographic and customer diversification across 50+ countries. Fitch noted potential upgrade pathway if leverage declines further while maintaining organic growth, with 2.0x downgrade sensitivity threshold. Moody's characterizes Tyco as having leading market positions, significant engineering value-add, and strong ability to weather cyclical downturns through working capital release.​

FY2025 Record Financial Performance & Earnings Acceleration:
TE Connectivity reported record FY2025 results for fiscal year ended September 26, 2025, demonstrating strong operational momentum: (a) net sales of $17.3 billion, up 9% reported and 6% organically YoY, driven primarily by Industrial Segment growth; (b) GAAP operating margin of 19% and adjusted operating margin of 20% (up 80 bps YoY), each a company record; (c) GAAP diluted EPS from continuing operations of $6.16 and adjusted EPS of $8.76 (up 16% YoY, exceeding guidance); (d) free cash flow of $3.2 billion with greater than 100% conversion, representing a record.​

Q4 FY2025 highlights included: (a) net sales of $4.75 billion (up 17% reported, 11% organic YoY), exceeding guidance; (b) GAAP operating margin of 19% and adjusted operating margin of 20% (up 130 bps YoY); (c) adjusted EPS of $2.44 (up 25% YoY); (d) cash flow from operations of $1.4 billion; (e) free cash flow of $1.2 billion with nearly $650 million returned to shareholders. The Industrial Segment delivered exceptional performance with 24% reported growth (18% organic), driven by bolt-on acquisitions and 80% Digital Data Networks growth (AI revenue tripling to $900 million YoY).​

Strategic Growth & AI/Data Center Positioning:
TE Connectivity is leveraging its technology leadership to capture secular growth trends: (a) Digital Data Networks: AI revenue surged to $900 million in FY2025 (triple YoY), supported by connector and thermal management solutions for data center buildout; (b) Automation & Connected Living: 15% organic growth, benefiting from industrial electrification and IoT adoption; (c) Aerospace, Defense & Marine: 9% growth driven by elevated military spending and commercial aviation recovery; (d) Energy: 83% YoY growth, supporting grid modernization and renewable infrastructure; (e) Automotive: 4% YoY growth with strong Asian content gains, supported by EV adoption and ADAS electronics expansion.​

FY2025 orders increased to $17.0 billion (up from $15.6 billion in FY2024), with book-to-bill of 0.99x providing 12+ months of revenue visibility. Orders in Digital Data Networks surged significantly, underpinning management's confidence in sustained double-digit growth trajectory through FY2026.​

FY2026 Guidance & Capital Allocation:
Management provided strong FY2026 outlook: (a) Q1 FY2026 sales guidance of ~$4.5 billion (17% reported, 11% organic YoY); (b) Q1 adjusted EPS of ~$2.53 (23% YoY growth); (c) full-year organic sales growth expected in double digits (supported by Industrial segment momentum and normalized Transportation segment); (d) gross margin expected to expand 100+ bps in Q1 FY2026. Capital allocation remains balanced: $2.2 billion returned to shareholders in FY2025 (dividends + buybacks) and $2.6 billion deployed for bolt-on acquisitions, while maintaining investment-grade leverage metrics.​

Balance Sheet & Liquidity Excellence:
TE Connectivity maintains a strong and flexible capital structure: (a) total debt of approximately $6.1 billion (June 2025), consisting primarily of long-term notes issued by Tyco Electronics Group; (b) cash and marketable securities of $672 million; (c) net debt of approximately $5.4 billion; (d) net debt/EBITDA ratio of ~1.3x (well below Fitch's 2.0x downgrade threshold); (e) adjusted EBITDA of approximately $4.1 billion (TTM September 2025); (f) interest coverage exceeding 20x on EBITDA basis.​

Operating cash flow for FY2025 totaled $4.1 billion, with capital expenditures of approximately $900 million, resulting in free cash flow of $3.2 billion. Management targets sustained free cash flow conversion above 100%, enabling simultaneous debt reduction, shareholder returns, and strategic acquisitions.​

Risk & Monitoring Considerations:

Leverage Threshold: Fitch's 2.0x downgrade sensitivity implies limited headroom; debt-funded acquisitions exceeding $2-3 billion could pressure ratings.

Cyclicality: Industrial connector demand is inherently cyclical; macro slowdown or inventory normalization could pressurize margins and FCF.

Capex Intensity: Overseas fabs and manufacturing expansion may require elevated capex, moderating FCF conversion.

End-Market Concentration: Automotive represents ~30-35% of sales; significant EV transition risks or OEM consolidation could impact volumes.

Overall Assessment: Tyco Electronics Group is recommended OVERWEIGHT – 5-6% within global industrial/connectors/electronics 10-12% core allocation. The company represents a high-quality investment-grade credit (A3/A-) with exceptional operational momentum, strategic positioning in AI/data center infrastructure, and fortress balance sheet. Strengths include: (a) 40%+ market share in engineered connectors with high switching costs; (b) record FY2025 sales, margins, and cash flow; (c) AI revenue tripling to $900 million with $900M+ organic AI opportunity ahead; (d) 6% organic growth and 80 bps margin expansion YoY; (e) conservative leverage (1.3x net debt/EBITDA); (f) strong free cash flow generation ($3.2B FY2025, >100% conversion); (g) balanced capital allocation (shareholder returns + strategic acquisitions); (h) upgrade potential if debt declines below 1.0x while maintaining organic growth. Risks include: (a) cyclical connector demand and macro slowdown; (b) leverage approaching 2.0x threshold from debt-funded acquisitions; (c) automotive segment concentration and EV disruption; (d) inventory normalization in distribution channels. RECOMMENDED: Overweight industrial technology leader with secular AI/data center tailwinds, record profitability, fortress balance sheet, and investment-grade stability.

Monitor: (1) FY2026 guidance delivery (organic growth, margin expansion); (2) AI revenue sustainability (target: $2B+ by FY2027); (3) Net debt/EBITDA trajectory (target: <1.0x long-term); (4) M&A deployment pace and debt impact; (5) Macro environment and order velocity; (6) Gross margin trajectory (100+ bps expansion potential).

issuers.name: UPM-KYMMENE OYJ
issuers.creditComment:
UPM-KYMMENE OYJ
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Downgraded Oct 2025) | Baa1/Negative (Moody's Outlook Revised Nov 2025) – Finnish Forest Products Leader; Cyclical Downturn; Elevated Leverage
​

Credit Momentum: Negative (Downgrades) – UPM-Kymmene Oyj 
 has faced significant headwinds. S&P downgraded the company from 'BBB+' to 'BBB' in October 2025, citing continued soft pulp and paper markets. Moody's affirmed the Baa1 rating in November 2025 but revised the outlook to Negative from Stable, reflecting persistently elevated leverage. Both agencies cite market conditions well below historical norms.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are constrained by: (a) significant exposure to the structurally declining graphic paper business (~25% of sales); (b) commodity-like pricing for key products; (c) gross leverage (Moody's adjusted) remaining above 3.0x in Q3 2025; (d) negative free cash flow in recent quarters due to high dividend distributions. The negative outlooks suggest downgrade risk if market conditions persist.
​

Financial Profile: UPM's leverage has breached its 2.0x net leverage target, reaching 2.4x by Q3 2025. Moody's adjusted gross leverage exceeds 3.0x. The company has maintained high dividends (€820m in 9M 2025) despite weak earnings, consuming roughly two-thirds of operating cash flow. Liquidity is adequate (~€2bn in committed facilities plus €300m bilateral RCFs).
​

Overall Assessment: UNDERWEIGHT – 1-2% within Global Paper/Forest Products 3-4% allocation. Cyclical pressure credit (BBB/Baa1 Negative). High downgrade risk. Monitor: (1) Recovery in pulp and paper demand (especially China); (2) Dividend policy sustainability; (3) Market prices for key products; (4) Cost-saving actions.

issuers.name: VALE OVERSEAS LIMITED
issuers.creditComment:
VALE OVERSEAS LIMITED
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (Fitch Upgraded Oct 2025) | BBB/Positive (S&P Upgraded Sept 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's Affirmed Nov 2025) – Financing Vehicle for Vale SA; Global Iron Ore Leader; Strong Deleveraging; Upgraded by Fitch & S&P
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgrades) – Vale Overseas Limited is the financing subsidiary of Vale S.A., with debt fully guaranteed by the parent. In late 2025, Vale received significant positive rating actions. Fitch upgraded Vale to 'BBB+' (from BBB) in October 2025 with a stable outlook. S&P upgraded Vale to 'BBB' (from BBB-) in September 2025 with a positive outlook. Moody's affirmed the Baa2 rating in November 2025 with a stable outlook.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) global leadership in iron ore and nickel; (b) low-cost operations; (c) reduced litigation risk following the Mariana settlement; (d) robust balance sheet with leverage <1.0x. The upgrades acknowledge Vale's improved ESG governance and financial discipline despite commodity price volatility.
​

Financial Profile: Vale maintains a very strong financial profile. Gross Debt is managed around $10-12 billion target. Net Debt/EBITDA is consistently low (0.3x-0.8x range). The company generates strong free cash flow, supporting dividends and share buybacks. The recently issued hybrid notes were rated 'BBB-' by Fitch and 'BB+' by S&P.
​

Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Global Metals & Mining 5-6% allocation. Top-tier mining credit (BBB+/Baa2) with improving trajectory. Monitor: (1) China iron ore demand; (2) Iron ore and nickel prices; (3) Execution of "Value over Volume" strategy; (4) Tailings dam safety/ESG compliance.

issuers.name: VALERO ENERGY CORP
issuers.creditComment:
VALERO ENERGY CORP
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Affirmed May 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's Affirmed May 2025) – Largest US Independent Refiner; Strong FCF; Disciplined Capital Allocation
​
Credit Momentum: Stable – Valero Energy Corp. maintains a solid investment-grade profile. S&P affirmed the 'BBB' Foreign Currency LT credit rating with a Stable outlook on May 27, 2025. Moody's affirmed the 'Baa2' LT Local Currency rating with a Stable outlook on May 29, 2025. The stable outlooks reflect Valero's operational excellence, refining crack spreads, and strong free cash flow generation. Q2 2025 results showed net income of $714 million and strong shareholder returns ($695 million through dividends and buybacks).
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) dominant position as the largest independent US refiner (capacity ~3.2 million barrels/day); (b) diversified asset base and geographic footprint; (c) ability to navigate commodity cycles via stable operations; (d) strong refining margins (crack spreads); (e) disciplined capital allocation. Constraints include commodity refining margin volatility and energy transition risks (long-term oil demand).
​
Financial Profile: Valero demonstrates fortress financial discipline. The company repaid $251 million of maturing Senior Notes in April 2025 while maintaining a regular quarterly dividend ($1.13/share in Q2 2025). Strong operating cash flow supports both debt management and shareholder returns. Liquidity is robust with access to capital markets and cash flow generation. Leverage is moderate and well-managed through refining cycle fluctuations.
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within US Refining 5-6% allocation. Core IG refiner (BBB/Baa2) with fortress financials. Monitor: (1) Crude and refined product spreads; (2) Global refining utilization rates; (3) Energy transition/demand risks; (4) Maintenance capex and growth capex discipline.

​

Credit Momentum: Stable with Upside Potential – Veolia Environnement S.A. 
 maintains solid investment-grade ratings. S&P affirmed the 'BBB/A-2' rating in November 2025 with a stable outlook, noting steady EBITDA growth and disciplined capital deployment. Moody's affirmed Baa1 in April 2025 with a stable outlook. Both agencies view the company as well-positioned despite debt-funded acquisitions.
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings are supported by: (a) position as the world's largest environmental services company; (b) ~40% of EBITDA from low-risk, long-term municipal water contracts with inflation indexation; (c) diversified waste and energy services; (d) strong market positions globally. Veolia is expected to maintain "comfortable credit headroom" per S&P.
​

Financial Profile: Veolia maintains FFO/Net Debt around 21%, well above the 18% downgrade threshold. The company announced the "Clean Earth" acquisition (€2.6bn) for 2026, which will increase debt to ~€27.1bn by 2027. S&P forecasts Debt/EBITDA will stay around 4.0x, maintaining rating headroom. Disposals of ~€2bn over 2025-2027 offset acquisition debt.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 3-4% within Global Waste/Environmental Services 5-6% allocation. Resilient quasi-utility credit (BBB/Baa1 Stable) with scale and diversification. Monitor: (1) Integration of Clean Earth acquisition; (2) European waste volumes recovery; (3) Municipal contract renewal rates; (4) Emerging markets exposure.

issuers.name: VERALTO CORP
issuers.creditComment:
VERALTO CORP
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Issue Rating Sept 2023) | Ba1/Stable (Moody's, estimated) – Measurement & Monitoring Equipment Spinoff from Fortive; Niche Markets; Growth Profile
​
Credit Momentum: Stable – Veralto Corp. is an industrial measurement and monitoring technology provider spun from Fortive. S&P assigned a 'BBB' issue-level rating to proposed senior unsecured notes in September 2023. The company provides advanced instruments, software, and services to industrial customers (pharmaceutical, semiconductor, water/wastewater, environmental monitoring). Limited recent 2025 formal rating updates in search results, but the company maintains stable credit fundamentals typical of diversified industrial equipment providers.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) niche market positions with recurring revenue streams; (b) strong customer relationships in regulated industries; (c) software/SaaS subscription growth; (d) solid operating margins. Typical constraints for industrial equipment companies include cyclical end-market exposure, technology risk, and customer concentration in some segments.
​
Financial Profile: Veralto maintains moderate leverage and solid liquidity as a recently spun entity (2024). The company invests in R&D to maintain competitive positioning in niche markets. Free cash flow generation supports debt service and reinvestment. Growth trajectory reflects underlying industrial equipment demand trends and market share gains.
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Specialty Industrials 4-5% allocation. Solid IG credit (BBB/Ba1) with niche positioning. Monitor: (1) End-market cyclicality (pharma, semiconductor, environmental); (2) Software/SaaS attach rates; (3) M&A strategy post-spin; (4) Integration synergies with Fortive post-separation.

issuers.name: VULCAN MATERIALS CO
issuers.creditComment:
VULCAN MATERIALS CO
Credit Rating: BBB+/Stable (Fitch Upgraded Aug 2025) | Baa1/Stable (Moody's, estimated) | BBB-/Stable (S&P, estimated) – Leading Construction Aggregates Supplier; Pricing Power; Margin Expansion
​
Credit Momentum: Positive (Upgraded by Fitch) – Vulcan Materials Co. is benefiting from infrastructure spending and pricing power. Fitch upgraded the Long-term IDR to 'BBB+' on August 28, 2025, with a Stable outlook, and upgraded the Short-Term IDR for commercial paper from F2 to F1. The company's leverage ratios have improved significantly, declining from 2.6x to 2.2x post-acquisition, with projections suggesting a 2.0x-2.5x range. EBITDA margins improved 200bps in 2024, and Fitch expects an additional 100-150bps improvement in 2025 driven by pricing and operational efficiency.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) market leadership in construction aggregates across 23 states with an extensive quarry network; (b) strong pricing power and disciplined capital allocation; (c) infrastructure spending tailwinds (IIJA); (d) operating efficiency improvements; (e) strategic acquisitions expanding competitive moat. The construction aggregates market has favorable long-term demand drivers from infrastructure and housing cycles.
​
Financial Profile: Vulcan has successfully integrated recent acquisitions and improved operational leverage. Net debt has been rationalized post-M&A. The company maintains strong liquidity through cash generation and credit facilities. Free cash flow supports debt reduction and disciplined shareholder returns. Management targets continued EBITDA margin expansion and leverage optimization through the cycle.
​
Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within US Building Materials 5-6% allocation. Upgraded IG credit (BBB+/Baa1) with pricing power. Monitor: (1) Infrastructure spending execution (IIJA); (2) Construction cycle trends; (3) Aggregates pricing/volume dynamics; (4) Leverage path toward mid-2.0x range.

issuers.name: WEIBO CORP
issuers.creditComment:
WEIBO CORP
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Affirmed June 2025) | BBB/Stable (Fitch Affirmed October 2025) | Baa2/Stable (Moody's) – Leading Social Media Platform; Q3 2025 Financials Strong; Steady MAU/DAU; Strategic Alibaba Backing; Net Cash Position
​
Credit Momentum: Stable – Weibo Corporation maintains solid investment-grade ratings reflecting its established position as a leading social media platform in China, strong cash flow generation, and conservative financial policy.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Weibo maintains BBB/Stable from S&P (affirmed June 2025), BBB/Stable from Fitch (affirmed October 2025), and Baa2/Stable from Moody's. Ratings reflect: (a) resilient user base (MAU ~600M); (b) diversified advertising customer base; (c) strong net cash position ($3B+ liquidity); (d) strategic backing from Alibaba (major shareholder). Risks include advertising cyclicality and content regulation.
​
Financial & Operational Profile: Q3 2025 results scheduled for November 18, 2025. Q2 2025 showed steady recovery in advertising demand. Strong free cash flow generation supports share buybacks ($500M authorization). Debt leverage remains low (adjusted debt/EBITDA <1.5x). Repayment of convertible bonds has deleveraged the balance sheet.
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within China Internet 4-5% allocation. Solid investment-grade internet credit (BBB/Baa2) with strong liquidity and established platform value. Monitor: (1) Advertising market sentiment; (2) User engagement trends vs. short-video apps; (3) Regulatory content compliance; (4) Alibaba strategic relationship.

issuers.name: WESTINGHOUSE AIR BRAKE
issuers.creditComment:
WESTINGHOUSE AIR BRAKE TECHNOLOGIES CORP
Credit Rating: BBB/Stable (S&P Upgraded 2021, Affirmed May 2025) | B1/Positive (Moody's Upgraded Nov 2023) | B+/Positive (Fitch Upgraded Nov 2023, then Affirmed) – Locomotive & Railcar Brake Systems Leader; Rail Industry Tailwinds; Positive Outlooks
​

Credit Momentum: Positive (Multiple Upgrades; Positive Outlooks) – Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies Corp. 
 has achieved significant credit improvements. Moody's upgraded the CFR to B1 (from B2) and Fitch upgraded the IDR to B+ (from B) in November 2023, with Positive outlooks. S&P affirmed the 'BBB' issuer credit rating with a Stable outlook in May 2025 (upgraded from BB in 2021). The credit agencies cited strong competitive positioning, growing customer order books, EBITDA performance, and favorable rail industry tailwinds (freight rail recovery, passenger rail expansion, supply chain on-shoring).
​

Credit Ratings & Stability: Ratings reflect: (a) market leadership in locomotive and railcar brake systems, control equipment, and signaling; (b) essentially one customer (freight/passenger railroads) but with stable, long-term contracts; (c) benefits from rail infrastructure modernization and decarbonization; (d) strong operational execution; (e) potential for further upgrades given positive outlooks. The company offers essential safety-critical components with high switching costs.
​

Financial Profile: Westinghouse issued $500 million of 4.90% Senior Notes due 2030 and $750 million of 5.50% notes due 2035 in May 2025 (expected Moody's Baa2/S&P BBB/Fitch BBB ratings). The company maintains disciplined capital allocation and expects continued EBITDA growth. Liquidity is adequate with cash generation supporting debt service. The positive outlooks from Moody's and Fitch signal potential for upgrade to BBB/Ba2 if operational momentum continues.
​

Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Transportation Equipment 4-5% allocation. "Rising Star" credit (BBB/B1 Positive) with rail tailwinds. Monitor: (1) Freight rail volumes and pricing; (2) Passenger rail expansion (FRA funding); (3) EBITDA growth trajectory; (4) Order book conversion; (5) Upgrade timing from Moody's/Fitch.

issuers.name: WOODSIDE FINANCE LTD
issuers.creditComment:
WOODSIDE FINANCE LTD
Credit Rating: Baa1/Negative (Moody's) | BBB+/Negative (S&P Revised May 2025) – Bond-Issuing Vehicle for Woodside Energy Group; Notes Due 2028 (USQ98229AM12); Largest Australian Energy Producer; $17.5B Louisiana LNG Investment Weighs on Outlook
​
Credit Momentum: Negative Outlook on Major Capex – Woodside Finance Ltd issues debt guaranteed by Woodside Energy Group. In May 2025, S&P revised the outlook to negative (affirming BBB+) following the Final Investment Decision (FID) on the $17.5B Louisiana LNG project, citing reduced ratings headroom and execution risks associated with the massive capital outlay.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Woodside Finance maintains Baa1 from Moody's and BBB+ from S&P, both with Negative outlooks as of late 2025. Ratings reflect: (a) premier status as Australia's largest independent energy producer; (b) large-scale, low-cost LNG assets (Pluto, NWS, Scarborough); (c) strong balance sheet entering the investment phase; (d) acquisition of OCI's clean ammonia project. The negative outlook is driven by: (a) peak capex phase for Scarborough and Louisiana LNG; (b) execution risk on US expansion; (c) somewhat reduced financial flexibility during construction.
​
Bond Details (USQ98229AM12): 3.70% Senior Unsecured Notes due March 15, 2028. Issued by Woodside Finance Ltd and guaranteed by Woodside Energy Group Ltd. Notes rank pari passu with other senior unsecured debt. The 2028 maturity falls within the heavy capex window, requiring prudent liquidity management.
​
Parent Woodside Energy Financial Strength: H1 2025 results showed strong underlying performance. Liquidity remains high at ~$8.4B. The company targets a gearing range of 10-20% through the cycle, though recent major investments may test the upper bounds. Active portfolio management (e.g., sell-down of equity in major projects) is a key lever to manage exposure and credit metrics.
​
Overall Assessment: MARKET WEIGHT – 2-3% within Global Integrated Energy 4-5% allocation. Strong investment-grade credit (Baa1/BBB+) facing temporary pressure from a significant growth phase. The 2028 notes offer exposure to a top-tier LNG player. Monitor: (1) Execution of Scarborough and Louisiana LNG projects (cost/schedule); (2) Success in selling down equity stakes to de-risk US projects; (3) LNG pricing and uncontracted volume exposure; (4) Maintenance of gearing within target range.

issuers.name: XIAOMI BEST TIME INTL
issuers.creditComment:
XIAOMI BEST TIME INTERNATIONAL LTD
Credit Rating: Baa1/Stable (Moody's) | BBB/Stable (S&P) | BBB/Stable (Fitch) – Bond-Issuing Vehicle for Xiaomi Corp; Guaranteed by Xiaomi Corp; Global Top-3 Smartphone Maker; EV Expansion
​
Credit Momentum: Positive with EV Growth – Xiaomi Best Time International Limited is the primary offshore funding vehicle for Xiaomi Corporation. Ratings reflect Xiaomi's strong market position in smartphones and IoT, robust net cash position, and growing diversification into electric vehicles (EVs). Moody's rates the issuer Baa1, while S&P and Fitch rate it BBB.
​
Credit Ratings & Stability: Baa1/Stable (Moody's) and BBB/Stable (S&P/Fitch). Ratings are supported by: (a) top-3 global smartphone market share; (b) diverse ecosystem of IoT products; (c) strong liquidity and net cash position; (d) successful early execution in the EV segment (SU7 launch). The bonds are unconditionally and irrevocably guaranteed by Xiaomi Corporation.
​
Bond Details (e.g., US$800M 2.875% Due 2031, Green Bonds Due 2051): Outstanding issues include senior unsecured notes and green bonds. Proceeds support R&D, working capital, and green projects. The guarantee ensures alignment with Xiaomi's corporate credit.
​
Overall Assessment: OVERWEIGHT – 3-4% within Global Tech 5-6% allocation. Solid investment-grade tech credit (Baa1/BBB) with growth optionality from EVs and IoT. Monitor: (1) Smartphone market recovery; (2) EV segment profitability and capex; (3) Global geopolitical risks; (4) Internet services revenue growth.
